## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Validation And Secret Policy Slice

Status: In progress

Context:
- Paste transactions and typed coalescing are in place.
- The next M1.2 gap was validation state plus password/clipboard policy. This
  needs to be established before field-level copy/cut commands exist, because
  secret fields must not leak through semantics, debug capture, tests, or
  future adapters.

Changes:
- Added typed semantic-state getters for text selection, read-only state,
  obscured/redacted value state, and clipboard policy in
  [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart).
- Added `TextClipboardPolicy` to
  [text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart) and
  exported it from [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Added `enabled`, `readOnly`, `validationError`, and clipboard-policy
  semantics to `TextInput` and `TextArea`.
- Made read-only fields consume typed/pasted/deleting input without mutating
  while still allowing cursor movement.
- Made disabled owned text fields skip autofocus, expose disabled semantics,
  and ignore input.
- Redacted semantic `value` for obscured `TextInput` values and exposed a
  redacted clipboard policy by default.
- Updated `PasswordInput` to pass through enabled/read-only/validation state
  and explicitly use redacted clipboard policy.
- Added semantic and widget tests covering validation, read-only, disabled,
  redaction, and clipboard-policy state.

Decisions:
- Recorded the durable secret-policy decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep this slice semantics-first. Field-level copy/cut commands should be
  added later and use the same `TextClipboardPolicy` rather than defining a
  separate clipboard behavior.

Findings:
- `SelectionArea` already owns subtree-level clipboard writes, but editable
  fields are not currently selectable/copyable through field commands. That
  makes semantics/policy the right first step before adding copy/cut bindings.
- Redacting `SemanticNode.value` for obscured fields is necessary now because
  the semantic tree is already used by tests and debug surfaces.
- Owned focus nodes can safely honor `enabled`; caller-provided focus nodes
  retain caller ownership of `canRequestFocus`, matching the existing `Focus`
  widget contract.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation, tests, and docs.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/password_input.dart test/password_input_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/password_input_test.dart` remains blocked because local Dart
  is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury_widgets` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with field-level copy/cut enforcement, history navigation, or
  horizontal scroll. The strongest next technical risk is horizontal scroll
  because long command/composer input is visually broken today.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 TextInput Horizontal Scroll

Status: In progress

Context:
- Long single-line command/composer input was still visually broken: bounded
  `TextInput` clipped from the left edge and could hide the cursor entirely.
- The editing model is grapheme-safe, but horizontal viewport math belongs in
  rendering because it depends on terminal cell width, obscured display width,
  and live constraints.

Changes:
- Added render-object horizontal scroll state to
  [text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart).
- `RenderTextInput.performLayout` now computes displayed width, keeps the
  active cursor/selection extent visible, and snaps scroll-left to displayed
  grapheme boundaries so wide characters are not split at the viewport edge.
- `RenderTextInput.paint` now paints only graphemes fully inside the visible
  display-cell window and paints the trailing cursor cell when scrolled to the
  end.
- Added `TextInput` tests for bounded-width trailing cursor visibility,
  scrolling back to the start, wide-grapheme boundary handling, obscured-field
  display width, and visible selected range after scrolling.
- Updated M1.2 tracker notes to mark `TextInput` horizontal scroll complete
  while keeping `TextArea` horizontal scroll open.

Decisions:
- Recorded the durable render-state decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep this slice focused on single-line input. `TextArea` needs a separate
  horizontal viewport decision because it combines per-line horizontal
  scrolling with vertical scroll and possible future soft-wrap.

Findings:
- The render layer already had enough terminal width information to solve this
  without changing `TextEditingValue`.
- Obscured fields must scroll by displayed obscuring-character width, not raw
  secret grapheme width.
- Avoiding partial wide-grapheme rendering prevents cells outside the widget
  bounds from being touched by a wide character at the right edge.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed `TextInput` implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_input_test.dart` remains blocked because local
  Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with either `TextArea` horizontal scroll, field-level copy/cut
  enforcement, or history navigation. `TextArea` horizontal scroll is the
  natural follow-up if we want to close the horizontal-rendering gap fully.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 TextArea Horizontal Scroll

Status: In progress

Context:
- `TextInput` horizontal scroll was complete, but multiline fields still
  clipped long lines from column zero.
- `TextArea` combines vertical scrolling, line-local cursor columns, selection
  ranges, and future soft-wrap considerations, so it needed a separate render
  pass rather than blindly sharing `TextInput` code.

Changes:
- Added horizontal scroll state to
  [text_area.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart).
- `RenderTextArea.performLayout` now keeps the active cursor/selection extent
  visible horizontally on the active line while preserving existing vertical
  scroll behavior.
- `RenderTextArea.paint` now renders only full graphemes inside the visible
  display-cell window, so wide characters are not split at the left or right
  edge.
- Added `TextArea` tests for bounded-width trailing cursor visibility,
  scrolling back to line start, wide-grapheme boundary handling, selected range
  visibility after scrolling, and resetting horizontal scroll when moving to a
  short line.
- Updated M1.2 tracker notes so horizontal scroll is no longer listed as an
  open built-in text-field rendering gap. Soft-wrap remains a separate future
  feature, not part of this slice.

Decisions:
- Generalized the horizontal-scroll decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md) from `TextInput` to editable text render
  objects.
- Use one horizontal viewport offset for the `TextArea` render object. It
  resets when the active line fits, which avoids carrying a long-line scroll
  offset into short lines.

Findings:
- The existing vertical scroll state and line-offset helpers were enough to
  add horizontal scroll without changing `TextEditingValue`.
- A single render-level horizontal viewport is pragmatic for non-wrapped
  multiline editing. Per-line horizontal offsets would make the inactive lines
  less predictable and complicate selection painting.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed `TextArea` implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_area_test.dart` remains blocked because local
  Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with field-level copy/cut enforcement or history navigation.
  Copy/cut is the better next safety slice because `TextClipboardPolicy` now
  exists but is not yet enforced by field commands.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Field Copy Cut Enforcement

Status: In progress

Context:
- `TextClipboardPolicy` existed in semantics, but focused text fields did not
  enforce it through actual keyboard commands.
- `SelectionArea` already expected Ctrl+C to copy and bubble when no selection
  exists, but `runTui` intercepted Ctrl+C before dispatching. That meant real
  runtime copy behavior could not work consistently even though widget tests
  could exercise it.

Changes:
- Added `TextEditingController.hasSelection`, `selectedText`, and
  `deleteSelection` in
  [text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart).
- Added Ctrl+C/Ctrl+X handling for `TextInput` and `TextArea` selections.
- `TextClipboardPolicy.allowed` writes selected text to `Clipboard.instance`.
- `TextClipboardPolicy.redacted` writes masked content instead of raw content,
  preserving newlines for `TextArea`.
- `TextClipboardPolicy.disabled` consumes selected-field copy/cut commands
  without writing or bubbling.
- Read-only fields can copy selected text but do not cut.
- Semantic copy actions are exposed only when the field is enabled, has a
  selection, and clipboard policy allows a copy action.
- Updated `runTui` so Ctrl+C dispatches first and exits only when ignored,
  preserving the escape hatch while letting copy handlers work.
- Added controller, `TextInput`, `TextArea`, semantics, and runtime tests for
  selected text extraction, copy, cut, redaction, disabled policy, read-only
  behavior, bubbling, and Ctrl+C exit fallback.

Decisions:
- Recorded the runtime Ctrl+C first-refusal decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep copy/cut at the widget layer for now. App-kernel command registration
  can later expose these as semantic commands, but the policy enforcement
  belongs with the field that owns selection state.

Findings:
- The runtime Ctrl+C guard needed to become a fallback rather than a preemptive
  intercept. This aligns with existing `SelectionArea` behavior and keeps
  unhandled Ctrl+C as a guaranteed exit path.
- Redacted cut is safe because the clipboard receives masked content while the
  field deletion still records through the controller undo stack.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with history navigation, completion, richer keymaps, or
  large-paste scheduling. History navigation is the most contained next
  editing behavior; richer keymaps should probably wait until app-kernel
  command semantics are closer.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 TextInput History Navigation

Status: In progress

Context:
- RFC 0014 calls for command history to be layered above the editing value,
  not mixed into undo/redo or render objects.
- `TextInput` is used inside composed widgets such as command palettes and
  autocompletes where Up/Down often belong to the parent widget. History
  navigation therefore needed to be explicit instead of a default key grab.

Changes:
- Added `TextHistoryController` in
  [text_history.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/editing/text_history.dart).
- `TextHistoryController` stores committed entries, skips empty values and
  consecutive duplicates by default, enforces a max length, captures the
  current draft on first history navigation, and restores the draft after
  navigating past the newest entry.
- Exported `TextHistoryController` from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Added optional `historyController` and `commitHistoryOnSubmit` parameters to
  `TextInput`.
- `TextInput` now handles plain Up/Down as history navigation only when a
  history controller is supplied. Without one, Up/Down continue bubbling to
  parent widgets.
- Pressing Enter commits the submitted value to history before `onSubmit`
  runs when `historyController` is present and `commitHistoryOnSubmit` is true.
- Editing operations reset active history browsing so Down no longer restores
  a stale draft after the user modifies a recalled entry.
- Semantic state for text fields can now expose `historyCount`,
  `historyIndex`, and `historyBrowsing`.
- Added controller, widget, and semantic tests for history commit, navigation,
  draft restore, parent bubbling, submit ordering, and browsing reset.

Decisions:
- Recorded the history-controller decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep history on `TextInput` for this slice. `TextArea` already uses Up/Down
  for multiline cursor movement; multiline composer history should wait for
  richer keymap/app-kernel command semantics.

Findings:
- Separating submission history from undo/redo keeps each concept clear:
  undo/redo replays editing transactions inside one value, while submission
  history swaps accepted values into the field.
- Opt-in history avoids a subtle regression in composed widgets. Command
  palettes and autocomplete dropdowns can keep using ancestor key bindings for
  Up/Down until they deliberately choose a history policy.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/editing/text_history.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` in `packages/fleury` is blocked by local Dart 3.6.0
  parsing SDK-3.10+ test syntax already in the package.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with completion scaffolding or large-paste scheduling. The
  completion slice should probably start with controller/model state and
  range replacement before any popup UI so it can later share primitives with
  command palette and autocomplete.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Completion Scaffolding

Status: In progress

Context:
- RFC 0014 calls completion an editing-aware adjunct that can read the current
  value, selection, token/range context, and validation state.
- The existing `Autocomplete` widget has popup behavior, but M1.2 needs a core
  completion primitive that command palette, autocomplete, agent composers, and
  future popup UI can all share.

Changes:
- Added `TextCompletionOption`, `TextCompletionState`, and
  `TextCompletionController` in
  [text_completion.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/editing/text_completion.dart).
- Added `TextEditingModel.replaceRange` for arbitrary grapheme-snapped range
  replacement and `TextEditingController.replaceRange` so completion accepts
  are undoable editing transactions.
- Exported completion primitives from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Added optional `completionController` to `TextInput`.
- `TextInput` now lets active completion state own Up/Down option movement
  before submission history, accepts the selected completion with Tab, and
  closes active completion on Escape before calling `onEscape`.
- `TextInput` still bubbles Tab when completion has no selected option, so
  focus traversal and parent key bindings remain usable.
- Semantic state for text fields can now expose `completionActive`,
  `completionQuery`, `completionRangeStart`, `completionRangeEnd`,
  `completionOptionCount`, and `completionSelectedIndex`.
- Added editing, controller, widget, and semantic tests for completion option
  defaults, open/update/move/accept behavior, range replacement, undo, key
  precedence, Escape behavior, Tab bubbling, and semantic state.

Decisions:
- Recorded the completion-state-first decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep popup rendering out of this slice. The primitive now exists for popup
  UI and provider policies to build on without coupling those choices to the
  core text field.

Findings:
- Completion acceptance should be a normal undoable edit. Applying completion
  through `TextEditingController.replaceRange` preserves Ctrl+Z behavior,
  whereas assigning `controller.value` would reset editing history.
- Active completion needs priority over submission history for Up/Down. When
  both controllers are present, the focused completion interaction is the more
  specific mode; history stays inactive until completion closes.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/editing/text_completion.dart lib/src/editing/text_editing.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/editing/text_completion_test.dart test/editing/text_editing_model_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` in `packages/fleury` remains blocked by local Dart
  3.6.0 parsing SDK-3.10+ test syntax already in the package.
- `dart test test/editing/text_completion_test.dart test/editing/text_editing_model_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with either rendered completion UI/provider integration or
  large-paste scheduling. Large-paste scheduling is likely the better next
  foundation slice because completion now has a core model, while paste
  responsiveness still affects all heavy CLI workflows.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Large Paste Scheduling

Status: In progress

Context:
- Bracketed paste already routes through `TextInputClaimant.onPaste`, and
  paste already records as one undo transaction.
- Large pasted blobs can still monopolize the event loop if applied in one
  synchronous controller mutation. Developer tools, agent consoles, config
  editors, and log/composer workflows need paste to remain responsive.

Changes:
- Added `TextPastePolicy`, `TextPasteProgress`, and `TextPasteSession` in
  [text_paste.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/editing/text_paste.dart).
- `TextPastePolicy` decides when paste should be chunked and produces
  grapheme-safe chunks without splitting emoji or combining sequences.
- `TextEditingController.paste` now supports explicit coalescing so scheduled
  paste chunks still undo as one paste transaction.
- Added `pastePolicy` to `TextInput` and `TextArea`.
- `TextInput` normalizes single-line paste before chunking, so CR/LF handling
  is stable even when chunks are small.
- `TextArea` preserves raw newlines while using the same paste scheduler.
- `TextInput` and `TextArea` apply the first chunk immediately, then schedule
  remaining chunks with `TuiBinding.addPostFrameCallback`, falling back to a
  microtask when no binding is installed.
- User edits, movement, history navigation, completion accept, undo/redo,
  controller swaps, disable/read-only transitions, and disposal cancel any
  pending paste chunks.
- Semantic state for text fields and text areas now exposes
  `pasteInProgress`, `pasteInsertedLength`, and `pasteTotalLength`.
- Added policy/session tests, controller coalesced-paste undo tests, `TextInput`
  and `TextArea` chunked-paste behavior tests, cancellation tests, and semantic
  paste-progress tests.

Decisions:
- Recorded the large-paste scheduling decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep the scheduler widget-owned for now, because scheduling depends on
  runtime frame/post-frame services, while chunk policy/session state remains
  framework-core editing code.

Findings:
- Normalizing single-line paste before chunking avoids CRLF boundary bugs where
  `\r\n` could otherwise become two spaces if split across chunks.
- Coalesced paste chunks need an explicit controller flag. Globally coalescing
  every paste event would make two separate paste operations undo as one edit,
  which is not what users expect.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/editing/text_paste.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/editing/text_paste_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` in `packages/fleury` remains blocked by local Dart
  3.6.0 parsing SDK-3.10+ test syntax already in the package.
- `dart test test/editing/text_paste_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue M1.2 with either rendered completion UI/provider integration or
  richer keymap scaffolding. Rendered completion UI is the more user-visible
  text-editing feature now that completion state and paste scheduling are in
  place, but richer keymaps may be better deferred until M1.3 command/app
  shell semantics are active.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Rendered Completion UI

Status: In progress

Context:
- Core completion state, range replacement, Tab acceptance, and semantics now
  exist, but developers still need a rendered provider-backed completion
  experience.
- Existing `Autocomplete` proves anchored overlays work, but it owns its own
  filtering and replacement path. The new completion primitive needs a widget
  that composes with `TextCompletionController` instead.

Changes:
- Added `CompletionTextInput`, `TextCompletionRequest`,
  `TextCompletionProvider`, `TextCompletionRequestBuilder`, and
  `defaultTextCompletionRequest` in
  [completion_text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/completion_text_input.dart).
- Exported the new completion input APIs from
  [fleury_widgets.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart).
- `CompletionTextInput` wraps core `TextInput` with an `Anchor`/`Follower`
  overlay menu, provider-backed options, and a current-word request builder.
- The rendered menu uses `TextCompletionController` as the source of truth, so
  core `TextInput` still handles Up/Down selection movement, Tab acceptance,
  Escape dismissal, semantic field state, and undoable range replacement.
- Added `TextInput.onCompletionAccepted` so higher-level widgets can observe
  accepted options without duplicating core key handling.
- Completion menu rows contribute semantics as `menu`/`menuItem` nodes with
  query, row count, selected row, labels, details, and row indices.
- Added widget tests for provider query/range extraction, menu rendering,
  Down+Tab acceptance, accepted-option callbacks, current-word replacement,
  Escape dismissal ordering, Tab bubbling when closed, and menu semantics.

Decisions:
- Recorded the core-versus-widget completion split in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Leave existing `Autocomplete` untouched for this slice. It can later be
  refactored onto `CompletionTextInput` if the API proves stable.

Findings:
- Keeping `TextInput` as the owner of completion key handling avoids two
  sources of truth for Tab/Escape/Up/Down behavior. The widget package only
  supplies provider policy and rendering.
- The default current-word request builder should use grapheme boundaries, not
  naive code-unit walking, so completion ranges do not split emoji or composed
  characters before the caret.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/completion_text_input.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/completion_text_input_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/completion_text_input_test.dart` remains blocked because
  local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury_widgets` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_input_test.dart` remains blocked because local
  Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- M1.2 is now largely down to richer keymap scaffolding and IME extension
  points. Richer keymaps should probably wait until M1.3 app-shell commands
  are active, so the next practical move is to either add explicit IME/
  composing extension seams or start M1.3 `FleuryApp` shell work.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 IME Composition Seams

Status: Completed

Context:
- M1.2 needed a future-safe input-method seam without turning MVP scope into
  native terminal IME protocol support.
- The text editing model already carried a `composing` range, but widgets and
  controllers did not expose a usable update/commit/cancel flow.

Changes:
- Added grapheme-snapped composing range helpers to
  [text_editing.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/editing/text_editing.dart):
  `setComposingRange`, `clearComposing`, `updateComposing`, and
  `commitComposing`.
- Added `TextEditingController` composition APIs in
  [text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart):
  `composing`, `hasComposingRange`, `setComposingRange`, `clearComposing`,
  `updateComposingText`, `commitComposing`, and `cancelComposing`.
- Interim composition updates now avoid undo history; committing a changed
  composition records one undo step back to the pre-composition value.
- Cancelling composition restores the pre-composition value without creating
  redo history.
- `TextInput` and `TextArea` semantic state now exposes
  `composingActive`, `composingStart`, and `composingEnd`.
- Added model, controller, and semantic tests for composing range behavior,
  commit replacement, single-line normalization, cancellation, undo grouping,
  and semantic query state.

Decisions:
- Recorded the composition seam decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep full native terminal IME protocol support out of the MVP. This slice
  only gives future adapters durable model/controller/semantic behavior.

Findings:
- Composition commit should be one undo transaction, not a sequence of interim
  edits. This mirrors how users think about a completed input-method choice.
- `undo` during an active uncommitted composition should cancel the composition
  first rather than skipping past it into older undo history.
- `clearComposing` is intentionally not an undoable commit; adapters that want
  committed text should call `commitComposing`.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/editing/text_editing.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart test/editing/text_editing_model_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/editing/text_editing_model_test.dart test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- M1.2 is now down to richer keymap scaffolding. If that remains intentionally
  deferred to app-kernel command work, the next implementation move should be
  M1.3 `FleuryApp` shell.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.3 Command Registry V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.3 needs a `FleuryApp` shell, but the first stable primitive should be
  structural app commands. Screens, palettes, key hints, tester APIs, and
  future adapters all need named commands before they need default shell UI.
- Existing `KeyBindings` already owns keyboard routing, so app commands should
  layer metadata and invocation semantics over that path rather than bypass it.

Changes:
- Added [commands.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/commands.dart) with:
  `CommandId`, `CommandContext`, `AppCommand`, `CommandInvocationStatus`,
  `CommandInvocationResult`, `CommandRegistry`, `CommandRegistryScope`, and
  `CommandScope`.
- Exported app-command APIs from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- `CommandRegistry` supports parent/local command resolution with nearest
  scope winning, visible/enabled predicates, active command discovery, direct
  async-capable invocation, and typed completed/disabled/notFound/failed
  results.
- `CommandScope` installs a scoped registry, emits shortcut bindings through
  the existing `KeyBindings` path, and contributes semantic command nodes.
- Added typed semantic state accessors for `commandCategory` and
  `commandCount`.
- Added [command registry tests](../../packages/fleury/test/app/command_registry_test.dart)
  for parent/local resolution, invocation results, command semantics,
  invisible commands, enabled shortcuts, and disabled shortcuts.

Decisions:
- Recorded the command-registry-first decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep command enablement synchronous for v0. Async work can still run from a
  command, but availability should come from ordinary app state and rebuilds.

Findings:
- Keyboard shortcut support is best handled by having `CommandScope` emit
  `KeyBinding`s for local command shortcuts. This preserves the existing focus
  chain, sequence handling, bubbling, and hint-bar path.
- Semantic command nodes should be contributed by scopes for their local
  commands. Full active-registry semantics can be added once `FleuryApp` owns
  the app-level registry and screen scopes.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/command_registry_test.dart` remains blocked because
  local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Final targeted analyzer pass across the command-registry and composition
  files passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.3 with screen registry and `FleuryApp` shell/controller, then
  wire registry-backed palette/key hints, tester command invocation, status,
  and debug inspector evidence.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.3 FleuryApp And Screen Registry V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- Command registry v0 gave M1.3 named commands and shortcut dispatch. The next
  missing layer was the app shell: registered screens, active screen state, and
  a root app scope that commands/tests/widgets can address structurally.
- The RFC keeps top-level screens separate from `Navigator` so persistent app
  sections do not overload the modal/route stack.

Changes:
- Added [app.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/app.dart) with
  `ScreenId`, `FleuryScreen`, `ScreenController`, `ScreenControllerScope`,
  `FleuryAppController`, `FleuryAppScope`, `FleuryApp`, `ActiveScreenView`,
  and `FleuryCommandContext`.
- Exported the app-shell APIs from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- `FleuryApp` now installs root command, screen, and app scopes; emits
  app-level command shortcuts through `KeyBindings`; contributes app command
  semantic nodes; and exposes app/screen state through semantics.
- `ActiveScreenView` renders the active registered screen and can optionally
  keep inactive screens mounted with `preserveState`.
- Screen-local commands are scoped through `CommandScope`; inactive preserved
  screens disable their command scope so hidden shortcuts do not stay active.
- Added `CommandScope.enabled` for active/inactive scope control.
- Added typed semantic state accessors for `screenId`, `screenShortTitle`,
  `activeScreenId`, and `screenCount`.
- Added [Fleury app tests](../../packages/fleury/test/app/fleury_app_test.dart)
  for screen activation, active screen rendering, app/screen semantics, global
  command shortcuts, and active screen-local command resolution.

Decisions:
- Recorded the registered-screen-controller and inactive-command-scope
  decisions in [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep `FleuryApp` optional: tiny apps can still mount ordinary widgets, while
  production apps can opt into app commands/screens.

Findings:
- Building screen content through an inner context is required so screen
  builders can see their screen-local `CommandScope`.
- Preserved screens need more focus policy before they can be the default. The
  v0 default renders only the active screen; `preserveState` exists for apps
  that want to experiment while hidden command scopes remain disabled.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.3 with a minimal status model, then registry-backed command
  palette/key hints, tester invocation helpers, and debug inspector surfaces.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.3 Status Model And AppStatusBar

Status: In progress

Context:
- RFC 0012 treats status as app-kernel state for active screen, task,
  diagnostics, and capability fallback, not as arbitrary footer text.
- The current app shell needed a shared status model before registry-backed
  palettes, key hints, tester helpers, and inspector surfaces can show a
  coherent app picture.

Changes:
- Added [status.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/status.dart) with
  `StatusSeverity`, `StatusItem`, `StatusController`, and `AppStatusBar`.
- Added `FleuryApp.status` as a derived status builder over
  `FleuryAppController`, so status can track active screen and command state.
- Added `StatusController` to `FleuryAppController` and
  `FleuryCommandContext.status` so commands can update status directly when an
  app does not use a derived status builder.
- `AppStatusBar` renders current status items and contributes semantic status
  nodes with status IDs, severities, optional command action IDs, and count
  state.
- Added typed semantic state accessors for `statusId` and `statusCount`.
- Exported the status APIs from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Expanded [Fleury app tests](../../packages/fleury/test/app/fleury_app_test.dart)
  for status controller updates, derived status rendering, semantic status
  state, command-linked status actions, and direct command-context status
  updates.

Decisions:
- Recorded the status-as-kernel-service decision in
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md).
- Keep `AppStatusBar` deliberately small. It proves rendering and semantics,
  while richer app chrome can wait for the proof-app shell.

Findings:
- Derived status and imperative status updates need different behavior. When a
  `FleuryApp.status` builder is absent, command-context status updates must not
  be cleared by unrelated command registry notifications.
- Status action links should reference `CommandId`; invocation wiring can be
  added once tester helpers and registry-backed palette/key-hint adapters are
  in place.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed implementation and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/src/app/status.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  remains blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.3 with registry-backed command palette/key hints, tester
  invocation helpers, and debug inspector surfaces.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.3 Registry-Backed Command Palette And Key Hints

Status: In progress

Context:
- The existing command palette was a useful widget-level callback palette, but
  app-kernel commands need one source of truth for IDs, labels, descriptions,
  categories, shortcuts, visible/enabled state, and invocation results.
- A palette presented from shell/modal chrome may sit outside the active
  screen's `CommandScope`, so it cannot rely only on the nearest registry
  lookup to see screen-local commands.

Changes:
- Extended [command_palette.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/command_palette.dart)
  with command IDs, categories, shortcut labels, enabled state, richer search
  text, disabled-row styling, and command row semantic metadata.
- Added `AppCommandPalette` in `fleury_widgets` as the registry-backed adapter
  over the existing callback palette.
- Added `CommandRegistry.isVisible` and `CommandRegistry.invokeCommand` in
  [commands.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/commands.dart) so command
  surfaces can check policy and invoke the exact command object they resolved.
- `AppCommandPalette` now folds active screen commands in before app-global
  commands, de-dupes by `CommandId`, and invokes the concrete selected command
  instance. This preserves screen-local shadowing even when the palette is
  mounted at app shell level.
- Exported `AppCommandPalette` from
  [fleury_widgets.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart).
- Expanded [command palette tests](../../packages/fleury_widgets/test/command_palette_test.dart)
  for registry-backed invocation, palette-row semantics, disabled commands,
  and active-screen command priority over app commands.
- Expanded [app shell tests](../../packages/fleury/test/app/fleury_app_test.dart)
  to confirm app command shortcuts appear in `KeyHintBar`.

Decisions:
- Keep command registry, command identity, and invocation in core; keep the
  rendered registry-backed palette adapter in `fleury_widgets`.
- Treat active screen commands as higher priority than app-global commands in
  app-level palette surfaces, and invoke the concrete resolved command rather
  than re-resolving by ID through the app registry.

Findings:
- `CommandRegistry.invoke(id)` alone is insufficient for shell-level palettes:
  when a screen command and an app command share the same ID, invoking by ID
  through the app registry can run the app command. `invokeCommand` centralizes
  policy/error recording while preserving the already-resolved priority.
- Palette semantic tests must inspect actual palette rows, not just any
  semantic command node, because the app shell also contributes command
  semantics for global commands.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed command/app palette implementation and
  tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/src/app/status.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/command_palette.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/command_palette_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and
  `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/command_palette_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` is
  blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury_widgets` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Finish M1.3 with tester command invocation helpers and debug inspector
  command/app-state surfaces.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.3 Tester Helpers And Debug App-State Surfaces

Status: Complete

Context:
- After commands, screens, status, key hints, and the registry-backed palette,
  M1.3 still needed test ergonomics and debug visibility. Tests should be able
  to invoke command IDs directly, and the inspector should answer basic app
  questions without bespoke app-kernel wiring.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryTester.commandRegistry`, `FleuryTester.invokeCommand`, and
  `FleuryTester.lastCommandResult` in
  [fleury_tester.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart).
- Tester command resolution now checks nearest local command scopes first,
  then active screen commands, then app/parent commands. This matches modal and
  screen shadowing while still supporting app-shell test contexts.
- Added app semantic state for latest command ID and latest invocation status
  in [app.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/app.dart) and typed accessors
  in [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart).
- Expanded the debug Tree tab in
  [debug_panel.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart) to
  show active screen, screen count, command count, status count, latest command
  result, and command metadata from the semantic snapshot.
- Expanded [Fleury app tests](../../packages/fleury/test/app/fleury_app_test.dart)
  and [debug shell tests](../../packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart)
  for tester command invocation, active-screen command priority, latest command
  semantics, and debug app/command summaries.

Decisions:
- Keep tester command resolution aligned with user-visible command priority:
  focused/local scopes, then active screen, then app/global parents.
- Keep debug app-kernel state fed through the semantic graph rather than adding
  a separate app debug provider.

Findings:
- Latest command result belongs in app semantics because tester assertions,
  debug inspection, and future replay/debug-capture hooks all need the same
  low-cost status.
- A debug panel can provide useful app/command evidence now without knowing
  about `FleuryAppController`, which keeps it usable for apps that contribute
  semantic app nodes without adopting every shell primitive.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for changed tester, app, semantics, debug, and test
  files.
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/debug/debug_state.dart lib/src/app/app.dart lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed with no issues in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed with no issues in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and
  `packages/fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/command_palette_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` is
  blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `packages/fleury_widgets` requires
  SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` returned no old framework or
  source-package names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Start M1.4 by creating the example subpackage proof app skeleton and use it
  to pressure-test the app shell, command palette, status, text input,
  semantics, and debug surfaces before moving to later data/effects work.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.4 Proof App Package Skeleton

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.3 made the app shell usable enough to move beyond unit-style coverage.
  The next forcing function is a package-level proof app that combines
  commands, screens, status, palette, text input, table data, diagnostics, and
  tester semantics in one retained app.
- The Phase 0 scenario already names `packages/fleury_example_console` as the
  current-cycle proof harness.

Changes:
- Added [packages/fleury_example_console](../../packages/fleury_example_console)
  as a standalone internal package with path dependencies on `fleury` and
  `fleury_widgets`.
- Added [ProofConsoleApp](../../packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart)
  with Overview, Runs, Transcript, and Diagnostics screens declared through
  `FleuryApp`.
- Wired global and screen-local app commands for navigation, command palette,
  fake task start/cancel, diagnostics, stream toggling, debug capture, and run
  filter focus.
- Added app status items, `KeyHintBar`, `AppStatusBar`, `AppCommandPalette`
  access, `TextInput` filters/composer, a `Table` run fixture, `ProgressBar`,
  deterministic transcript events, and a debug capture counter.
- Added [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  covering initial app semantics, command-driven navigation, fake task status,
  run filtering, diagnostics/debug capture, and latest command result state.
- Added [bin/fleury_example_console.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/bin/fleury_example_console.dart)
  as a runnable entrypoint using `runTui`.

Decisions:
- Keep this as a package under `packages/` rather than a loose example file so
  it can grow tests, fixtures, benchmarks, and eventually a local CI target.
- Use local deterministic state only. No Dune, ACP, network, or subprocess
  dependency enters this cycle.

Findings:
- The repo has no root Dart workspace yet. The new package is valid as a
  standalone Dart package, but local `dart pub get` and normal `dart test` are
  blocked until the local SDK is updated to the package constraint.
- For immediate analyzer feedback, `dart analyze` can reuse the existing
  `fleury_widgets` package config with `--packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json`.

Validation:

## 2026-05-31 - M1.4 Proof Workflow Coverage

Status: In progress

Context:
- The proof app package existed, but the next acceptance pressure was an
  actual workflow: open the command palette, navigate, filter/select table
  rows, submit composer text, append streamed logs, and surface debug capture
  evidence.

Changes:
- Added screen-local proof commands for focusing the runs table, focusing the
  transcript composer, and appending a deterministic transcript/log burst.
- Added focus nodes for the runs table and transcript composer so workflow
  tests exercise focus and input routing through app commands instead of
  directly mutating controllers.
- Wired table `onSelect` to append a transcript event for the selected run.
- Wired deterministic log bursts to append three transcript events while the
  stream is enabled; the command returns disabled while the stream is paused.
- Expanded [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to cover in-app command palette navigation, runs table selection semantics
  and activation, composer submission, log burst append/disabled behavior, and
  debug snapshot evidence visible from Transcript.

Findings:
- The proof app immediately validates the M1.3 priority model: tests can
  invoke active screen commands such as table/composer focus without needing
  a build context inside that screen.
- Deterministic local "stream" commands give enough pressure for app-shell,
  text, and transcript behavior before the worker/task model exists.

  to assert navigation semantics, app command palette row metadata, progress
  state, selected table cell row/column state, composer value/focus, transcript
  log state, diagnostic capability/fallback state, and debug-capture action.

  no core semantic model changes were needed for this slice.
- The proof app now gives a concrete regression target for the semantic app

## 2026-05-31 - M1.6 Task Controller V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- The proof app's fake worker was still ad hoc booleans and integers. M1.6
  needs framework-native async work with structured progress, output,
  cancellation, restart behavior, result/error state, and semantic visibility.

Changes:
- Added [task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart) with
  `TaskController`, `TaskContext`, `TaskProgress`, `TaskOutput`,
  `TaskResult`, `TaskStatus`, `TaskCanceled`, and `TaskStatusView`.
- Exported the task API from [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart)
  so it is available through both `package:fleury/fleury_core.dart` and
  `package:fleury/fleury.dart`.
- Added [task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart)
  covering success, failure, cancellation, restart/stale-run handling, reset,
  and task semantics.
- Replaced the proof console fake-worker booleans with `TaskController` in
  [fleury_example_console.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart).
- Expanded [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to assert task semantics, output source, progress state, cancel action, and
  canceled status.

Decisions:
- Keep commands as triggers for work, not owners of worker state. The task
  controller owns lifecycle, progress, output, restart, result/error, and
  semantics.
- Keep subprocess/isolate details out of v0. The first worker API should be a
  lightweight async binding surface; subprocess handoff remains a separate
  explicit case.
- Use per-run task output by clearing output when a task starts. That avoids
  mixing stale logs into current task semantics while preserving the latest
  run's output after completion/cancellation.
- Let `cancel()` and `reset()` settle the public `start()` future promptly with
  a canceled result. Runner code may still unwind later, but late progress and
  output are ignored by generation/status checks.

Findings:
- `TaskStatusView` gives the semantic graph a durable `SemanticRole.task`
  surface without forcing app code to duplicate task status annotations.
- Stale restarted runs must resolve as canceled even if the old runner throws
  after a newer run has already completed. Otherwise restarts leak irrelevant
  failures into callers.
- The proof console now gives M1.6 realistic pressure: command enablement,
  status bar state, progress UI, transcript activity, and semantic task state
  all update from one controller.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  completed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` in `packages/fleury` is still blocked by existing
  analyzer issues outside this slice under local Dart 3.6.0:
  duplicate `_` parameters in `test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart`, missing
  identifiers in `test/widgets/key_chord_constants_test.dart`, and existing
  parameter-name lint infos.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart` is blocked because local Dart is
  3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` is blocked because local Dart is
  3.6.0 and `fleury_example_console` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:

- Continue M1.6 with the subprocess/terminal handoff boundary or structured
  effect-event hooks, depending on which gap blocks terminal diagnose and
  scenario benchmark work next.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.6 Terminal Handoff And Process Task V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.6 cannot stop at a fake worker. Fleury needs a path for real developer
  subprocesses and external-editor style handoff without corrupting terminal
  mode, leaking framework frames into child-process output, or making the
  portable core depend on `dart:io`.

Changes:
- Added `TerminalHandoffDriver` and `withTerminalHandoff` to
  [terminal_driver.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/terminal_driver.dart).
- Implemented handoff in [FakeTerminalDriver](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/fake_driver.dart)
  with counters, write suppression during handoff, and a resize event after
  resume.
- Implemented handoff in [PosixTerminalDriver](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart)
  by restoring cooked input/user-facing terminal modes before the operation,
  suppressing framework writes during the handoff, re-entering the previous
  mode afterward, flushing cleanup bytes, and forcing a resize/repaint event.
- Added [process_task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/process_task.dart)
  with `ProcessTaskCommand`, `ProcessTaskController`, `ProcessTaskResult`, and
  `ProcessTaskException`.
- Exported process tasks only from [fleury.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury.dart)
  and exported the handoff seam from [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Added [process_task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart)
  covering stdout/stderr capture, non-zero exit failure metadata,
  cancellation, and optional terminal handoff.
- Expanded [fake_driver_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart)
  for handoff suspend/resume behavior, write suppression, and inactive-driver
  fallback.

Decisions:
- `TerminalHandoffDriver` stays platform-neutral. It belongs in core because
  it is a terminal lifecycle contract, not process spawning.
- `ProcessTaskController` stays native-only. Real subprocess execution depends
  on `dart:io`, so it is exported through `package:fleury/fleury.dart` rather
  than `fleury_core.dart`.
- Handoff suppresses framework frame writes while the child operation owns the
  terminal and sends a resize event after resume so the next frame repaints
  from current terminal dimensions.

Findings:
- The existing POSIX driver already had the hard pieces for job-control
  suspend/resume. Generalizing that shape gives subprocess/external-editor
  handoff a small, testable seam.
- `ProcessTaskController` can reuse `OutputCapture` to split stdout/stderr into
  ordered task output while preserving source/severity metadata for status,
  logs, semantics, and future debug capture.
- Cancellation must kill the active process as well as settle the task future.
  It also needs to handle the race where cancellation is requested before
  `Process.start` finishes.

Validation:
- `dart format` completed for the new terminal/process files and tests.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/terminal_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/fake_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart lib/fleury.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart lib/src/terminal/terminal_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/fake_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/fleury.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.6 with either structured effect-event hooks or first sanitized
  process-output policy integration. External editor API shape remains pending.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.6 Structured Task Events

Status: In progress

Context:
- The task model had structured state, but devtools/replay/testing still had to
  infer ordering from controller snapshots. M1.6 needs lightweight event hooks
  for workers and subprocesses without committing to a full replay artifact.

Changes:
- Added `TaskEventKind` and generic `TaskEvent<T>` to
  [task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart).
- Added bounded `TaskController.events` history with sequence numbers, run IDs,
  status snapshots, progress payloads, output payloads, result values, errors,
  and stack traces.
- Emitted events for task start, progress, output, success, failure,
  cancellation, and reset.
- Added task event semantic state in [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart):
  `taskEventCount` and `lastTaskEventKind`.
- Exported `TaskEvent` and `TaskEventKind` from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Expanded [task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart)
  to assert event ordering, run IDs, bounded history, failure payloads,
  cancellation behavior, reset event state, and semantic event state.
- Expanded [process_task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart)
  to assert process task output/failure/cancellation events.
- Expanded [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to assert fake-worker task event semantics.

Decisions:
- Keep task event history bounded by `maxEventEntries`, defaulting to 400. The
  controller should be useful for devtools and tests without becoming an
  unbounded replay database.
- Use sequence numbers and run IDs, not timestamps, for v0. Replay-clock and
  fake-time semantics should be defined before event time becomes public
  behavior.
- Keep full replay artifacts deferred. `TaskEvent` is the first effect hook,
  not a file format or deterministic replay runner.

Findings:
- Event history makes stale-run behavior explicit: restart emits cancellation
  for the old run, then start/output/success for the new run, while late stale
  failures remain ignored.
- Process tasks inherit useful event ordering automatically because stdout,
  stderr, exit, and cancellation already flow through the core task controller.
- Semantic event state gives inspectors and tests a cheap checkpoint without
  traversing the whole event list.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  completed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` is blocked because local Dart is
  3.6.0 and `fleury_example_console` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.6 with sanitized process/task output policy integration or move
  into M1.7 terminal diagnose once M1.6 evidence is strong enough.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.6 Process Output Safety Slice

Status: In progress

Context:
- Process task output was captured and structured, but still stored raw process
  line text in task output, event history, semantics, and future debug/replay
  surfaces. The security plan requires untrusted subprocess output to be safe
  before downstream UI and artifacts see it.

Changes:
- Extended `TaskOutput` in [task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart)
  with `sanitized`, `truncated`, and `originalLength` metadata.
- Extended `TaskContext.write` so trusted app code can pass output safety
  metadata when it has already transformed output.
- Extended `TaskStatusView` and [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart)
  with `outputSanitized`, `outputTruncated`, and `outputOriginalLength`.
- Updated [process_task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/process_task.dart)
  to run process stdout/stderr lines through `sanitizeForDisplay`, cap lines
  with `maxOutputLineLength`, and write only transformed text to task output
  and event history.
- Expanded [process_task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart)
  to cover raw terminal-control replacement and long-line capping.
- Expanded [task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart)
  and [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to assert output safety metadata in task semantics.

Decisions:
- Sanitize at the native process-task boundary. Once bytes are task output,
  semantics, debug capture, or future replay data, they should already be safe
  display text unless a later trusted-rich-output API explicitly opts in.
- Cap process output per logical line with `maxOutputLineLength`, defaulting to
  4096 runes. This prevents one hostile line from bloating task/event/debug
  memory while keeping the first visible portion available for diagnosis.
- Preserve transformation metadata. Developers and future inspectors should be
  able to tell when Fleury changed output before display or storage.

Findings:
- Existing `sanitizeForDisplay` was the right first boundary: it replaces ESC,
  C0/C1 controls, and DEL with visible replacement characters. This does not
  implement a trusted ANSI parser yet, but it prevents raw active controls from
  entering task surfaces.
- Output capping should count runes, not UTF-16 code units, so truncation does
  not split surrogate pairs.
- This is a process-output subset of M1.11/TCS.4, not the full sanitized output
  pipeline. OSC-specific policy, links, image escapes, markdown, and redaction
  hooks remain pending.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/process_task.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  completed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` is blocked because local Dart is
  3.6.0 and `fleury_example_console` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue toward M1.7 terminal diagnose or finish another M1.11 sanitizer

## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Terminal Diagnose JSON V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- The roadmap and RFC 0013 call for `fleury diagnose --json` as the
  machine-readable terminal profile contract. The existing CLI had a human
  diagnose command, while capability detection was duplicated between the CLI
  shell handshake and `PosixTerminalDriver`.

Changes:
- Centralized static/env-derived capability detection in
  [capabilities.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart)
  with helpers for color mode, image protocol, and tmux/screen detection.
- Updated [posix_driver.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart)
  and the `fleury shell` handshake to use the shared capability detection path.
- Added [diagnostics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart)
  with `TerminalDiagnosis`, profile/environment/capability reports, structured
  diagnostic messages, fallbacks, warnings, and unsupported-feature reporting.
- Exported diagnosis and capability detection APIs from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Wired [bin/fleury.dart](../../packages/fleury/bin/fleury.dart) so
  `fleury diagnose --json` prints the structured diagnosis while the default
  command keeps a readable markdown report.
- Added [diagnostics_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart)
  covering environment-derived capabilities, `NO_COLOR`, forced-color fallback,
  fake-driver diagnosis JSON, fallback codes, warning codes, and unsupported
  feature reporting.

Decisions:
- Keep diagnosis passive for this slice. It should be safe in CI, tmux, SSH,
  and IDE consoles before Fleury adds active probe sequences.
- Make capability detection shared framework behavior, not a CLI-only helper,
  so runtime startup, diagnosis, proof apps, and future tests agree.
- Model fallbacks and warnings as structured message codes now. Human wording
  can change, but compatibility fixtures and proof-app diagnostics need stable
  code-level evidence.

Findings:
- The old CLI detection missed the `CLICOLOR_FORCE` path and only treated
  `$TMUX` as multiplexer evidence; sharing `PosixTerminalDriver` detection
  fixes that inconsistency.
- `TerminalDriver` already has enough information for a useful passive
  diagnosis: size, interactivity, and capability summary. The richer active
  probes can layer on top later without changing the initial JSON shape.
- The local SDK prevents executing the CLI or tests, but analyzer coverage
  still validates the public API and CLI wiring against the existing package
  config.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/fleury_core.dart bin/fleury.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin lib test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart` completed in
  `packages/fleury`; it reported three existing info-level
  `avoid_renaming_method_parameters` lints in selection/ticker binding files,
  not errors in this slice.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart` is blocked because local
  Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart --packages=.dart_tool/package_config.json bin/fleury.dart diagnose --json`
  is blocked because the package config language version is above the local
  Dart 3.6.0 runtime.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Add real-terminal `fleury diagnose --json` evidence once a compatible Dart
  SDK is available.
- Continue M1.7 with capability requirement/resolution types, or move to the
  next Phase 1 wedge if the proof app needs data-table/benchmark pressure
  first.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Capability Requirement Resolver

Status: In progress

Context:
- `fleury diagnose --json` can now report terminal state, but widgets and
  services still needed a typed way to declare what they want from a terminal
  and explain fallback/blocking behavior to semantics, diagnostics, inspector,
  and tests.

Changes:
- Added [capability_requirements.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart)
  with `TerminalFeature`, `CapabilityLevel`, `CapabilityRequirement`,
  `CapabilityFallback`, `CapabilityResolutionState`, and
  `CapabilityResolution`.
- Added pure resolution helpers for single and multiple requirements over
  `TerminalCapabilities`, plus explicit `additionalAvailableFeatures`,
  `policyBlockedFeatures`, and `unsafeFeatures` inputs for session/policy
  context.
- Added `CapabilityResolution.toSemanticState()` and
  `SemanticState.capabilityResolution` so inspectors/tests can consume
  resolved capability state without inventing ad hoc keys.
- Exported the capability requirement API from
  [fleury_core.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart).
- Added [capability_requirements_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart)
  covering color fidelity, image protocol resolution, explicit session
  features, required unsupported blocking, preferred fallback degradation,
  prohibited policy disablement, required policy blocks, semantic state export,
  and ordered list resolution.

Decisions:
- Keep the first resolver pure and data-oriented. Widget integration should
  use this model instead of each widget inventing its own fallback booleans.
- Treat required blocked/unsafe/unavailable features as blocking. Preferred and
  optional missing features can still satisfy the caller when they degrade or
  omit enhancements.
- Use explicit additional/policy/unsafe feature sets for v0 rather than
  prematurely growing `TerminalCapabilities` into a large report. The richer
  profile can still feed the same resolver later.

Findings:
- `TerminalCapabilities` is enough for color, alternate screen, cursor,
  image-protocol, and tmux passthrough decisions, but SSH, OSC 52, OSC 8,
  synchronized output, and restricted ANSI parsing need policy/session context.
- The semantic graph already had `terminalCapability`,
  `capabilityRequirement`, and `activeFallback`; adding
  `capabilityResolution` completes the minimum resolved-state quartet.
- Dart accepts `CapabilityLevel.required` as an enum value under the current
  analyzer, so the API can match RFC 0013 terminology.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart` is blocked
  because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- `dart test test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  is blocked for the same SDK reason.
- Stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old framework/source
  names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Wire capability resolutions into first-party widgets starting with image,
  links/markdown, clipboard, and proof-app diagnostics.
- Re-run terminal diagnosis and requirement tests once a compatible Dart SDK is
  available.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Image Capability Semantics

Status: In progress

Context:
- The capability resolver needed a real widget integration to prove it was not
  just a detached data model. `Image` is the best first surface because it
  already chooses between native Kitty/iTerm2/Sixel output and glyph fallback
  renderers.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticRole.image` in
  [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart).
- Added optional `semanticLabel` to `Image`, `Image.file`, `Image.bytes`, and
  `Image.decoded` in [image.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/image.dart).
- Wrapped `Image` output in automatic semantics that resolve the preferred
  `TerminalFeature.inlineImages` capability against the active `MediaQuery`
  terminal profile.
- Image semantic state now reports `terminalCapability`,
  `capabilityRequirement`, `capabilityResolution`, `activeFallback`,
  `imageProtocol`, `imageGlyph`, `colorMode`, `tmuxPassthrough`, `frameIndex`,
  and `frameCount`.
- Added image capability semantic tests in
  [image_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/test/image_test.dart) for
  glyph fallback and active Kitty native-image paths.

Decisions:
- Preserve image render behavior and add semantic evidence around it. The
  renderer should not change until capability policy itself requires a new
  behavior.
- Treat native images as a preferred capability with a glyph fallback, not a
  required feature. This matches the product promise: users should still see
  useful visuals in terminals without native image protocols.
- Keep decorative images valid by allowing a null semantic label while still
  exposing capability/fallback state for diagnostics.

Findings:
- `MediaQuery` already carries enough terminal profile data for this widget:
  color mode, image protocol, and tmux passthrough.
- The image widget now becomes a concrete proof point for the semantic app
  graph: devtools/tests can ask why the image degraded without knowing renderer
  internals.
- This slice also gives later proof-app diagnostics a ready-made capability
  surface to display.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart` completed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart format lib/src/image.dart test/image_test.dart` completed in
  `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart` passed
  in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/image.dart test/image_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/image_test.dart` is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and
  `fleury_widgets` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.

Next:
- Continue first-party capability integration with markdown/OSC 8 link policy
  or clipboard policy, then surface the diagnosis/resolution state in the
  proof-app Diagnostics screen.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Markdown Link Policy Semantics

Status: In progress

Context:
- RFC 0013 requires markdown links to stay safe by default: visible link text
  plus visible URL, no raw OSC 8 hyperlink escapes for untrusted content. The
  renderer already followed the visible URL rule, but semantics did not expose
  the policy/fallback reason.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticRole.link` in
  [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart).
- Updated the capability resolver so policy-disabled resolutions can carry a
  fallback label when the requirement declares one.
- Updated [markdown_text.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/markdown_text.dart)
  so rendered markdown links also add semantic link nodes.
- Link semantic state now reports `terminalCapability=osc8Hyperlinks`,
  `capabilityRequirement=prohibited`,
  `capabilityResolution=disabledByPolicy`, `activeFallback=visible URL`,
  `linkUrl`, `linkScheme`, `safeLinkScheme`, and
  `osc8Policy=disabledByDefault`.
- Added [markdown_text_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/test/markdown_text_test.dart)
  coverage for safe HTTPS link semantics, custom-scheme safety metadata, and
  no link semantics inside fenced code blocks.

Decisions:
- Keep `MarkdownText` as a safe renderer, not an OSC 8 emitter. Trusted
  hyperlink output can be a future opt-in mode over the same resolver/policy
  surface.
- Expose link semantic nodes separately from visual `TextSpan`s. That keeps
  rendering simple while giving tests, inspectors, and future adapters an
  inspectable URL/policy surface.
- Do not attach `SemanticAction.open` yet. A semantic action should imply a
  framework-handled action path; link opening needs an explicit command/policy
  design.

Findings:
- The existing inline parser was the right place to collect links because it
  naturally ignores fenced code blocks and unbalanced markdown.
- The semantic-only link nodes use `EmptyBox`, so they do not claim terminal
  cells or change rendered markdown output.
- Policy-disabled resolutions need fallback labels just as degraded
  resolutions do; "blocked but visible as URL" is a meaningful product state.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart format lib/src/markdown_text.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.

  passed in `packages/fleury`.
