## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Clipboard Policy Reporting

Status: In progress

Context:
- The capability/security plan needs clipboard behavior to be observable without
  making OSC 52 or platform clipboard writes implicit, invisible side effects.
  Text controls already had copy-policy concepts, but runtime clipboard writes
  only returned the selected transport.

Changes:
- Added `ClipboardWritePolicy` and `ClipboardWriteReport` in
  [clipboard.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/clipboard.dart).
- Preserved the existing `Clipboard.write()` API by delegating it through
  `writeWithReport()` and returning only the selected `ClipboardWriteResult`.
- `SystemClipboard.writeWithReport()` now reports selected transport,
  capability resolution, policy, payload size, OSC 52 encoded length, SSH
  state, in-process fallback, platform-tool attempts, and OSC 52 emission.
- `TestClipboard.writeWithReport()` now returns a degraded in-process fallback
  report for deterministic tests.
- Added semantic getters for clipboard capability/policy/reporting state in
  [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart).
- `TextInput` and `TextArea` now expose copy-policy semantic state for allowed,
  disabled, and redacted clipboard behavior.
- Added clipboard report tests in
  [clipboard_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart)
  and text-control clipboard semantic expectations in
  [semantics_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/semantics/semantics_test.dart).

Decisions:
- Keep clipboard ergonomics small for app code (`write()` remains the simple
  path) while making the richer state available to diagnostics, semantic
  snapshots, tests, and the proof app through `writeWithReport()`.
- Treat runtime clipboard transport and text-control copy policy as related but
  distinct semantic surfaces. A field can allow/redact/disable copy regardless
  of whether the terminal ultimately uses a platform tool, OSC 52, or only the
  in-process register.
- Represent an in-process-only clipboard write as a deliberate fallback, not as
  silent failure, because in-app paste can still work and diagnostics should say
  exactly what happened.

Findings:
- The existing layered clipboard strategy was a good fit for reporting because
  platform tool, OSC 52, and in-process fallback already had clear boundaries.
- OSC 52 size limits are policy, not renderer behavior; exposing the encoded
  length gives developers enough evidence to explain why a large copy degraded.
- The local Dart 3.6.0 analyzer treats repeated `_` callback parameters as
  duplicate names, so clipboard tests use named discard parameters for local
  compatibility.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/runtime/clipboard.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/runtime/clipboard.dart lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart lib/src/terminal/capabilities.dart lib/src/terminal/capability_requirements.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/src/runtime/clipboard.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib test` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib bin test`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  is blocked because local Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`.
- Word-boundary stale-name scan over `docs` and `packages` found no old
  framework/source names.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Surface terminal diagnosis and capability-resolution state in the proof app
  Diagnostics screen so these model/reporting slices become visible in a real
  app workflow.
- Re-run the clipboard and semantic tests when a compatible Dart SDK is
  available.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.7 Proof App Capability Diagnostics

Status: In progress

Context:
- After image, markdown-link, and clipboard integrations, the capability model
  needed to appear in the proof app. Otherwise M1.7 would remain a set of
  isolated primitives rather than something app developers can inspect in a real
  screen.

Changes:
- Replaced the proof app Diagnostics placeholder with a deterministic
  `MediaQuery`/`TerminalCapabilities`/`diagnoseTerminal` snapshot in
  [fleury_example_console.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart).
- The Diagnostics screen now renders semantic capability rows for color
  fidelity, inline images, markdown links, clipboard writes, and OSC 52
  clipboard behavior.
- The summary diagnostic node now reports schema version, terminal size, color
  mode, image protocol, fallback/warning/unsupported counts, clipboard policy,
  OSC 52/OSC 8 policy, debug captures, streaming state, and row count.
- Updated
  [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to assert degraded inline-image fallback, policy-disabled OSC 8 link
  fallback, available clipboard-write policy, and degraded OSC 52 fallback.

Decisions:
- Keep the proof app diagnostics deterministic by deriving from `MediaQuery`
  instead of probing the real terminal during tests.
- Use the same resolver and diagnosis types as the real CLI path so the proof
  app validates app composition without inventing a separate fixture model.
- Represent per-capability rows as semantic diagnostics. The parent node is the
  screen summary; rows prove individual available/degraded/disabled outcomes.

Findings:
- The proof app is now a useful forcing surface for capability architecture:
  one screen exercises color, native image fallback, link policy, clipboard
  policy, OSC 52 uncertainty, debug counters, and streaming state.
- Passive diagnosis still cannot prove OSC 52 support. The app therefore shows
  policy-gated status plus a degraded resolver row until a real write report or
  active probe provides stronger evidence.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.

  passed in `packages/fleury`.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.8 Scenario Benchmark Runner V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.8 needed to turn the Phase 0 scenario benchmark lab into executable
  evidence. The existing `benchmark_harness` suite measures useful hot loops,
  but it does not emit app-shaped JSON records or exercise a retained app
  journey.

Changes:
- Added
  [scenario_benchmarks.dart](../../packages/fleury/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart)
  as a separate scenario runner.
- Runner options include `--list`, `--filter=`, `--json`, `--save=`,
  `--warmup=`, `--iterations=`, `--seed=`, and `--size=COLSxROWS`.
- Added `SB.1 Time To Counter App`, which mounts a small `FleuryApp`, renders
  the first frame, counts first-frame ANSI bytes, invokes an increment command,
  renders the updated frame, and queries semantics.
- Scenario JSON records now include schema version, scenario ID/name, Fleury
  version, git SHA placeholder, Dart version, run mode, OS, CPU count, terminal
  profile, terminal size, seed, warmup/measured iterations, start time,
  duration, metrics, candidate thresholds, pass state, and notes.
- Updated [benchmark README](../../packages/fleury/benchmark/README.md) with the
  scenario runner commands and first scenario shape.

Decisions:
- Keep scenario benchmarks separate from `benchmark/all.dart` for now. They are
  slower, app-shaped, and baseline-oriented, while `all.dart` should remain the
  fast microbenchmark sweep.
- Use candidate thresholds as JSON metadata, not hard gates, until the first
  compatible-SDK baselines establish local variance.
- Use `FleuryTester` for v0 scenario execution so the first runner can measure
  command invocation, rendering, and semantics without adding new runtime
  machinery.

Findings:
- The benchmark lab's schema can be represented without extra dependencies.
  `dart:io` and `dart:convert` are enough for options, environment metadata,
  JSON output, and saved artifacts.
- `SB.1` gives useful adoption/performance signal even before larger widgets
  land: mount cost, first-frame cost, command-to-frame latency, semantic query
  cost, command count, semantic node count, and emitted bytes.
- Local `dart analyze benchmark` can validate the runner, but `dart run` cannot
  execute it under Dart 3.6.0 because the package and dependencies require a
  newer language version.

Validation:
- `dart format benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` completed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze benchmark` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` is blocked because local
  Dart is 3.6.0 and `fleury` requires SDK `^3.10.4`; direct compilation reports
  the package language version as too high for the installed SDK.

Next:
- When a compatible Dart SDK is available, run `SB.1` with `--json` and save the
  first baseline artifact.
- Add the next M1.8 scenario after baseline execution is possible. The planned
  order is DataTable 100k Rows, then Text Editing Composer Stress.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.9 DataTable Render Island V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.9 is the first data-heavy widget milestone. The existing `Table` eagerly
  composes every cell as widgets, which is the wrong foundation for the 100k-row
  target.
- The goal for this slice was to prove the semantic render-island pattern without
  changing the old small-table API.

Changes:
- Added
  [data_table.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/data_table.dart) with
  `DataTable`, `DataTableColumn`, `DataTableController`, stable row-key support,
  keyboard movement, fixed/flex columns, fixed header, selected-row activation,
  sort/filter semantic metadata, and direct cell-buffer painting.
- Exported the new DataTable API from
  [fleury_widgets.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart).
- Added a direct `characters` dependency to `fleury_widgets` because DataTable
  clips cell text by grapheme/display width.
- Added
  [data_table_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/test/data_table_test.dart)
  covering 100k-row visible rendering, virtualized semantics, keyboard window
  scrolling, stable selected keys, sort/filter metadata, and sanitized/clipped
  cell painting.

Decisions:
- Keep `Table` as the small compositional table and introduce `DataTable` as the
  large-data render island.
- DataTable semantics are produced from the render object's last visible range:
  tests render before querying semantics when visible-window state matters.
- Do not add the DataTable 100k scenario to the core `fleury` runner yet. The
  core package cannot depend on `fleury_widgets`; this needs a widgets-package or
  root-level scenario runner.
- Keep proof-app adoption as the next integration step. A quick attempt showed
  the proof app suite has broader command/focus settling instability, so the
  render-island foundation should land independently before app-harness adoption.

Findings:
- The render-island pattern can preserve useful semantics without mounting every
  cell: the semantic table reports total row/column counts, virtualized state,
  visible start/end, selected key, header cells, and visible body rows/cells.
- `DataTable` currently exposes sort/filter state as app-provided metadata; it
  does not yet own sorting/filtering policy.
- Copy/export behavior is still the major missing piece before M1.9 can close.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze --packages=../fleury_widgets/.dart_tool/package_config.json lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console` after reverting proof-app adoption
  from this slice.
- `dart analyze lib test` in `packages/fleury_widgets` still reports five
  existing info-level `use_null_aware_elements` lints in `command_palette.dart`
  and `table.dart`; they are unrelated to DataTable.

Next:
- Add a widgets-package or root-level scenario runner for `SB.3 DataTable 100k
  Rows`.
- Integrate `DataTable` into the proof app Runs screen after the proof-app
  command/focus settling issue is handled or the test is reshaped around rendered
  output and semantics.
- Add copy/export behavior and app-owned sort/filter helpers.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.8/M1.9 Scenario Benchmark Execution

Status: In progress

Context:
- The previous journal entries treated scenario execution as blocked by a local
  Dart 3.6.0 SDK. Current local `dart --version` is 3.12.1, so that blocker is
  stale.
- M1.9 needed the planned `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` scenario in a package that
  can depend on `fleury_widgets`.

Changes:
- Added
  [fleury_widgets scenario_benchmarks.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart)
  with `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows`.
- Added
  [fleury_widgets benchmark README](../../packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/README.md)
  with package-local runner commands.
- Updated the core [benchmark README](../../packages/fleury/benchmark/README.md)
  to point high-level widget scenarios to the widgets package.
- Fixed core `SB.1 Time To Counter App` so command-to-frame measurement uses an
  explicit listenable counter model. Before the fix, the scenario was executable
  but failed because the command completed while the semantic counter value stayed
  at `0`.

Decisions:
- Keep package-local scenario runners while the schema is still settling and
  package dependencies differ. A shared runner helper can come later, after the
  scenario output shape proves stable.
- Keep `SB.3` in `fleury_widgets` rather than making core import the widget
  catalog.
- Treat the 3-iteration JSON runs as smoke evidence, not stable release
  baselines. Stable baselines still need saved artifacts, repeated runs, and
  environment variance notes.

Findings:
- `SB.3` validates the render-island claim at scenario level: the smoke run
  exercised 100k rows, jumped to the final row, selected `RUN-199999`, and only
  requested 62 unique rows across the journey.
- In the 3-iteration smoke run, `SB.3` reported page-move p95 around 1.8 ms,
  semantic-query p95 around 3.6 ms, and `pass: true`.
- Core `SB.1` now reports `counterValue: 1` and `pass: true` under Dart 3.12.1.

Validation:

- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` passed in

  `packages/fleury_widgets`.

  `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3` passed with default
  iterations in `packages/fleury_widgets`.

Next:
- Add saved baseline artifacts or a baseline-results doc once the scenario set is
  ready to compare across machines/runs.
- Continue M1.9 with DataTable copy/export and proof-app adoption.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.9 DataTable Copy Export V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.9 still needed copy/export evidence after the first render-island and SB.3
  benchmark slices.
- The goal for this slice was to prove selected-row export/copy without taking
  on a full spreadsheet-like cell/range selection model.

Changes:
- Added selected-row copy/export API to
  [data_table.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/data_table.dart):
  `DataTableExportFormat`, `DataTableExportOptions`,
  `DataTableExportResult`, `DataTableCopyOptions`, `DataTableCopyResult`, and
  `exportDataTableRows`.
- Added Ctrl+C selected-row copy in `DataTable` using
  `Clipboard.instance.writeWithReport`, with `copySelectedRow`, `copyOptions`,
  and `onCopy` widget hooks.
- Added table and selected-row semantic `copy` actions plus clipboard/copy
  policy state.
- Sanitized DataTable export and semantic cell values before copy/semantic
  exposure, including control-character replacement, newline/tab normalization,
  and common ANSI escape stripping.
- Extended `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` to use `TestClipboard`, copy the final
  selected row, and report `copySelectedRowUs` in JSON metrics.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README and implementation trackers to reflect
  selected-row copy/export evidence.

Decisions:
- Start with selected-row export/copy, not cell/range selection. This gives
  developer-visible clipboard behavior and benchmark coverage while leaving
  range UX open for proof-app pressure.
- Keep export formatting as a pure helper over `rowCount`, `columns`, and
  `cellBuilder`; DataTable copy calls the same helper for exactly one selected
  row.
- Use Fleury's clipboard service for DataTable copy instead of direct platform
  writes so transport reports, policy, tests, and future diagnostics remain
  consistent with text controls.

Findings:
- Selected-row copy adds bounded work to the virtualized path: it calls the cell
  builder only for the selected row and does not force off-screen rows.
- The first SB.3 copy smoke run reported `copySelectedRowUs` p95 around 4.3 ms
  across three measured iterations with 100k rows.
- DataTable still needs richer copy UX before M1.9 is complete: cell/range
  selection, hidden-column policy, and proof-app-driven defaults should come
  after the current selected-row baseline.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  completed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze benchmark` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`; the run selected `RUN-199999`, requested
  62 unique rows, and reported `copySelectedRowUs` p95 at 4329 us.

Next:
- Continue M1.9 with proof-app adoption once the proof-app command/focus
  settling issue is addressed or the proof tests are reshaped.
- Add richer DataTable copy behavior from proof pressure: selected cell/range,
  hidden-column inclusion policy, and user-visible export options.
- Add app-owned sorting/filtering helpers beyond semantic metadata.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.9 Proof App Runs DataTable Adoption

Status: In progress

Context:
- After selected-row copy/export landed, M1.9 needed app-shaped pressure from
  the proof app rather than only isolated widget tests and SB.3.
- The broader proof app suite still has command/status/transcript settling
  failures unrelated to DataTable, so this slice focused on the Runs workflow.

Changes:
- Replaced the proof app Runs screen's compositional `Table` with
  [DataTable](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/data_table.dart).
- Switched Runs selection state to `DataTableController`.
- Added stable row keys from run IDs, DataTable filter metadata, keyboard
  movement, activation, and selected-row copy coverage in
  [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart).
- Moved Runs filtering into a `ListenableBuilder` over the filter controller so
  the DataTable updates directly when the filter text changes.
- Adjusted DataTable semantic focus to read the current `FocusNode` during
  semantic snapshot creation, avoiding stale focus state without changing the
  core focus rebuild model.

Decisions:
- Keep proof-app Runs filtering local to the screen for now. This makes the
  forcing scenario deterministic without inventing framework-owned filtering
  policy before M1.9 proves the table API.
- Keep broader proof-app command/status settling as a separate app-kernel/debug
  issue. The DataTable adoption should not be blocked by unrelated command
  palette, transcript, and status synchronization failures.

Findings:
- The focused Runs workflow now validates the DataTable integration surface:
  filter narrows semantic row count, row keys are stable run IDs, Arrow Down
  moves selection, Enter appends the selected run event, and Ctrl+C copies the
  selected row through Fleury clipboard.
- The full proof app suite still fails in non-Runs tests around status text,
  command palette presentation, composer transcript updates, and diagnostics
  capture rendering. These failures existed before this adoption slice and need
  an app-kernel/test-settling pass.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --plain-name runs` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`; the run selected `RUN-199999`, requested
  62 unique rows, and reported `copySelectedRowUs` p95 at 5203 us.
- `dart test test/widgets/focus_modal_traversal_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.

Known failures:
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` still fails outside the Runs tests.
  Remaining failures are in `commands navigate and start the fake task`,
  `command palette can drive app navigation`,
  `composer submission and log burst update transcript`, and
  `diagnostics capture updates status and transcript state`.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_input_test.dart --plain-name focus` still fails
  the placeholder cursor focus test; this appears unrelated to DataTable and
  was not changed in this slice.

Next:
- Investigate the proof-app command/status/transcript settling issue as an
  app-kernel/test harness slice before calling the proof app broadly green.
- Continue DataTable v1 with richer cell/range copy and owned sort/filter
  helpers after the proof-app Runs workflow has had more pressure.

## 2026-05-31 - Proof App Settling And Semantic Proxy Rebuilds

Status: Complete

Context:
- The proof app Runs workflow was green with `DataTable`, but the full proof
  app suite still failed around command/status updates, command palette
  semantics, task-progress assertions, and transcript/debug surfaces.
- The goal for this slice was to separate true framework bugs from test-shape
  issues and make the example subpackage a reliable app-shaped regression
  harness again.

Changes:
- Fixed custom semantic proxy element updates in
  [semantics.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart),
  [commands.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/commands.dart), and
  [app.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/app.dart) so updated proxy
  widgets rebuild their child subtrees after parent `setState`.
- Updated
  [FleuryTester](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart) so
  app-owned command invocation records and reports results through the app
  registry even when the nearest context has a screen/local registry.
- Fixed
  [RenderSizedBox](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_objects.dart)
  so fixed width/height values are constrained before child layout and parent
  constraint reconciliation.
- Reshaped proof app tests in
  [proof_console_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart)
  to render virtualized command-palette rows before semantic row queries and to
  check task status from the screen that actually exposes task-progress
  semantics.

Decisions:
- Semantic proxy elements must call the normal update path and rebuild children
  when their widget changes. Parent `setState` can otherwise replace the proxy
  widget while leaving stale descendants mounted below it.
- Tester command invocation should report through the registry that owns the
  resolved command. This preserves app-wide last-command state while keeping
  nearest/local command resolution semantics intact.

Findings:
- The proof app failures were not DataTable regressions. They exposed a deeper
  semantic-proxy bug that stale-rendered children below `Semantics`,
  `CommandScope`, and `FleuryApp` wrappers after parent state updates.
- Command palette list rows are virtualized; tests that assert row semantics
  must render the palette at the target size before querying visible row nodes.
- Fixed-size render boxes must still honor tighter incoming constraints. The
  palette failure reproduced when a `SizedBox(width: 36)` was laid out under a
  narrower parent column.

Validation:
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_layout_test.dart test/rendering/render_flex_test.dart test/widgets/basic_additions_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/rendering/render_objects.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/app/commands.dart lib/src/app/app.dart lib/src/rendering/render_objects.dart`
  passed with two existing info-level `use_null_aware_elements` lints in app
  and command semantic map literals.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`; the run selected `RUN-199999`, requested
  62 unique rows, reported `page_move_p95_us=1340`, and stayed under candidate
  thresholds.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Old framework-name, stale branch-path, and temporary debug-file scan found no
  matches in `docs` or `packages`.

Next:
- Continue M1.9 with richer DataTable cell/range copy and owned sort/filter
  helpers, or move to M1.10 debug inspector expansion if app-level visibility
  becomes the higher-leverage next slice.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.9 DataTable Cell Ranges And Row-Order Helpers

Status: Complete for M1.9

Context:
- The previous M1.9 slices proved virtualization, stable row keys, selected-row
  copy/export, SB.3 benchmark coverage, and proof-app adoption.
- Remaining v1 gaps were richer cell/range copy and framework-owned
  sort/filter helpers beyond passive semantic metadata.

Changes:
- Added `DataTableSelectionMode.cell` plus controller-backed selected column,
  anchor, focus, and `DataTableSelectionRange` state in
  [data_table.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/data_table.dart).
- Added Shift-extended rectangular selection with Arrow keys, range-aware
  painting, table/row/cell semantic range metadata, and selected-cell copy
  actions.
- Extended `exportDataTableRows` with rectangular column ranges and added
  range-aware `DataTableCopyResult` data.
- Added `DataTableFilterDescriptor`, `DataTableSortDescriptor`, and
  `buildDataTableRowOrder` so apps can use first-party sort/filter behavior
  while preserving render-island virtualization.
- Exported the new DataTable APIs from
  [fleury_widgets.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart).
- Updated the proof app Runs screen in
  [fleury_example_console.dart](../../packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart)
  to use `buildDataTableRowOrder` for its filter flow.
- Updated SB.3 notes in
  [scenario_benchmarks.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart)
  to distinguish hot-path virtualization metrics from sort/filter helper tests.
- Marked M1.9 and the DataTable v1 workstream items complete in the
  implementation trackers.

Decisions:
- Keep row selection as the default DataTable mode. Cell/range behavior is an
  explicit mode so command tables stay simple while dense inspection tables can
  opt into rectangular selection and copy.
- Keep sorting/filtering as pure row-order helpers. The render island should
  remain a visible-row renderer; apps map source rows to view rows through the
  returned order instead of forcing the widget to materialize all row widgets.

Findings:
- The rectangular copy path can reuse the existing export helper if export
  options understand column ranges. That keeps sanitization and CSV/TSV quoting
  shared between row and range copy.
- Semantic state is the right place to expose selected column ID, selection
  mode, row/column bounds, and range sizes. Tests can assert the selection
  model without inspecting private controller fields.
- Proof-app filtering became cleaner when expressed as row-order mapping:
  filter state belongs to the app, while DataTable only receives the filtered
  view row count and builders.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`; the run selected `RUN-199999`, requested
  62 unique rows, reported `page_move_p95_us=2263`, and stayed under candidate
  thresholds.

Next:
- Start M1.10 debug inspector expansion unless a final M1.9 audit finds a
  missing DataTable v1 invariant. The first useful inspector slice should make
  semantic graph, focus, commands, task state, frame/runtime signals, and
  capability fallbacks easier to inspect while continuing to feed the proof
  app.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Inspector Frame Reasons And Semantic Summaries

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.9 DataTable v1 is complete. The next milestone is M1.10 debug inspector
  expansion.
- The existing debug Tree tab already showed semantic node counts, focused
  nodes, app state, and command rows. The Rebuilds tab was still a placeholder,
  and task/capability semantic state was not surfaced directly.

Changes:
- Added a best-effort `reason` field to
  [FrameEvent](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart).
- Updated [run_tui.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart)
  so scheduled frames carry lightweight reasons: initial, build, post-frame,
  hot-reload, debug-key, debug-console, resize, key, text-input, paste, and
  mouse.
- Replaced the Rebuilds-tab placeholder in
  [debug_panel.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart) with
  latest-frame reason, total cost, dirty-cell count, slow-frame count,
  worst-frame/max-dirty summaries, phase sparklines, and recent frame rows.
- Expanded the Tree tab with enabled-command counts, task summaries, and
  capability/fallback summaries derived from semantic nodes.
- Added debug inspector tests in
  [debug_shell_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart)
  for task/capability semantic summaries and Rebuilds-tab frame diagnostics.
- Marked M1.10/RDT.3 in progress in the implementation trackers.

Decisions:
- Use best-effort frame schedule reasons before designing full replay
  causality. This gives developers immediate insight without freezing a replay
  artifact schema.
- Keep task and capability inspector views sourced from semantic nodes rather
  than adding separate debug-only providers.

Findings:
- The debug event stream already had the right shape for this slice. Adding one
  string reason to `FrameEvent` was enough to make the Rebuilds tab explain the
  common cause of the latest frame.
- Semantic task and capability state are already rich enough to support useful
  inspector rows. This reinforces the semantic app graph as a real
  implementation surface, not just a testing API.
- The Rebuilds tab still does not answer source-level dirty-region causality.
  That needs deeper BuildOwner/render-tree instrumentation and should be a
  later M1.10 slice.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.

- `dart test test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_monitors_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- Continue M1.10 with deeper semantic graph browsing and dirty-region/source
  visibility, or add a targeted debug-capture hook if the next proof-app issue
  needs capture-to-test evidence.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Semantic Outline And Dirty Bounds

Status: In progress

Context:
- The first M1.10 slice made the inspector explain frame schedule reasons and
  summarize task/capability semantic nodes.
- Remaining inspector gaps included actual semantic graph browsing and more
  useful dirty-region information than a plain dirty-cell count.

Changes:
- Extended [FrameEvent](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart)
  with nullable `dirtyBounds`.
- Updated [run_tui.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart) to
  record dirty-cell count and bounding rectangle from the real ANSI diff path
  whenever debug frame listeners exist, regardless of paint-flash mode.
- Updated [debug_panel.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart)
  so Live/Rebuilds tabs show dirty bounds and recent frame rows include the
  dirty rectangle.
- Added a preorder semantic graph outline to the Tree tab, showing role,
  label/value, focus/selection/enabled/busy state, actions, and validation
  errors for the first visible nodes.
- Added tests in
  [debug_shell_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart)
  for semantic outline output and Rebuilds-tab dirty bounds.
- Added a runtime integration test in
  [run_tui_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart)
  proving debug frame events include dirty bounds without paint flash enabled.

Decisions:
- Dirty bounds should be captured when debug listeners exist, not only when
  paint flash is enabled. The visual overlay is optional; the diagnostic data
  should still be correct.
- Start graph browsing as a textual preorder outline over the semantic
  snapshot. Interactive tree selection, expand/collapse, and search can wait
  until usage proves what navigation controls are worth adding.

Findings:
- The previous dirty-cell count was tied to paint-flash capture, so it could
  report zero when the debug panel was watching but paint flash was off. The
  new callback path keeps count/bounds accurate while only allocating a dirty
  cell list when the overlay needs one.
- Initial frames can merge reasons such as `build+initial` because mounting
  can schedule a build before the explicit initial frame microtask runs. That
  is acceptable for best-effort frame diagnostics.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_monitors_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.10 with source-level dirty causality, richer effect inspector
  rows, or a targeted capture-to-test hook. The current slice answers "what
  semantic graph exists?" and "what screen region changed?", but not yet "which
  widget/source object made it change?".

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Debug Inspector Dirty Causality

Status: In progress

Context:
- Active milestone was M1.10: debug inspector expansion.
- Previous slices added frame schedule reasons, Rebuilds-tab timing/dirty
  diagnostics, task/capability semantic summaries, dirty bounds from the real
  diff path, and a preorder semantic graph outline.
- The next gap was source-level dirty causality: "which widget or render object
  actually made this frame necessary?"

Changes:
- Added an internal debug-only invalidation collector at
  `packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_invalidation.dart`.
- `Element.markNeedsBuild()` now records build invalidation labels for the
  widget/state pair when debug frame listeners exist.
- `RenderObject.markNeedsPaint()` now records the originating render object
  label once, while preserving existing nearest-repaint-boundary propagation.
- `FrameEvent` now carries `dirtySources`, and `runTui` drains the pending
  build/paint source labels into each emitted debug frame.
- The Live and Rebuilds tabs now show source summaries and expanded recent
  dirty source rows.
- Added debug-panel coverage for rendered dirty sources and a `runTui`
  integration test proving a real state update reports the originating
  `_CounterApp/_CounterAppState` source.
- Updated M1.10/RDT.3 tracker notes and recorded the dirty-causality collector
  decision.

Decisions:
- Use a bounded debug-only collector for source labels instead of designing a
  full replay-causality schema during M1.10.
- Keep labels best-effort and human-readable for now: `build:Widget/State` and
  `paint:RenderObject`.

Findings:
- The existing invalidation paths are compact enough to instrument narrowly:
  `Element.markNeedsBuild()` for rebuild causes and
  `RenderObject.markNeedsPaint()` for render update/paint causes.
- Initial mounts and debug-panel self-updates can produce useful but noisy
  labels; this is acceptable for the current inspector because it exposes the
  real runtime loop rather than pretending debug UI has no cost.
- The collector remains off the normal path unless a debug-frame subscriber is
  attached.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart lib/src/debug/debug_invalidation.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/rendering/render_object.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart lib/src/widgets/framework.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue proof-driven semantic-action or fallback hardening where existing
  first-party widgets or the proof app advertise stable actions. Prefer
  routing through existing controllers/commands over adding new API. Keep ACP,
  Dune/`dune_cli`, public adoption work, isolate-backed indexing, and full
  replay deferred unless the proof app reveals a concrete blocker.


- `dart test test/widgets/state_test.dart test/widgets/reassemble_test.dart test/widgets/overlay_test.dart test/widgets/repaint_boundary_test.dart test/widgets/selection/selection_area_test.dart test/integration/end_to_end_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/debug/debug_monitors_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale framework-name/path `rg` sanity check returned no matches.

Next:
- Continue M1.10 with the remaining inspector gaps: richer effect/task
  inspector views, terminal capability/profile panels, and then the narrower
  capture-to-test hook points from RDT.2/RDT.4.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Terminal Profile And Capability Inspector

Status: In progress

Context:
- Active milestone remains M1.10: debug inspector expansion.
- The previous slice made rebuild/paint causality visible. The remaining
  inspector gap with the highest immediate value was runtime terminal
  capability/profile visibility: "what terminal am I running in, what did the
  framework detect, and what fallback/warning state exists?"

Changes:
- Extended `DebugController` with an on-demand `TerminalDiagnosis` provider.
- `runTui` now installs a provider that calls `diagnoseTerminal` against the
  active driver and `Platform.environment`, so the panel reflects current size,
  interactivity, capabilities, fallbacks, warnings, and unsupported features
  without recording per-frame data.
- The Tree tab now renders a `terminal profile` section and a `terminal
  capabilities` section before semantic tree details when diagnosis data is
  available.
- Added debug-panel coverage for terminal profile/capability/fallback/warning
  rows.
- Updated M1.10/RDT.3 tracker notes and recorded the terminal diagnosis
  provider decision.

Decisions:
- Keep terminal capability/profile state on an on-demand debug provider rather
  than on frame events. Capability diagnosis is runtime context, not a per-frame
  event, and should not bloat frame diagnostics or future replay schemas.

Findings:
- The existing `diagnoseTerminal` model is already the right inspector source:
  it contains profile, environment, capabilities, fallback messages, warnings,
  and unsupported features without requiring active terminal probes.
- The Tree tab is carrying several inspector responsibilities now. It is useful
  for M1.10, but a later inspector interaction pass should consider a separate
  Capabilities tab or better section navigation once tab/key UX is expanded.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_state.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/debug/debug_monitors_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.10 with fuller effect/task inspector views, richer semantic graph
  interaction, or the RDT.2/RDT.4 capture-to-test hook points.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Task/Effect Inspector Aggregates

Status: In progress

Context:
- Active milestone remains M1.10: debug inspector expansion.
- The Tree tab already listed semantic task nodes, but it did not summarize the
  effect/task picture an app developer wants first: how many tasks exist, which
  are running/failing/canceled/succeeded/idle, and how much event activity is
  represented.

Changes:
- Updated the Tree-tab task section to render `effects/tasks` aggregate rows:

  and failed tasks and assert aggregate status/event rows.
- Updated M1.10/RDT.3 tracker notes and recorded the decision to derive first
  task/effect inspector summaries from semantic task nodes.

Decisions:
- Keep first task/effect inspector aggregation semantic-backed instead of
  adding a task registry/debug provider. This keeps inspector, tests, and future
  adapter surfaces aligned on the semantic app graph.

Findings:
- `TaskStatusView` and the proof app already contribute enough semantic task
  state for useful aggregation: `taskStatus`, `taskEventCount`,
  `lastTaskEventKind`, progress current/total, and optional progress label.
- A future interaction pass can still add drilldown into task output/history if
  capture-to-test work needs it.

Validation:

  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart` passed.

Next:

  capture-to-test hook points. The inspector now answers focus/command through
  semantics, dirty region/source through frame diagnostics, capability/profile
  through terminal diagnosis, and task/effect status through semantic task
  aggregates.

## 2026-05-31 - RDT.2/RDT.4 Bounded Debug Capture Hooks

Status: In progress

Context:
- M1.10 inspector work had enough terminal/frame/semantic/task visibility to
  start the capture-to-test prototype from RDT.2/RDT.4.
- The goal was not full replay. The goal was a bounded structured snapshot that
  can help turn difficult runtime bugs into focused tests.

Changes:
- Added `packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart` with
  `DebugCaptureRecorder`, `DebugCaptureSnapshot`, and `DebugOutputSummary`.
- Extended `DebugEvents` with input and terminal-diagnosis debug events.
- `runTui` now emits terminal diagnosis events on startup and resize, and
  emits input debug events for key/text/paste/mouse/resize input.
- `DebugCaptureRecorder` listens to the existing debug event stream and keeps
  bounded frame, input, terminal diagnosis, and output-summary records.
- `DebugCaptureSnapshot.toJson()` serializes frame metadata, input/resize
  summaries, terminal diagnosis, optional output summaries, and optional
  semantic snapshots.
- Semantic snapshot serialization redacts node values and sensitive semantic
  state fields when `redactedValue`, `obscureText`, or `clipboardRedacted` is
  present.
- Exported the capture recorder/snapshot from `fleury_test.dart` as a
  test-only helper.
- Added `debug_capture_test.dart` for redacted snapshot serialization.
- Added a `runTui` integration test proving the recorder observes terminal,
  resize, key input, and frame records.
- Updated RDT.2/RDT.4 tracker notes and recorded the bounded-recorder decision.

Decisions:
- Prototype capture-to-test as a bounded recorder over hook events rather than
  a full replay artifact format.
- Keep redaction inside snapshot serialization so semantic test/capture paths
  cannot accidentally write secret field values even when a node carries a raw
  value internally.

Findings:
- The existing debug stream is the right spine for capture hooks: frame events
  were already there, and input/terminal events fit without a new global
  service.
- The capture shape now covers terminal profile, input/resize, frame metadata,
  redacted semantics, and output summaries. Fake time markers and richer
  worker/process capture should be proven by the proof-app scenario rather than
  invented abstractly.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed.

Next:
- Continue with the proof-app capture-to-test workflow: capture a deterministic
  Runs-screen resize/worker/log/palette/cancel scenario and use the snapshot to
  write or strengthen a regression test. Richer semantic graph interaction can
  follow after capture proves what tree navigation needs.

## 2026-05-31 - RDT.4 Proof-App Capture-To-Test Workflow

Status: Complete

Context:
- Active work continued the RDT.2/RDT.4 capture-to-test slice under M1.10.
- The previous slice created bounded capture hooks and core/runtime tests, but
  the proof app had not yet shown that the captured shape was useful for a real
  app-shaped workflow.

Changes:
- Exported `DebugEvent`, `FrameEvent`, `FrameDebugEvent`, and
  `InputDebugEvent` from `fleury_test.dart` so tests can build targeted capture
  snapshots without importing private `src/` debug internals.
- Added a proof-app test that drives the example console through the Runs
  screen, resize metadata, worker start, table focus, keyboard selection,
  debug capture, status assertions, and output-summary capture.
- The new test serializes a `DebugCaptureSnapshot` with the real semantic tree
  and verifies that the JSON contains the app active screen, last command,
  selected DataTable row, selected run label, running task status, frame dirty
  source, input sequence, and output summary.
- Removed the now-unnecessary private debug-events import from the core capture
  test.
- Updated M1.10/RDT tracker notes and recorded the test-library export
  decision.

Decisions:
- Keep the event record exports test-only through `fleury_test.dart`. They are
  useful for regression construction, but they should not imply a stable
  production replay artifact format.

Findings:
- The proof app can already exercise a meaningful capture-to-test path without
  more framework code: app commands, DataTable semantics, status semantics, and
  serialized semantic snapshots are enough to reconstruct the important
  workflow facts.
- The captured workflow uses synthetic frame/command records plus a real
  semantic tree. That is appropriate for deterministic regression tests; full
  runtime replay can wait until a real bug needs it.
- RDT.4 can be treated as complete for the current MVP. RDT.2 remains partial

## 2026-05-31 - M1.10 Semantic Graph Interaction

Status: Complete

Context:
- Active work continued the last M1.10 inspector gap.
- The Tree tab already summarized semantic state and rendered a preorder graph
  outline, but large apps need a way to inspect one semantic node without
  scanning the full dump.

Changes:
- Added a semantic cursor to `DebugController`.
- Scoped Arrow Down, Arrow Up, and Home debug key handling to the open Tree tab.
  These keys are not consumed while debug is off or while another tab is active.
- Reworked the Tree-tab semantic outline into a `semantic graph` section with
  a cursor position, selected-node details, a bounded graph window, and
  selected-node state/action/flag summaries.
- Exported `DebugEvents` through `fleury_test.dart` so debug shell tests can
  use the test-only helper surface rather than importing debug internals.
- Added tests for selected semantic-node details and Tree-tab key scoping.
- Marked RDT.3 and M1.10 complete for the MVP.

Decisions:
- Keep semantic graph browsing minimal for now: cursor, selected details, and a
  windowed outline. Full graph search/filtering can wait until a larger app
  proves the required UX.
- Consume semantic cursor keys only when the debug shell is open on the Tree
  tab so normal app navigation is preserved everywhere else.

Findings:
- The inspector now satisfies the M1.10 acceptance criteria: focus and command
  state come from semantic snapshots, selected-node details answer semantic
  node questions, Rebuilds answers dirty region/frame cost/source questions,
  task/effect summaries answer effect status, and terminal/capability rows
  answer fallback questions.
- This is enough debug-inspector capability for the current MVP. The next Phase
  1 gap should move to M1.11 sanitized output rather than adding more inspector
  UI.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_test.dart lib/src/debug/debug_state.dart lib/src/debug/debug_shell.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.

Next:
- Start M1.11 sanitized output pipeline. Use the current sanitizer,
  process-task/output semantics, markdown/link policies, clipboard redaction,
  and debug-capture redaction as the existing base; fill the concrete gaps with
  focused tests and shared behavior rather than starting from a new design.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.11 Terminal Sequence Redaction

Status: In progress

Context:
- Active work moved from completed M1.10 inspector work into M1.11 sanitized
  output.
- The first M1.11 slice already sanitized subprocess output and capped long
  lines, but `sanitizeForDisplay` still replaced only the leading ESC/C1 byte.
  That left printable CSI/OSC/DCS/APC payloads visible in output, which is not
  safe for OSC 52 clipboard data, OSC 8 URLs, or inline-image payloads.

Changes:
- Tightened `sanitizeForDisplay` so terminal control sequences collapse as
  units:
  - CSI sequences redact parameters/intermediates/final bytes as one visible
    replacement glyph.
  - OSC sequences redact payloads through BEL or ST terminators.
  - DCS/SOS/PM/APC strings redact payloads through ST terminators, covering
    Sixel/DCS and Kitty/APC-style image data.
  - Unterminated OSC payloads consume the rest of the unsafe payload instead
    of leaking it.
- Updated process task default decoding to `Utf8Codec(allowMalformed: true)`
  so malformed subprocess bytes become visible replacement characters instead
  of failing the task.
- Updated rendering and integration expectations for CSI collapse.
- Added sanitizer tests for CSI, OSC 52, OSC 8, DCS/Sixel, APC/Kitty image
  payloads, and unterminated OSC.
- Added process-task tests for OSC payload redaction and malformed UTF-8
  tolerance.
- Updated M1.11/TCS tracker notes and decision log entries.

Decisions:
- Treat raw terminal control strings as active content and redact the whole
  sequence payload at the display/storage boundary.
- Use malformed-tolerant UTF-8 for native subprocess output by default.

Findings:
- Replacing only ESC made rendered output inert, but it still leaked sensitive
  payload text. The safer invariant is: active terminal payloads never enter
  visible text, task output, task events, semantics, or future debug capture.
- Dart string escapes around BEL are easy to misread; tests now construct BEL
  explicitly where ambiguity would hide the terminator.
- The M1.11 sanitizer surface is stronger, but final redaction-hook audit work
  remains before the milestone should close.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/rendering/text_sanitizer.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart test/rendering/text_sanitizer_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/rendering/render_text_test.dart test/integration/end_to_end_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/text_sanitizer_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/rendering/render_text_test.dart test/integration/end_to_end_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart test/rendering/render_text_test.dart test/integration/end_to_end_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/src/markdown_text.dart test/data_table_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

Next:
- Continue M1.11 with the remaining redaction-hook audit: ensure secret-shaped
  values stay out of semantics, debug capture, clipboard surfaces, markdown,
  and output summaries, then decide whether M1.11 can close for the MVP.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.11 Redaction Hook Audit

Status: Complete

Context:
- Active work finished the last M1.11 sanitizer gap after terminal-sequence
  redaction and malformed process UTF-8 handling were already in place.
- The remaining risk was not raw terminal controls. It was diagnostic or
  semantic surfaces re-exposing values that widgets had already marked as
  redacted.

Changes:
- Updated `TextInput` so `TextClipboardPolicy.redacted` withholds the semantic
  value, marks `redactedValue`, and suppresses completion query state.
- Updated `TextArea` so redacted clipboard policy withholds the semantic value
  and emits `redactedValue`.
- Tightened debug capture serialization so redacted nodes redact validation
  errors and query/token-shaped state.
- Tightened debug Tree-tab selected-node details, outline summaries, and state
  summaries so semantic redaction flags are honored in the live inspector.
- Added tests for redacted text-control semantics, debug capture redaction, and
  Tree-tab selected-node redaction.
- Marked M1.11 and TCS.4 complete for the MVP.

Decisions:
- Treat redacted clipboard policy as semantic value redaction for first-party
  text controls.
- Make debug capture and inspector summaries consume semantic redaction flags,
  instead of relying on each widget to protect every downstream diagnostic
  surface.

Findings:
- Redaction has to be a graph contract. Once a semantic node says a value is
  redacted, every exporter, inspector, adapter, and capture path must preserve
  that decision.
- Labels and hints remain visible for redacted nodes. The protected surface is
  the user-provided value, validation text, query text, and secret-shaped state.
- The M1.11 MVP surface now covers terminal controls, process bytes, output
  caps, markdown links, image protocols, DataTable export/copy, clipboard
  reports, text-control semantics, debug capture, and inspector summaries.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/rendering/text_sanitizer.dart lib/src/effects/process_task.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/runtime/clipboard.dart test/rendering/text_sanitizer_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/text_sanitizer_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/clipboard_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/markdown_text.dart lib/src/image.dart lib/src/data_table.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart test/image_test.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/markdown_text_test.dart test/image_test.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in

## 2026-05-31 - M1.12 Local Distribution Path

Status: Complete

Context:
- Active work moved from M1.11 output safety into M1.12.
- The goal was not public release hygiene. It was a credible local path for a
  developer to bootstrap the workspace, run the proof app, run examples, invoke
  the Fleury CLI, and prove the standalone binary route.

Changes:
- Added `tool/fleury_dev.dart`, a repo-local Dart launcher with commands for:
  `bootstrap`, `list`, `proof`, `core-demo`, `widget-demo`, `cli`,
  `check --quick`, `activate-cli`, and `build-cli`.
- Added `docs/implementation/local-distribution-path.md` to capture the local
  command contract plus deferred pub.dev, standalone binary, Homebrew, npm
  wrapper, `create-fleury-app`, docs-site, and Dune/`dune_cli` tracks.
- Updated the workspace README with the launcher commands and local optional
  CLI paths.
- Added a proof-app README with direct and launcher-based run commands.
- Updated ADE.2, M1.12, the implementation index, and the decision log.
- Cleaned analyzer info surfaced by the new quick check in command/app/table
  semantic-state construction.

Decisions:
- Use a repo-local Dart launcher rather than adding a root `pubspec.yaml` or
  monorepo tool while package boundaries are still moving.
- Treat `activate-cli` and `build-cli` as developer-local routes for now, not
  as public release artifacts.

Findings:
- A tiny launcher is enough for M1.12. It gives stable commands without
  committing to Melos, a root package, or public package layout.
- The standalone binary route is viable today: `build-cli` compiles the local
  `fleury` executable and the generated binary can run `diagnose --json`.
- Quick checks are useful as a distribution smoke test because they validate
  package analyzes plus one core example, one widgets example, and the proof
  app.

Validation:
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --help` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart list` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --dry-run bootstrap` passed.

Status: Complete

  `packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase1-core-2026-05-31.json`.
- Ran and saved the widgets `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` baseline to
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase1-widgets-2026-05-31.json`.

  READMEs, and Phase 1 exit criteria with baseline evidence.

Findings:
- `SB.1` passed with command-to-frame p95 254 us, first-frame p95 61 us, and
  semantic-query p95 102 us across 20 measured iterations.
- `SB.3` passed with page-move p95 772 us, selected-row copy p95 310 us, and
  semantic-query p95 2497 us across 20 measured iterations over 100k rows.
- These are strong first baselines against the current candidate thresholds,
  but thresholds should stay informational until variance across machines and
  repeated runs is understood.

## 2026-05-31 - M1.2 Text Keymap And Word Movement

Status: Complete

Context:
- Phase 1 exit review left M1.2 open for "richer keymap scaffolding".
- The text model also still lacked the word movement promised by the M1.2
  acceptance criteria.

Changes:
- Added `TextEditingKeymap`, `TextEditingKeyBinding`, and
  `TextEditingKeyAction`.
- Added default single-line and multiline keymaps plus Emacs-style presets.
- Routed `TextInput` and `TextArea` non-text key handling through keymap
  intent resolution.
- Added grapheme-safe word movement over whitespace-delimited text runs to
  `TextEditingModel` and `TextEditingController`.
- Added keymap/model/widget tests for default chords, Ctrl/Alt word movement,
  Emacs-style presets, and selection extension.
- Switched command-palette result rows to eager child widgets so command row
  semantics are queryable before a render/layout pass.
- Marked M1.2 and TEE.2 through TEE.4 complete for the MVP.

Decisions:
- Keymaps resolve keys to editing intents only. Widgets still decide whether an
  intent is valid based on read-only state, completion state, history state,
  clipboard policy, and multiline behavior.
- Command palettes should use eager semantic rows. Data-scale virtualization
  belongs in `DataTable` and lazy list surfaces, not small command palettes.

Findings:
- `TextInput` and `TextArea` had converged enough that a shared keymap seam was
  cleaner than more widget-local branches.
- The command-palette semantic failures were not caused by keymaps directly.
  They exposed that lazy list rows are not mounted before layout, which is the
  wrong behavior for palette semantics.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/editing/text_editing.dart lib/src/editing/text_keymap.dart lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart lib/src/widgets/text_area.dart test/editing/text_editing_model_test.dart test/editing/text_keymap_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.

- `dart test test/editing/text_completion_test.dart test/editing/text_paste_test.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/command_palette.dart test/command_palette_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/command_palette_test.dart test/completion_text_input_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` and
  `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_example_console`

## 2026-05-31 - M2.2 LogRegion V0

Status: In progress

Context:
- Phase 2 needs production developer-tool widgets before Dune/`dune_cli`.
- The highest-compounding first wedge is a structured log/transcript region:
  it exercises streaming output, sanitization, copy/export, selection,
  semantics, lazy rows, and later ProcessPanel/agent transcript needs.

Changes:
- Added `LogRegion` in `fleury_widgets` with `LogEntry`,
  `LogRegionController`, copy options/results, export options/results, and
  `exportLogEntries`.
- Kept core `LogView` untouched as the runtime stdout/stderr capture view and
  named the app-facing widget `LogRegion` to avoid public import ambiguity.
- `LogRegion` supports tail-following selection through `ListView.builder`,
  Ctrl+C selected-entry copy through the framework clipboard service,
  sanitized line rendering/export, max-line truncation, severity/source/id
  semantic state, and log-level semantic aggregate state.
- Migrated the proof app Transcript screen from an eager custom column to
  `LogRegion` while preserving existing visible transcript lines and semantic
  source assertions.
- Updated proof-app tests to render after transcript navigation before checking
  lazy-mounted row text.

Decisions:
- Keep `LogRegion` lazy by default. Off-screen row semantics are intentionally
  absent until rendered; source-entry copy/export remains available without
  mounted rows.
- Treat `LogRegion` as the first M2.2 slice, not the whole log/process
  toolkit. Terminal output regions, LogView/LogRegion convergence, search,
  streaming markdown, diff/code, and process panels remain follow-up slices.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/log_region.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/log_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.2 LogRegion Scenario Benchmark

Status: Complete

Context:
- `LogRegion` needed objective pressure before more terminal-output widgets are
  built on top of it.
- The scenario lab already defines `SB.4 Log Tailing And Scrollback`, so the
  next useful slice was to add a package-local widgets benchmark rather than a
  standalone microbenchmark.

Changes:
- Added `SB.4 LogRegion Tailing And Scrollback` to
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`.
- The scenario mounts a 100k-entry `LogRegion`, renders the initial tail,
  appends a 1000-entry burst with unsafe terminal payloads, verifies sanitized
  tail-follow rendering, jumps into scrollback, returns to the tail, copies the
  selected entry, and queries semantics.
- Saved the first Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-2026-05-31.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario benchmark lab, data-widget
  workstream, milestone notes, and peer scorecard.
- Adjusted `LogRegionController` so default tail-following controllers open at
  the tail once the lazy list knows its item count.

Findings:
- The first smoke run exposed that aggregate `LogRegion` semantics can hold a
  stale visible range because the wrapper builds before lazy-list layout writes
  `visibleRange` back to the controller. The benchmark now uses
  `controller.visibleRange` after render for visible-range correctness while
  visible row semantics still come from mounted rows.
- The first full 100k baseline passed comfortably against the candidate
  thresholds before search/filter was added. The saved baseline was later
  superseded by the `LogRegion` search/filter entry below.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/log_region.dart test/log_region_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/log_region_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` lists `SB.3` and `SB.4`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --rows=10000 --json`
  passed as a smoke run.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-2026-05-31.json --json`
  passed and saved the full baseline.

Next:
- Run broader validation and stale-name checks.
- Continue M2.2 with either terminal output regions/ProcessPanel groundwork,
  or a streaming markdown slice.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.2 LogRegion Search And Filter

Status: Complete

Context:
- `LogRegion` needed source-entry search/filter before it could be a credible
  transcript/log surface for dense developer tools.
- The filter path had to preserve the security model: searchable/exported
  content should be sanitized visible text, not raw terminal control payloads.

Changes:
- Added `LogRegionFilterDescriptor` with query, source, severity, and
  case-sensitivity fields.
- Added `buildLogRegionEntryOrder` and extended `exportLogEntries` so apps can
  filter source entries without depending on mounted row widgets.
- `LogRegion` now renders filtered row order, reports filtered aggregate
  semantic state, and keeps source `rowIndex` separate from filtered
  `viewIndex` for row semantics and copy callbacks.
- Search covers id, source, severity labels, and sanitized message text.
  Unsafe OSC/ANSI payload content remains unsearchable if it is not visible
  after sanitization.
- Optimized the linear query path by precomputing the matcher once per filter
  pass, avoiding per-row source-set allocation, skipping sanitization work for
  safe messages, and using an allocation-free ASCII case-insensitive matcher.
- Extended `SB.4` with filter-query timing, filtered ANSI bytes, match count,
  and filtered visible range.

Decisions:
- Keep this as optimized linear search for now. The 100k baseline is good
  enough for a first slice, but true typeahead over larger retained logs should
  be revisited with indexing, debounce policy, or worker-backed query execution.
- Preserve durable source-entry identity in copy/export results even when the
  current UI is filtered.

Findings:
- A 10k-row smoke run after optimization produced filter-query p95 9963 us.
- The saved 100k-row baseline now reports append-burst p95 9591 us,
  scrollback-jump p95 3513 us, copy-selected-entry p95 8608 us,
  filter-query p95 68785 us, and semantic-query p95 3630 us.
- The filter-query p95 is the new pressure point inside `SB.4`; it is within
  the current candidate threshold but should stay visible in launch comparison
  work against Textual/OpenTUI/Bubble Tea style log surfaces.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/log_region.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/log_region_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/log_region_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --rows=10000 --json`
  passed as a smoke run.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-2026-05-31.json --json`
  passed and saved the updated full baseline.

Next:
- Run broader quick checks and docs sanity.
- Continue M2.2 with either terminal output regions/ProcessPanel groundwork,
  streaming markdown, or a peer-equivalent log comparison before adding more
  log-surface API.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.4 ProcessPanel V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- Phase 2 needs native-process UI affordances without turning Fleury into a
  shell DSL.
- The right composition point is the existing process/task model:
  `ProcessTaskController` already owns execution, cancellation, terminal
  handoff, bounded output, output sanitization, and structured events, while
  `LogRegion` owns scalable output display, filtering, copy, and log semantics.

Changes:
- Added `ProcessPanel` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `buildProcessOutputLogEntries` to map `TaskOutput` into structured
  `LogEntry` rows with source, severity, sequence id, and sanitizer/truncation
  metadata.
- `ProcessPanel` displays process status, command display name, progress label
  and bar, a `LogRegion` output body, filtered output, selected-output copy,
  task semantics, log semantics, exit-code state, latest output safety state,
  and Escape cancellation while running.
- Added current/last command metadata to `ProcessTaskController` so native
  process UI can report the command while the process is still running and
  clear it on reset.
- Exported `ProcessPanel` from `fleury_widgets`.

Decisions:
- Do not introduce a second process-output store in widgets. The process
  controller remains the source of truth; the panel renders/controller-binds it.
- Do not force the proof app's fake worker through `ProcessPanel`. It is a
  generic deterministic task, not a native subprocess. A later proof-app slice
  should either add a real process fixture or introduce a generic `TaskPanel`
  if that is the actual forcing case.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/process_panel.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/process_panel_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/effects/process_task.dart ../fleury/test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/process_panel_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/effects/process_task_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.

Next:
- Run broader quick checks and docs sanity.
- Continue M2.4 with a real native-process proof scenario, CommandRunner
  affordances, or terminal-output region convergence. If proof-app task
  pressure stays generic, consider a separate `TaskPanel` rather than widening
  `ProcessPanel`.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.4 ProcessCommandRunner V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- `ProcessPanel` made process state visible, but M2.4 also needs process work
  to participate in Fleury's app-command model: command palette entries,
  shortcuts, enabled/cancel state, and semantic actions.
- The app-kernel RFC keeps effects/process execution adjacent to commands.
  Commands should start or cancel work; task controllers remain responsible for
  long-running result, output, errors, and replay/debug hooks.

Changes:
- Added native `ProcessCommandRunner` in `package:fleury/fleury.dart`.
- `ProcessCommandRunner` builds start/cancel `AppCommand`s around a
  `ProcessTaskController` and `ProcessTaskCommand`, including stable/default
  command IDs, titles, descriptions, category, shortcuts, semantic start/cancel
  actions, restart policy, terminal handoff options, start mode, and cancel
  signal.
- Start command invocation is non-blocking: it starts the process and returns
  command completion immediately. Process success/failure/cancellation remains
  observable through `ProcessTaskController`.
- Added `ProcessCommandScope`, a listening `CommandScope` wrapper that rebuilds
  process command semantics and shortcut enabled state as the controller starts
  and stops.
- Exported `ProcessCommandRunner` and `ProcessCommandScope` from `fleury.dart`.

Decisions:
- Keep this API in the native umbrella because it depends on `dart:io` process
  start modes/signals through `ProcessTaskController`.
- Do not make command invocation wait for process completion. That would block
  app command surfaces on long-running work and conflate invocation state with
  task state.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/process_command_runner.dart lib/fleury.dart test/effects/process_command_runner_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/effects/process_command_runner_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/process_panel_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.

Next:
- Run broader quick checks and docs sanity.
- Continue M2.4 with a real native-process proof scenario that uses
  `ProcessCommandScope` plus `ProcessPanel`, or move to terminal-output region
  convergence if the proof scenario would duplicate the same behavior.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.4 Native Process Proof Screen

Status: Complete

Context:
- Isolated `ProcessPanel` and `ProcessCommandRunner` tests proved the pieces,
  but M2.4 needed app-shaped pressure inside the example subpackage before the
  process workflow could be considered credible.
- The proof app's fake worker should remain generic `TaskController` pressure;
  the real subprocess path belongs in a separate Process screen.

Changes:
- Added a Process screen to `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- Added global `Go to Process` navigation and process status bar state.
- Wired a `ProcessTaskController` and `ProcessCommandRunner` around
  `dart --version`.
- The Process screen installs `ProcessCommandScope` and displays the command
  through `ProcessPanel`, including `LogRegion` output and in-process copy
  policy.
- Expanded the proof-app test suite to navigate to the Process screen, inspect
  process start/cancel command semantics, run the native command, wait for the
  task to succeed, and assert command display, exit code, process output
  semantics, log row count, and status bar update.
- Updated the proof-app scenario doc to mark Process as a Phase 2 pressure
  screen rather than a Phase 1 first-slice requirement.

Decisions:
- Keep `dart --version` as the first real subprocess fixture. It is local,
  deterministic enough for the developer machine, quick, and does not need
  network access or repo-specific external tools.
- Keep the fake worker and native process as separate proof surfaces. The
  former pressures generic task composition; the latter pressures native
  process command/panel integration.
