## 2026-05-31 - M2.4 TerminalOutputRegion

Status: Complete

Context:
- Core `LogView` and `LogConsole` are good minimal runtime/debug views over
  `LogBuffer`, but app-authored terminal-output panes need the newer
  `LogRegion` affordances: lazy rows, filtering, copy/export semantics,
  sanitization, and semantic log/list-item state.
- This is the terminal-output convergence slice for M2.4: captured stdout/stderr
  should not be isolated from the production log-region surface.

Changes:
- Added `TerminalOutputRegion` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `buildTerminalOutputLogEntries` to map runtime `LogBuffer` lines into
  structured `LogEntry` rows with source, severity, stable-in-buffer row id, and
  terminal-output metadata.
- `TerminalOutputRegion` can read an explicit `LogBuffer` or the nearest
  `LogBufferScope`, listens for new captured output, and renders through
  `LogRegion` with filtering, copy policy, focus, and tail-follow controller
  support.
- Exported `TerminalOutputRegion` from `fleury_widgets`.

Decisions:
- Keep `LogView`/`LogConsole` as the minimal runtime/debug tail views. They are
  still useful for low-overhead display and the debug overlay.
- Use `TerminalOutputRegion` for app-facing captured-output panes where
  semantic rows, copy, filtering, and sanitizer behavior matter.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal_output_region.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal_output_region_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/log_region_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

Next:
- Run broader quick checks and docs sanity.
- Decide whether M2.4 needs benchmark pressure for captured-output adapters or
  whether the next implementation step should move to M2.3 SearchPanel/File
  Browser or M2.1 forms.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.3 SearchPanel V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.3 needs a general search surface beyond command palettes: result rows
  from files, logs, symbols, process output, diagnostics, or future agent
  transcripts should share one filter/selection/copy/semantics contract.
- The implementation should reuse existing retained primitives rather than
  introducing a bespoke search runtime: `TextInput`, `ListView.builder`,
  semantic nodes, clipboard reports, and the terminal sanitizer are already the
  right foundations.

Changes:
- Added `SearchResult`, `SearchPanel`, `SearchResultMatcher`,
  `SearchPanelCopyOptions`, `SearchPanelCopyResult`, `buildSearchResultOrder`,
  and `exportSearchResult` in `fleury_widgets`.
- `SearchPanel` provides a query field, fuzzy subsequence filtering by default,
  keyboard result navigation, Enter activation, optional custom matcher,
  selected-result Ctrl+C copy, stable source-result indexes plus filtered view
  indexes, and semantic region/list-item state.
- Result rendering and export sanitize terminal controls and flatten line
  breaks before display, search, semantics, and copy.
- Exported the new search surface from `package:fleury_widgets/fleury_widgets.dart`.

Decisions:
- Keep search-panel result filtering as source-order row-order helpers, not a
  ranking engine. Apps can pre-rank results or provide a custom matcher without
  Fleury overcommitting to scoring policy in v0.
- Return both source result index and filtered view index from copy/activation
  paths. This follows `LogRegion` and avoids off-by-filter bugs in app code.
- Build the panel on lazy `ListView.builder` instead of command-palette eager
  rows because general search result sets can be much larger than command
  palettes.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/search_panel.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/search_panel_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/search_panel_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/search_panel_test.dart test/command_palette_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.

Next:
- Continue M2.3 with TreeTable/FileBrowser or wire `SearchPanel` into the proof
  app when a concrete cross-screen/search scenario is ready.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.3 FileBrowser V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- The existing `FilePicker` is useful for simple selection, but M2.3 needs a
  production file browsing surface: lazy rows, semantic tree items, safe
  path/name rendering, filterable entry order, selected-path copy, and source
  identity separate from filtered view position.
- File and directory names are untrusted terminal content. They can contain
  controls or line breaks, so the widget must follow the same sanitizer policy
  as logs/search/results before exposing names through display, search,
  semantics, or clipboard text.

Changes:
- Added `FileBrowser` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `FileBrowserEntry`, `FileBrowserEntryType`,
  `FileBrowserFilterDescriptor`, `FileBrowserController`,
  `FileBrowserCopyOptions`, `FileBrowserCopyResult`,
  `buildFileBrowserEntryOrder`, and `exportFileBrowserEntry`.
- `FileBrowser` lists local filesystem entries with directories first,
  optional hidden-file visibility, optional app entity filtering, query
  filtering, lazy visible rows through `ListView.builder`, Enter/Right directory
  navigation, Backspace/Left parent navigation, file activation callbacks,
  selected-path Ctrl+C copy, and semantic tree/tree-item state.
- Rendering, search text, semantic path/name state, and copied/exported paths
  pass through the terminal sanitizer and line-break flattening.
- Exported the new browser surface from `package:fleury_widgets/fleury_widgets.dart`.

Decisions:
- Keep `FilePicker` intact for simple file selection. `FileBrowser` is the new
  Phase 2 production surface instead of a breaking rewrite of the old picker.
- Use semantic `tree`/`treeItem` roles for filesystem browsing even though v0
  shows one directory level at a time. This preserves the meaning layer for a
  future expanded tree/table browser.
- Copy sanitized paths by default. Activation callbacks still receive the
  structured entry, but semantics and clipboard text must not leak terminal
  control payloads from hostile filenames.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/file_browser.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/file_browser_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/file_browser_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/file_browser_test.dart test/file_picker_test.dart test/tree_test.dart test/search_panel_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.

Next:
- Continue M2.3 with TreeTable or add proof-app file/search pressure if a
  concrete browser workflow is useful before TreeTable.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.3 Tree Semantic Hardening

Status: Complete

Context:
- TreeTable should not invent a separate meaning model while the existing
  `Tree` widget lacks semantic tree/tree-item nodes.
- FileBrowser now uses `tree`/`treeItem` semantics for filesystem browsing, so
  the base tree widget should expose the same role family and sanitizer policy
  before a hierarchical data-table surface builds on it.

Changes:
- Added `label` to `Tree` for semantic tree labeling.
- Added aggregate `SemanticRole.tree` state: row count, root count, expanded
  count, visible range when available, selected index, and selected row key.
- Added semantic `treeItem` rows with stable row keys, row index, depth,
  branch/expanded state, child count, selected state, open/activate actions,
  and sanitizer metadata.
- Sanitized tree labels before rendering or exposing them through semantics,
  including terminal control sequence redaction and line-break flattening.

Decisions:
- Treat the existing `Tree` as the semantic foundation for TreeTable rather
  than building TreeTable with a separate hidden tree model.
- Use positional row keys for v0 because `TreeNode` has no public stable id.
  TreeTable can introduce explicit row keys when it needs durable identity
  across dynamic data updates.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/tree.dart test/tree_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Note: an optional broader `test/goldens_test.dart` run still shows existing
  command-palette modal golden drift; this Tree slice does not change that
  golden, and the required quick gate remains green.

Next:
- Continue M2.3 with an actual TreeTable surface now that base tree semantics
  are in place.

## 2026-05-31 - M2.3 TreeTable V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.3 named TreeTable as the remaining production-widget gap after
  `SearchPanel`, `FileBrowser`, and base `Tree` semantic hardening.
- TreeTable needs the table ergonomics developers expect from dense tools while
  preserving tree meaning: explicit row keys, expansion, filtered descendant
  discovery, visible-row laziness, semantic tree items, semantic cells, and
  safe copy/export.

Changes:
- Added `TreeTable` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `TreeTableNode`, `TreeTableRow`, `TreeTableController`,
  `TreeTableFilterDescriptor`, `TreeTableExportOptions`,
  `TreeTableExportResult`, `TreeTableCopyOptions`, `TreeTableCopyResult`,
  `TreeTableCellBuilder`, `buildTreeTableRows`, and `exportTreeTableRows`.
- `TreeTable` reuses `DataTableColumn` and `DataTableExportFormat` for column
  and TSV/CSV behavior while owning tree-specific expansion and row identity.
- Implemented lazy visible rows through `ListView.builder`, Enter/Right
  expansion, Left collapse/parent traversal, leaf activation callbacks,
  selected-row Ctrl+C copy, hidden descendant discovery during filtering, and
  sanitizer-safe rendering/search/semantics/export.
- Exposed aggregate tree semantics plus visible `treeItem` rows and
  per-visible-cell `tableCell` nodes with row key, column id/index, tree depth,
  selection, branch/expanded state, copy action, and sanitizer metadata.
- Exported the TreeTable surface from
  `package:fleury_widgets/fleury_widgets.dart`.

Decisions:
- Build TreeTable as a dedicated tree/table composition rather than wrapping
  `DataTable` directly. `DataTable` remains the high-volume flat render island;
  TreeTable needs tree item semantics and branch navigation first. It still
  reuses the DataTable column/export vocabulary.
- Require explicit node keys. Base `Tree` can use positional row keys, but
  production hierarchical data needs durable identity for expansion, copy,
  filtering, and future proof-app workflows.
- Filtering ignores collapsed expansion state and returns matching rows with
  ancestors. This makes hidden descendants discoverable without forcing apps to
  mutate expansion state for search.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/tree_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/tree_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_table_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_table_test.dart test/tree_test.dart test/file_browser_test.dart test/search_panel_test.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

Next:
- Decide whether M2.3 needs proof-app file/search/tree pressure or whether the
  next production-widget gap should move to M2.1 forms or M2.2 JSON/diff/code
  views.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.3 TreeTable Benchmark And Index Pressure

Status: Complete

Context:
- TreeTable had isolated widget tests and proof-app pressure, but no large-data
  benchmark evidence.
- The scenario lab already reserved `SB.5` for streaming markdown, so TreeTable
  needed a non-conflicting benchmark id.

Changes:
- Added `SB.11 TreeTable Hierarchy Filter And Copy` to the
  `fleury_widgets` scenario benchmark runner.
- The scenario builds a 100k-leaf hierarchy plus branch nodes, renders one
  expanded branch, expands another branch, pages and jumps through visible
  rows, filters for a collapsed descendant, copies the selected filtered row,
  verifies sanitizer behavior, and queries semantic tree/tree-item state.
- Added `TreeTableSearchIndex` so large trees can precompute sanitized
  searchable text when the data changes and reuse an exact-token posting list
  for ID/path/symbol-style lookups on the UI path.
- Updated `TreeTable` and `buildTreeTableRows` to accept the optional index
  while preserving the direct scan path for small trees and fuzzy fallback
  queries.
- Added `TreeTableFilterMode.exactToken` so the indexed posting-list path is an
  explicit lookup mode; default filtering remains fuzzy contains/subsequence
  matching.
- Saved the first baseline to
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated benchmark docs, the scenario lab, M2.3 milestone notes, the data
  widget workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Use `SB.11` instead of reusing `SB.5` because `SB.5` is already the streaming
  markdown scenario.
- Keep index construction measured separately from query latency. Large
  hierarchies need a data-change-time cost model, not hidden work during every
  keystroke.
- Do not let benchmark acceleration silently narrow default search semantics.
  Exact-token lookup is opt-in through the filter descriptor.

Findings:
- The first unindexed `SB.11` run validated correctness but exposed filtered
  descendant reveal as too slow: 676896 us p95 over 100k leaves.
- A reusable index with exact-token postings changes the saved baseline:
  100100 indexed rows, index-build p95 1851310 us, filter-query p95 4074 us,
  selected-row copy p95 8002 us, semantic-query p95 1979 us, and page-move p95
  11640 us.
- This is a good launch story for ID/path/symbol lookup in large trees. It does
  not automatically solve fuzzy contains/subsequence typeahead over all fields;
  that remains a debounce, worker, richer-index, or render-island question.

Validation:
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/tree_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/tree_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_table_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- TreeTable tests now cover indexed filtering parity, sanitizer behavior, and
  the difference between default fuzzy filtering and exact-token indexed lookup.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --size=120x32 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed with the indexed path.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --rows=100000 --size=120x32 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the indexed baseline.
- `dart test test/tree_table_test.dart test/tree_test.dart test/file_browser_test.dart test/search_panel_test.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 34 markdown files have resolving local
  links.

  or resize/terminal-matrix pressure.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.1 Forms Progressive Interaction Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.1 was the clearest untouched Phase 2 production-toolkit gap.
- The prototype-first plan called for one deterministic connection setup form
  that proves a shared workflow definition can drive full-screen UI,
  prompt-mode fallback, and semantic tests.

Changes:
- Added `FormDefinition`, `FormFieldSpec`, `FormOption`, `FormController`,
  `FormValues`, `FormSubmitResult`, `FormPanel`, `FormPanelLayout`,
  `FormPrompt`, and `FormPromptSession` to `fleury_widgets`.
- Added text, secret, select, and checkbox field specs with initial values,
  required-field validation, custom validators, submit results, errors, dirty
  state, submitted state, and prompt-mode parsing.
- Added `SemanticRole.form` and `SemanticRole.formField` in core semantics.
- `FormPanel` now renders the same field definition as full-screen or inline
  UI with focus traversal, submit/cancel buttons, field-level semantic state,
  validation errors, and redacted secret values.
- Added a proof-app Connection screen backed by the same connection setup
  field definition. The test fills the form by keyboard, submits it, and
  verifies transcript feedback.
- Updated M2.1 milestone notes, progressive prototype evidence, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Start forms with a model-first API rather than isolated field widgets. The
  launch value is one definition feeding full-screen, inline, prompt, and test
  projections.
- Treat form/form-field as first-party semantic roles. Generic regions are too
  weak for validation, redaction, prompt fallback, and future adapter behavior.
- Keep wizard/page-flow, async validation, remote/browser projection, and a
  broad field catalog out of this slice until proof workflows require them.

Findings:
- Existing `TextInput`, `PasswordInput`, `Select`, `Checkbox`, `Button`, and
  `FocusTraversalGroup` were enough to build the first form projection without
  adding new focus primitives.
- Secret handling composes through `PasswordInput` clipboard redaction and the
  form-field wrapper redacting semantic values.
- Prompt-mode fallback is viable as a controller/session model; it should not
  be tied to terminal rendering yet.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/form.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/form_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart test/controls_test.dart test/select_test.dart test/number_input_test.dart test/password_input_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 34 markdown files and 793 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- Continue M2.1 only if a proof workflow needs richer form behavior; otherwise
  move to the next production-toolkit gap such as JSON/diff/code views,
  streaming markdown pressure, resize storms, or real-terminal compatibility.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.2 JsonView Structured Payload Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.2 already had `LogRegion`, `ProcessPanel`, and terminal-output surfaces,
  but no structured developer-document view.
- JSON is a common payload shape for CLIs, diagnostics, agents, subprocess
  output, API responses, and future Dune workflows, so it is a better next
  production-widget pressure point than expanding forms before a proof workflow
  asks for more form behavior.

Changes:
- Added `JsonView` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `JsonViewDocument`, `JsonViewController`, `JsonViewRow`,
  `JsonViewCopyOptions`, `JsonViewCopyResult`, `JsonValueType`,
  `JsonViewCopyMode`, `buildJsonViewRows`, and `exportJsonViewRow`.
- Added `SemanticRole.json` and `SemanticRole.jsonNode` in core semantics.
- `JsonView` supports parsed JSON strings or already-materialized Dart values,
  collapsible object/array rows, JSON pointer/path state, root and selected
  type metadata, parse-error semantics, subtree or line copy, keyboard
  navigation, focused aggregate semantics, lazy visible-row mounting, and
  terminal-control sanitization before display, semantics, and copy/export.
- Added the proof-app Payload screen with a screen-local focus command,
  `JsonView` payload fixture, safe terminal-control payload rendering, selected
  JSON copy, and transcript feedback.
- Updated M2.2 milestone notes, the data-widget workstream, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Treat JSON as a first-party semantic document surface, not just a generic
  `Tree` wrapper. Path, pointer, parse errors, type names, and subtree copy are
  stable developer-tool concepts that tests and future adapters should be able
  to query directly.
- Sanitize JSON string values and object keys before JSON encoding for display
  or copy. Escaped JSON text alone is inert, but it can still reveal hidden
  OSC/CSI/DCS payload contents that the terminal security policy should
  collapse.
- Do not add JSON filtering/indexing or a benchmark yet. The first useful slice
  is structured inspection, path semantics, safe copy, parse errors, and proof
  app pressure.

Findings:
- Existing `ListView.builder`, `FocusWithin`, `KeyBindings`, `Clipboard`, and
  semantic APIs were enough to build a reusable structured-document surface.
- Aggregate focused semantics need `FocusWithin` plus an owned `FocusNode` when
  focus can arrive through child list mounting; simply reading an optional
  external focus node is too weak for proof-app assertions.
- The same sanitizer rule from logs applies to JSON values: active control
  payloads should not become visible or copyable just because the view is
  structured.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/json_view.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/json_view_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/json_view_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/json_view_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 34 markdown files and 801 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- Continue M2.2 with DiffView/CodeView/MarkdownView pressure, or move to theme
  expansion/terminal compatibility if those become more urgent for launch.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.2 DiffView Structured Change Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.2 had logs, process output, terminal output, and JSON payload inspection,
  but no first-party change-review surface.
- Diffs are central to developer tools, agent workflows, reviews, patches, and
  future Dune workflows, so the next production-widget slice should make
  unified diffs structured, navigable, copyable, safe, and semantically
  testable.

Changes:
- Added `DiffView` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `DiffDocument`, `DiffLine`, `DiffLineKind`, `DiffViewController`,
  `DiffViewCopyMode`, `DiffViewCopyOptions`, `DiffViewCopyResult`,
  `parseUnifiedDiff`, and `exportDiffSelection`.
- Added `SemanticRole.diff` and `SemanticRole.diffLine` in core semantics.
- `DiffView` parses unified diffs into file header, hunk header, context,
  addition, deletion, metadata, and marker rows with file path, hunk index,
  old/new line numbers, aggregate file/hunk/addition/deletion counts, selected
  line/hunk copy modes, focused aggregate semantics, lazy visible-row mounting,
  and terminal-control sanitization before display, semantics, and copy/export.
- Added the proof-app Changes screen with a screen-local focus command,
  selected-hunk copy, unsafe terminal-control fixture text, and transcript
  feedback.
- Updated M2.2 milestone notes, the data-widget workstream, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Treat diffs as a first-party semantic document surface, not colored raw text.
  File path, hunk index, old/new line numbers, addition/deletion stats, and
  selected-hunk copy are core developer-tool concepts that tests and future
  adapters should query directly.
- Start with unified diff parsing and selected-line/hunk copy. Side-by-side
  display, inline word diffing, folding, file filters, and benchmark pressure
  remain follow-up work until a larger proof workflow requires them.
- Apply the same output-safety boundary as logs and JSON: active terminal
  payloads in patch content are collapsed before render, semantics, and
  clipboard/export.

Findings:
- `ListView.builder`, `FocusWithin`, `KeyBindings`, and the clipboard report
  API again provided enough infrastructure for a reusable production widget.
- Unified diff parsing is lightweight enough for the first slice, but launch
  claims should not imply large-diff indexing, folding, or side-by-side review
  support yet.
- Proof-app pressure caught the useful default copy behavior: developer review
  flows want hunk copy more than only current-line copy.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/diff_view.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/diff_view_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

- `dart test test/diff_view_test.dart test/json_view_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 34 markdown files and 809 local links
  resolve.

Next:

## 2026-06-01 - M2.2 CodeView Structured Source Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.2 had first-party surfaces for logs, process output, JSON payloads, and
  unified diffs, but no reusable source-code inspection widget.
- Source snippets are a core developer-tool and agent workflow surface. The
  useful first slice is not a full syntax engine; it is safe rendering,
  line-level structure, line numbers, copy/export, and semantic testability.

Changes:
- Added and exported `CodeView` in `fleury_widgets`.
- Added `CodeDocument`, `CodeLine`, `CodeLineKind`, `CodeViewController`,
  `CodeViewCopyMode`, `CodeViewCopyOptions`, `CodeViewCopyResult`,
  `parseCodeDocument`, and `exportCodeSelection`.
- `CodeView` parses source into sanitized line rows with source-line
  classification, line numbers, indentation, line/non-empty/comment/blank
  counts, selected-line or whole-document copy, focused aggregate semantics,
  lazy visible-row mounting, and terminal-control sanitization before display,
  semantics, and copy/export.
- Added `showLineNumbers` and `tabSize` source-shape state to parsed
  documents, and expose selected code-line kind through aggregate semantics.
- Added focused `CodeView` tests for parser shape, optional line-number
  display, aggregate/row semantics, copy/export, and unsafe terminal payload
  collapse.
- Added the proof-app Source screen with a screen-local focus command,
  unsafe terminal-control fixture text, selected-line copy, and transcript
  feedback.
- Updated M2.2 milestone notes, the data-widget workstream, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Treat source snippets as a first-party semantic document surface, not just
  styled text. Line numbers, source-shape counts, indentation, selected-line
  copy, and sanitized line state are stable developer-tool concepts that tests
  and future adapters should query directly.
- Start with whole-line classification rather than token-level highlighting.
  The framework needs a safe, robust, copyable code surface first; language
  grammars, symbol indexing, folding, and token navigation can follow when a
  proof workflow requires them.
- Keep display text and clipboard/export text sanitized and tab-expanded.
  Active terminal payloads embedded in source fixtures are collapsed before
  render, semantics, and copy/export.

Findings:
- Existing `ListView.builder`, `FocusWithin`, `KeyBindings`, clipboard report
  APIs, and semantic roles were enough for another reusable document surface.
- The proof app is now useful as a developer-tool suite forcing case: table,
  tree, JSON, diff, source, logs, process, forms, and diagnostics all move
  through the same app-kernel/test path.
- Whole-line source classification is enough for M2.2 semantics, but launch
  claims should avoid implying full syntax highlighting, folding, or symbol
  search until those are implemented.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/code_view.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/code_view_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/code_view_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/code_view_test.dart test/diff_view_test.dart test/json_view_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches

- Local markdown link check passed: 43 markdown files and 825 local links

## 2026-06-01 - M2.2 MarkdownView Structured Document Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.2 had first-party surfaces for logs, process output, JSON payloads,
  unified diffs, and source snippets, but not a navigable Markdown document
  viewer.
- `MarkdownText` already handled lightweight inline/help rendering. The missing
  framework piece was a production document surface with row navigation,
  stable block/link semantics, safe copy/export, and app-kernel proof pressure.

Changes:
- Added and exported `MarkdownView` in `fleury_widgets` alongside the existing
  `MarkdownText`.
- Added `MarkdownDocument`, `MarkdownBlock`, `MarkdownBlockKind`,
  `MarkdownLink`, `MarkdownViewController`, `MarkdownViewCopyMode`,
  `MarkdownViewCopyOptions`, `MarkdownViewCopyResult`,
  `parseMarkdownDocument`, and `exportMarkdownSelection`.
- Added `SemanticRole.markdown` and `SemanticRole.markdownBlock` in core
  semantics.
- `MarkdownView` parses supported Markdown into sanitized visible rows with
  heading/list/link/code counts, selected-block or whole-document copy,
  visible URL fallback link semantics, focused aggregate semantics, lazy
  visible-row mounting, and terminal-control sanitization before display,
  semantics, and copy/export.
- Kept fenced-code links inert for semantic/link discovery, preserving the
  safety behavior expected from code blocks.
- Added focused `MarkdownView` tests for parser shape, aggregate/block/link
  semantics, selected-block copy, whole-document export, and unsafe terminal
  payload collapse.
- Added the proof-app Docs screen with a screen-local focus command, visible
  URL semantics, unsafe terminal-control fixture text, selected-block copy, and
  transcript feedback.
- Updated M2.2 milestone notes, the data-widget workstream, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Keep `MarkdownText` as the lightweight renderer and add `MarkdownView` as the
  document surface. This avoids overloading a simple inline widget with
  controller, selection, copy/export, and aggregate semantics.
- Treat Markdown as a first-party semantic document surface. Heading/list/link
  counts, block kind, selected block, visible URL fallback policy, and safe copy
  are framework behavior that tests and future adapters should query directly.
- Continue visible URL rendering for links and mark OSC 8 as disabled by
  default. Rich trusted hyperlink output remains a future policy-gated feature.

Findings:
- The existing Markdown block/inline parser was enough for first-slice
  document semantics; no external Markdown package was needed for MVP pressure.
- `ListView.builder` rows must stay one-line widgets. Link semantics are
  exposed as hidden semantic siblings of the list rather than children inside
  each row, so virtualization and row layout stay predictable.
- Markdown document safety needs both rendered-text sanitization and source
  export sanitization. Hidden OSC payloads in blockquotes or links should not
  become visible or copyable.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/markdown_text.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/markdown_view_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/markdown_view_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/code_view_test.dart test/diff_view_test.dart test/json_view_test.dart test/markdown_text_test.dart test/markdown_view_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 43 markdown files and 833 local links
  resolve.

Next:

  so the next best slice is likely theme/terminal compatibility hardening,
  scenario benchmark expansion, or any remaining Phase 2 launch-risk item in
  the implementation docs.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.5 Component Theme V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.2's named document surfaces are complete at v0. The next untouched Phase
  2 item was M2.5 theme and component-theme expansion.
- Core already has `ThemeData.extensions`, so the first theme slice should use
  that seam rather than expanding core `ThemeData` with every widget-package
  token.

Changes:
- Added and exported `FleuryWidgetTheme` in `fleury_widgets`.
- `FleuryWidgetTheme` carries widget-package defaults for control focus,
  disabled controls, switch on/off styling, progress filled/track styling, and
  Markdown document block styling.
- Wired `Checkbox`, `Toggle`, `Radio`, `Switch`, disabled `Button`,
  `ProgressBar`, and `MarkdownView` to resolve component defaults from
  `ThemeData.extensions`.
- Kept explicit widget styles authoritative. For example, explicit
  `ProgressBar.filledStyle` and `ProgressBar.trackStyle` override component
  theme defaults.
- Wrapped the proof app in a custom `Theme` with `FleuryWidgetTheme` tokens and
  added proof-app coverage that the Docs screen receives themed Markdown
  heading styling.
- Updated M2.5 milestone notes, the proof-app scenario screen table, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep core `ThemeData` small. Widget-package component tokens live in a typed
  extension because they do not need to exist for every platform host or
  minimal Fleury app.
- Start with high-reuse, low-risk tokens rather than a full design-system pass:
  controls, progress bars, and Markdown document blocks. Data-table/log/code
  severity and syntax token themes should be added only when proof workflows
  show real duplication or API need.
- Theme defaults must not beat explicit widget parameters. Fleury should feel
  Flutter-like here: ambient theme provides defaults, explicit constructor
  styles are local overrides.

Findings:
- The existing extension mechanism was enough for component themes; no core
  theme API change was needed.
- `ProgressBar` needed nullable style parameters to distinguish "use theme
  default" from "explicitly use empty style." This preserves existing default
  rendering while making themed defaults possible.
- The proof app is now carrying a real component theme, so future widget slices
  can catch accidental hard-coded styling when they enter the app path.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/component_theme.dart lib/src/controls.dart lib/src/progress_bar.dart lib/src/markdown_text.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/component_theme_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/component_theme_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/component_theme_test.dart test/controls_test.dart test/progress_bar_test.dart test/markdown_view_test.dart test/theme_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 43 markdown files and 833 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- Reassess whether M2.5 needs a second theme slice now, or whether the next
  highest-risk Phase 2 work should be real-terminal compatibility, active
  capability probes, or targeted debug/replay hardening.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Opt-In Active Terminal Probes V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.10 is the next broad terminal-correctness risk after passive diagnosis
  and component-theme v0.
- The existing M1.7 decision was right for app startup: keep passive
  env-derived capabilities as the default. Phase 2 needs real evidence, but
  active probes must stay explicit because they write escape sequences and
  wait on terminal replies.

Changes:
- Added `TerminalProbeTransport`, `runTerminalProbeSuite`,
  `TerminalProbeReport`, `TerminalProbeResult`, and `TerminalProbeStatus`.
- The first probe suite checks:
  - primary device attributes as a sentinel response;
  - Kitty keyboard status through `CSI ? u`;
  - Kitty graphics query support through a non-displaying query action followed
    by primary DA.
- Added `fleury diagnose --probe` and `--probe-timeout=<ms>`.
- `fleury diagnose --json --probe` now includes `activeProbes`.
- Non-TTY execution returns structured skipped evidence instead of attempting
  terminal writes.
- Exported the probe model from `fleury_core.dart` for tests, alternate hosts,
  and future PTY/matrix runners.
- Updated M2.10, the terminal capability/security workstream, and the decision
  log.

Research notes:
- [Kitty keyboard docs](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/keyboard-protocol/)
  define `CSI ? u` status queries and `CSI ? flags u` replies.
- [Kitty graphics docs](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/)
  recommend a graphics query followed by primary DA to distinguish unsupported
  terminals from protocol replies.
- [Xterm control-sequence docs](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html)
  define primary device attributes (`CSI c`) and its `CSI ? ... c` replies.

Decisions:
- Keep active probe output as diagnosis evidence first, not runtime capability
  mutation. Confirmed features can later feed capability summaries if the
  real-terminal matrix proves this is reliable.
- Use primary DA as a sentinel for optional protocol probes so "no optional
  reply, but terminal answered" becomes unsupported instead of an ambiguous
  timeout.
- Keep the probe transport in core but the stdio implementation in the native
  CLI. That preserves browser/PTY/fake-host options without importing
  `dart:io` into `fleury_core.dart`.

Findings:
- The current `InputParser` intentionally consumes some terminal replies, so
  active probing should not be bolted onto normal TUI input streams yet.
- The CLI is the right first collection point because it can briefly own raw
  stdin/stdout, restore modes immediately, and emit a bug-report artifact.
- Running `diagnose --probe` in this Codex environment correctly skipped
  active probes because stdout was not a terminal.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/terminal/terminal_probe.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/terminal/terminal_probe_test.dart bin/fleury.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/terminal_probe.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart lib/fleury_core.dart bin/fleury.dart test/terminal/terminal_probe_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/terminal_probe_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/terminal/capability_requirements_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart run bin/fleury.dart diagnose --json` in `packages/fleury` produced
  passive diagnosis JSON.
- `dart run bin/fleury.dart diagnose --json --probe` in `packages/fleury`
  produced `activeProbes.skippedReason` because stdout was not a terminal.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 838 local links resolve.

Next:
- Run `fleury diagnose --json --probe` manually in real macOS terminals and
  record the matrix for Terminal.app, iTerm2, Kitty, Ghostty, Alacritty,
  WezTerm, tmux, SSH, and Windows Terminal where feasible.
- Decide after matrix evidence whether active probe confirmations should feed
  `TerminalCapabilities` or remain diagnostic-only.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Semantic Accessibility Snapshot V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.8 was still untouched after the terminal probe slice. The launch plan
  defers broad accessibility claims, but the framework still needs a concrete
  semantic fallback layer that can support tests, prompt mode, debug capture,
  and future adapters.
- Rendered cells are the wrong source for accessibility and fallback output:
  cells do not know actions, validation, redaction, capability fallback, or
  workflow state.

Changes:
- Added `AccessibilitySnapshot`, `AccessibilityNode`,
  `buildAccessibilitySnapshot`, and `SemanticTreeAccessibility`.
- Snapshots derive from `SemanticTree` and preserve source semantic id, role,
  role label, label, safe value, hint, validation error, state descriptions,
  sorted semantic actions, children, JSON export, and plain-text narration.
- State descriptions now cover disabled/focused/selected/busy,
  checked/unchecked, expanded/collapsed, redacted values, progress,
  collection ranges, text/data selection, capability fallback, clipboard
  policy, status severity, and paste progress.
- Redacted, obscured, or clipboard-redacted semantic values are omitted from
  accessibility values and plain-text narration.
- Added `FleuryTester.accessibilitySnapshot()` so tests can assert this layer
  directly.
- Exported the accessibility snapshot model through `fleury_core.dart` and
  `fleury_test.dart`.
- Updated M2.8, the semantic app graph workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat this as a text-first semantic fallback model, not a full OS
  screen-reader bridge. Native platform adapters can translate it later once
  launch foundations are stable.
- Keep the first API small and derived. Widgets should continue contributing
  semantic nodes; accessibility output should not add another metadata path.
- Include enough state to make fallback output useful for production developer
  tools: validation, action availability, data range, progress, clipboard
  policy, and capability fallback.

Findings:
- Existing semantic state already carries most of the required fallback
  material. The missing piece was a stable projection that redacts and narrates
  it consistently.
- Sorting actions in the snapshot makes text/JSON output deterministic without
  changing semantic node action sets.
- This strengthens the semantic app graph pitch in a developer-visible way:
  the graph is no longer only for tests and inspector panels; it can drive
  fallback interaction and future accessibility adapters.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart`

- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.

  passed.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.1/M2.8 Prompt Fallback Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.8's accessibility snapshot existed, but prompt-mode fallback still had to
  use `FormPromptSession`'s ad hoc prompt state instead of the semantic graph.
- M2.1's original form slice deliberately started from one definition feeding
  full-screen, inline, prompt, and test surfaces. The missing proof was that
  prompt mode can publish the same semantic/accessibility evidence without
  mounting a visual widget tree.

Changes:
- Added `FormPromptSession.semanticTree`.
- Added `FormPromptSession.accessibilitySnapshot`.
- Prompt sessions now expose:
  - form semantics with prompt layout, field count, error count, dirty,
    submitted, valid, completed, cancelled, active field id, and
    submit/cancel actions;
  - form-field semantics for every field with active prompt selection,
    prompt position/count, field id/type, required state, option count,
    validation errors, displayed values, and secret redaction;
  - accessibility narration through the shared semantic-derived
    `AccessibilitySnapshot`.
- Extended accessibility state mapping for form and form-field state:
  field counts, error counts, prompt layout, active field, required fields,
  secret fields, field type, active prompt, prompt position, and option count.
- Added prompt fallback tests that verify active prompt state, validation
  errors, select display labels, secret redaction, completed form actions, and
  plain-text narration safety.
- Updated M2.1, M2.8, the semantic app graph workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Non-widget fallback projections can publish semantic trees directly. Requiring
  a hidden widget tree just to recover semantics would make prompt mode feel
  bolted on.
- Prompt-mode values should display user-facing labels where possible. Select
  fields expose option labels and checkbox fields expose yes/no in the semantic
  fallback projection.
- Secret form fields should set explicit redaction flags, not only omit values,
  so accessibility/debug/future adapters can explain that a value exists but is
  intentionally hidden.

Findings:
- The existing form controller already had enough state for a strong prompt
  fallback projection; the gap was only the projection boundary.
- Adding this path makes the semantic app graph more than an inspection layer:
  it now actively unifies visual and prompt-mode UX.
- The accessibility projection needed a few form-specific state descriptions to
  be useful in plain text, but this did not require changing the semantic node
  model.

Validation:
- `dart format ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart lib/src/form.dart test/form_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/form.dart test/form_test.dart ../fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart test/controls_test.dart test/select_test.dart test/password_input_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8/RDT Accessibility In Debug Capture

Status: Complete

Context:
- Accessibility snapshots now exist and prompt fallback uses them, but targeted
  debug capture still serialized only raw semantic JSON. That is useful for
  tests, but less useful as a human-readable fallback artifact.
- Full replay remains out of scope. The right move is to enrich bounded capture
  with safe derived evidence, not invent a replay file format.

Changes:
- `DebugCaptureSnapshot` now accepts an optional explicit
  `AccessibilitySnapshot`.
- When a semantic tree is captured, `DebugCaptureSnapshot.toJson()` derives
  accessibility/fallback output from that tree automatically.
- Capture JSON now includes an `accessibility` section with node count,
  redacted plain-text narration, and the accessibility root tree.
- Added debug-capture tests for semantic-derived accessibility redaction and
  explicit accessibility snapshots without a semantic tree.
- Extended proof-app capture-to-test coverage so the proof app verifies
  accessibility narration for captured app/table/status facts.
- Updated M2.8, M2.11, the semantic app graph workstream, the replay/devtools
  workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep accessibility capture derived from semantics. Capture artifacts should
  inherit semantic redaction and should not parse rendered terminal cells.
- Include plain text in the capture artifact now. It gives future bug reports
  and test authoring a compact human-readable fallback view while keeping the
  structured tree available.
- Allow explicit accessibility snapshots for non-widget fallback flows such as
  prompt sessions that may not have a mounted element tree.

Findings:
- The existing capture serializer already had the right redaction posture for
  semantic nodes; deriving accessibility output from the same tree kept the
  implementation small.
- Proof-app capture now proves three linked layers from one scenario:
  semantic graph, accessibility/fallback narration, and output summaries.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - M2.5 Production Widget Theme Coverage

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.5 already established `FleuryWidgetTheme` for controls, progress, and
  MarkdownView. The remaining scoped theme pressure was data/log/document state
  styling, where the widget suite still had duplicated hardcoded styles.
- This should improve app-level polish and API ergonomics without expanding
  core `ThemeData` or turning Fleury into a full terminal design system.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryWidgetTheme` defaults for data selection, data separators,
  data empty states, log severity rows, diff line kinds, and JSON parse errors.
- Wired `DataTable`, `TreeTable`, `LogRegion`, `DiffView`, and `JsonView` to
  resolve those defaults while preserving explicit widget-style overrides.
- Extended the proof app theme so the Runs and Changes screens exercise the
  production-widget tokens under an app-shaped workflow.
- Added focused component-theme tests for data, log, diff, JSON, explicit
  override behavior, and proof-app style pressure.
- Updated M2.5, the data/widget workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep these tokens in the widget package extension. Core `ThemeData` remains
  lean, while production widgets can still share repeated state styling.
- Do not add broad code syntax or document typography tokens yet. Diff/log/data
  state styles have clear duplication today; richer code/document styling can
  wait for stronger proof-app pressure.
- Make `separatorStyle` nullable on DataTable and TreeTable so theme defaults
  can apply only when callers do not provide an explicit separator style.

Findings:
- The existing widget APIs already had nullable selected styles, so data
  selection theming was low-risk. Separators needed a small API adjustment
  because non-null defaults prevented the theme extension from participating.
- Log and diff style hardcoding was the clearest duplication. Moving those
  through `FleuryWidgetTheme` makes a real app feel more coherent without
  touching semantics, export, copy, or virtualization paths.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - SB.7 Resize Storm Benchmark

Status: Complete

Context:
- The benchmark lab's next implemented scenario after DataTable, LogRegion,
  and TreeTable was `SB.7 Resize Storm`.
- Resize correctness is a launch-facing terminal robustness claim. It should
  be measured with a mounted app-shaped surface instead of inferred from
  isolated render microbenchmarks.

Changes:
- Added `SB.7 Resize Storm` to the `fleury_widgets` scenario benchmark runner.
- Added `--resize-events=N` with a default of 500 events so full resize storms
  and short smoke runs use the same scenario code.
- The scenario keeps a DataTable, LogRegion, and TextInput mounted while
  alternating eight terminal sizes from 32x8 to 200x60.
- Each resize validates safe visible output plus semantic table, log, and
  text-field nodes.
- Saved the first SB.7 Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-resize-storm-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the benchmark README, scenario lab, RRE workstream, milestones, and
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep SB.7 in `fleury_widgets`, not core `fleury`, because the realistic
  resize workload depends on DataTable and LogRegion.
- Validate semantics on every resize. The point of the scenario is not only
  frame timing; it also protects the semantic/testing contract during terminal
  dimension churn.
- Make resize-event count configurable but keep the default at the planned 500
  events.

Findings:
- The saved baseline ran 5 measured iterations with 500 resize events each
  over a 100k-row DataTable plus 5k-entry LogRegion.
- Baseline result: resize-frame p95 488 us, semantic-query p95 593 us,
  max resize frame 8498 us, max semantic query 10399 us, and zero unsafe
  visible frames across 2500 measured resize frames.
- The benchmark now provides a concrete guardrail before deeper layout or
  dirty-region optimization work.

Validation:
- `dart format benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` listed `SB.7 Resize Storm`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.7 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --resize-events=12 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.7 --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --rows=100000 --resize-events=500 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-resize-storm-2026-06-01.json --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the baseline.

## 2026-06-01 - SB.10 Proof-App Journey Benchmark

Status: Complete

Context:
- The benchmark lab's next scenario after SB.7 is `SB.10 Proof-App Journey`.
- The proof app is the current-cycle integration harness before Dune or
  `dune_cli`; it needs benchmark evidence, not only unit-style workflow tests.

Changes:
- Added a proof-app-local scenario benchmark runner at
  `packages/fleury_example_console/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`.
- Added `SB.10 Proof-App Journey`, covering app startup, command palette
  navigation, diagnostics, fake task start, DataTable filter/copy,
  transcript composer/log stream, native Dart-version process success,
  debug capture, semantics, and accessibility output.
- Added proof-app benchmark docs and saved the first baseline at
  `packages/fleury_example_console/benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-journey-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the scenario lab, RRE workstream, RDT workstream, milestones,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep SB.10 in `fleury_example_console`. This scenario measures the integrated
  app package and should not turn the core or widget benchmark runners into
  app-package aggregators.
- Include native process run-to-success timing, but keep it separate from
  ordinary interaction timings because OS process spawn variance is expected.
- Treat the proof-app Phase 2 exit criterion as satisfied once SB.10 exists
  alongside the proof app tests and capture-to-test workflow.

Findings:
- The 10-iteration baseline passed with full-journey p95 101284 us,
  command-palette p95 13979 us, runs-filter p95 6906 us, runs-copy p95
  3260 us, transcript p95 6952 us, process run-to-success p95 53184 us,
  diagnostics p95 3761 us, debug-capture p95 8400 us, and semantic-query p95
  754 us.
- The final semantic snapshot had 82 semantic nodes and 82 accessibility
  nodes. The debug capture artifact was about 31 KB.
- Unsafe visible frames stayed at zero through the integrated workflow.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - SB.2 Text Editing Composer Stress Benchmark

Status: Complete

Context:
- The benchmark lab still lacked scenario evidence for Fleury's strong
  text/input launch claim.
- The right scope was core `fleury`, not `fleury_widgets`, because the scenario
  measures `TextInput`, `TextArea`, the shared editing controller/model,
  paste scheduling, history, completion-state acceptance, redaction, and
  semantics.

Changes:
- Added `SB.2 Text Editing Composer Stress` to
  `packages/fleury/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`.
- Added `--text-chars=N` with a 10000-character default fixture.
- The scenario mounts a `FleuryApp` with a command composer, longform editor,
  and secret field, then measures cursor movement, selection replacement,
  undo/redo, chunked paste completion, history navigation, completion
  acceptance, secret rendering/redaction, and semantic queries.
- Saved the first SB.2 Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-text-editing-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the benchmark README, scenario lab, text-editing workstream,
  reactive-render workstream, milestones, implementation README, and decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Keep rendered completion popup benchmarking out of SB.2. The core scenario
  should prove the shared editing state and `TextInput` acceptance path;
  `CompletionTextInput` rendering belongs to a later `fleury_widgets` scenario
  if it becomes launch-critical.
- Keep candidate thresholds informational until multiple machines and terminal
  modes have enough variance data.

Findings:
- The 10-iteration baseline passed with cursor-move p95 798 us,
  insertion/deletion p95 641 us, selection p95 2191 us, chunked paste
  completion p95 18573 us, undo/redo p95 1339 us, history navigation p95
  721 us, completion acceptance p95 698 us, semantic-query p95 508 us,
  10 paste frames, and redacted secret semantics.
- The scenario gives the framework a concrete, repeatable proof point for
  composer/editor responsiveness before any public "best-in-class input"
  claims.

## 2026-06-01 - Semantic Action Invocation Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- RDT.1 had semantic query coverage, but tests and future adapters still had no
  semantic-first way to activate the current app graph. The missing behavior was
  visible once the app kernel gave commands, screens, status items, and controls
  stable semantic identity.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticActionCallback`, `SemanticActionContributor`, and
  `SemanticActionInvocationResult`/status types to the semantic core and test
  exports.
- Added `FleuryTester.invokeSemanticAction`, resolving the current semantic
  node by id or semantic filters, rejecting disabled/unsupported actions, and
  dispatching through contributor-owned semantic subtrees.
- Wired semantic actions for app-authored `Semantics`, app/global commands,
  registered screens, status items, `TextInput`, `TextArea`, buttons,
  checkboxes, toggles, and radios.
- Taught the app semantic contributor to publish registered screen nodes missing
  from the mounted child subtree, so default active-screen rendering still lets
  tests navigate to inactive registered screens by semantics.
- Added focused tests for semantic action invocation across app-authored nodes,
  app commands, screen navigation, status actions, text fields, text areas,
  buttons, disabled buttons, checkboxes, toggles, and radios.
- Updated the implementation README, RDT workstream, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Dispatch semantic actions through semantic contributors instead of widget
  internals. The semantic graph remains the durable testing and future-adapter
  surface, while generated nodes such as command entries and screen entries are
  executed by the element that owns their runtime identity.
- Use children-first dispatch so nested command scopes and widget-owned semantic
  nodes get the first chance to handle actions before app-level contributors.
- Publish only missing screen nodes from the app contributor. This keeps inactive
  screens discoverable without duplicating screen semantics when preserved
  screens are already mounted.

Findings:
- Disabled explicit nodes correctly return `disabled`; filtered lookup still
  requires nodes to advertise the requested action, so disabled controls are
  intentionally not found unless the caller passes the disabled node snapshot.
- Inactive screens are not mounted by default when `ActiveScreenView` does not
  preserve state, so app-level screen summaries are necessary for semantic
  navigation to be robust.
- The invocation model is enough for the current app/text/control surfaces, but
  richer production widgets should add action handlers only when their concrete
  user actions are clear.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/button_test.dart test/controls_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 860 local links resolve.

Next:
- Continue Phase 2 production-widget work with semantic action handlers added
  opportunistically where widget behavior is stable and valuable to test by
  role/label/action.

## 2026-06-01 - Production Widget Semantic Actions Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- After the first RDT.1 invocation slice, several production widgets advertised
  semantic actions but still relied on keyboard-only tests. The highest-value
  next step was to wire handlers for widgets whose actions are already stable:
  `DataTable`, `LogRegion`, and `SearchPanel`.

Changes:
- Added `DataTable` semantic action handling through its render-island semantic
  contributor. Tests can now focus, select, activate, and copy the table or
  mounted visible row/cell nodes by semantic action.
- Added semantic copy handling to `LogRegion` at both region and selected-row
  levels.
- Added semantic focus, submit/copy, and selected-row activate/copy handling to
  `SearchPanel`.
- Made `SearchPanel` rebuild its aggregate focus semantics from the ambient
  `FocusManager` so the region-level `focused` flag updates after semantic
  focus.
- Added focused tests for semantic action invocation on `DataTable`,
  `LogRegion`, and `SearchPanel`.
- Updated the implementation README, RDT workstream, DVW workstream, and
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Add production-widget semantic action handlers only where behavior is already
  stable and user-visible. For this slice that means focus/select/activate/copy
  on `DataTable`, copy on `LogRegion`, and focus/submit/activate/copy on
  `SearchPanel`.
- Keep lazy render-island row actions tied to mounted visible rows. Data and
  list widgets should not pretend off-screen rows have mounted action targets
  until a concrete virtualized action protocol is designed.

Findings:
- `DataTable` needed row/cell `activate` actions in addition to table-level
  activation so semantic row activation matches keyboard Enter behavior.
- `SearchPanel` row semantics are lazy-list semantics: after a focus-triggered
  rebuild, tests should render before invoking row-level semantic actions.
- Region-level actions remain valuable even when rows are lazy; they provide
  stable aggregate focus/submit/copy behavior without requiring all results to
  be mounted.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/search_panel_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches

## 2026-06-01 - Hierarchy Widget Semantic Actions Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- The prior production-widget action slice covered table/log/search surfaces.
  The next stable Phase 2 surfaces were hierarchy and filesystem widgets:
  `Tree`, `TreeTable`, and `FileBrowser`.

Changes:
- Added owned focus handling plus semantic focus/open/activate callbacks to
  `Tree`.
- Added semantic open, activate, copy, focus, and aggregate tree action handling
  to `TreeTable`.
- Added semantic focus/open/copy handling to `FileBrowser`, including row-level
  open/copy over mounted visible rows.
- Added focused tests for semantic Tree focus/open/activate, TreeTable
  open/activate/copy, and FileBrowser directory open, file activation, and path
  copy.
- Updated the implementation README, milestones, RDT workstream, DVW workstream,
  and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep hierarchy row actions scoped to mounted visible rows. Aggregate nodes
  remain the stable entry point for focus/open/copy, while row-level actions
  target only rows currently present in the semantic graph.
- `SemanticAction.open` expands/navigates rather than toggles closed. Keyboard
  Enter can still toggle branch rows where that is the established local UX.

Findings:
- `Tree` needed an owned focus node so semantic focus can be meaningful even
  when callers do not provide one.
- `TreeTable` and `FileBrowser` could reuse their existing controller and copy
  paths directly; semantic actions did not need a new model.
- Lazy hierarchy tests should continue rendering before row-level semantic
  invocation, matching the visible-row semantics already documented for
  render-island widgets.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_test.dart test/tree_table_test.dart test/file_browser_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

Next:
- Continue semantic action coverage for document/dialog/form/process widgets
  only where the action behavior is already stable, or pivot to real-terminal
  compatibility evidence if the next Phase 2 pressure point calls for it.

## 2026-06-01 - Document View Semantic Actions Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- JSON, diff, code, and markdown views are part of the Phase 2 production
  toolkit and proof app. They advertised copy/open/focus actions, but semantic
  invocation still needed to route through their existing controller and safe
  copy/export behavior.

Changes:
- Added semantic branch open and row/aggregate copy handling to `JsonView`.
- Added semantic aggregate and selected-row copy handling to `DiffView`.
- Added semantic focus and selected-line/aggregate copy handling to `CodeView`.
- Added semantic aggregate and selected-block copy handling to `MarkdownView`.
- Added focused tests for JSON semantic open/copy, diff semantic hunk copy,
  CodeView semantic focus/copy, and markdown semantic block copy.
- Updated the implementation README, milestones, RDT workstream, DVW workstream,
  and decision log.

Decisions:
- Route document-view semantic actions through the same sanitized copy/export
  paths as keyboard workflows. This keeps semantic testing, debug capture, and
  future adapters aligned with existing redaction and terminal-control safety.
- Keep row-level copy selected-row oriented for lazy document rows. Aggregate
  copy remains available for the current selection even when callers do not
  target a specific row.

Findings:
- Document-view controllers already had the right selection/copy boundary, so
  semantic actions did not require new public API.
- `JsonView` is the only document view in this slice with a stable non-copy row
  action; `open` expands JSON object/array branches without toggling them
  closed.
- The proof-app document screens now have a clearer path to semantic-first
  tests and future adapter operation without depending on rendered cell text.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/json_view_test.dart test/diff_view_test.dart test/code_view_test.dart test/markdown_view_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

Next:
- Remaining semantic-action candidates are dialog/form/process/palette edge
  cases and any proof-app workflows where adapter-style tests would materially
  improve confidence. Real-terminal compatibility evidence remains the other
  major Phase 2 gap.

## 2026-06-01 - Workflow And Modal Semantic Actions Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- RDT.1 had reached production data, hierarchy, and document widgets. The
  remaining stable action surfaces were workflow and modal controls:
  `FormPanel`, `ProcessPanel`, `Dialog`, and `CommandPalette`.

Changes:
- Added semantic submit/cancel handling to `FormPanel`, reusing
  `FormController.submit`, `onSubmit`, and `onCancel`.
- Added owned form-field focus nodes so `SemanticAction.focus` can move focus
  to a specific visual form field by role and label.
- Added semantic cancel handling to `ProcessPanel`, reusing
  `ProcessTaskController.cancel`.
- Added semantic dismiss handling to `Dialog`, reusing nearest navigator pop.
- Added command-palette semantic focus/submit/dismiss handling plus row-level
  command activation through the same selected-command and command callback
  paths used by keyboard invocation.
- Added focused tests for form submit/cancel/focus, process semantic cancel,
  dialog semantic dismiss, and command-palette submit/dismiss/row activate.
- Updated the implementation README, milestones, RDT workstream, EWP
  workstream, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Workflow/modal semantic actions should not create parallel state. They route
  through existing controller, callback, command, or navigation behavior so
  semantic-first tests exercise the same app paths as keyboard and button use.

Findings:
- `FormPanel` needed explicit field focus nodes because the aggregate field
  semantics wrap different controls (`TextInput`, `PasswordInput`, `Select`,
  `Checkbox`) and semantic focus needs a durable field target.
- `ProcessPanel` did not need a new action model; the controller already owns
  cancellation state, event recording, and listener notification.
- Palette row activation can be semantic-first without changing selection:
  targeting the row invokes that command directly, while aggregate submit still
  invokes the current selected row.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart test/process_panel_test.dart test/dialog_test.dart test/command_palette_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 884 local links resolve.

Next:
- After this coverage pass, pivot RDT.1 toward gaps discovered by proof-app
  workflows rather than continuing broad action wiring for its own sake.

## 2026-06-01 - Terminal Compatibility Findings Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- Phase 2 M2.10 had opt-in active probes, but the JSON evidence still exposed
  raw probe results without explaining whether passive/env-derived diagnosis
  agreed with active terminal behavior.
- The next practical step toward a real-terminal matrix was a comparison layer
  that can classify agreement and mismatches without changing runtime startup.

Changes:
- Added `TerminalCompatibilityStatus`, `TerminalCompatibilityFinding`, and
  `TerminalCompatibilityReport` to the terminal diagnosis model.
- `TerminalDiagnosis.withActiveProbes` now attaches compatibility findings for
  Kitty keyboard and Kitty graphics support.
- Compatibility findings classify active/passive evidence as confirmed,
  active-confirmed, passive-unverified, unsupported, or inconclusive.
- `fleury diagnose` human output now includes a compatibility findings section
  when active probe evidence exists, while JSON includes the same structured
  report under `compatibility`.
- Exported the compatibility model from `fleury_core.dart`.
- Added diagnostics tests for active-confirmed, passive-unverified, and
  skipped/inconclusive compatibility evidence.
- Updated README current actions, M2.10 milestone notes, TCS workstream, and
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Active probe evidence remains diagnostic evidence, not runtime capability
  override input. Real terminals, tmux, SSH, IDE consoles, and latency can
  change probe behavior; runtime summaries should only consume confirmed probe
  features after matrix evidence proves stable policy.

Findings:
- Kitty keyboard has no passive support claim today (`attempted`), so a
  successful active probe is intentionally reported as active-confirmed rather
  than as a passive/active agreement.
- Kitty graphics can be passive-detected through environment-derived image
  protocol. If an active probe does not confirm that support, the new report
  flags the finding as passive-unverified for matrix follow-up.
- Skipped non-TTY probes now still produce compatibility findings, but all are
  inconclusive with the skipped reason preserved.

Validation:
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/terminal/terminal_probe_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart cli diagnose --json --probe --probe-timeout=1`
  passed and emitted an active-probe skipped report plus two inconclusive
  compatibility findings in this non-interactive stdout environment.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 886 local links resolve.

Next:
- Next M2.10 evidence should be either a real-terminal matrix capture workflow
  using `fleury diagnose --probe --json` or a practical PTY fixture if one can
  be kept portable and non-flaky.

## 2026-06-01 - Terminal Matrix Capture Workflow Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- The compatibility report made probe output matrix-ready, but collecting
  entries still required ad hoc shell commands and manual wrapping.
- M2.10 needs repeatable evidence capture before Fleury can claim real-terminal
  compatibility or promote active probe findings into runtime capability
  summaries.

Changes:
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix`.
- The command runs `packages/fleury/bin/fleury.dart diagnose --json`, enables
  `--probe` by default, validates the JSON output, wraps it as a
  `fleuryTerminalMatrixEntry`, and writes a labeled JSON artifact.
- Added options for `--label`, `--output`, `--probe-timeout`, and `--no-probe`.
- Matrix entries include raw diagnosis plus a compact summary of terminal
  identity, capabilities, active probe statuses, and compatibility findings.
- Added [terminal-compatibility-matrix.md](terminal-compatibility-matrix.md)
  and a default `docs/implementation/terminal-matrix/` artifact directory with
  review instructions.
- Linked the matrix tracker from the implementation README and updated M2.10,
  TCS, root README, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep matrix capture in the repo-local launcher for now. The public
  `fleury diagnose` command owns evidence generation; labeling, artifact
  paths, and review workflow are internal until real-terminal captures prove
  the process is stable enough to expose.

Findings:
- The non-interactive Codex environment is still useful as a smoke test:
  stdout is not a terminal, so active probes skip and the matrix summary shows
  two inconclusive compatibility findings.
- The default output path is intentionally under implementation docs so
  reviewed captures can become evidence, but generated JSON should be reviewed
  before committing.

  passed and produced a valid `fleuryTerminalMatrixEntry` with two

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
