## 2026-06-01 - TreeTable Search Index Hardening Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.3's `TreeTable` surface was functionally complete, but the saved SB.11
  100k-leaf baseline showed `TreeTableSearchIndex` construction at 1851310 us
  p95. Query, copy, and semantic timings were already credible, so index
  construction was the hierarchy-search bottleneck most worth addressing
  before larger worker/debounce policy.

Changes:
- Replaced per-entry column-text maps in `TreeTableSearchIndex` with indexed
  private lists keyed by one shared column-id map.
- Removed deep unmodifiable copies for private index entries and token
  postings; those collections are constructed locally and never exposed.
- Replaced regex tokenization with an ASCII scanner for the existing
  `A-Za-z0-9_:-` token contract.
- Added a regression test proving exact-token search still supports durable
  symbol tokens such as `zz-target_42` and `team:runtime` without substring
  matches.
- Saved a new SB.11 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-index-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, M2.3 milestone notes, DVW workstream,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Optimize the existing synchronous index path before adding worker-backed,
  debounce, or cache policy. The public API did not need to change for this
  slice; the gap was avoidable private allocation and token scanning cost.

Findings:
- A three-iteration local comparison before the scanner pass showed
  index-build p95 moving from roughly 1695639 us to 1404710 us after dropping
  per-entry/per-index private map copies.
- The scanner pass moved the same three-iteration shape to 746316 us p95.
- The saved five-iteration baseline landed at 1040888 us p95, still a
  substantial improvement over the earlier committed 1851310 us baseline while
  preserving the same 100100 indexed rows and exact-token lookup behavior.
- Worker-backed or debounced index construction may still be useful for
  interactive data replacement, but it should now be judged as UX policy rather
  than compensation for an obviously wasteful hot path.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/tree_table.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/tree_table.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/tree_table_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --iterations=3 --warmup=1 --rows=100000 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed after the scanner pass with index-build
  p95 746316 us and filter-query p95 5175 us.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --iterations=5 --warmup=2 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-index-2026-06-01.json --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the follow-up baseline with
  index-build p95 1040888 us, filter-query p95 8564 us, selected-row copy p95
  13886 us, and semantic-query p95 4767 us.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 944 local links resolve.

Next:
- For M2.3, only add worker/debounce/caching policy if proof-app or future
  product workflows show visible jank during large hierarchy replacement.
- Otherwise, continue the next Phase 2 gap: real-terminal matrix evidence,
  targeted debug-capture/replay hook pressure, or Windows driver scoping.

## 2026-06-01 - Debug Capture Artifact Query Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.11 was still open even though debug capture could serialize redacted
  semantics, accessibility output, inputs, frames, and output summaries.
- The remaining MVP gap was not full replay. It was the lack of a reusable
  way to turn one captured artifact into deterministic regression assertions;
  the proof-app test was hand-walking nested capture JSON.

Changes:
- Added `DebugCaptureArtifact` and `DebugCaptureSemanticNode` in the debug
  capture module.
- `DebugCaptureArtifact.fromSnapshot` and `.fromJson` wrap the serialized
  capture shape and expose query helpers for inputs, frames, output summaries,
  semantic nodes, focused node id, semantic node count, and accessibility
  plain text.
- Semantic-node queries can filter by role, label, value, focus, selection,
  action, and required state entries.
- Exported the artifact/query types from `package:fleury/fleury_test.dart`.
- Added a focused debug-capture test proving artifact queries over inputs,
  frame dirty sources, output summaries, accessibility narration, and semantic
  state.
- Reworked the proof-app capture-to-test regression to use
  `DebugCaptureArtifact` instead of local JSON traversal helpers.
- Marked M2.11 complete for the MVP and updated the RDT workstream,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat the queryable captured snapshot as the MVP replay-hook shape. Full
  event replay and shareable replay artifacts remain deferred, but developers
  now have a structured way to convert a captured bug artifact into a test.

Findings:
- The existing capture JSON already had enough facts for useful regressions:
  command/input sequence, frame reasons and dirty sources, output summaries,
  semantic state, and redacted accessibility text.
- Pulling those facts through `DebugCaptureArtifact` removes a proof-app
  one-off helper and gives future examples/Dune work a common test surface.

## 2026-06-01 - Windows Native Driver V0 Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.9 was the largest untouched platform-correctness item in Phase 2.
- `runTui` still fell back to `PosixTerminalDriver` everywhere, while the
  POSIX driver explicitly said Windows needed a separate implementation.
- Microsoft documents Windows console virtual terminal support through
  `SetConsoleMode`: output needs virtual-terminal processing, and input can
  be converted into virtual-terminal sequences through the virtual-terminal
  input flag. POSIX signal handling and Windows console-mode handling should
  not live in the same driver.

Changes:
- Added shared native terminal mode sequences in
  `terminal_sequences.dart` and routed `PosixTerminalDriver` through them.
- Added `WindowsTerminalDriver` with the same `TerminalDriver` and
  `TerminalHandoffDriver` contracts as POSIX.
- The Windows driver uses Dart stdin line/echo mode for raw input, Windows
  `SetConsoleMode` via FFI for virtual terminal input/output when available,
  polling for resize events instead of POSIX `SIGWINCH`, and the same
  terminal handoff shape as POSIX.
- Added `createNativeTerminalDriver`, which chooses `WindowsTerminalDriver`
  on Windows and `PosixTerminalDriver` elsewhere.
- Updated `runTui` default-driver fallback to use the native-driver selector.
- Exported `WindowsTerminalDriver` and `createNativeTerminalDriver` from the
  native `package:fleury/fleury.dart` umbrella.
- Added `ffi` as the native package dependency needed for Windows console
  mode calls.
- Added focused tests for driver selection and safe non-Windows no-op behavior
  in the native Windows console-mode controller.
- Updated M2.9 milestone notes, the terminal workstream, implementation README,
  and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep Windows as a separate native driver rather than adding platform branches
  to `PosixTerminalDriver`. The APIs and lifecycle differ enough that sharing
  the interface and escape-sequence helpers is the right boundary.
- Treat this as M2.9 V0, not complete real-Windows certification. macOS can
  prove type selection, analyzer coverage, and non-Windows FFI no-op behavior;
  real Windows Terminal/conhost/IDE behavior still needs matrix evidence.

Findings:
- The current framework boundary was already good for this: `TerminalDriver`
  and `TerminalHandoffDriver` were sufficient for a Windows implementation
  without touching render, input-dispatch, widget, or app-shell code.
- Resize is the biggest lifecycle difference in this slice. POSIX uses
  `SIGWINCH`; Windows V0 polls terminal size while active and emits resize
  events only on change.
- Windows virtual-terminal mode is a native console concern, so it belongs in
  the driver rather than in capability detection or renderer code.

Validation:
- `dart pub get` in `packages/fleury` passed and added `ffi`.
- `dart format lib/src/terminal/terminal_sequences.dart lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/native_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart lib/fleury.dart test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/terminal_sequences.dart lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/native_driver.dart lib/src/terminal/posix_driver.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart lib/fleury.dart test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart pub get` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart pub get` in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 953 local links resolve.

Next:
- Add Windows Terminal/conhost/PowerShell/IDE entries through the terminal
  matrix when a real Windows environment is available.
- Keep M2.9 open until real Windows validation confirms raw input,
  virtual-terminal output, resize polling, restore, and handoff behavior.

## 2026-06-01 - Windows Console Mode Planning Hardening Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- The Windows driver V0 wired `SetConsoleMode` through FFI, but the mode bit
  decisions still lived inside the native controller. macOS can analyze that
  code, but it could not prove the exact input/output flag plan without a
  real Windows host.
- M2.9 should keep moving locally by making the cross-platform-testable parts
  of Windows behavior explicit.

Changes:
- Added `WindowsConsoleModePlan` and `planWindowsConsoleModes`.
- The native Windows console-mode controller now asks the pure planner for
  desired input/output modes and only performs FFI `SetConsoleMode` calls when
  the plan reports a change.
- Added tests proving interactive mode enables virtual-terminal input and
  output flags, raw-input opt-out skips virtual-terminal input, already-enabled
  flags are not marked changed, and missing input/output handles are handled
  independently.
- Updated M2.9 milestone notes, the TCS workstream, implementation README, and
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep console-mode bit planning pure and tested outside the FFI adapter. This
  does not replace real Windows matrix validation, but it prevents the
  platform-specific driver from hiding basic mode-planning mistakes until a
  Windows run.

Findings:
- The Windows driver has a clean seam: desired console modes can be tested
  without opening `kernel32.dll`, while the native controller remains
  responsible only for `GetConsoleMode`/`SetConsoleMode` calls and restoration.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.

  passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 953 local links resolve.

Next:
- Keep M2.9 open until Windows Terminal/conhost/PowerShell/IDE matrix entries
  validate raw input, virtual-terminal output, resize polling, restore, and
  handoff behavior.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.4 External Editor Handoff Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.4 already had native subprocess execution, process command binding,
  `ProcessPanel`, `TerminalOutputRegion`, and terminal handoff. The remaining
  local UX gap was external-editor handoff: a common workflow in developer
  tools where the TUI must restore the user's terminal, let an editor own
  stdio, then resume cleanly.
- This belongs on the native `package:fleury/fleury.dart` surface because it
  uses temp files, `dart:io` processes, and inherited stdio.

Changes:
- Added `editTextInExternalEditor` and related result/command/temp-file types
  in `external_editor.dart`.
- The helper resolves explicit commands, `$VISUAL`, `$EDITOR`, and platform
  fallback editors; writes the initial text to a temp buffer; runs the editor
  with inherited stdio; and returns edited text, changed state, exit code,
  file path, editor command metadata, process command metadata, and command
  source.
- Wired external-editor execution through `withTerminalHandoff` when a
  `TerminalDriver` is provided, so POSIX, Windows, fake, and future native
  drivers all use the same suspend/resume contract.
- Added test hooks for process running and temp-file creation so the behavior
  is unit-testable without launching a real editor.
- Exported the helper from the native `fleury.dart` umbrella, not from
  `fleury_core.dart`.
- Updated M2.4 milestone notes, the effects/process workstream, the
  implementation README, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep this function-sized and text-buffer-oriented. External-editor UX is
  important, but broad shell/process orchestration should stay in app commands,
  task controllers, or future concrete workflow widgets.
- Treat environment editor values as shell commands so common settings like
  `EDITOR="code --wait"` work. Structured explicit commands remain available
  for apps that already know the executable/arguments.
- Default non-zero editor exits to an `ExternalEditorException` with the full
  result attached, while allowing callers to opt into result-only handling.

Findings:
- The existing `TerminalHandoffDriver` seam was sufficient. No render,
  app-kernel, process-panel, or platform-driver changes were needed.
- The native/core boundary is holding: editor support can be useful to Dart
  CLI apps without making browser/remote hosts depend on `dart:io`.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/external_editor.dart packages/fleury/lib/fleury.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/external_editor_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/external_editor.dart lib/fleury.dart test/effects/external_editor_test.dart`

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Accessibility State Summary Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- `AccessibilitySnapshot` already derived a safe text-first fallback view from
  semantic nodes, including role, label, safe value, validation, actions,
  collection ranges, progress, capability fallback, clipboard policy, and
  redaction.
- The Phase 2 proof app and production widgets now expose richer semantic
  state for app shell, commands, tasks, tables, trees, logs, JSON, diffs, code,
  and Markdown. Without allow-listed summaries, accessibility/fallback output
  stayed too generic for real developer-tool bug reports and prompt fallback.

Changes:
- Expanded `buildAccessibilitySnapshot` state narration with allow-listed
  app-kernel, command, task, output-safety, view, row/cell, JSON, diff, code,
  Markdown, and tree expansion summaries.
- Preserved redaction boundaries by omitting selected keys and row keys from
  nodes whose semantic state marks values as redacted/obscured/clipboard
  redacted.
- Added focused accessibility tests for app/command/task/table summaries,
  developer-document summaries, row/cell summaries, and redacted selected-key
  omission.
- Updated M2.8 milestone notes, the semantic-app-graph workstream, the
  replay/devtools workstream, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep the accessibility state projection allow-listed. This gives fallback
  output useful production-widget facts without dumping arbitrary semantic
  state maps that could leak secrets or freeze unstable internals.

Findings:
- The semantic graph already carries the right production app facts; the gap
  was projection quality. No widget APIs needed to change.
- Accessibility/fallback, debug capture, and targeted regression artifacts all
  benefit from the same semantic-derived state summaries.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - RRE.3 DataTable Mouse Hit Selection Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- RRE.3 still had one direct evidence gap: `DataTable` proved the render-island
  pattern for semantics, focus, keyboard selection, copy/export, theme-derived
  styling, and benchmarks, but hit testing was not covered.
- Fleury already has a paint-time `PointerRouter` and `GestureDetector`; the
  right table solution should reuse that routing and the render-island layout
  metrics rather than mounting per-cell widgets.

Changes:
- Added `DataTableViewportMetrics`, emitted by `RenderDataTable` after layout.
  It records the painted visible row range, body top, table width, column
  starts, and column widths.
- Wrapped `DataTable`'s render-island child in `GestureDetector` and maps
  left-click taps through focus rect plus viewport metrics.
- Row selection mode now lets mouse clicks select visible rows. Cell selection
  mode maps clicks to rendered columns, and Shift-click extends the rectangular
  controller range through the same `DataTableController` path as keyboard
  Shift+Arrow.
- Exported `DataTableViewportMetrics` alongside `RenderDataTable` so the
  public render-object constructor does not expose an unavailable type.
- Added focused tests for row click selection, click-to-focus semantics, and
  Shift-click cell range extension.
- Updated RRE.3, Data Virtualization, M1.9 DataTable notes, implementation
  README, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Use render-island viewport metrics as the hit-test bridge. This keeps
  hit selection aligned with exactly what was painted and avoids per-cell
  widget composition or a second hit-test tree.
- Keep mouse clicks as selection, not activation. Keyboard Enter and semantic
  activate still own the `onSelect` activation path.

Findings:
- The existing pointer router was enough; the missing piece was table-local
  geometry. `FocusNode.rect` provides the absolute table origin after render,
  and `RenderDataTable` provides the row/column layout.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/data_table.dart test/data_table_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart test/component_theme_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 906 local docs links resolve.

Next:
- Use the now-closed render-island proof as the baseline before adding any
  further high-volume widget render islands.

## 2026-06-01 - RRE.4 Repaint Boundary Debug Metrics Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- RRE.4 still had a direct evidence gap after M1.10: the inspector could show
  frame reasons, phase costs, dirty cells/bounds, dirty source labels,
  semantics, task state, and terminal/capability fallback rows, but repaint
  boundary behavior was not visible.
- The goal was a developer-facing cache-hit/cache-miss signal, not a full
  profiler or a production runtime cost.

Changes:
- Added `RepaintBoundaryFrameStats` and a debug-only
  `RepaintBoundaryDebugStats` collector around repaint-boundary paint/copy
  behavior.
- `FrameEvent` now carries repaint-boundary totals, repainted/cache-hit/empty
  counts, and copied-cell counts.
- `runTui` enables collection only while debug listeners are active and attaches
  the frame stats to emitted debug frames.
- The DebugPanel Live and Rebuilds tabs now show a `Boundaries` row.
- Debug capture frame JSON now serializes repaint-boundary stats when present.
- Added focused runtime, debug-panel, and capture tests for first-paint repaint,
  second-frame cache hits, panel output, and serialized capture output.
- Updated RRE.4, RDT.3, M1.10, the implementation README, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep repaint-boundary metrics debug-gated on `DebugEvents` listeners. This
  gives developers and capture artifacts useful rendering insight without
  changing the normal paint-path cost model.
- Track cache behavior as frame-level counters first. Per-boundary identities,
  flame charts, and retained profiler timelines should wait for concrete
  profiling scenarios.

Findings:
- The existing repaint-boundary cache path had the right seam: the render
  object knows whether it repainted, reused cached cells, had empty bounds, and
  how many cells it copied.
- `FrameEvent` remains the right carrier for inspector/capture frame facts; no
  new replay artifact shape was needed.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/rendering/render_repaint_boundary.dart lib/src/debug/debug_events.dart lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart lib/src/runtime/run_tui.dart lib/fleury_core.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.

  in `packages/fleury` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - SB.5 Streaming Markdown Baseline Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- The Phase 2 docs still had an important pressure gap: `MarkdownView` existed
  with safe links, copy, document semantics, and proof-app Docs screen coverage,
  but streaming markdown was still a pending scenario in the benchmark lab and
  peer scorecard.
- Streaming rich text is a launch-relevant workload for developer tools,
  agent transcripts, long-running command explanations, and help/documentation
  panes. It also stresses sanitization and semantic link/block state in a way
  static MarkdownView tests do not.

Changes:
- Added `SB.5 Streaming Markdown` to the `fleury_widgets` scenario benchmark
  runner.
- The scenario appends markdown chunks containing headings, lists, table-like
  rows, code fences, links, inline code, partial paragraph chunks, long
  paragraphs, unsafe OSC payloads, and unsafe link schemes.
- It measures per-chunk parse cost, per-chunk frame cost, combined chunk
  update cost, final render, selected-block copy, semantic query, ANSI bytes,
  document shape, unsafe-link count, sanitized/truncated block counts,
  copied-byte count, unsafe-frame count, and RSS delta.
- Saved the first Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-streaming-markdown-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, RRE tracker, data/widget
  workstream, peer scorecard, milestones, implementation README, and decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Treat `SB.5` as the baseline gate before designing an incremental Markdown
  parser. The first 1000-chunk saved run keeps full-document parse-on-append
  under the candidate update budget, so parser complexity should wait for
  larger fixtures, richer wrapping pressure, or peer-equivalent runs.
- Keep SB.5 in `fleury_widgets`, not core, because it depends on the
  high-level `MarkdownView` widget package.

Findings:
- Full-document parse-on-append is currently acceptable for the launch fixture:
  1000 chunks per iteration, chunk-update p95 13428 us, chunk-parse p95
  12588 us, chunk-frame p95 926 us, semantic-query p95 2155 us, and zero unsafe
  visible frames across the saved 5-iteration run.
- The render portion is much smaller than parse cost on this workload. If this
  regresses, the next optimization should likely target parser/incremental
  document state before render-object work.

Validation:
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` showed `SB.5 Streaming Markdown`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.5 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed with chunk-update p95 2498 us.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.5 --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-streaming-markdown-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the baseline.
- `dart test test/markdown_view_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 929 local docs links resolve.

Next:
- Leave incremental Markdown parsing deferred for now. Good next Phase 2
  candidates are dashboard update pressure (`SB.6`), overlay/command-palette
  churn (`SB.8`), subprocess handoff/output pressure (`SB.9`), or real-terminal
  matrix evidence, depending on which launch claim needs the most support.

## 2026-06-01 - SB.6 Dashboard Update Pressure Baseline Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- After `SB.5`, the benchmark lab still had no direct pressure for dashboards
  with many small retained surfaces updating together.
- This matters for CI dashboards, deploy tools, ops consoles, and agent status
  views where progress bars, gauges, counters, sparklines, charts, and status
  rows update frequently without being a table/log workload.

Changes:
- Added `SB.6 Dashboard Update Pressure` to the `fleury_widgets` scenario
  benchmark runner.
- The scenario keeps a compact dashboard mounted with progress bars, gauges,
  sparklines, a bar chart, counters, and status rows, then drives repeated
  tester updates.
- It measures mount, first render, per-update pump cost, per-update frame
  cost, combined update cost, semantic query, ANSI bytes, surface counts,
  progress semantic count, unsafe-frame count, and RSS delta.
- Saved the first Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-dashboard-update-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, RRE tracker, peer
  scorecard, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Use tester-driven dashboard ticks first, not a wall-clock animation scheduler.
  This isolates retained update/render cost across many small surfaces. Real
  animation coalescing and scheduler pressure should be a later slice if a
  launch claim needs it.
- Keep `SB.6` in `fleury_widgets` because the fixture depends on high-level
  dashboard widgets such as `ProgressBar`, `Gauge`, `Sparkline`, and
  `BarChart`.

Findings:
- The first dashboard fixture was well below the candidate 16 ms update budget
  over 20 measured iterations, 400 ticks per iteration, and 23 dashboard
  surfaces. This initial saved baseline was later refreshed by the RRE
  Dashboard Paint-Only Widget Audit entry after adding layout stats.
- Current dashboard pressure does not justify render optimization work. The
  next useful dashboard slice would need to add real scheduler/coalescing
  pressure, animation timers, or peer-equivalent fixtures.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - SB.8 Overlay And Command Palette Churn Baseline Slice

Status: Complete

Context:
- After `SB.6`, Fleury still needed direct pressure on overlays, command
  palettes, modal focus, app command discovery, semantic actions, route
  cleanup, and disabled visible-but-inert commands.
- This matters for launch because command palettes are a visible app-framework
  affordance. Developers will judge the feature by latency, focus correctness,
  stale-action safety, and app-kernel integration, not by internals.

Changes:
- Added `SB.8 Overlay And Command Palette Churn` to the `fleury_widgets`
  scenario benchmark runner.
- The scenario mounts a `FleuryApp` with one active screen command plus a
  scaled app command registry, opens `AppCommandPalette` through
  `Navigator.present`, filters fuzzy results, keyboard-selects the intended
  row when fuzzy matches are ambiguous, invokes through Enter, semantic submit,
  and semantic row activate, dismisses through Escape and semantic dismiss, and
  probes a disabled command.
- Added `selectionUs`, semantic mismatch counters, stale palette counters,
  route-depth mismatch counters, and unexpected invocation counters to make the
  correctness side of the benchmark visible.
- Fixed a semantic stale-action gap by adding child-pruning support to
  `Semantics` and using it from navigator route semantics: inactive/leaving
  route nodes remain visible, but their child control/action semantics are not
  exported.
- Added command-palette regression coverage for dismissed stale semantics,
  repeated palette cycles, repeated app-palette cycles, large exact-query
  cycles, and mixed keyboard/semantic cycles.
- Saved the first Phase 2 baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-overlay-command-palette-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, semantic app graph
  tracker, reactive render engine tracker, peer scorecard, milestones,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep SB.8 in `fleury_widgets` because it depends on the high-level
  `AppCommandPalette` widget while exercising core `FleuryApp`, navigator,
  semantics, and command registry behavior.
- Preserve inactive route nodes for route/debug state, but prune inactive route
  child semantics so preserved state does not leak stale modal controls or
  command actions.
- When app command semantics and palette row semantics share role/label, use
  the row `SemanticNode` for palette activation in tests and benchmarks rather
  than relying on role/label uniqueness.
- Treat the first 1000-command fuzzy-filter baseline as a performance gap, not
  a release claim. Correctness is proven; latency is not yet top-tier.

Findings:
- Correctness is strong in the first full baseline: 20 measured iterations,
  40 cycles per iteration, 1000 app commands, 24 command invocations and 16
  dismissals per journey, zero stale palette semantics, zero route-depth
  mismatches, zero selected-row mismatches, zero action failures, and zero
  unexpected invocations.
- The stale-route semantics issue was real: navigator preserved route
  subtrees for state, and the semantic collector previously walked inactive
  route children. The route-level pruning fix closes that without unmounting
  preserved state.
- App-level command semantics and palette-row command semantics intentionally
  duplicate role/label for the same command. Node-targeted semantic activation
  is the correct adapter/test path when a UI has multiple command surfaces.
- Performance is the gap: with 1000 commands, open p95 is 33667 us, filter p95
  is 98705 us, selection p95 is 15239 us, action p95 is 29610 us, settle p95
  is 93096 us, and full cycle p95 is 247544 us. Next optimization should focus
  on command search/indexing, palette rebuild scope, and route settle cost.

Validation:
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --list` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` showed `SB.8 Overlay And Command Palette Churn`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.8 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed with cycle p95 9747 us and zero stale
  palette semantics.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.8 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-overlay-command-palette-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the baseline.
- `dart test test/command_palette_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/navigator_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 941 local docs links resolve.

Next:
- Optimize app command palette search and route settle/open cost before using
  the command palette as a launch differentiator.
- The remaining benchmark-shaped launch gap is `SB.9 Subprocess Handoff And
  Untrusted Output`; real-terminal matrix evidence remains a separate
  compatibility track.

## 2026-06-01 - SB.8 Command Palette Latency Optimization

Status: Complete

Context:
- The first `SB.8` baseline proved overlay correctness but made the command
  palette unfit as a launch differentiator: 1000-command filter p95 was 98705
  us, settle p95 was 93096 us, and full-cycle p95 was 247544 us.
- Earlier implementation notes had intentionally kept command-palette rows
  eager for immediate semantic row discovery. The new app-shaped evidence
  showed that assumption no longer held for real app command registries.

Changes:
- Switched `CommandPalette` result rows from eager `ListView(children: ...)` to
  lazy `ListView.builder`.
- Added cached per-command search fields so query updates do not rebuild lower-
  cased joined search text for every command.
- Made stable command IDs searchable in `AppCommandPalette` and simple
  `CommandPalette` entries.
- Ranked command matches as exact, prefix, contains, then fuzzy subsequence
  while preserving source order inside each rank. This keeps exact command-id
  queries predictable even when fuzzy digit permutations also match.
- Updated `SB.8` to query stable command IDs instead of assuming every fuzzy
  target row remains present in the semantic graph.
- Added command-palette tests for bounded visible row semantics and stable
  command-id filtering.
- Saved the optimized baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-overlay-command-palette-optimized-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, RRE tracker, app-kernel
  tracker, data-virtualization notes, peer scorecard, milestones,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Reverse the earlier eager-row command-palette decision. Command palettes can
  be large enough that visible-row virtualization is a core app-framework
  requirement, not a data-widget-only concern.
- Keep aggregate command-palette semantics authoritative for collection row
  count, filter text, selected key, and visible range. Row-level semantics are
  now visible-row semantics, matching other lazy production widgets.
- Search stable IDs by default. Developers and tests need exact command
  targeting for large registries, and IDs are already the app-kernel contract
  for commands.

Findings:
- The optimized full baseline uses 20 measured iterations, 40 cycles per
  iteration, and a 1000-command registry. It keeps all correctness counters
  clean: zero stale palette semantics, zero route-depth mismatches, zero
  selected-row mismatches, zero action failures, and zero unexpected
  invocations.
- Latency moved from a clear gap to a strength on this fixture: open p95
  1615 us, filter p95 1121 us, selection p95 545 us, action p95 2316 us,
  settle p95 227 us, full-cycle p95 6429 us, and semantic-query p95 618 us.
- Row semantics are now bounded to the mounted visible rows. Tests or adapters
  that need row-level command semantics must operate after a render/layout pass
  and should target visible rows or aggregate palette actions.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/command_palette.dart test/command_palette_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/command_palette_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.8 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed with cycle p95 4187 us and zero
  correctness mismatches.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.8 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-overlay-command-palette-optimized-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the optimized baseline.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "command palette can drive app navigation"`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed after updating the proof app test
  to query the palette by stable command ID and render before row semantic
  assertions.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_example_console`
  passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 943 local docs links resolve.

Next:
- The remaining benchmark-shaped launch gap is still `SB.9 Subprocess Handoff
  And Untrusted Output`; real-terminal matrix evidence remains a separate
  compatibility track.

## 2026-06-01 - SB.9 Subprocess Handoff And Untrusted Output

Status: Complete

Context:
- After the optimized `SB.8` command-palette baseline, the remaining
  benchmark-shaped launch gap was subprocess handoff plus untrusted process
  output.
- Existing primitives already included `ProcessTaskController`,
  `ProcessPanel`, `ProcessCommandRunner`, `TerminalOutputRegion`,
  `TerminalHandoffDriver`, and `editTextInExternalEditor`, so this slice needed
  evidence more than a new shell/process abstraction.

Changes:
- Added `SB.9 Subprocess Handoff And Untrusted Output` to the
  `fleury_widgets` scenario runner.
- The fixture writes a temporary Dart subprocess program and runs real process
  paths for 1 MB target colored output, stderr output, non-zero exit,
  cancellation, and terminal handoff.
- Added an external-editor handoff leg using `editTextInExternalEditor` with a
  fake process runner and real temp-file text mutation.
- Added a deterministic `TerminalOutputRegion` streaming leg with unsafe
  stdout/stderr-like lines to measure frame timing while checking visible
  output safety.
- Recorded process run, failure run, cancellation latency, editor handoff,
  streaming-frame, panel-render, terminal-output-render, copy, semantic-query,
  captured-output, sanitizer, handoff, and unsafe-artifact metrics.
- Saved the baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-subprocess-output-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, effects/process
  workstream, terminal/security workstream, reactive render engine tracker,
  peer scorecard, milestones, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Measure subprocess launch readiness with a hybrid real-process plus
  output-region scenario instead of broadening Fleury into a shell framework.
- Keep process execution/cancellation/exit metadata in `ProcessTaskController`;
  keep app-facing display/copy/filter semantics in `ProcessPanel`,
  `TerminalOutputRegion`, and `LogRegion`.
- Treat unsafe-output success as an artifact contract: visible cells, copied
  text, and semantic nodes must stay free of raw escape payloads and secret
  payload text.

Findings:
- The saved baseline uses 10 measured iterations on Dart 3.12.1 over the
  default 100k-row scaled fixture. It targets 1,000,000 process bytes and
  stores 4288 sanitized output records.
- Timings are launch-shaped for this harness: process-run p95 647254 us,
  failure-run p95 629356 us, cancellation p95 11823 us, editor-handoff p95
  4194 us, stream-frame p95 6230 us, process-panel-render p95 10649 us, and
  semantic-query p95 1965 us.
- Correctness counters are clean: terminal handoff restored in all four paths,
  four handoff calls/suspends/resumes, zero unsafe visible frames, zero unsafe
  visible/copy/semantic artifact leaks, stderr captured, failure exit code 7
  captured, and cancellation output observed before kill.

Validation:

  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.9 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=100000 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.9 --warmup=2 --iterations=10 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-subprocess-output-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the baseline.
- `dart test test/process_panel_test.dart test/terminal_output_region_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/effects/external_editor_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.

- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 markdown files and 948 local links

Next:

  next benchmark work should come from cross-machine variance, peer-equivalent

## 2026-06-01 - SB.4 LogRegion Indexed Filtering

Status: Complete

Context:
- The original `SB.4 LogRegion Tailing And Scrollback` baseline was strong on
  append, scrollback, copy, and semantics, but retained-log filtering was still
  the slowest visible interaction: filter-query p95 was 68785 us on the
  100k-entry fixture.
- `LogRegion` already used lazy visible-row mounting, source/view index
  separation, sanitized search fields, and semantic copy. The remaining gap was
  large retained-log typeahead, not rendering or row semantics.

Changes:
- Added `LogRegionSearchIndex`, an explicit app-owned search index over
  sanitized log entry fields.
- Added optional `LogRegion.searchIndex` wiring so large retained-log views can
  use indexed token filtering while the default path remains a simple optimized
  linear scan.
- Kept `LogRegionSearchIndex.refresh()` explicit so apps can choose whether to
  rebuild eagerly after appends, debounce, incrementally maintain, or move index
  work off the UI path later.
- Added focused coverage for indexed filtering and append refresh behavior in
  `packages/fleury_widgets/test/log_region_test.dart`.
- Updated `SB.4` to build and pass a search index, refresh it after the append
  burst, record `searchIndexBuildUs`, and save the indexed baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-indexed-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the widgets benchmark README, scenario lab, data-virtualization
  workstream, implementation README, milestones, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep the index explicit rather than silently building per query inside
  `LogRegion`. The widget should not hide a several-hundred-millisecond index
  construction cost inside an interaction path.
- Optimize for predictable token search first. This gives strong behavior for
  IDs, sources, severities, file-like labels, and common log terms while keeping
  fuzzy or ranked search policy available for a later proof need.
- Treat worker/debounce/incremental construction as a follow-up product policy,
  not a blocker for the first indexed filtering slice.

Findings:
- The saved indexed baseline uses 20 measured iterations on Dart 3.12.1 over
  the 100k-entry fixture plus a 1000-entry append burst.
- Filter-query p95 improved from 68785 us to 35979 us and stays below the
  candidate 80 ms budget for the fixture.
- Append-burst p95 rose from 9591 us to 21859 us because the benchmark refreshes
  the index after appending; this remains below the candidate 33 ms append
  budget but makes refresh policy worth revisiting for long-running streams.
- Search-index build p95 is 319669 us. That cost is acceptable as explicit
  setup evidence, but not as hidden typeahead work. It should become debounced,
  incremental, or worker-backed before broad retained-log search claims.
- Copy-selected-entry p95 is 12068 us, scrollback-jump p95 is 4988 us,
  semantic-query p95 is 2874 us, RSS delta is 54611968 bytes, and the filtered
  result count remains the expected 1000 entries.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/log_region.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/log_region_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/log_region.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/log_region_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/log_region_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --rows=100000 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-indexed-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the indexed baseline.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 markdown files and 950 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- The next implementation slice should come from the remaining Phase 2 launch
  gaps, with special attention to whether indexed search construction needs a
  general worker/debounce pattern shared by `LogRegion`, `TreeTable`, and later
  search-heavy widgets.

## 2026-06-01 - Debounced Task Controller

Status: Complete

Context:
- The indexed `SB.4` LogRegion follow-up and the earlier `SB.11` TreeTable
  work both reached the same policy question: large search/index work needs
  restartable debounce/cancel behavior, but that policy should not live inside
  one widget.
- The existing `TaskController` already owns the right observable state for
  Fleury workflows: progress, output, result, error, cancellation, bounded
  events, and semantic task exposure.

Changes:
- Added `DebouncedTaskController<T>` to core `packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart`.
- The controller wraps an underlying `TaskController<T>` and exposes
  `schedule`, `runNow`, `cancel`, and `reset`.
- Pending scheduled work is coalesced into the latest runner, pending futures
  complete with a canceled `TaskResult`, and active work is canceled by default
  when a newer scheduled run arrives.
- Exported `DebouncedTaskController` from `package:fleury/fleury_core.dart`
  and therefore from `package:fleury/fleury.dart`.
- Added focused tests for coalescing pending work, preventing pending work from
  starting after cancel, canceling running stale work, and bypassing debounce
  with `runNow`.
- Updated the effects/workflow tracker, data-widget tracker, milestones,
  implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Put debounce policy in core effects as a task wrapper, not in `LogRegion`,
  `TreeTable`, or `SearchPanel`. Search-heavy widgets and apps should share
  the same restart/cancel/progress/event behavior.
- Keep the primitive honest: this is not off-main indexing. It gives apps a
  reusable policy for coalescing and canceling work; CPU-bound builders still
  need cooperative chunking or isolate/worker boundaries before broad
  off-main-indexing claims.
- Preserve `TaskController` as the semantic source of truth. The debounced
  wrapper delegates task state instead of adding a second status/event model.

Findings:
- The wrapper composes cleanly with the existing `TaskController` without
  touching task semantics or `TaskStatusView`.
- `Duration.zero` scheduling is useful for tests and for callers that want the
  same cancel/restart semantics without waiting for a debounce window.
- This is the right foundation for later LogRegion/TreeTable/SearchPanel
  integration, but it should not be wired into those widgets until a concrete
  app flow defines ownership of index data, progress UI, and stale-result
  handling.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 markdown files and 950 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- A later data-widget slice can use `DebouncedTaskController` to drive
  app-owned `LogRegionSearchIndex` or `TreeTableSearchIndex` refreshes, but
  only once the proof app has a concrete long-lived search/index workflow.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof App Debounced Global Search

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous slice added `DebouncedTaskController` but intentionally stopped
  short of wiring it into widgets without a concrete app flow.
- `SearchPanel` already had the right result-surface contract: query input,
  lazy rows, semantic result state, activation, and sanitized copy. The missing
  proof was app-owned async/debounced result production.

Changes:
- Added a new proof app Search screen and navigation command.
- Added app-owned global search state: query controller, result focus/list
  controller, `DebouncedTaskController<List<SearchResult>>`, resolved query,
  and result list.
- Built a deterministic search corpus across screens, runs, framework tree
  nodes, payload/diff/source/docs fixtures, and transcript events.
- Wired search query changes through `DebouncedTaskController.schedule`; the
  task reports progress/output and only publishes results if the query is still
  current.
- Rendered results with `SearchPanel` and kept activation semantic-driven:
  selected rows can navigate to the matching app screen and append transcript
  evidence.
- Added a proof-app regression for global search debounce, task semantics,
  result semantics, semantic activation, navigation, and transcript feedback.
- Updated `SB.10 Proof-App Journey` to include the Global Search path and saved
  a refreshed baseline at
  `packages/fleury_example_console/benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-global-search-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated benchmark docs, scenario lab, data-widget tracker, effects tracker,
  milestones, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep `SearchPanel` as a result surface instead of adding async/debounce
  ownership to the widget. Apps differ in how they search local indexes, remote
  services, command registries, files, logs, and agent transcripts.
- Use proof-app pressure to prove the composition: `DebouncedTaskController`
  owns result production and task semantics; `SearchPanel` owns query/list
  rendering, result semantics, copy, and activation.
- Do not claim off-main search yet. The proof app search task is synchronous
  and debounced; later large indexes still need chunking or worker/isolate
  boundaries.

Findings:
- The new proof-app test validates that a debounced Global Search query for
  `API deploy smoke` produces one result, exposes task progress as
  `1 matches`, exposes selected result key `run.RUN-1002`, activates through a
  semantic list-item action, navigates to the Runs screen, and records
  transcript evidence.
- The refreshed `SB.10` 10-iteration baseline passes. Full journey p95 is
  227678 us, global-search p95 is 89844 us, command-palette p95 is 15848 us,
  runs-filter p95 is 5481 us, process run-to-success p95 is 71441 us,
  semantic-query p95 is 1328 us, selected global search key is
  `run.RUN-1002`, and unsafe-frame count remains zero.
- Global-search p95 includes the intentional 60 ms debounce window, so its
  threshold should be interpreted as user-facing typeahead latency rather than
  pure search computation.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "global search"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_example_console`
  passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.10 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.10 --warmup=1 --iterations=10 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-global-search-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed and saved the refreshed baseline.

## 2026-06-01 - Cooperative Task Yielding For Index Builders

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous slices gave Fleury a restartable debounce primitive and proved
  app-owned debounced Global Search in the proof app.
- The remaining gap was CPU-heavy index construction: debounce can coalesce
  stale work, but synchronous index builders still risk monopolizing input and
  render handling while giving no task-progress or cancellation checkpoints.
- `LogRegionSearchIndex` and `TreeTableSearchIndex` were the right pressure
  points because `SB.4` and `SB.11` already measure 100k-scale retained data.

Changes:
- Added core `TaskYieldPolicy` and `TaskYieldCheckpoint` in
  `packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart`.
- Exported the cooperative-yield API through `package:fleury/fleury_core.dart`
  and therefore `package:fleury/fleury.dart`.
- Added `LogRegionSearchIndex.empty`,
  `LogRegionSearchIndex.buildCooperatively`, `appendFromCooperatively`, and
  `refreshCooperatively`.
- Added `TreeTableSearchIndex.buildCooperatively`, preserving the same preorder
  row/index contract as the synchronous builder while yielding between batches.
- Updated `SB.4` and `SB.11` so their index build paths run through
  `TaskController` with cooperative yield/progress checkpoints.
- Saved refreshed cooperative baselines:
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-cooperative-index-2026-06-01.json`
  and
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-cooperative-index-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated milestone docs, workstream trackers, benchmark docs, scenario lab,
  and decision log.

Decisions:
- Cooperative yielding is a core task primitive, not a widget-local policy.
  Long-running app-owned work should share the same progress, cancellation,
  events, and semantic task model as other Fleury work.
- Keep the log/tree indexes explicit and app-owned. The widget hot path should
  not hide index construction or silently choose eager, debounced, incremental,
  or worker-backed policy.
- Do not claim off-main indexing yet. This slice yields to the event loop and
  checks cancellation; isolate/worker-backed construction remains a separate
  launch-hardening decision.

Findings:
- `TaskYieldPolicy` composes cleanly with `TaskController` and can be tested
  without fake time: small item budgets force progress/yield checkpoints, and
  cancellation is observed immediately after the yield resumes.
- The refreshed `SB.4` 5-iteration cooperative baseline passes on the 100k-entry
  fixture: journey p95 549948 us, search-index-build p95 375479 us,
  append-burst p95 17553 us, filter-query p95 21771 us, semantic-query p95
  4505 us, search index progress current 100000, append refresh progress
  current 101000.
- The refreshed `SB.11` 5-iteration cooperative baseline passes on the
  100100-row hierarchy: journey p95 1013912 us, index-build p95 826676 us,
  filter-query p95 5706 us, semantic-query p95 1826 us, index progress current
  100100, search-index row count 100100.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/log_region.dart lib/src/tree_table.dart test/log_region_test.dart test/tree_table_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/log_region_test.dart test/tree_table_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.4 --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-logregion-cooperative-index-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the refreshed baseline.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.11 --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-treetable-cooperative-index-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed and saved the refreshed baseline.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene search for old pre-Fleury paths/names returned no matches
  in `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, and `tool`.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 markdown files and 963 local links
  resolve.

Next:
- The next indexing slice should be driven by a concrete proof workflow:
  either add a larger long-lived index/search screen to the proof app, or add a
  measured isolate/worker-backed index builder behind the same explicit index
  APIs once cooperative yielding is no longer enough.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof App Indexed Logs Workflow

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous slice added cooperative task yielding and proved it in core
  task tests plus `SB.4` / `SB.11` widget-scale benchmarks.
- The next journal instruction was to make the next indexing slice concrete:
  either prove a larger long-lived index/search workflow in the proof app, or
  move directly to isolate-backed builders.
- A proof-app workflow was the better next step because it exercises the
  framework as developers will experience it: screens, commands, task
  semantics, indexed filtering, append refresh, transcript feedback, and
  benchmark evidence.

Changes:
- Added a new proof app `Indexed Logs` screen and `Go to Indexed Logs` command.
- Added app-owned retained-log state: deterministic `LogEntry` fixtures,
  filter controller/focus, `LogRegionController`, `TaskController`, and
  optional `LogRegionSearchIndex`.
- Added `Build Proof Log Index` and `Append Indexed Log Burst` commands.
- Wired index build through
  `LogRegionSearchIndex.buildCooperatively` with `TaskYieldPolicy`.
- Wired append refresh through `LogRegionSearchIndex.refreshCooperatively`.
- Rendered the filtered retained rows through `LogRegion`, preserving source
  row count, selected key, filter text, sanitized output, and copy semantics.
- Kept the active or partially refreshed index hidden from the widget while a
  cooperative refresh is running, preventing `LogRegion`'s synchronous
  `searchIndex.refresh()` path from racing with the task-owned refresh.
- Added a proof-app regression for index build, task progress/output, indexed
  filtering, unsafe output sanitization, append refresh, and transcript
  evidence.
- Updated `SB.10 Proof-App Journey` to include the Indexed Logs workflow and
  saved a refreshed baseline at
  `packages/fleury_example_console/benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-indexed-logs-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated benchmark docs, scenario lab, implementation README, workstream
  trackers, milestones, decision log, and proof-app README.

Decisions:
- Prove cooperative indexing in a product-shaped proof-app flow before adding
  isolate-backed index APIs.
- Keep the index app-owned and explicit. The screen chooses when to build and
  refresh; `LogRegion` remains a view/filter surface.
- Hide in-progress indexes from `LogRegion` while cooperative refresh is
  running. This avoids a mixed ownership bug where the widget's synchronous
  refresh path can mutate the same index while a task is appending entries
  cooperatively.

Findings:
- The focused proof-app test validates that the Indexed Logs screen builds a
  192-row index, reports task progress `index proof logs complete`, filters
  `target:payment` to 48 rows, selects `IDX-1000`, sanitizes unsafe fixture
  content, appends three rows, refreshes the index to 195 rows, and records
  transcript evidence.
- The refreshed `SB.10` 10-iteration baseline passes. Full journey p95 is
  301300 us, global-search p95 is 86040 us, indexed-logs p95 is 63013 us,
  command-palette p95 is 15504 us, process run-to-success p95 is 60119 us,
  semantic-query p95 is 1093 us, indexed log row count is 195, filtered row
  count is 49, progress current is 195, selected indexed-log key is `IDX-1000`,
  and unsafe-frame count remains zero.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "indexed logs"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_example_console`
  passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.10 --warmup=1 --iterations=1 --json`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.10 --warmup=1 --iterations=10 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-indexed-logs-2026-06-01.json`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed and saved the refreshed baseline.

Next:
- The next indexing decision can now be made from stronger evidence. If proof
  workflows stay responsive with cooperative yielding, continue hardening
  fuzzy/ranked search and cached query indexes. If a larger proof flow exposes
  input stalls, add an isolate-backed builder behind the same explicit
  index/task ownership model.

## 2026-06-01 - Ranked SearchResultIndex For SearchPanel

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous proof-app slice added Indexed Logs and left the next decision at
  either heavier indexing or hardening fuzzy/ranked search and cached query
  indexes.
- `SearchPanel` still defaulted to source-order fuzzy filtering while
  `CommandPalette` already had cached exact/prefix/contains/fuzzy ranking.
- The proof app now has Global Search, so the next launch-shaped improvement
  was to make the general result surface predictably ranked without moving
  async search, remote search, worker, or paging policy into the widget.

Changes:
- Added public `SearchResultIndex` in `fleury_widgets` as a sanitized cached
  index over `SearchResult` fields.
- Changed default `buildSearchResultOrder` behavior to return exact, prefix,
  contains, then fuzzy subsequence matches in stable source order inside each
  rank.
- Preserved custom matcher behavior as source-order filtering so apps can keep
  caller-owned policies and compatibility.
- Cached a `SearchResultIndex` inside `SearchPanel` for the current `results`
  list, avoiding repeated sanitized/lowercase field construction on every
  query or render pass.
- Exported `SearchResultIndex` from `package:fleury_widgets/fleury_widgets.dart`.
- Routed proof-app Global Search through `SearchResultIndex` before rendering
  through `SearchPanel`.
- Added widget tests for ranked ordering, sanitized metadata search, and custom
  matcher source-order semantics.
- Saved refreshed proof-app benchmark evidence at
  `packages/fleury_example_console/benchmark/results/phase2-proof-app-ranked-search-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated the scenario lab, implementation README, milestones, data-widget and
  agent-workflow trackers, benchmark README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Make small/medium result ranking a first-party `SearchResultIndex` behavior
  because developers expect search panels to select the obvious exact/prefix
  hit before loose fuzzy matches.
- Keep search production app-owned. `DebouncedTaskController`, remote calls,
  large indexes, isolate/worker boundaries, streaming, and paging should remain
  explicit app policy instead of hidden inside `SearchPanel`.
- Keep custom matchers source-order to avoid surprising callers that already
  rank or filter their own result lists.

Findings:
- The new unit tests prove exact title matches sort before prefix, contains,
  and fuzzy matches; sanitized metadata is searchable; and unsafe terminal
  payload contents do not become searchable text.
- The focused proof-app Global Search test still passes with selected result
  key `run.RUN-1002` and semantic activation into the Runs screen.
- The refreshed `SB.10` 10-iteration baseline passes. Full journey p95 is
  296062 us, global-search p95 is 84221 us, indexed-logs p95 is 73452 us,
  command-palette p95 is 15076 us, process run-to-success p95 is 58716 us,
  semantic-query p95 is 993 us, selected global search key is `run.RUN-1002`,
  selected indexed-log key is `IDX-1000`, and unsafe-frame count remains zero.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/search_panel.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/search_panel_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/search_panel.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/search_panel_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart`
  passed.
