## 2026-06-01 - M2.1 Wizard/Page-Flow Forms

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.1 forms already had shared full-screen, inline, prompt-mode, scalar,
  secret, select, multi-select, numeric, date, path, async validation,
  snapshots, semantics, accessibility, and proof-app pressure.
- The remaining form launch gap was page-flow/wizard support for dense setup
  workflows. The proof app Connection screen had become a nine-field workflow,
  which was enough concrete pressure to justify adding a wizard projection.

Changes:
- Added `FormWizardStep`, `FormWizardController`, and `FormWizard` to
  `fleury_widgets`.
- Extended `FormPanel` with a filtered field projection plus optional title,
  action-row, and aggregate-form semantics controls so wizard rendering can
  reuse the same field widgets and semantics without duplicating form state.
- Added wizard semantics: layout `wizard`, total field count, visible field
  count, step count, current step id/title/index/position, and back/forward
  availability.
- Added async-aware semantic next/submit behavior. `SemanticAction.increment`
  validates the current step and waits for async field validators before moving
  forward; final `SemanticAction.submit` reuses the existing form submit path.
- Exported the wizard APIs from `package:fleury_widgets`.
- Converted the proof app Connection screen to a three-step wizard:
  basics, runtime, and secret confirmation.
- Added focused widget tests for wizard navigation gating, hidden/visible field
  semantics, invalid/valid submit behavior, cancel behavior, async next
  validation, and invalid step-definition guardrails.
- Updated M2.1 milestone notes, implementation README current actions,
  semantic-app-graph workstream notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Build wizard/page-flow as a projection over `FormDefinition` and
  `FormController`, not as a second form model. This keeps values, validation,
  async state, redaction, snapshots, and submission aligned across full-screen,
  inline, prompt, and wizard render modes.
- Require wizard steps to cover every form field exactly once. A wizard that
  hides a required field or duplicates a field creates an unrecoverable
  validation/debugging state, so invalid step definitions fail early.
- Keep step navigation semantics generic for now: increment/decrement represent
  next/back, and submit is exposed only on the final step. More specialized
  semantic actions should wait for broader evidence across non-form workflows.

Findings:
- The existing async-validator generation model was sufficient for page-flow
  validation. The main adjustment was to make the semantic next action return
  the awaited validation future instead of firing and forgetting.
- Reusing `FormPanel` through a visible-field projection avoided a second copy
  of text/select/checkbox/date/path/multi-select rendering and kept
  form-field semantics consistent.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/form.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/form_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/form.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/form_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/form_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "connection screen"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1074 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Treat M2.1 as closed for the MVP. Future form work should come from concrete
  product pressure, not broadening the base form contract.
- Continue Phase 2 from remaining launch gaps: real-terminal matrix evidence,
  Windows validation, accessibility/API stabilization, public adoption assets,
  or another proof-driven production-widget gap.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Wizard Form Accessibility Fallback

Status: Complete

Context:
- The M2.1 wizard/page-flow slice added rich semantic form state: layout
  `wizard`, visible field count, active step id/title/position, total step
  count, and back/forward availability.
- M2.8 accessibility/fallback output already summarized generic form state,
  but it did not yet preserve the new wizard facts. That would make prompt
  fallback, debug capture, and future adapters aware that a node is a form
  while losing the active page-flow context.

Changes:
- Extended the allow-listed form accessibility summary with
  `visibleFieldCount`, `stepCount`, `currentStepPosition`, `currentStepId`,
  `currentStepTitle`, `canGoBack`, and `canGoForward`.
- Added a focused core accessibility test proving wizard form state appears in
  text-first fallback output without dumping arbitrary state.
- Extended the `FormWizard` widget test to assert fallback state on the first
  and final wizard steps.
- Extended the proof app Connection-screen test to assert fallback state across
  the Basics, Runtime, and Secret wizard steps.
- Updated M2.8 milestone notes, implementation README, semantic-app-graph
  workstream notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep wizard fallback facts allow-listed inside the existing form summary.
  Page-flow state is essential for adapters, but arbitrary semantic-state dumps
  are still the wrong API and can bypass redaction discipline.
- Keep fallback wording human-readable and queryable: visible field count,
  step position/count, current step title/id, and available back/forward
  direction are enough for the current wizard proof.

Findings:
- `_formState` intentionally returns a single combined state string, matching
  the existing accessibility style for aggregate widgets. Tests should search
  that state text rather than expecting every aggregate fact to be a separate
  list entry.
- The same fallback path now describes a wizard from three evidence layers:
  a pure core semantic node, the reusable `FormWizard`, and the integrated
  proof-app Connection flow.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Terminal Matrix Stdio Preservation

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.10 still needs reviewed real-terminal matrix entries before Fleury can make
  strong terminal compatibility claims.
- A local `terminal-matrix` smoke capture exposed a tooling problem before any
  real entries should be trusted: the launcher used `Process.run` to collect
  `fleury diagnose --json`, so diagnose saw piped stdout and marked the entry
  non-interactive even when the launcher itself was run from a PTY.

Changes:
- Added `fleury diagnose --json-output=<path>`, which writes the stable
  diagnose JSON to a file without requiring stdout to be piped.
- Updated `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix` to run diagnose with
  `ProcessStartMode.inheritStdio`, preserving the tested terminal for
  `stdin.hasTerminal`, `stdout.hasTerminal`, and active probe writes.
- Terminal-matrix entries now record a sanitized command with
  `--json-output=<matrix-diagnosis-json>` instead of a temp path.
- Added a focused CLI test proving `diagnose --json-output` writes parseable
  JSON and leaves stdout empty.
- Updated M2.10 milestone notes, terminal matrix docs, terminal matrix README,
  terminal capability/security workstream notes, implementation README, and
  the decision log.

Decisions:
- Preserve inherited stdio for matrix captures. Active probes and TTY detection
  measure stdio; piping stdout to parse JSON invalidates the evidence.
- Keep `--json` as stdout JSON for normal scripting and add `--json-output` for
  matrix capture. This avoids broad API churn while giving terminal evidence
  collection a truthful path.

Findings:
- After the fix, a Codex PTY smoke capture reported `stdinIsTerminal: true`,
  `stdoutIsTerminal: true`, and `isInteractive: true`; it still produced
  `review.status: needsAttention` because the PTY did not reply to the active
  probes. That is the correct outcome for workflow/tooling evidence.
- A non-PTY smoke capture still reports `review.status: nonInteractive`, which
  proves the review triage continues to distinguish pipe/control cases.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/bin/fleury.dart tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix --label=codex-pty-smoke --output=/tmp/fleury-codex-pty-smoke-fixed.json --probe-timeout=20`
  in a PTY passed and produced an interactive `needsAttention` entry with
  three timed-out active probes.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix --label=codex-noninteractive-smoke --output=/tmp/fleury-codex-noninteractive-smoke-fixed.json --probe-timeout=20`
  passed and produced a `nonInteractive` entry.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1074 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Real terminal entries are still required. The capture workflow is now less
  likely to generate false non-interactive evidence, so the next M2.10 step is
  reviewed captures from macOS Terminal, iTerm2, Kitty/Ghostty/WezTerm where
  available, tmux, SSH, and Windows hosts.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.9/M2.10 Native Diagnose Platform Evidence

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.9 still needs real Windows validation, but local review found a fixable
  issue before Windows captures should be trusted: `fleury diagnose` was
  constructing `PosixTerminalDriver` directly instead of using the native
  driver selector.
- M2.10 matrix entries also lacked explicit OS/Dart platform evidence, forcing
  reviewers to infer host platform from terminal environment strings.

Changes:
- `fleury diagnose` now uses `createNativeTerminalDriver()`, so Windows
  diagnosis captures exercise `WindowsTerminalDriver` on Windows and the POSIX
  driver elsewhere.
- Added `TerminalPlatformReport` and serialized optional `platform` evidence
  in diagnosis JSON: operating system, OS version, and Dart runtime string.
- Matrix summaries now include `summary.platform`, and the terminal-matrix
  launcher prints the captured platform alongside terminal/review status.
- Windows console-mode planning now enables delayed newline auto-return along
  with processed virtual-terminal output and virtual-terminal input.
- Updated M2.9/M2.10 milestone notes, terminal capability/security workstream
  notes, terminal matrix docs, terminal matrix README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Diagnose must go through the same native driver selection as `runTui`.
  Compatibility evidence that bypasses the Windows driver path would be
  misleading.
- Keep platform evidence narrow and non-secret: OS, OS version, and Dart
  version are enough to review matrix captures without serializing local
  hostname, executable paths, or user-specific shell state.
- Enable `DISABLE_NEWLINE_AUTO_RETURN` in the Windows output-mode plan. The
  Microsoft console documentation describes it as useful with VT processing for
  terminal-emulator-like final-column behavior, which matters for retained
  cell rendering.

Findings:
- The macOS CLI/matrix smoke now emits `platform.operatingSystem: macos` in
  both raw diagnosis JSON and compact matrix summaries.
- Real Windows entries are still required before closing M2.9. This slice only
  makes those entries more truthful and reviewable.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/fleury_core.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart packages/fleury/bin/fleury.dart tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_core.dart lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart bin/fleury.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/windows_driver.dart test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/terminal/diagnose_cli_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/native_driver_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.

  plus a Python JSON assertion confirmed platform evidence is present.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Toaster Notification Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Toaster` is already a public production widget with overlay, stacking,
  auto-dismiss, severity, and action-hotkey behavior.
- Before this slice, toasts were visible rendered text and hotkeys only. They
  did not expose a first-class semantic node, so tests, debug capture,
  prompt/fallback output, and future adapters had to infer notification state
  from cells.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticRole.notification`.
- Wrapped each visible toast in a semantic notification node with stable
  per-toast ID, label, severity, stack position/count, auto-dismiss timing,
  dismiss action, and optional activate action with action label/key state.
- Routed semantic dismiss and activate through the same `_dismiss` and action
  callback paths used by auto-dismiss and hotkeys.
- Added allow-listed accessibility/fallback notification state for severity,
  stack position, auto-dismiss timing, and optional action label/key.
- Added focused `Toaster` tests for notification semantics, semantic dismiss,
  semantic activate, and accessibility snapshot output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat transient toasts as semantic notifications, not only overlay paint.
  Notifications are part of app state and should be inspectable and operable
  without screen scraping.
- Keep notification fallback state allow-listed. Severity, stack position,
  auto-dismiss timing, and action label/key are useful and safe; arbitrary
  toast/widget internals should not leak into fallback output.

Findings:
- Overlay entries participate in the normal element tree, so `Semantics`
  wrappers inside the toaster overlay are visible to `FleuryTester.semantics()`
  and `AccessibilitySnapshot` without special overlay traversal code.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/toaster.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/toaster_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/toaster.dart test/toaster_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/toaster_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1075 local
  links resolve.

Next:

  semantic roles or fallback summaries for widgets that do not yet expose
  stable user-visible behavior.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Tabs Semantics And Hidden Page Pruning

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Tabs` is a stable public navigation primitive in `fleury_widgets`, used by
  the proof-app/debug workflow family, but it only rendered labels and switched
  content. It did not expose tab nodes, semantic tab actions, or fallback state.
- `Tabs` keeps every page mounted through `IndexedStack` so page state survives
  switching. Before this slice, semantic collection still walked every mounted
  child, which meant hidden tab content could leak into tests, accessibility
  snapshots, debug capture, prompt fallback, or future adapters.

Changes:
- Added a semantic-child traversal hook and implemented it for `IndexedStack`
  so only the active child participates in semantic snapshots and
  semantic-action dispatch.
- Added first-class `Tabs` semantics: each tab label now contributes a
  `SemanticRole.tab` node with selected/focused state, focus/select/activate
  actions, tab index/position/count, and Alt+digit shortcut state.
- Added allow-listed accessibility/fallback tab state for tab position/count
  and shortcut narration.
- Added focused core semantics coverage for `IndexedStack` visible-child
  pruning.
- Added focused `Tabs` tests for tab semantic nodes, semantic selection,
  inactive page semantic pruning, and accessibility fallback output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- State-preserving hidden pages may stay mounted, but they must leave the
  semantic graph unless they are the currently visible page.
- Tab semantic actions should route through the same controller/focus path as
  keyboard tab switching, not a separate testing-only action path.

Findings:
- This aligns `IndexedStack` with the route-pruning rule already used by
  `Navigator`: preserved state is not the same thing as visible app meaning.
- The fix belongs in core semantic traversal instead of a one-off Tabs wrapper,
  so future pagers and state-preserving surfaces inherit the same behavior.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/basic.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart packages/fleury/test/semantics/semantics_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/tabs.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/tabs_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart lib/src/widgets/basic.dart lib/src/testing/fleury_tester.dart test/semantics/semantics_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/tabs.dart test/tabs_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/semantics_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/tabs_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1075 local
  links resolve.

  flow but still lack semantic actions, fallback state, or hidden-state

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Menu Overlay Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Menu` is a stable public overlay control with keyboard navigation,
  separators, disabled rows, nested submenus, focus traps, and integration
  coverage with `Toaster`.
- The semantic role set already had `menu` and `menuItem`, but `Menu` did not
  contribute trigger, menu body, or item nodes. Tests, debug capture,
  prompt/fallback output, and future adapters had to infer menu state from
  rendered cells.

Changes:
- Added optional `Menu.semanticLabel` so app authors can name arbitrary trigger
  widgets in semantic snapshots.
- Wrapped the trigger as a semantic button with focus/open/close/activate
  actions, expanded state, and menu item count state.
- Wrapped open menu panels as semantic menu nodes with focus/close actions,
  expanded state, depth, selected index, and item count state.
- Wrapped selectable leaf and submenu rows as semantic menu item nodes with
  selected/focused/enabled state, submenu expanded state, item position/count,
  child item count, and activate/open actions routed through the existing menu
  controller paths.
- Added allow-listed accessibility/fallback menu state for menu item counts and
  row position/count.
- Added focused `Menu` tests for semantic trigger open, menu item activation,
  submenu open, and accessibility fallback output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat overlay menus as semantic app surfaces, not just painted overlay rows.
- Preserve the existing lazy-list contract: menu item nodes are visible rows
  mounted by render/layout, so row-level semantic assertions/actions in tests
  should render or capture a frame after opening the menu.

Findings:
- The trigger and menu body semantics are available immediately after semantic
  open, but `ListView.builder` row semantics are intentionally mounted by the
  first render pass. This matches existing DataTable/SearchPanel/log behavior
  and avoids eager row mounting just for semantic tests.
- `Menu` needed a `semanticLabel` because its trigger can be any widget; trying
  to infer a stable label from arbitrary child widgets would be brittle.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/menu.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/menu_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/menu.dart test/menu_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/menu_test.dart test/integration_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- Run final hygiene for this slice (`git diff --check`, stale old-name scan,
  markdown link scan), then continue proof-driven M2.8 gaps only where stable
  public widgets have real user-visible behavior and missing semantic/action
  coverage.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Select Picker Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Select` is a stable public form/picker control with keyboard and mouse
  behavior, disabled options, an anchored overlay list, and an applied-value
  marker.
- After the `Menu` slice, `Select` was the next credible overlay-control gap:
  it had visible open/pick behavior but no semantic trigger, option-list, row,
  selected-value, disabled-option, or fallback state.

Changes:
- Added optional `Select.semanticLabel` so app authors can name the picker
  independently from the changing collapsed value.
- Wrapped the collapsed trigger as a semantic button with focus/open/close/
  activate actions, expanded state, selected value, selected option label, and
  option count state.
- Wrapped the open option panel as a semantic menu node with focus/close
  actions, selected highlight index, applied value index, and option count
  state.
- Wrapped option rows as semantic menu-item nodes with select/activate actions,
  option value, selected highlight state, disabled state, checked applied-option
  state, option index, and option position/count.
- Reused the allow-listed menu accessibility/fallback state for option-list
  counts and option position/count; checked state identifies the applied option.
- Added focused `Select` tests for semantic trigger open, semantic option
  select, disabled option state, and accessibility fallback output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Model `Select` as an overlay picker using the existing menu/menu-item
  semantic contract until a dedicated combobox/listbox role earns its way in
  through more than one widget.
- Keep the same lazy-row testing contract as `Menu`: render or capture a frame
  after opening before targeting row-level option semantics.

Findings:
- The existing `SemanticAction.select`, checked state, expanded state, and menu
  fallback summary are enough to make `Select` testable and adapter-friendly
  without broadening the role taxonomy today.
- Naming the picker separately from the current option label matters; otherwise
  a test or adapter has to chase a moving label as the selected value changes.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/select.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/select_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/select.dart test/select_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/select_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 FilePicker Tree Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `FilePicker` is the older/simple filesystem selector. It has stable
  keyboard behavior for directory navigation, file selection, hidden-file
  filtering, and parent-directory traversal, but previously exposed no semantic
  tree or row actions.
- `FileBrowser` already proved the richer production filesystem semantic
  contract. The right move was to align `FilePicker` with that tree/tree-item
  shape instead of adding a dedicated file-picker role.

Changes:
- Added `FilePicker.semanticLabel` with a default `Files` tree label.
- Wrapped the picker as a `SemanticRole.tree` with focus, navigate, and open
  actions, current-directory value, selected-entry state, row count, hidden
  policy, and sanitized path values.
- Wrapped each directory/file row as a `SemanticRole.treeItem` with selected
  state, sanitized path value, row/type metadata, and semantic open action when
  the row can be opened or selected.
- Routed semantic row open through the same directory/file behavior as Enter:
  directories navigate in place and files call `onSelected`.
- Reused the existing collection accessibility/fallback path for row count and
  selected tree-item state rather than widening the fallback taxonomy.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep `FileBrowser` as the richer production filesystem surface and make
  `FilePicker` a semantically aligned simple selector. This preserves the
  existing widget boundary while making both testable through the same tree
  contract.
- Do not add a new `filePicker` semantic role yet. The proven behavior is
  hierarchical navigation plus row opening, which `tree`/`treeItem` already
  represent.

Findings:
- The direct no-semantics scan no longer lists `file_picker.dart`; remaining
  stable-input candidates are `color_picker.dart`, `number_input.dart`, and
  `tooltip.dart`, while `password_input.dart` and several visual primitives are
  likely already covered by composition or should stay deferred until a proof
  flow needs them.
- `NumberInput` deserves a separate design check before adding direct
  semantics because it currently behaves like constrained text entry, not a
  stepper/spin button.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/file_picker.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/file_picker_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/file_picker.dart test/file_picker_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/file_picker_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/file_picker_test.dart test/file_browser_test.dart test/tree_test.dart test/integration_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1078 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue M2.8 with the next stable control only when it has a concrete
  user-visible semantic/action gap. Likely candidates are `ColorPicker` for
  selectable swatches or a carefully designed `NumberInput` numeric-field
  contract; avoid treating visual chart/buffer primitives as launch blockers.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 ColorPicker Swatch Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `ColorPicker` is a stable mutually-exclusive palette selector with keyboard
  movement, committed selection on navigation, and custom palette support.
- It previously rendered useful swatches but exposed no semantic picker,
  selected-color state, row actions, or custom labels for app-specific palettes.

Changes:
- Added `ColorPicker.semanticLabel` for the picker/list label.
- Added `ColorPicker.semanticColorLabelBuilder` so custom app palettes can name
  swatches without relying on generic RGB/index labels.
- Wrapped the picker as a `SemanticRole.list` with focus/navigate actions,
  selected-color value/state, palette size, and collection row/column state.
- Wrapped each swatch as a radio-like `SemanticRole.radio` with checked and
  selected state, select/activate actions, stable color value strings, color
  position/count, and ANSI/indexed/RGB metadata.
- Routed semantic select/activate through the same `onChanged` path as
  keyboard movement while requesting picker focus.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Model the picker as a semantic list of radio-like swatches instead of adding
  a dedicated color-picker role. The role taxonomy already describes the
  behavior: one selected option from a list.
- Keep color-specific fallback summaries out of core for now. The existing
  collection fallback plus checked/selected swatch state gives useful tests and
  adapter hooks without widening the allow-listed fallback model prematurely.

Findings:
- The default ANSI labels are useful enough for the built-in palette, but
  custom palettes need app-owned semantic labels; otherwise an RGB value like
  `rgb:10,20,30` cannot communicate business meaning such as "Brand primary".
- The remaining direct no-semantics scan is now mostly visual primitives plus
  composition-covered controls. `NumberInput` and `Tooltip` are the next
  plausible stable controls, but `NumberInput` needs a deliberate numeric-field
  contract rather than pretending it is a stepper.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/color_picker.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/color_picker_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/color_picker.dart test/color_picker_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

- `dart test test/color_picker_test.dart test/file_picker_test.dart test/select_test.dart test/menu_test.dart test/integration_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1081 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue only with stable public widgets that expose app input or state.
  Re-evaluate `NumberInput` as constrained numeric text entry, not a hidden
  spin button, before adding direct semantics.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 NumberInput Semantic Text-Field Metadata

Status: Complete

Context:
- `NumberInput` is a constrained `TextInput`, not a stepper: users type
  numeric text, invalid characters are rejected, and min/max clamping happens
  on submit.
- `Stepper` already owns `SemanticRole.spinButton` and increment/decrement
  actions. Giving `NumberInput` the same role would make semantics lie about
  available behavior.

Changes:
- Added `TextInput.semanticLabel` so wrappers and form controls can expose a
  durable semantic name even when placeholder text is only an example.
- Added additive `TextInput.semanticState`, merged below core text-editing
  state so core safety facts such as redaction, selection, paste, and clipboard
  policy remain authoritative.
- Added `NumberInput.semanticLabel` and passed numeric metadata through the
  `TextInput` semantic seam: `fieldType`, parsed `numericValue`, min/max,
  negative/decimal policy, number format, and clamp-on-submit state.
- Kept `NumberInput` on `SemanticRole.textField` with existing focus, clear,
  submit, value, selection, and accessibility behavior.
- Rebuilt `NumberInput` semantic state when the controller changes or clamping
  mutates text, preventing stale numeric metadata after user edits or semantic
  submit.
- Added focused core `TextInput` coverage for `semanticLabel`/`semanticState`
  and `NumberInput` coverage for numeric field state, accessibility fallback,
  and semantic submit clamping.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG/TEE docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Do not model `NumberInput` as a spin button until it has real nudge
  behavior. Typed numeric entry is a text field with numeric constraints.
- Add a small generic semantic extension seam to `TextInput` instead of
  wrapping `NumberInput` in an outer semantic node. This keeps text editing
  actions owned by the actual field and gives future specialized text controls
  the same safe metadata path.

Findings:
- Additive semantic state must not override core text-field safety state.
  `TextInput` now merges extra state first and core selection/redaction/
  clipboard/paste state afterward.
- Since `NumberInput` computes semantic state above the inner `TextInput`, it
  must rebuild on controller changes even though the visible text field can
  repaint from its own listener.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/text_input_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/number_input.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/number_input_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/text_input_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/number_input.dart test/number_input_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/number_input_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/number_input_test.dart test/form_test.dart test/password_input_test.dart test/autocomplete_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1084 local

Next:

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Autocomplete Suggestion Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Autocomplete` is a stable public text-control adjunct with live filtering,
  parent-owned Up/Down behavior, anchored overlay suggestions, Enter/Esc
  handling, and integration coverage inside forms.
- It previously relied on the underlying `TextInput` for field behavior but did
  not expose the suggestion list, highlighted suggestion, query state, row
  activation, or fallback output through the semantic graph.

Changes:
- Added `Autocomplete.placeholder` and optional `Autocomplete.semanticLabel`.
  The placeholder labels the underlying `TextInput`; the semantic label names
  the suggestion menu independently from the current query.
- Wrapped the suggestion overlay as a semantic menu with focus/close actions,
  expanded state, completion query, selected row index, and suggestion count
  state.
- Wrapped suggestion rows as semantic menu-item nodes with selected/focused
  state, suggestion text value, select/activate actions, query state, and
  suggestion position/count.
- Routed semantic select/activate through the same `_pick` path as Enter so the
  controller text, cursor selection, close behavior, and `onSelected` callback
  stay aligned.
- Reused the allow-listed menu accessibility/fallback state for suggestion
  count and suggestion position/count.
- Added focused `Autocomplete` tests for suggestion menu semantics, semantic
  activation, semantic close, and accessibility fallback output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs, the text-editing workstream, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Model autocomplete suggestions as a semantic menu over a labeled `TextInput`
  instead of adding a dedicated autocomplete role or refactoring it into the
  core completion subsystem.
- Preserve existing parent-owned Up/Down behavior. Autocomplete row actions are
  semantic/selective entry points; keyboard navigation stays in the existing
  key-binding path.

Findings:
- This gives `Autocomplete` useful adapter/test semantics without duplicating
  `CompletionTextInput`. The two widgets still serve different API shapes:
  provider/range completion versus option filtering/fill.
- Row-level suggestion semantics follow the same lazy visible-row contract as
  Menu and Select: tests render/capture a frame after opening before targeting
  suggestion rows.

Validation:

  passed.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Tooltip Information Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `Tooltip` is a stable public informational widget with focus-triggered
  overlay behavior.
- It previously painted useful help text, but tests, fallback output, and
  future adapters had to infer that help text from rendered cells instead of
  querying the semantic graph.

Changes:
- Added `Tooltip.semanticLabel`, defaulting to `Tooltip`.
- Wrapped the focused tooltip owner as a semantic region with sanitized message
  value/hint and `tooltipVisible` state.
- Wrapped the visible overlay text as a semantic text node only while the
  overlay is mounted.
- Sanitized CR/LF/tab and unsafe display runes before exposing tooltip message
  values through semantics.
- Rebuilt semantic state when the tooltip shows, hides, or updates its message
  or semantic label.
- Added focused tooltip tests for visible semantic help text and hidden-state
  pruning.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat tooltip as semantic help text, not an action surface. It has no user
  action beyond focus-triggered visibility, so region/text semantics and
  visible state are enough.
- Keep the overlay semantic node mounted only while visible. The owner region
  remains queryable while hidden, which gives fallback adapters the help text
  without exposing a stale overlay node.

Findings:
- Tooltip is a good example of a semantic-only launch win: no rendering or
  interaction changes were needed, but tests can now assert the help contract
  without scraping terminal output.
- The remaining no-direct-semantics inventory is mostly visual primitives,
  composed controls, and widgets that need a product-shaped reason before
  receiving dedicated semantic summaries.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 PasswordInput Secret-Field Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- `PasswordInput` already composed `TextInput(obscureText: true)` and used the
  redacted clipboard policy, so raw secret values were protected in semantics,
  copy, debug capture, and accessibility output.
- After the `TextInput.semanticLabel`/`semanticState` seam was added for
  specialized text fields, standalone password fields still lacked explicit
  secret-field metadata unless they were wrapped by `FormPanel`.

Changes:
- Added optional `PasswordInput.semanticLabel`.
- Added additive `PasswordInput.semanticState` for app-specific secret-field
  facts.
- Passed semantic metadata through `TextInput` while making
  `fieldType: secret` and `redacted: true` authoritative for the wrapper.
- Preserved core `TextInput` redaction behavior: semantic value remains
  omitted, `redactedValue` and `clipboardRedacted` remain true, and clipboard
  policy remains redacted.
- Added focused `PasswordInput` tests for semantic label, custom metadata,
  secret-field fallback state, redacted values, and accessibility output.
- Updated M2.8/RDT/SAG/TEE docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep `PasswordInput` as text-field semantics, not a new password role. It is
  typed text entry with stricter redaction and clipboard policy.
- Let app-specific metadata flow through the same generic text-field seam, but
  keep wrapper-owned secret facts authoritative so callers cannot accidentally
  make a password field look non-secret through semantics.

Findings:
- `PasswordInput` was composition-covered for raw value safety, but adding
  `fieldType: secret` gives tests, prompt fallback, and future adapters a
  stable way to distinguish secret text entry from ordinary obscured visuals.
- The form layer already publishes secret metadata on outer form-field nodes;
  this slice closes the standalone widget case without changing form behavior.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/password_input.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/password_input_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/password_input.dart test/password_input_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/password_input_test.dart test/form_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1090 local
  links resolve.
- Direct no-semantics inventory still lists `PasswordInput` because it remains
  composition-based over `TextInput`; the semantic contract is covered by the
  wrapped text-field node and focused tests.

Next:
- Continue Phase 2 from either terminal compatibility evidence work or another
  proof-driven widget/API gap with concrete launch value. Avoid adding
  semantics to visual-only primitives without a proof workflow that needs a
  stable summary.

## 2026-06-01 - RDT/EWP Debug Capture Task-Event Summaries

Status: Complete

Context:
- `TaskEvent` history already gave workers and subprocesses bounded event
  ordering for devtools, semantics, tests, and future replay hooks.
- `DebugCaptureArtifact` could query inputs, frames, output summaries,
  semantics, and accessibility, but workflow event history was only indirectly
  visible through semantic summary counts.
- Full replay remains intentionally deferred, so the useful launch slice is a
  safe metadata path rather than raw event replay.

Changes:
- Added `DebugTaskEventSummary` to debug capture.
- Added bounded task-event storage to `DebugCaptureRecorder` with
  `recordTaskEvent` and `recordTaskEvents`.
- Added `taskEvents` serialization to `DebugCaptureSnapshot`.
- Added `DebugCaptureArtifact.taskEvents`, `taskEventsFor`, and
  `hasTaskEvent` query helpers for regression tests.
- Exported `DebugTaskEventSummary` through `package:fleury/fleury_test.dart`.
- Added focused debug-capture tests proving task-event capture, artifact
  queries, bounded retention, sanitized progress labels, and no raw task
  output/error/result leakage.
- Updated M2.11/RDT/EWP docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep captured task events metadata-only until a future full replay design has
  a comprehensive redaction policy. The capture preserves event source,
  sequence, run ID, kind, status, progress totals/label, output source/severity,
  output safety flags, value type, and error type.
- Do not serialize raw task output text, result values, error messages, or
  stack traces. Those fields can contain app secrets or unstable internals.

Findings:
- This fills a real replay/devtools hook gap without broadening the launch
  promise into deterministic replay. Tests can now assert that a workflow
  started, produced output metadata, failed/succeeded/canceled, and retained
  bounded ordering from the capture artifact itself.
- Task-event summaries complement semantic task state: semantics explain the
  current app meaning, while capture task-event summaries explain recent
  workflow progression.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart packages/fleury/lib/fleury_test.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue Phase 2 from either external terminal matrix evidence or another
  local framework gap that strengthens production app robustness.

## 2026-06-01 - RDT/EWP Debug Capture Time Markers

Status: Complete

Context:
- RDT.2/EWP.1 still called out fake-time and replay-clock markers as pending
  even though input, resize, frame, terminal, semantic, accessibility, output,
  and task-event capture hooks were already covered.
- The animation layer already has `Clock` and `FakeClock`, so the missing piece
  was capture evidence, not a second timing system.
- Full replay remains deferred; this slice keeps the launch promise to
  targeted regression evidence.

Changes:
- Added `DebugTimeMarker` to debug capture as a deterministic marker with
  source, label, elapsed microseconds, optional sequence, and fake-time flag.
- Added `DebugTimeMarker.fromClock` so tests and replay prototypes can record
  elapsed time from the existing `Clock`/`FakeClock` abstraction.
- Added bounded time-marker storage to `DebugCaptureRecorder` plus snapshot
  serialization.
- Added `DebugCaptureArtifact.timeMarkers`, `timeMarkersFor`, and
  `hasTimeMarker` query helpers.
- Exported `DebugTimeMarker` through `package:fleury/fleury_test.dart`.
- Added focused debug-capture tests for sanitized marker labels, artifact
  queries, `FakeClock`-driven elapsed values, and bounded retention.
- Updated M2.11/RDT/EWP docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Represent replay-clock evidence as explicit capture markers, not wall-clock
  timestamps. Artifacts should be deterministic and host-independent.
- Keep task events timestamp-free. Task streams explain workflow ordering;
  time markers explain caller-owned fake/replay clock progress.

Findings:
- This closes the planned fake-time hook without making replay broader than
  the current MVP needs. A failing workflow can now capture "after input",
  "after debounce", or "after worker" points alongside inputs, frames,
  semantics, output summaries, and task-event summaries.
- The marker API belongs in `fleury_test` for now. It is useful for regression
  artifacts and replay prototypes, but it does not need to be part of the app
  runtime API surface yet.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart packages/fleury/lib/fleury_test.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart lib/fleury_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/effects/task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:

## 2026-06-01 - TEE/RDT Completion Menu Semantic Actions

Status: Complete

Context:
- `CompletionTextInput` already rendered provider-backed completion rows and
  exposed menu/menu-item semantic nodes, but row semantics advertised select
  without handling semantic actions.
- `Autocomplete` already proved the right action shape for suggestion menus:
  the input field stays a text field, while the overlay menu supports
  close/focus and rows support select/activate.
- Agent/developer composers need completions to be operable through semantic
  actions, not only keyboard events.

Changes:
- Added semantic focus/close actions to the `CompletionTextInput` completion
  menu.
- Added semantic select/activate actions to completion rows.
- Routed row select/activate through the existing completion controller range
  replacement path, preserving the same outcome as Tab acceptance.
- Added row value/focus/action/query/position/count metadata for stable tests
  and fallback summaries.
- Added focused widget tests for semantic row metadata, semantic activation,
  accepted-option callbacks, text selection, and semantic close without
  clearing input text.
- Updated TEE/RDT/M1.2 docs, the README execution summary, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Keep completion suggestions modeled as semantic menu/menu-item surfaces over
  a core text field, matching `Autocomplete` and avoiding a separate completion
  role until more widgets prove it necessary.
- Reuse `TextCompletionController` and `TextEditingController.replaceRange`
  for semantic activation rather than creating a second acceptance path.

Findings:
- The gap was not rendering or controller capability; it was action dispatch.
  Fixing it makes completion UI testable through the same semantic graph that
  future adapters and agent workflows will use.
- This strengthens the "strong text/input" launch pillar without expanding
  public runtime API surface.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/completion_text_input.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/completion_text_input_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/completion_text_input.dart test/completion_text_input_test.dart`

  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:

## 2026-06-01 - RDT/DVW Composition Table Semantic Actions

Status: Complete

Context:
- The composition-based `Table` already exposed table shape, cell coordinate,
  selection state, and table-level semantic actions.
- Unlike newer production surfaces such as `DataTable`, those actions did not
  dispatch through a `Semantics.onAction` handler, so semantic tests/adapters
  could see actions that were not operable.
- `Table` should stay the simple small-grid widget; `DataTable` remains the
  large render-island path.

Changes:
- Added table-level semantic action dispatch for focus/select/activate.
- Added body-cell semantic focus/select/activate actions for interactive
  tables.
- Routed table and cell actions through the existing `FocusNode`,
  `TableController.selectedIndex`, and `onSelect` callback used by keyboard
  interaction.
- Added focused tests proving table activate invokes the selected row, body-cell
  select moves focus and row selection, and body-cell activate selects and
  invokes the row.
- Updated RDT/DVW/M1.1 docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Do not add a separate row abstraction to the simple composition table just to
  make actions work. Body-cell actions are enough for the current small-grid
  widget, while row-rich and large-data behavior belongs to `DataTable`.
- Reuse existing controller/focus/callback paths for semantics so keyboard,
  tests, and future adapters observe the same behavior.

Findings:
- This was a semantic-action consistency gap, not a data-widget architecture
  gap. Closing it makes the original `Table` honest: exposed actions now have
  matching behavior.
- Keeping the fix scoped avoids confusing `Table` with `DataTable`; the latter
  still owns virtualization, stable row keys, copy/export, range selection, and
  benchmark pressure.

Validation:

  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/table.dart test/table_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:

  first-party widgets more robust and automatable. Avoid inventing broader

## 2026-06-01 - EWP/RDT TaskStatusView Semantic Cancel

Status: Complete

Context:
- `TaskStatusView` exposed `SemanticAction.cancel` while a task was running,
  but the semantic node did not handle the action.
- Process-specific cancellation was already wired through `ProcessPanel`, but
  the generic task-status surface should be operable without requiring a
  process panel or app-specific command.
- This is part of the workflow semantics launch pillar: task status, progress,
  and cancellation need to be inspectable and actionable through the semantic
  graph.

Changes:
- Added `Semantics.onAction` to `TaskStatusView` while the controller is
  running.
- Routed semantic cancel through `TaskController.cancel`.
- Added a focused widget test proving semantic cancel completes through
  `FleuryTester.invokeSemanticAction`, updates task status, records a canceled
  task event, completes the pending task result as canceled, and removes the
  cancel action after settlement.
- Updated EWP/RDT/M1.6 docs, the README execution summary, and the decision
  log.

Decisions:
- Keep generic task cancellation owned by `TaskController`. Widgets and app
  commands should dispatch into the controller rather than maintaining a second
  cancellation state machine.
- Treat semantic task actions as behavior, not descriptive metadata. If a
  running task node advertises cancel, tests and adapters must be able to
  invoke it.

Findings:
- This closes a real overpromised-semantic gap in core effects. Generic task
  status, process panels, and app commands now share the same cancellation
  model.
- The change does not broaden worker/process architecture; it simply makes the
  existing controller contract reachable through the semantic graph.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/effects/task.dart test/effects/task_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/effects/task_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue with local semantic-action/fallback hardening where existing widgets
  expose actions, workflow state, or fallback state that should be directly
  operable and testable. Keep larger workflow engines, Dune/`dune_cli`, ACP,
  and full replay deferred.

## 2026-06-01 - SAG/RDT Navigator Semantic Close And Dismiss

Status: Complete

Context:
- Navigator and route nodes exposed close/dismiss actions in the semantic tree,
  but those actions were descriptive only.
- Keyboard Esc/back already routes through `NavigatorState.maybePop`, including
  `PopScope` guard behavior. Semantic automation should use the same path.
- This closes another overpromised semantic action in the core app/navigation
  surface without expanding routing or app-kernel API.

Changes:
- Added semantic action handling to the root `Navigator` semantic node.
- Added active-route semantic action handling to `_RouteHost`.
- Routed close and dismiss actions through `maybePop` for both navigator-level
  and route-level semantic nodes.
- Added focused widget tests for navigator close, page-route close, and
  modal-route dismiss via `FleuryTester.invokeSemanticAction`.
- Added PopScope guard coverage proving semantic route close uses the same
  blocked-pop path as keyboard Esc/back instead of bypassing guards.
- Updated SAG/RDT/milestone docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Semantic close/dismiss must preserve the guarded navigation contract. Use
  `maybePop`, not unconditional `pop`, so `PopScope` and future back policies
  remain authoritative.
- Route-level semantic actions stay active-route-only. Inactive/leaving route
  nodes can remain visible for stack/debug state, but hidden route children and
  stale route actions should not be invokable.

Findings:
- The existing semantic-action dispatcher was already able to route actions to
  core `Semantics` contributors; the missing piece was a navigator-owned
  handler.
- This improves testing, accessibility/fallback, and future adapter readiness
  in a user-visible way: route dismissal is now behavior, not just metadata.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/navigator.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/navigator_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/widgets/navigator.dart test/widgets/navigator_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/navigator_test.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue semantic-action/fallback hardening only where the existing widget or
  app surface already owns stable user-visible behavior. Avoid visual-only
  semantic actions and keep ACP, Dune/`dune_cli`, isolate-backed indexing, and
  full replay out of the current cycle unless a proof-app gap forces them.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT Proof-App Authored Semantic Actions

Status: Complete

Context:
- The proof app is the current product-shaped pressure harness before
  Dune/`dune_cli`.
- Its sidebar screen rows and Diagnostics report nodes advertised semantic
  navigation/diagnose/capture actions, but some of those app-authored nodes did
  not execute behavior.
- This was not a framework API gap; it was a proof-app consistency gap where
  app-authored semantics needed to use the app-kernel primitives already in
  place.

Changes:
- Routed sidebar list-item `SemanticAction.navigate` through
  `FleuryAppController.screens.activate`.
- Routed Diagnostics report `diagnose` and `captureDebug` semantic actions
  through the existing `CommandRegistry`.
- Routed Diagnostics capability-row `diagnose` semantic actions through the
  same command path.
- Added proof-app tests for sidebar semantic navigation, Diagnostics semantic
  debug capture, Diagnostics semantic diagnose, and row-level diagnose action.
- Updated RDT/ADW/M1.4 docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Proof-app authored semantic actions should be behavioral. If a node advertises
  an action, it should dispatch through the same screen controller, command
  registry, callback, or widget controller as visible UI.
- Keep this proof-app-local. No new framework API is needed while the existing
  semantic action callback and app-kernel command primitives can express the
  behavior.

Findings:
- `Semantics.onAction` was sufficient for app-authored proof-app nodes.
- The command-registry path works for both report-level and row-level
  diagnostic actions, while the sidebar only needs the existing
  `ScreenController`.
- The proof app now better demonstrates Fleury's claim that semantic app graph
  nodes are operable app meaning, not passive annotations.

## 2026-06-01 - SAG/M2.8 Diagnostic Accessibility Fallback

Status: Complete

Context:
- The proof app Diagnostics screen already publishes semantic terminal profile,
  capability, policy, and debug-capture state.
- The text-first accessibility/fallback projection only exposed generic
  capability and clipboard rows, so report-level diagnostic state was visible
  to semantic tests but weak in prompt fallback, debug capture, and future
  adapter output.
- This is an M2.8-style fallback improvement: allow-list stable, safe
  diagnostic facts rather than dumping arbitrary semantic-state maps.

Changes:
- Added diagnostic accessibility summary generation for terminal size, color
  mode, image protocol, capability-row count, fallback/warning/unsupported
  counts, debug-capture count, streaming state, and OSC policy state.
- Added core accessibility tests for diagnostic fallback text and plain-text
  export.
- Added proof-app Diagnostics assertions proving terminal profile and
  debug-capture facts appear in accessibility fallback output.
- Hardened the proof-app indexed-log test to wait for both target progress and
  terminal succeeded state before asserting completion, after the full quick
  gate exposed a running/succeeded race.
- Updated SAG/M2.8/RDT docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep diagnostic fallback output allow-listed. Terminal diagnosis can contain
  environment-specific details, so fallback text should expose stable profile,
  count, policy, and capture facts rather than arbitrary diagnostic maps.
- Keep the projection semantic-derived. Diagnostics fallback must not parse
  rendered cells or duplicate the terminal report model.

Findings:
- The existing `SemanticState` keys were already sufficient; the missing piece
  was safe projection into `AccessibilitySnapshot`.
- This strengthens the terminal-correctness and testing/devtools launch claims:
  a diagnostic report is now queryable and narratable from the semantic layer,
  not only visible as terminal text.
- The quick gate exposed a separate proof-app race: cooperative index progress
  could reach the final count before the task status changed to `succeeded`.
  The test now waits for both facts, which better matches the task model.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury/test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue fallback/accessibility or semantic-action hardening where proof-app
  workflows expose stable state that should be queryable, narratable, and
  operable through the semantic graph. Keep projections allow-listed and
  avoid public API expansion unless the proof app or widget suite proves the
  need.

## 2026-06-01 - SAG/M2.8 App Status Accessibility Fallback

Status: Complete

Context:
- `AppStatusBar` and status items already publish semantic count, item ID,
  severity, value, command identity, and activate actions.
- The text-first accessibility/fallback projection only narrated status
  severity, leaving the status bar weaker in prompt fallback, debug capture,
  and future adapter output than its semantic tree.
- This is an M2.8-style fallback improvement: keep status fallback output
  allow-listed and semantic-derived rather than dumping arbitrary app status
  state.

Changes:
- Expanded status accessibility summary generation to include status item
  count, status item ID, and severity.
- Added core accessibility coverage proving status count, item identity,
  severity, value, command identity, and activate actions are queryable from
  `AccessibilitySnapshot`.
- Added proof-console coverage proving the app status bar exposes count,
  screen/debug item identity, debug capture value, command identity, and
  activate action through the text-first fallback projection.
- Updated SAG/M2.8/RDT docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep app-status fallback summaries limited to count, item ID, and severity.
  Values, command IDs, and actions already have first-class accessibility
  fields, so the status-specific summary should not duplicate arbitrary state
  or leak app internals.
- Keep the projection semantic-derived. Status fallback must not parse rendered
  cells or duplicate `AppStatusBar` rendering logic.

Findings:
- The existing app-kernel status semantics were already strong enough; the
  missing piece was the safe fallback narration.
- This strengthens the app-kernel and testing/devtools launch claims: status
  bars now provide useful text-first evidence for app mode, long-running task
  state, and debug capture affordances without special-casing a product UI.
- The proof app remains the right harness for finding these gaps because it
  exercises command-driven status items, screen state, task/process state, and
  debug actions in one app.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury/test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue proof-driven hardening. Good next candidates are remaining
  first-party widgets or proof-app workflows where a semantic node already
  advertises stable state/actions but the accessibility/debug projection still
  loses launch-relevant information. Keep ACP, Dune/`dune_cli`, public
  adoption work, isolate-backed indexing, and full replay deferred unless the
  proof app reveals a concrete core blocker.

## 2026-06-01 - SAG/M2.8 Search And Log Accessibility Fallback

Status: Complete

Context:
- Phase 2 data-heavy widgets already exposed useful semantic state for
  `SearchPanel`, `LogRegion`, and their rows, but the text-first
  accessibility/fallback projection only preserved generic collection counts,
  filter text, and selected keys.
- The proof app exercises both surfaces through Global Search and Indexed
  Logs, so losing result totals, filtered counts, selected category/source,
  log filter details, and source/view row positions weakened debug capture and
  future adapter output.
- A validation pass also caught a regression from the previous status slice:
  generic `severity` state on non-status widgets was being narrated as
  `status severity ...`.

Changes:
- Added allow-listed search fallback summaries for total results, filtered
  result count, selected index, selected category/source, and row
  category/source.
- Added allow-listed log fallback summaries for total entries, filtered entry
  count, active filter details, case-sensitivity, follow-tail, copy-prefix
  policy, selected index, and last key.
- Added `viewIndex` to row/cell fallback output so source indexes and visible
  view indexes are both narratable for filtered lists.
- Tightened domain guards so shared keys such as `source`, `selectedIndex`,
  and `severity` do not accidentally create unrelated task/context/trace/search
  fallback summaries.
- Preserved generic `severity ...` fallback text for non-status widgets while
  keeping app-status nodes on the richer `status ...` fallback path.
- Added core accessibility tests plus `SearchPanel`, `LogRegion`, approval
  regression, and proof-console Global Search/Indexed Logs coverage.
- Updated SAG/M2.8, data-virtualization, RDT docs, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep search/log fallback summaries domain-keyed and allow-listed. Totals,
  filter state, selected result context, and row positions are stable enough
  for adapters and debug artifacts; arbitrary metadata and generic shared keys
  are not.
- Keep generic severity wording stable. Widgets such as approvals and log rows
  use severity without being app-status surfaces.

Findings:
- `SearchPanel` and `LogRegion` already had the right semantic data. The
  missing piece was a safe projection that made that data visible to prompt
  fallback and debug capture without scraping terminal cells.
- Shared semantic-state keys are useful but easy to over-project. Domain
  summaries should require domain-specific markers before consuming generic
  helpers such as `source` or `selectedIndex`.
- The proof app continues to be the right pressure harness because it caught
  both production-widget projection gaps and an accessibility wording
  regression outside the changed widget family.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury/test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/search_panel_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/log_region_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze test/search_panel_test.dart test/log_region_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart test test/search_panel_test.dart`, `dart test test/log_region_test.dart`,
  and `dart test test/approval_prompt_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,

Next:
