  loses stable semantic facts in fallback/devtools output. Otherwise pivot to
  the next active Phase 2 hardening area: reviewed real-terminal capability
  matrix evidence, Windows-driver validation notes, or package-adoption assets
  when the user is ready to move beyond core implementation.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Active Probe Evidence For Capability Resolution

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.10 already had passive diagnosis, opt-in active probes, passive/active
  compatibility findings, matrix capture, review triage, timeout
  classification, PTY transport smoke coverage, JSON-output matrix hardening,
  and OS/Dart platform evidence.
- The remaining design question was whether confirmed active probe evidence
  should feed runtime capability summaries. The answer for this slice is yes,
  but only explicitly: active probes remain opt-in and default startup
  detection remains passive/env-derived.
- The existing capability resolver already accepted
  `additionalAvailableFeatures`, so the missing piece was a safe bridge from
  compatibility-confirmed probe evidence to that explicit resolver input.

Changes:
- Added `TerminalCompatibilityReport.confirmedAvailableFeatures`, covering
  features with `confirmed` or `activeConfirmed` compatibility status.
- Added `TerminalCompatibilityReport.activeConfirmedFeatures` for features the
  probe confirmed even though passive detection did not.
- Serialized both feature sets in compatibility JSON for diagnosis and matrix
  consumers.
- Added `TerminalCapabilityReport.toCapabilities()` and
  `TerminalDiagnosis.passiveCapabilities` so code using diagnosis evidence can
  pass the original passive capability summary to requirement resolution.
- Added `TerminalDiagnosis.confirmedAvailableFeatures` as the narrow bridge
  from opt-in probe comparison to
  `resolveCapabilityRequirement(additionalAvailableFeatures: ...)`.
- Added terminal diagnostics tests proving active-only Kitty graphics evidence
  can satisfy an `imageKitty` requirement while passive-only detection still
  degrades to half-block fallback.
- Updated M2.10, TCS docs, the terminal matrix checklist, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Active probe evidence must not change default app startup detection. It can
  feed capability resolution only after an app/tool explicitly collects or
  loads diagnosis evidence and passes the confirmed feature set.
- Keep passive and active evidence visible separately. Compatibility JSON now
  exposes confirmed feature sets, but the passive capability report remains the
  original env-derived summary.

Findings:
- The existing `additionalAvailableFeatures` hook was the right integration
  point. It avoids inventing a second resolver and keeps policy/unsafe feature
  handling in the existing capability requirement model.
- This moves M2.10 from pure evidence collection toward actionable terminal
  compatibility without making active probes part of normal app launch.
- The remaining M2.10 work is now mostly evidence quality: reviewed real
  terminal matrix entries and any platform-specific probes that have clear
  latency/security behavior.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/terminal/diagnostics.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1092 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue M2.10 with real terminal matrix evidence when an interactive
  terminal is available, or continue Phase 2 core hardening through small
  proof-driven slices. Keep default runtime startup passive until matrix
  evidence justifies any automatic capability promotion.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT MessageList Semantic Row Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `MessageList` already exposed protocol-neutral transcript semantics and
  selected-row copy, but rows could not be selected through semantic
  activation.
- Other production list surfaces such as search results, conversations,
  context items, and tables already let tests/adapters operate selection
  through semantic actions. Transcript automation should not have to scrape
  rendered cells or depend on keyboard events just to choose a message before
  copy/export.
- This is an ADW/RDT slice: improve protocol-neutral workflow semantics
  without adding ACP/provider/session callbacks.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every `MessageList` row.
- Routed message-row activation through the existing `MessageListController`
  selection path: activation disables follow-tail and sets `selectedIndex` to
  the activated row.
- Kept row copy selected-row oriented. After activation, the selected row
  exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path.
- Added `MessageList` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a
  message row and then exposes copy on that row.
- Added proof-console Transcript coverage proving app transcript messages can
  be semantically selected and that the parent message-list selected key
  updates.
- Updated ADW/RDT docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `MessageList` activation selects rows; it does not call app-specific message
  callbacks. Provider/session routing remains an app or adapter concern.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a message and copying a message
  are distinct actions, which keeps tests, adapters, and future accessibility
  bridges predictable.

Findings:
- `ListController.jumpToIndex` only scrolls; it intentionally does not change
  selection. Semantic activation therefore sets `selectedIndex` directly after
  turning off follow-tail.
- The proof app caught the visible-row detail: the test should activate a row
  present in the current semantic tree rather than assuming earlier transcript
  entries are visible.
- This strengthens the workflow claim that Fleury UIs can be operated through
  their semantic app graph, not only inspected.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/message_list.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/message_list_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/message_list.dart test/message_list_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/message_list_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across `README.md`,
  `docs`, `packages`, and `tool` returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1095 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue ADW/RDT semantic-action hardening where first-party workflow
  widgets expose concrete selection, copy, open/close, cancel, submit, or
  activation behavior. Keep provider-specific workflow semantics and ACP
  package work deferred.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT TaskGraph Semantic Row Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `TaskGraph` already exposed protocol-neutral plan/task semantics and
  selected-node copy, but non-selected task nodes could not be selected through
  semantic activation.
- Plan/task workflows need operable semantics for tests, debug tooling, and
  future adapters before those tools can copy or inspect a selected task.
- This is an ADW/RDT slice: strengthen workflow-widget semantics while keeping
  task execution, scheduling, and protocol-specific behavior out of
  `fleury_widgets`.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every `TaskGraph` task row.
- Routed task-node activation through the existing `TaskGraphController`
  selection path by setting `selectedIndex` to the activated row.
- Kept task-node copy selected-row oriented. After activation, the selected
  node exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path.
- Added `TaskGraph` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a task
  node, updates controller selection, and exposes copy on the selected node.
- Added proof-console Overview coverage proving a visible workflow-plan task
  can be selected semantically and that the parent task graph reports the
  updated selected task ID.
- Updated ADW/RDT docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `TaskGraph` activation selects nodes; it does not start, cancel, schedule, or
  execute tasks. Workflow behavior remains app-owned.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a task and copying a task are
  distinct actions, which keeps tests, adapters, and future accessibility
  bridges predictable.

Findings:
- The proof app caught the lazy-list contract again: row-level semantic action
  targets must be live in the rendered viewport. The proof coverage now
  activates a visible non-selected task row, while package-level widget
  coverage proves the controller selection path directly.
- `TaskGraphController.selectedIndex` was already the right source of truth for
  selection. No new row model or scheduler hook was needed.
- This continues the larger pattern that Fleury's semantic app graph should be
  both inspectable and operable for real workflow surfaces.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/task_graph.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/task_graph_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`

## 2026-06-01 - DVW/RDT CodeView Semantic Line Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `CodeView` already exposed aggregate code semantics, focus/navigate, selected
  line copy, whole-document copy, source-line classification, and sanitized
  source text, but non-selected visible source lines could not be selected
  through semantic activation.
- Source inspection is a core developer-tool workflow. Tests, debug tooling,
  and future adapters should be able to select a visible source line
  semantically before copying or inspecting selected-line state.
- This is a DVW/RDT slice: strengthen document-view operability without adding
  symbol navigation, language-server integration, or copy-on-activate behavior.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every visible `CodeView` source line.
- Routed source-line activation through the existing `CodeViewController`
  selection path: activation focuses the code view and sets `selectedIndex` to
  the activated line.
- Kept line copy selected-line oriented. After activation, the selected line
  exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path.
- Added `CodeView` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a
  source line, focuses the code view, updates aggregate selected state, and
  exposes copy on the selected line.
- Added proof-console Source coverage proving a visible source line can be
  semantically selected after safe source-copy behavior and that the parent code
  view reports the updated selected line number and line kind.
- Updated DVW/RDT docs, milestones/README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `CodeView` activation selects lines; it does not copy text, open symbols, or
  imply language-server navigation. Symbol-aware actions remain a future app or
  widget-layer concern.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a source line and copying a
  source line are distinct actions, matching the data/widget surfaces already
  hardened in this phase.

Findings:
- `CodeViewController.selectedIndex` was already the right source of truth for
  selection. The new action path only needed a small focus-plus-select helper.
- The proof app Source screen is useful pressure because it keeps the unsafe
  source-line copy assertion intact, then proves selection can move to a
  different declaration line through semantic activation.
- This continues the launch direction that Fleury document and data views
  should be semantically operable, not only inspectable.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/code_view.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/code_view_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,

- `git diff --check` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - DVW/RDT DiffView Semantic Line Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `DiffView` already exposed aggregate diff semantics, focus/navigate,
  selected-line copy, selected-hunk copy, file/hunk/line metadata, and sanitized
  diff text, but non-selected visible diff rows could not be selected through
  semantic activation.
- Diff inspection is a core developer-tool workflow. Tests, debug tooling, and
  future adapters should be able to select a visible diff row semantically
  before copying the selected line or containing hunk.
- This is a DVW/RDT slice: strengthen document-view operability without adding
  patch application, git operations, review-state mutation, or copy-on-activate
  behavior.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every visible `DiffView` row.
- Routed diff-row activation through the existing `DiffViewController`
  selection path: activation focuses the diff view and sets `selectedIndex` to
  the activated row.
- Kept diff copy selected-row oriented. After activation, the selected row
  exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path, honoring the
  configured line or hunk copy mode.
- Added `DiffView` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a diff
  row, focuses the diff view, updates aggregate selected state, and exposes
  copy on the selected row.
- Added proof-console Changes coverage proving a visible deletion row can be
  semantically selected after safe hunk-copy behavior and that the parent diff
  view reports the updated selected old-line state.
- Updated DVW/RDT docs, milestones/README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `DiffView` activation selects rows; it does not apply patches, open review
  state, mutate git state, or imply provider review behavior.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a diff row and copying a diff
  row or hunk are distinct actions, matching the document/data surfaces already
  hardened in this phase.

Findings:
- `DiffViewController.selectedIndex` was already the right source of truth for
  selection. The new action path only needed a small focus-plus-select helper.
- The proof app Changes screen is useful pressure because `PatchReview`
  composes `DiffView`: file activation remains patch-review selection, while
  diff-row activation stays inside the nested diff surface.
- This continues the launch direction that Fleury document and data views
  should be semantically operable, not only inspectable.

## 2026-06-01 - DVW/RDT LogRegion Semantic Row Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `LogRegion` already exposed aggregate log semantics, selected-row copy, safe
  export, filtering, and indexed search, but non-selected visible log rows could
  not be selected through semantic activation.
- Log/debug surfaces are a core data-heavy workflow. Tests, debug tooling, and
  future adapters should be able to select a visible log entry semantically
  before copying or inspecting selected-entry state.
- This is a DVW/RDT slice: strengthen data-widget operability without adding
  log processing, acknowledgement, transport, or protocol behavior.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every visible `LogRegion` row.
- Routed log-row activation through the existing `LogRegionController`
  selection path: activation leaves follow-tail mode and sets `selectedIndex`
  to the activated filtered view row.
- Kept row copy selected-row oriented. After activation, the selected row
  exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path.
- Added `LogRegion` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a
  visible row, disables follow-tail, updates aggregate selected state, and
  exposes copy on the selected row.
- Added proof-console Indexed Logs coverage proving a filtered visible log row
  can be semantically selected and that the parent log region reports the
  updated selected key and selected index.
- Updated DVW/RDT docs, milestones/README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `LogRegion` activation selects rows; it does not parse, execute,
  acknowledge, or mutate log data. Higher-level log workflows stay app-owned.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a log entry and copying a log
  entry are distinct actions, matching `MessageList` and `TaskGraph`.

Findings:
- The existing `LogRegionController` already had the right behavior seam:
  `followTail = false` plus `selectedIndex = viewIndex` describes deliberate
  user selection without touching source-entry identity.
- The proof app's Indexed Logs screen is the right pressure point because it
  proves activation against filtered, indexed, sanitized entries rather than a
  toy two-row list.
- This continues the larger semantic app graph direction: virtualized data
  widgets should expose live visible rows as operable semantic nodes while
  aggregate/export/search APIs continue to work from source data.

## 2026-06-01 - DVW/RDT MarkdownView Semantic Block Activation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `MarkdownView` already exposed aggregate document semantics, focus/navigate,
  selected-block copy, whole-document copy, visible-link fallback state, and
  sanitized Markdown text, but non-selected visible blocks could not be selected
  through semantic activation.
- Documentation, model-output, and help surfaces are core developer-tool
  workflows. Tests, debug tooling, and future adapters should be able to select
  a visible Markdown block semantically before copying the selected block.
- This is a DVW/RDT slice: strengthen document-view operability without adding
  link opening, rich document navigation, parser changes, or copy-on-activate
  behavior.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticAction.activate` to every visible `MarkdownView` block.
- Routed block activation through the existing `MarkdownViewController`
  selection path: activation focuses the Markdown view and sets `selectedIndex`
  to the activated block.
- Kept Markdown copy selected-block oriented. After activation, the selected
  block exposes the existing sanitized `SemanticAction.copy` path, honoring the
  configured block or document copy mode.
- Added `MarkdownView` widget coverage proving semantic activation selects a
  block, focuses the Markdown view, updates aggregate selected state, and
  exposes copy on the selected block.
- Added proof-console Docs coverage proving a visible Markdown block can be
  semantically selected after safe block-copy behavior and that the parent
  Markdown view reports the updated selected block state.
- Updated DVW/RDT docs, milestones/README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `MarkdownView` activation selects blocks; it does not open links, copy text,
  change parser behavior, or imply a richer document navigation model.
- Keep copy separate from activation. Selecting a Markdown block and copying a
  block or document are distinct actions, matching code, diff, log, message,
  and task surfaces already hardened in this phase.

Findings:
- `MarkdownViewController.selectedIndex` was already the right source of truth
  for block selection. The new action path only needed a focus-plus-select
  helper and row-level semantic action wiring.
- `MarkdownText` remains untouched as the lightweight inline renderer; this
  change is scoped to the document-inspection widget.
- The proof app Docs screen now pressures both sides of the Markdown contract:
  unsafe selected-block copy and semantic selection of a different visible
  block.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - DVW/RDT LogRegion Semantic Focus

Status: Complete

Context:
- The decision log and RDT/DVW docs already treated `LogRegion` as a stable
  focus/select/activate/copy surface, but current code only exposed aggregate
  copy plus row activation.
- Logs, terminal-output adapters, transcripts, and indexed output regions are
  core production widgets. Tests, debug tooling, prompt fallback, and future
  adapters should be able to focus the aggregate log region semantically before
  navigating or selecting rows.
- This is a DVW/RDT slice: close an overpromised semantic action gap by routing
  focus through the same list focus path used by keyboard interaction.

Changes:
- Added an owned `FocusNode` path to `LogRegion`, while still honoring an
  app-supplied focus node.
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.focus` and `SemanticAction.navigate` to the
  `SemanticRole.log` node.
- Routed aggregate focus/navigation actions to the backing list focus node.
- Updated visible row activation so activating a log row focuses the log region,
  leaves follow-tail mode, and selects the filtered view row.
- Added package tests for aggregate semantic focus, focused row activation, and
  `TerminalOutputRegion` row activation through the inherited `LogRegion`
  behavior.
- Added proof-console Indexed Logs coverage proving semantic focus on the
  aggregate log node before filtered row activation.
- Updated DVW/RDT/EWP docs, milestones/README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `LogRegion` owns a focus node when apps do not provide one. Aggregate focus
  should not depend on external focus wiring.
- Row activation stays selection-only plus focus. It does not parse, execute,
  acknowledge, or otherwise mutate log data.
- `TerminalOutputRegion` continues to be an adapter over `LogRegion`; it
  inherits focus and row activation rather than adding a second output-region
  interaction model.

Findings:
- The row activation implementation already selected the filtered view index
  and left follow-tail mode. The missing part was focus ownership plus aggregate
  semantic dispatch.
- `TerminalOutputRegion` tests are a good guardrail because they prove the
  process/output adapter benefits from `LogRegion` hardening without bespoke
  semantics.
- The earlier MarkdownView journal entry was accidentally inserted before the
  CodeView/DiffView/LogRegion entries; this pass moved it back to chronological
  order before adding the current entry.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT TraceTimeline Semantic Focus

Status: Complete

Context:
- `TraceTimeline` already exposed trace/timeline semantics, aggregate submit and
  copy, selected-event copy, and row activation, but the aggregate timeline node
  could not be focused semantically.
- Workflow/debug timelines are protocol-neutral developer-tool surfaces. Tests,
  debug tooling, prompt fallback, and future adapters should be able to focus a
  timeline before navigating or activating trace rows.
- This is an ADW/RDT slice: close a semantic-operability gap without turning
  `TraceTimeline` into a replay log, distributed tracing system, provider
  protocol model, or ACP fixture layer.

Changes:
- Added an owned `FocusNode` path to `TraceTimeline`, while still honoring an
  app-supplied focus node.
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.focus` and `SemanticAction.navigate` to the
  `SemanticRole.traceTimeline` node.
- Routed aggregate focus/navigation actions to the backing list focus node.
- Updated trace-row activation and row copy so row actions focus the timeline
  before updating controller selection or copying selected event text.
- Preserved selected trace identity across refreshed event lists so live
  proof-app timelines do not reset selection after app state changes.
- Added package coverage proving semantic focus, row activation, selected-trace
  identity preservation across refreshes, and selected-event copy.
- Added proof-console Diagnostics coverage proving aggregate semantic focus
  before selecting the diagnostics trace event.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `TraceTimeline` owns a focus node when apps do not provide one. Aggregate
  focus should be available for timelines in tests and future adapters without
  requiring app-level focus wiring.
- Trace event activation focuses and selects; it does not start work, replay
  events, open a provider trace, or mutate protocol state.
- Selected trace identity restoration defers across the next frame when the new
  selected event index is beyond the list controller's currently known item
  count. This keeps `ListController` clamping semantics intact and avoids
  widening the core list API for one widget's dynamic refresh path.

Findings:
- `TraceTimelineController.selectedIndex` and the existing `ListView` were
  sufficient for focus and activation. The only refresh wrinkle was that parent
  `didUpdateWidget` sees the new event list before child `ListView` updates its
  `itemCount`.
- The proof app exposed a useful dynamic-list issue: Diagnostics rebuilds a
  fresh trace-event list from current app state, so selected trace identity must
  be preserved by event ID instead of raw index whenever possible.
- Post-frame identity reconciliation is the narrowest correct fix for selected
  trace IDs that move beyond the old list length. Tests now model the
  render-then-pump boundary used by Fleury's frame scheduler.
- The proof app Diagnostics screen remains the right pressure point because it
  exercises a timeline built from live proof-app task/process/diagnostic state
  without making Fleury own those workflows.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/trace_timeline.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/trace_timeline_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/trace_timeline.dart test/trace_timeline_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/trace_timeline_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "diagnostics capture updates status and transcript state"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across the repo
  returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1095 local
  links resolve.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT ContextPanel Semantic Focus

Status: Complete

Context:
- `ContextPanel` already exposed context-pack and context-item semantics, item
  activation, selected-item copy, and proof-app Overview adoption, but the
  aggregate context panel node could not be focused semantically.
- Context packs are live app-owned data. The proof app rebuilds context items
  from current app state, so semantic selection should follow stable item IDs
  across refreshes rather than falling back to raw indices.
- This is an ADW/RDT/SAG slice: close another protocol-neutral workflow
  operability gap while keeping retrieval, pruning, billing, provider stores,
  and ACP content-block mappings out of Fleury core launch scope.

Changes:
- Added an owned `FocusNode` path to `ContextPanel`, while still honoring an
  app-supplied focus node.
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.focus` and `SemanticAction.navigate` to the
  `SemanticRole.contextPanel` node.
- Routed aggregate focus/navigation actions to the backing list focus node.
- Updated item activation and row copy so row actions focus the context panel
  before updating controller selection or copying selected item text.
- Preserved selected context identity across refreshed item lists, deferring
  selection restoration across a frame when the retained item moved beyond the
  list controller's currently known item count.
- Added package coverage proving semantic focus, row activation, selected-item
  identity preservation across refreshes, and selected-item copy.
- Added proof-console Overview coverage proving aggregate semantic focus before
  selecting a context item.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `ContextPanel` owns a focus node when apps do not provide one. Context-pack
  focus should be available to tests, prompt fallback, debug tooling, and
  future adapters without app-level focus wiring.
- Context item activation focuses and selects; it does not retrieve context,
  prune context, mutate provider state, or imply an ACP content-block action.
- Selected context identity restoration uses the same narrow post-frame pattern
  as `TraceTimeline`, preserving `ListController` clamping semantics instead of
  adding a broader deferred-selection API to core lists.

Findings:
- `ContextPanelController.selectedIndex` and the existing `ListView` were
  sufficient for focus and activation. The missing pieces were focus ownership,
  aggregate semantic dispatch, and ID-based refresh reconciliation.
- The prior implementation reset selection to the first item whenever the item
  list identity changed. That was too brittle for app-shaped context packs
  rebuilt from changing transcript/task/token state.
- The proof app Overview screen is a useful context-pack pressure point because
  it exercises model-context semantics without making Fleury own provider
  retrieval or ACP data models.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT PatchReview Semantic Focus

Status: Complete

Context:
- `PatchReview` already exposed patch/file semantics, file activation,
  selected-file copy, nested `DiffView` review, and proof-app Changes adoption,
  but the aggregate patch-review node could not be focused semantically.
- Patch reviews are live code-review surfaces that may refresh file status,
  stats, summaries, or ordering as review state changes. Semantic selection
  should follow stable file identity (`id ?? path`) across refreshes rather
  than resetting to the first file.
- This is an ADW/RDT/SAG slice: close another protocol-neutral workflow
  operability gap while keeping git operations, patch application, provider
  review APIs, merge policy, and ACP content-block mappings out of Fleury core
  launch scope.

Changes:
- Added an owned `FocusNode` path to `PatchReview`, while still honoring an
  app-supplied focus node.
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.focus` and `SemanticAction.navigate` to the
  `SemanticRole.patchReview` node.
- Routed aggregate focus/navigation actions to the backing file-list focus
  node.
- Updated file activation and file-row copy so row actions focus the patch
  review before updating controller selection, jumping the nested diff, or
  copying selected file-summary text.
- Preserved selected patch-file identity across refreshed file lists, deferring
  selection restoration across a frame when the retained file moved beyond the
  list controller's currently known item count.
- Added package coverage proving semantic focus, file activation, selected-file
  identity preservation across refreshes, and selected-file copy.
- Added proof-console Changes coverage proving aggregate semantic focus before
  the existing nested `DiffView` focus/copy workflow.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `PatchReview` owns a focus node when apps do not provide one. Patch review
  focus should be available to tests, prompt fallback, debug tooling, and future
  adapters without app-level focus wiring.
- Patch file activation focuses, selects, and jumps the nested `DiffView`; it
  does not apply a patch, approve/reject review state, open provider APIs, or
  imply git operations.
- Selected file restoration uses `PatchReviewFile.id ?? path` and the same
  narrow post-frame pattern as `TraceTimeline` and `ContextPanel`, preserving
  `ListController` clamping semantics instead of adding a broader
  deferred-selection API to core lists.

Findings:
- `PatchReviewController.selectedIndex`, `PatchReviewFile.displayId`, and the
  existing `ListView` were sufficient for focus and activation. The missing
  pieces were focus ownership, aggregate semantic dispatch, and ID/path-based
  refresh reconciliation.
- Keeping file-level activation as selection plus nested-diff jump preserves
  the boundary between `PatchReview` and `DiffView`: patch files are the review
  summary, while diff lines and hunks remain owned by `DiffView`.
- The proof app Changes screen is the right pressure point because it exercises
  patch/file semantics and nested diff-copy behavior without making Fleury own a
  git client or provider review transport.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/patch_review.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/patch_review_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.

  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/patch_review_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "changes screen proves DiffView semantics and safe hunk copy"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT MessageList Semantic Focus

Status: Complete

Context:
- `MessageList` already exposed message-list/message semantics, sanitized
  selected-message copy/export, row activation, and proof-app Transcript
  adoption, but the aggregate transcript node could not be focused
  semantically.
- Transcripts are live append-heavy surfaces. Selection preservation must not
  fight `followTail`; appending while following the tail should continue to
  advance to the newest message.
- This is an ADW/RDT/SAG slice: close another protocol-neutral workflow
  operability gap while keeping session stores, provider message protocols,
  ACP content blocks, and app-owned transcript orchestration out of Fleury
  core launch scope.

Changes:
- Added an owned `FocusNode` path to `MessageList`, while still honoring an
  app-supplied focus node.
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.focus` and `SemanticAction.navigate` to the
  `SemanticRole.messageList` node.
- Routed aggregate focus/navigation actions to the backing list focus node.
- Updated row activation, row copy, and list Enter selection so actions focus
  the transcript before selecting/copying through `MessageListController`.
- Preserved selected message identity across refreshed message lists by stable
  non-null message ID when `followTail` is disabled.
- Preserved live tail-follow append behavior: when `followTail` is enabled,
  `ListController.pinToBottom` remains authoritative and advances selection to
  the newest appended message.
- Added typed semantic helpers for `messageId` and `selectedMessageId`.
- Added package coverage proving semantic focus/navigation, row activation
  focus, selected-message identity preservation across refreshes, and
  tail-follow append preservation.
- Added proof-console Transcript coverage proving aggregate semantic focus
  before selecting a transcript message.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `MessageList` owns a focus node when apps do not provide one. Transcript
  focus should be available to tests, prompt fallback, debug tooling, and
  future adapters without requiring app-level focus wiring.
- Message activation focuses and selects; it does not call app-specific
  message callbacks, run provider commands, open session state, or imply an ACP
  content-block action.
- Selected message restoration uses stable IDs only. Messages without IDs fall
  back to index clamping rather than unsafe text/author/status matching.
- ID-based restoration only runs when `followTail` is disabled. Live append
  behavior keeps following the newest message through the existing
  `ListController.pinToBottom` path.

Findings:
- `MessageListController` already had the important transcript behavior:
  `followTail` maps to `ListController.pinToBottom`. The implementation needed
  to preserve that instead of treating all refreshes like static list
  reordering.
- The same post-frame reconciliation pattern used by `TraceTimeline`,
  `ContextPanel`, and `PatchReview` works for selected message IDs that move
  beyond the list controller's currently known item count.
- The proof app Transcript screen is a useful pressure point because composer
  submits and deterministic log bursts rebuild transcript entries while
  keeping transcript behavior protocol-neutral.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/message_list.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/message_list_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/message_list.dart test/message_list_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/message_list_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "composer submission and log burst update transcript"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT ConversationNavigator Semantic Navigation

Status: Complete

Context:
- `ConversationNavigator` already exposed conversation navigator/row
  semantics, query input, row activation, selected-conversation copy,
  accessibility state, and proof-app Transcript adoption.
- The aggregate navigator node exposed `focus` for the query input but not a
  semantic way to navigate into the result list, and refreshed conversation
  lists reset selection to the first row.
- Conversation/thread/session lists are live app-owned data. Selection should
  follow stable conversation IDs across status/message-count refreshes without
  making Fleury own provider sessions, app routes, persistence, ACP thread
  schemas, or transport state.

Changes:
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.navigate` to
  `SemanticRole.conversationNavigator`.
- Kept aggregate `focus` on the query input and routed aggregate `navigate` to
  the result list, falling back to the query input when no result rows exist.
- Updated row activation and row copy so semantic row actions focus the result
  list before selecting/copying through `ConversationNavigatorController`.
- Preserved selected conversation identity across refreshed conversation lists
  by stable conversation ID, deferring selection restoration across a frame
  when the retained row moved beyond the list controller's currently known
  item count.
- Preserved valid controller-provided initial selection on init/controller
  replacement instead of always resetting to the first row. Query text changes
  still reset to the first matching result.
- Added package coverage proving aggregate navigate, row activation focus,
  selected-conversation identity preservation across refreshes, selected
  conversation copy, and controller initial-selection preservation.
- Added proof-console Transcript coverage proving aggregate semantic navigate
  before selecting the Worker task conversation and preserving that selection
  across the transcript append refresh.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `ConversationNavigator` is a mixed query/list surface: aggregate `focus`
  should reach the query input, while aggregate `navigate` should reach the
  conversation result list.
- Conversation row activation focuses and selects; it does not open routes,
  load sessions, call provider APIs, mutate transport state, or imply an ACP
  thread action.
- Selected conversation restoration uses `ConversationEntry.id` and the same
  narrow post-frame pattern as other refreshed list-backed widgets, preserving
  `ListController` clamping semantics instead of adding a broader
  deferred-selection API to core lists.

Findings:
- `ConversationNavigatorController.selectedIndex`, required conversation IDs,
  and the existing query/list focus nodes were sufficient for semantic
  navigation and activation. The missing piece was preserving selected identity
  when app-owned conversation data refreshed.
- Focus/query behavior needs a slightly different contract than single-list
  widgets: users and adapters need query focus for typing, but result-list
  navigation for row operation.
- The proof app Transcript screen is the right pressure point because selecting
  a conversation appends a transcript event, which rebuilds the conversation
  list and previously risked resetting selection to the first thread.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/conversation_navigator.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/conversation_navigator_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/conversation_navigator.dart test/conversation_navigator_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/conversation_navigator_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "conversation navigator selects proof conversations"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across the repo
  returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1095 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue semantic-action hardening on the next protocol-neutral workflow
  surface with concrete controller-backed behavior.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW/RDT FileMentionPicker Semantic Navigation

Context:
- `FileMentionPicker` already exposed file mention picker/row semantics plus
  activation and copy, but it still behaved like a static picker from the
  semantic layer: aggregate actions did not distinguish query focus from result
  navigation, row actions did not consistently move focus to the result list,
  and refreshed result lists could reset the selected mention.
- The proof app Transcript composer is the pressure point because activating a
  file mention mutates composer state and can rebuild the picker from app-owned
  results.

Changes:
- Added aggregate `SemanticAction.navigate` to
  `SemanticRole.fileMentionPicker`.
- Kept aggregate `focus` on the query input and routed aggregate `navigate` to
  the result list, falling back to the query input when no result rows exist.
- Updated row activation and row copy so semantic row actions focus the result
  list before selecting/copying through `FileMentionPickerController`.
- Preserved selected mention identity across refreshed result lists by stable
  `FileMentionEntry.path`, deferring selection restoration across a frame when
  the retained row moved beyond the list controller's currently known item
  count.
- Preserved valid controller-provided initial selection on init/controller
  replacement instead of always resetting to the first result. Query text
  changes still reset to the first matching result.
- Added package coverage proving aggregate navigate, row activation focus,
  selected-mention identity preservation across refreshes, selected mention
  copy, and controller initial-selection preservation.
- Added proof-console Transcript coverage proving aggregate semantic navigate
  before inserting a file mention and preserving selected mention state after
  the composer log append.
- Updated SAG/ADW/RDT docs, README notes, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `FileMentionPicker` is a mixed query/results surface: aggregate `focus`
  should reach the query input, while aggregate `navigate` should reach the
  result list.
- File mention row activation focuses and selects; it does not index the
  filesystem, resolve language-server symbols, open files, mutate provider
  context, or imply an ACP content-block action.
- Selected mention restoration uses `FileMentionEntry.path` and the same
  narrow post-frame pattern as other refreshed list-backed widgets, preserving
  `ListController` clamping semantics instead of adding a broader
  deferred-selection API to core lists.

Findings:
- `FileMentionPickerController.selectedIndex`, stable entry paths, and the
  existing query/results focus nodes were sufficient for semantic navigation
  and activation. The missing piece was preserving selected identity when
  app-owned mention results refreshed.
- Query changes should still reset to the first matching row. That keeps typing
  behavior predictable while allowing non-query app refreshes to retain user
  selection.
- The proof app Transcript screen is a useful workflow test because file
  mention activation changes composer/log state and catches accidental
  selection resets across app rebuilds.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/file_mention_picker.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/file_mention_picker_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/file_mention_picker.dart test/file_mention_picker_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/file_mention_picker_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "file mention picker inserts composer mentions"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across the repo
  returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1095 local
  links resolve.

Next:
- Continue semantic-action hardening only where a proof workflow exposes a
  concrete controller-backed gap; otherwise pivot toward the next launch
  foundation milestone instead of expanding semantics for their own sake.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Terminal Matrix Audit Gate

Context:
- M2.10 had good capture mechanics: passive diagnosis, opt-in active probes,
  compatibility findings, review triage, platform evidence, and matrix entry
  generation with inherited stdio preserved.
- The remaining launch question is evidence quality. Real-terminal entries are
  still pending, and generated JSON should not be treated as reviewed
  compatibility evidence by default.
- This slice adds a readiness gate for collected entries rather than trying to
  manufacture missing terminal-emulator captures from the Codex environment.

Changes:
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit`.
- The audit scans `docs/implementation/terminal-matrix/` by default, accepts
  `--input=<dir>`, supports repeated `--target=<label>`, prints human output
  by default, and can print JSON with `--json`.
- The audit reports entry count, invalid-entry count, review-status counts,
  platform counts, per-target ready coverage, missing target labels, parsed
  entry summaries, and invalid-file details.
- Added `--strict`, which exits non-zero when any requested target lacks a
  `readyForReview` entry or invalid JSON files are present.
- Updated M2.10 docs, the terminal capability/security workstream, terminal
  matrix tracker, terminal-matrix README, and the decision log.
- Reordered the previous FileMentionPicker journal entry after
  ConversationNavigator so the journal tail reflects the actual implementation
  sequence.

Decisions:
- Matrix audit is a gate over evidence, not evidence itself. It can prove that
  captured files are present, parseable, and reviewed for target labels, but it
  cannot replace running the capture command inside macOS Terminal, iTerm2,
  Kitty, Ghostty, Alacritty, WezTerm, Windows Terminal, SSH, or tmux.
- Target matching is label-slug exact. That keeps launch evidence explicit and
  avoids silently counting an unrelated capture toward a named terminal.
- Strict mode requires ready reviewed entries, not merely any matching entry.
  Non-interactive and needs-attention captures remain useful diagnostics but do
  not satisfy target coverage.

Findings:
- The committed matrix directory currently has no JSON entries, so the default
  audit correctly reports zero entries and every default target as missing.
- A generated non-interactive `/tmp` smoke entry is parsed and counted, but it
  does not cover its requested target because `review.status` is
  `nonInteractive`. This is the desired behavior for launch evidence.
- The previous matrix tooling already preserved enough summary information for
  audits; the missing piece was a repo-local command that makes gaps visible
  without hand-reading individual JSON files.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --json` passed and reported
  zero committed entries with all default target labels missing.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --dry-run terminal-matrix-audit --strict --target=iterm2`
  passed and printed the planned scan/strict behavior.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix --label=codex-audit-smoke --output=/tmp/fleury-matrix-audit-smoke/codex-audit-smoke.json --no-probe`
  passed and generated a non-interactive smoke entry.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --input=/tmp/fleury-matrix-audit-smoke --target=codex-audit-smoke --json`
  passed and reported the smoke entry as matched but not covered because it was
  non-interactive.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --input=/tmp/fleury-matrix-audit-smoke --target=codex-audit-smoke --strict`
  exited with code 1 as expected; the validation wrapper asserted that failure
  mode successfully.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across the repo
  returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1095 local
  links resolve.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit` passed and reported zero
  committed entries, zero invalid entries, and 0/9 ready target labels.

Next:
- Continue M2.10 only with real-terminal captures or targeted matrix tooling
  gaps; otherwise use the audit output as the honest signal that compatibility
  evidence is pending.

## 2026-06-01 - ADW.1 Protocol-Neutral Workflow Snapshot

Context:
- ADW.1 still listed the reference workflow model as pending even though the
  underlying protocol-neutral records now exist: messages, tool calls,
  approvals, task graphs, model status, context items, file mentions,
  conversations, trace events, patch files, logs, and process output.
- M2.9 and M2.10 now need external terminal evidence, so the highest-value
  local implementation gap was tying the existing workflow records into a
  framework-shaped read model without importing ACP or provider runtime state.

Changes:
- Added `WorkflowSnapshot`, `WorkflowSummary`, and `WorkflowHealth` to
  `fleury_widgets`.
- The snapshot aggregates first-party workflow records and stores immutable
  lists/maps so apps can safely expose read-model summaries to semantics,
  tests, debug capture, or future adapters.
- Added lookup helpers for messages, tool calls, approvals, tasks, context
  items, file mentions, conversations, trace events, and patch files.
- Added summary health/count computation across active, attention, and failed
  workflow surfaces.
- Added safe semantic-state projection for workflow ID/title, health, counts,
  activity, failures, model, context, conversation, trace, patch, and log
  summary state.
- Exported the model from `package:fleury_widgets/fleury_widgets.dart`.
- Added typed `SemanticState` getters for workflow summary fields used by the
  proof app and future adapter tests.
- Wired the proof app Overview screen to construct a `WorkflowSnapshot` from
  its existing task, process, transcript, context, file mention, conversation,
  trace, patch, and model fixtures, then expose that summary through a
  semantic region.
- Added focused widget tests and proof-app Overview assertions.
- Updated ADW, SAG, RDT, README current actions, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- `WorkflowSnapshot` is a read model, not an agent runtime. It does not own
  routing, persistence, provider sessions, task execution, transcript storage,
  JSON-RPC, ACP content blocks, or adapter schemas.
- Health is intentionally coarse: failed beats needs-attention, which beats
  active, which beats idle. Apps can still expose richer domain state through
  their own records and widgets.
- The proof app should prove the aggregate model through semantic state on an
  existing workflow, not by adding a new visual dashboard just to display
  counts.

Findings:
- The first-party workflow record suite is now coherent enough to support an
  aggregate snapshot without inventing new provider concepts.
- The proof app already had all the needed source records; the missing piece
  was a stable summary object that could be tested and exposed semantically.
- Keeping the model in `fleury_widgets` preserves the core/widget boundary:
  core owns app, task, terminal, semantics, and runtime primitives; widgets own
  reusable developer-workflow data shapes and surfaces.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/workflow_snapshot.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/workflow_snapshot_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart` in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/workflow_snapshot.dart test/workflow_snapshot_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/workflow_snapshot_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "starts on overview and exposes app semantics"` in
  `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name hygiene scan for old pre-Fleury package names across the repo
  returned no matches.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Workflow Snapshot Accessibility Fallback

Context:
- ADW.1 added a protocol-neutral `WorkflowSnapshot` and the proof app Overview
  now exposes workflow health/count state through a semantic region.
- Before this slice, fallback/debug consumers could query raw workflow state
  but did not get a concise text-first summary of the aggregate workflow.
- This is a proof-driven M2.8 gap: the workflow region is real proof-app
  state, and the fallback layer should summarize it without dumping arbitrary
  metadata or adapter payloads.

Changes:
- Added typed `SemanticState` getters for workflow summary fields that were
  already emitted by `WorkflowSummary`: failed tool calls, model busy state,
  active/failed/warning trace counts, review issues, and log warning/error
  counts.
- Added an allow-listed workflow accessibility/fallback summary covering
  workflow ID/title/health, message/tool/task activity, model status, context
  items/tokens, file mentions, conversations, trace activity, patch files,
  review issues, and warning/error log counts.
- Added core accessibility coverage for workflow summary fallback output.
- Extended the proof app Overview test to assert that the new
  `Proof workflow snapshot` region produces useful fallback state from the
  same `WorkflowSnapshot`.
- Updated implementation README, M2.8 milestone notes, SAG, RDT, ADW, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep workflow fallback aggregate and allow-listed. It should expose durable
  health/activity/count facts, not raw transcript text, metadata maps,
  provider payloads, ACP schemas, or app-specific adapter details.
- Leave detailed row/item summaries owned by their widgets: messages, tool
  calls, context items, conversations, traces, patches, logs, and files still
  publish their own semantics when rendered.
- Do not broaden this into full replay or a workflow runtime. This slice only
  makes the existing read-model state usable in accessibility/debug fallback
  output.

Findings:
- The workflow snapshot already had enough state to describe proof-app health
  coherently; the missing bridge was the accessibility projection.
- The existing allow-listed accessibility pattern fit cleanly. The only code
  wrinkle was pluralization for irregular count labels, which the focused test
  caught (`log entries`).
- The proof app now proves workflow summary state at three layers: typed
  semantic state, text-first accessibility fallback, and the visible Overview
  flow built from the same records.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury/test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof-App Composer Completion Integration

Context:
- Core completion state and rendered `CompletionTextInput` already existed, and
  focused widget tests proved semantic menu activation.
- The proof app Transcript composer still used plain `TextInput`, so the
  integrated app did not pressure provider-backed completion in the workflow
  where developers will expect it: command-like composer input.
- M2.9 and M2.10 still need external terminal evidence, so the best local
  framework slice was to deepen the existing proof app around a launch pitch
  pillar: strong text/input with modern reactive semantics.

Changes:
- Replaced the proof app Transcript composer with `CompletionTextInput`.
- Added deterministic composer completions for slash commands
  (`/summarize`, `/diagnose`, `/run-task`) and file mentions from the existing
  proof file-mention fixture.
- Added a proof-app test that focuses the composer, types `/su`, inspects the
  semantic completion menu, activates `/summarize` by semantic node, verifies
  the range replacement, then submits the completed composer text.
- Updated README current actions, M1.2 milestone notes, TEE, RDT, ADW, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Use the first-party `CompletionTextInput` in the proof app instead of adding
  a custom composer completion path. This keeps completion behavior on the same
  controller/range-replacement/semantic-menu contract that framework users get.
- Keep completion suggestions app-owned. Fleury provides the text/completion
  mechanics; the proof app owns slash-command and mention suggestion data.
- Keep `FileMentionPicker` as a richer browsable reference picker. Inline
  mention completion complements it but does not replace path search, copy, or
  picker semantics.

Findings:
- The existing completion widget dropped into the app without changes to core
  editing. That is a good sign for the text/input architecture: provider
  policy, overlay rendering, semantic activation, and range replacement are
  already separated cleanly.
- Existing composer submission and file-mention flows stayed green after the
  replacement, so the change preserves the prior proof workflow while adding a
  more modern composer affordance.

Validation:

  passed.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof-App Composer History Integration

Context:
- The proof app composer now uses `CompletionTextInput`, but it still did not
  exercise `TextHistoryController`, even though command/submission history is a
  core part of the text-editing plan.
- Developer and agent composers need REPL-like recall for submitted prompts,
  while completion menus need to keep priority for Up/Down when suggestions
  are open.
- This was a local proof-app hardening slice that strengthens the text/input
  launch pillar without adding Dune, ACP, or new public API.

Changes:
- Added a `TextHistoryController` to the proof app Transcript composer and
  passed it through `CompletionTextInput`.
- Added a proof-app test that submits a note, verifies history semantic state,
  recalls the note with Up, restores the draft with Down, and proves Up still
  navigates the completion menu when slash-command suggestions are open.
- Updated README current actions, M1.2 milestone notes, TEE, RDT, ADW, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Use `TextHistoryController` directly instead of storing composer history in
  transcript state. History browsing is text-field behavior; transcript events
  remain submitted workflow output.
- Preserve completion priority over history navigation. When suggestions are
  open, Up/Down should move completion selection; history browsing applies only
  when the completion menu is closed.

Findings:
- `CompletionTextInput` already forwarded `historyController`, so the proof app
  could adopt submission history without framework code changes.
- The focused test confirms the desired composition: completion state and
  history state remain distinct, queryable through the text-field semantic
  state, and usable in the integrated app workflow.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.

  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof-App Stable Transcript Message Identity

Context:
- `MessageList` already preserves selection by stable message ID when users
  browse away from the tail.
- The proof app Transcript screen was still deriving message IDs from visible
  list indexes. That weakens the proof workflow because live transcript
  scrollback trims old rows and can shift index-derived semantic identities to
  different messages.
- The proof app also relied on widget-owned `MessageListController` state. That
  is acceptable for focused widget tests, but app-scale transcript selection
  should survive screen changes when the app owns the workflow state.

Changes:
- Added durable IDs to `TranscriptEvent` and assigned monotonically increasing
  IDs to appended transcript events.
- Updated proof-app workflow snapshot messages, global-search transcript
  results, and rendered transcript `MessageList` rows to use durable event
  IDs instead of current list indexes.
- Added an app-owned `MessageListController` and transcript focus node to the
  proof app, then passed them into the Transcript screen.
- Added a proof-app test that selects a streamed transcript message, switches
  screens, returns to Transcript, appends enough rows to trim old scrollback,
  and verifies the selected message identity and row index remain correct.
- Updated README current actions, ADW, RDT, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Transcript event identity belongs to the app data model, not the visible
  list index. Widget-level preservation can only be correct if app-supplied
  IDs remain stable across refresh and trimming.
- Keep tail-follow authoritative until the user browses. Once semantic
  activation selects a message, `MessageListController.followTail` becomes
  false and selected-message preservation should win across refreshes.
- Keep screen focus separate from selection preservation. Returning to the
  Transcript screen should preserve the selected message, but it should not
  imply the transcript list is focused unless the workflow requested focus.

Findings:
- The existing `MessageList` preservation behavior was sound; the integrated
  proof app needed stable IDs and an app-owned controller to exercise it under
  realistic append/trim pressure.
- This closes a real semantic-app-graph sharp edge: tests and future adapters
  can now trust transcript `messageId` as durable app meaning, not a transient
  row number.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "transcript selection preserves stable identity across appends"`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "composer submission|transcript selection|composer completions|composer history|file mention picker"`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/message_list_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "starts on overview and exposes app semantics"`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Chart Semantics And Proof Telemetry

Context:
- M2.8 is still open for proof-driven accessibility/fallback hardening.
- Gauge, Sparkline, BarChart, and LineChart already existed as useful terminal
  data-visualization widgets and appear in the dashboard benchmark story, but
  they were mostly visual-only from the semantic app graph's perspective.
- A leading TUI framework should make visual data testable, inspectable, and
  fallback-readable without asking tests or adapters to scrape braille/block
  glyphs from the terminal buffer.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticRole.chart` and typed chart state getters for chart type,
  series/point/bar/segment counts, min/max/latest values, x/y ranges,
  reference counts, interactivity, and cursor position.
- Added allow-listed chart fallback text to `AccessibilitySnapshot`.
- Wrapped Gauge, Sparkline, BarChart, and LineChart in semantic chart nodes.
- Added semantic focus/increment/decrement cursor actions to interactive
  LineChart.
- Added a proof-app Overview telemetry strip with Context pressure, Transcript
  trend, and Activity mix chart surfaces.
- Added focused widget tests for gauge, sparkline, bar-chart, and line-chart
  chart semantics/fallback, plus proof-app Overview assertions.
- Updated README current actions, M2.8 milestone notes, SAG, DVW, RDT, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat first-party terminal data visualizations as semantic chart surfaces,
  not decorative rendered text. Tests, debug capture, prompt fallback, and
  future adapters need data meaning from semantic state rather than cell
  scraping.
- Keep the first chart contract aggregate and typed. `SemanticRole.chart`
  covers the MVP data surface; per-series/per-point semantic nodes can follow
  only when a concrete workflow needs them.
- Let interactive LineChart expose semantic cursor movement because it already
  has a keyboard cursor model. Static charts remain read-only semantic
  summaries.

Findings:
- The chart widgets could adopt semantics without changing their render-object
  drawing paths, which keeps benchmark/render behavior stable while making the
  surfaces inspectable.
- Proof-app Overview now pressures dashboard-style visual data alongside
  workflow, context, task, and transcript semantics.

Validation:

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Task Event Trace Adapter

Status: Complete

Context:
- `TaskController` already records structured task events, and
  `TraceTimeline` already exposes protocol-neutral workflow/debug rows.
- The proof app Diagnostics screen only showed aggregate task/process trace
  rows, so live worker progress/output history was not inspectable through the
  same timeline UI.
- This slice connects effects to workflow diagnostics without expanding into
  full replay, provider protocols, ACP schemas, or a second output log.

Changes:
- Added reusable `TraceTimeline` adapters that convert `TaskEvent` histories
  into safe `TraceTimelineEntry` rows.
- Exported the adapters from `fleury_widgets`.
- Added typed semantic state for task event run ID, event sequence, event kind,
  output sequence/source/severity, and output safety flags.
- Added allow-listed accessibility fallback text for task-event rows.
- Wired proof-app Diagnostics to show recent fake-worker task events directly
  after the aggregate fake-task trace row, so progress/output history is visible
  in the integrated app flow.
- Added trace-timeline tests proving task-event conversion, recent-event
  limiting, sanitization, and the absence of raw output/error/stack text from
  trace exports and metadata.
- Added proof-app coverage proving live fake-worker progress/output events are
  queryable through trace-event semantics and fallback text.
- Updated README current actions, M2.8 milestone notes, EWP, RDT, ADW, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Task events should enter trace timelines as workflow metadata, not raw output
  payloads. Run IDs, sequence numbers, event kind, status, progress, source,
  severity, and safety flags are useful; raw output text, result values, error
  messages, and stack traces stay in output/debug channels with their own
  redaction policy.
- Proof-app Diagnostics should place live task-event rows near their aggregate
  task row. The timeline only renders visible rows semantically, so ordering
  matters for integrated test evidence.
- The adapter remains protocol-neutral and task-controller based. It does not
  create a replay artifact format, tracing backend, scheduler, or ACP event
  model.

Findings:
- The existing `TaskEvent` shape was already sufficient for useful diagnostics;
  the missing piece was a safe projection into the workflow UI.
- `TraceTimeline` metadata is merged into semantic state, so the adapter must
  sanitize or omit fields before creating entries rather than relying on row
  rendering to clean them later.
- The proof app exposed an important visible-row constraint: hidden list rows
  are not semantic nodes. Ordering recent task events immediately after the
  aggregate task row makes the integrated diagnostics path testable without
  scraping hidden state.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/trace_timeline.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/fleury_widgets.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/trace_timeline_test.dart packages/fleury_example_console/lib/fleury_example_console.dart packages/fleury_example_console/test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/trace_timeline.dart lib/fleury_widgets.dart test/trace_timeline_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_example_console` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart` in `packages/fleury`
  passed.
- `dart test test/trace_timeline_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets`
  passed.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart --name "commands navigate and start the fake task"` in

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Remaining Visualization Semantics

Status: Complete

Context:
- The prior chart pass made Gauge, Sparkline, BarChart, and LineChart
  semantic, but Histogram, Heatmap, CalendarHeatmap, and Digits still had
  weak or generic meaning despite being glyph-rendered data/value surfaces.
- For a leading TUI framework, terminal glyphs should be presentation, not the
  only inspectable truth. Tests, debug capture, prompt fallback, and future
  adapters need the underlying chart/value facts.
- This is a narrow M2.8/SAG/DVW/RDT slice. It does not add a visualization
  grammar, per-cell semantic nodes, chart interaction model, or public adapter
  protocol.

Changes:
- Added typed chart state fields for chart row count, column count, recorded
  point count, date range, and calendar week-start policy.
- Extended `AccessibilitySnapshot` chart fallback text to include those new
  chart facts.
- Wrapped `Heatmap` in `SemanticRole.chart`, exposing rows, columns, point
  count, and resolved value range.
- Wrapped `CalendarHeatmap` in `SemanticRole.chart`, exposing seven day rows,
  week columns, covered day count, recorded day count, value range, date range,
  and week-start policy.
- Wrapped `Histogram` in `SemanticRole.chart`, exposing bin count, source point
  count, in-range recorded point count, and resolved distribution range while
  hiding its implementation `BarChart` child semantics.
- Normalized `CalendarHeatmap` value keys to calendar days before rendering and
  semantics, matching the documented "time-of-day is ignored" contract.
- Wrapped `Digits` in `SemanticRole.text`, exposing the underlying text value
  so block-rendered clocks/counters are inspectable without scraping cells.
- Added focused widget tests for heatmap chart semantics/fallback, calendar
  heatmap chart semantics/fallback plus time-of-day bucketing, histogram chart
  semantics/fallback, empty histogram no-data semantics, and digits text
  semantics.
- Updated README current actions, M2.8 milestone notes, SAG, DVW, RDT, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Histograms, heatmaps, and calendar heatmaps are chart surfaces. They should use
  `SemanticRole.chart` and typed chart state instead of asking consumers to
  infer bins, rows, dates, and ranges from block glyphs.
- Digits is semantic text, not a chart. The large glyph display is visual
  styling over a textual value, so `SemanticRole.text` is the correct
  accessibility/debug shape.
- Do not add per-cell chart semantic nodes until a concrete workflow needs
  point-level inspection or interaction. Aggregate chart state is enough for
  the current launch contract.

Findings:
- CalendarHeatmap documentation already promised time-of-day bucketing, but
  the render path only matched exact date keys. Normalizing keys in the widget
  layer fixed rendering and kept semantic stats aligned with visible output.
- Existing chart fallback structure extended cleanly; no separate histogram or
  heatmap fallback path was needed.
- This closes the remaining obvious visual-data blind spots in first-party
  widgets without affecting render-object painting paths for normal data.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/heatmap.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/calendar_heatmap.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/histogram.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/digits.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/heatmap_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/calendar_heatmap_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/histogram_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/digits_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/heatmap.dart lib/src/calendar_heatmap.dart lib/src/digits.dart test/heatmap_test.dart test/calendar_heatmap_test.dart test/digits_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/heatmap_test.dart test/calendar_heatmap_test.dart test/digits_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/histogram.dart test/histogram_test.dart` in
