## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Canvas Semantic Seam

Context:
- The remaining visualization pass closed obvious chart/value blind spots, but
  the generic `Canvas` drawing primitive still had no way to expose app-authored
  meaning.
- Adding default semantics to every canvas would be wrong: generic drawings can
  sit under richer widgets, and wrapper widgets such as charts should own the
  durable semantic contract rather than forcing adapters to see both a chart and
  an implementation image.
- This is a narrow M2.8/SAG/RDT/DVW slice. It does not add per-pixel semantics,
  a generalized drawing-description language, canvas actions, or an
  accessibility adapter claim.

Changes:
- Added opt-in semantic fields to `Canvas`: `semanticRole`, `semanticLabel`,
  `semanticValue`, `semanticHint`, and `semanticState`.
- Kept plain `Canvas` semantic-silent by default. A canvas contributes a
  semantic node only when app code supplies a label/value/hint, custom state,
  or non-default role.
- Added resolved canvas marker and logical bounds to semantic state:
  `canvasMarker`, `canvasMinX`, `canvasMaxX`, `canvasMinY`, and `canvasMaxY`.
- Added typed `SemanticState` getters and text-first accessibility fallback
  output for canvas marker/bounds state.
- Added focused tests proving plain canvases add no semantic nodes, opt-in
  image semantics expose marker/bounds/value/hint, and custom canvases can opt
  into chart semantics while preserving chart-specific state.
- Updated M2.8 milestone notes, SAG, DVW, RDT, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep canvas semantics opt-in at the primitive boundary. Generic drawing
  surfaces can represent charts, images, diagrams, or decoration; app code must
  supply meaning before the semantic graph should include them.
- Default the opt-in role to `SemanticRole.image`, but allow
  `SemanticRole.chart` for custom plots that provide chart state.
- Always attach marker/bounds metadata once semantics are enabled. Those values
  are render-relevant, safe, and useful for tests/debug/fallback without
  exposing drawn cell content.

Findings:
- The existing `Semantics` wrapper and `SemanticState.merge` API were enough;
  no render-object semantic contributor was needed.
- This preserves the "presentation is not meaning" rule: first-party chart
  widgets expose chart facts, while app-authored canvas drawings can become
  inspectable when the app has domain meaning to publish.
- The first markdown local-link check command had an invalid Python lookbehind;
  rerunning with a simpler regex passed. No repository issue was found.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/canvas.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/canvas_test.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/semantics.dart lib/src/semantics/accessibility.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/canvas.dart test/canvas_test.dart` in
  `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/canvas_test.dart` in `packages/fleury_widgets` passed.
- `dart test test/semantics/accessibility_test.dart --name "chart state"` in
  `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Refined stale old-name/source-path scan returned no matches for the old
  framework name or old source path markers.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1103 local
  links.

Next:
- Continue with locally provable launch blockers. M2.8 now has a clean rule:
  add more semantic/fallback work only when a first-party widget or proof-app
  workflow exposes a concrete user-visible gap. Otherwise move to the next
  proofable launch-hardening slice rather than inventing semantics for
  decorative primitives.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Debug Inspector Compatibility Findings

Context:
- M2.10 already had active/passive terminal compatibility reports in diagnose
  JSON and matrix entries, but the in-app debug Tree tab only showed passive
  capability profile rows, fallback/warning counts, and unsupported features.
- For terminal correctness to be useful during app development, probe evidence
  should be visible in the running inspector, not only after exporting JSON.
- This is a narrow TCS/RDT/M2.10 slice. It does not collect real-terminal
  matrix entries, add new probes, or change runtime capability resolution.

Changes:
- Added an active-compatibility section to the debug Tree tab when
  `TerminalDiagnosis.compatibility` is available.
- The inspector now renders compatibility finding count, status-count rows for
  confirmed, active-confirmed, passive-unverified, unsupported, and
  inconclusive findings, and concise per-feature summaries with passive and
  active probe status.
- Expanded the debug shell terminal-diagnosis test to cover compatibility
  findings for a passively confirmed Kitty keyboard protocol and an
  active-confirmed Kitty graphics protocol.
- Updated README current actions, M2.10 milestone notes, TCS, RDT, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep active probes opt-in and matrix-reviewed. The inspector consumes the
  report when present; it does not start probes or promote probe results into
  startup behavior.
- Render counts plus per-feature summaries rather than dumping raw JSON. The
  debug panel needs fast human triage for mismatches and inconclusive evidence
  without becoming a full terminal-matrix viewer.

Findings:
- `TerminalDiagnosis` already carried the right compatibility model, so the
  debug panel only needed rendering and test coverage.
- This closes part of the earlier RDT inspector gap for richer capability
  drilldown. Remaining local debug gaps are policy-blocked feature summaries,
  sanitization counts, and redaction counts; real compatibility claims still
  require reviewed terminal matrix entries.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed after adding required braces around the new
  summary-row branch.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart --name "terminal diagnosis"` in
  `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Refined stale old-name/source-path scan returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1103 local
  links.

Next:
- Continue with locally provable launch hardening. Good candidates are the
  remaining debug-inspector safety summaries, off-main worker/isolate
  boundaries if a concrete indexed workload needs them, or concrete
  proof-app/widget gaps. Do not treat M2.10 real-terminal compatibility as
  complete until reviewed real-terminal matrix entries exist.

## 2026-06-01 - RDT/TCS Debug Inspector Safety Summary

Context:
- The debug Tree tab could show selected-node state and debug capture already
  serialized redacted/sanitized metadata, but app-scale safety status required
  manually selecting individual semantic nodes.
- Existing semantic state already marks redacted values, clipboard redaction,
  sanitized output, truncated output, and original output lengths across logs,
  process output, code/diff/markdown/json rows, traces, messages, and forms.
- This is a narrow RDT/TCS inspector slice. It does not add new public semantic
  keys, change sanitizer behavior, or expand debug capture schema.

Changes:
- Added a Tree-tab `safety state` section that aggregates current semantic
  graph signals:
  redacted node count, sanitized-output node count, truncated-output node
  count, and largest original output length.
- Counts are node-level and combine generic output state with task-output
  metadata while avoiding double-counting a node that has both generic and
  task-specific output fields.
- Expanded debug shell tests to assert redacted-node summaries and aggregated
  sanitized/truncated output summaries alongside existing task/capability
  semantics.
- Updated README current actions, M2.10 milestone notes, TCS, RDT, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Aggregate from existing semantic state instead of adding a separate safety
  model. The semantic graph is already the durable meaning layer for tests,
  debug capture, and future adapters.
- Keep the summary small and count-based. The debug panel should answer
  "is unsafe-content handling active here?" without leaking raw payloads or
  becoming a sanitizer report viewer.

Findings:
- Redaction and output-safety state was already broad across first-party
  widgets, so the inspector gap was visibility rather than missing metadata.
- This closes the local RDT gap for sanitization/redaction counts. Remaining
  debug-inspector capability work is richer policy-blocked feature drilldown;
  real terminal compatibility still depends on reviewed matrix entries.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed.

- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1103 local

## 2026-06-01 - RDT/TCS Debug Inspector Capability Resolution Drilldown

Context:
- The previous inspector safety slice left one local debug gap: policy-blocked
  and unsafe capability outcomes were visible only on individual semantic
  nodes, even though the capability resolver already publishes structured
  state.
- For launch, developers need to know whether a running app has degraded,
  policy-blocked, unsupported, unsafe, or required-blocked terminal features
  without hand-inspecting every node.
- This is a narrow RDT/TCS/M2.10 slice. It does not start active probes, change
  capability resolution, add a policy engine, or collect real-terminal matrix
  evidence.

Changes:
- Expanded the debug Tree-tab capabilities section with aggregate semantic
  capability-resolution counts: available, degraded, policy blocked,
  unsupported, unsafe, and required blocked.
- Added a capability-attention subsection that lists the first blocked,
  unsupported, or unsafe feature nodes with feature, requirement, resolution,
  and fallback context.
- Counted generic terminal capability resolutions and clipboard capability
  resolutions from existing `SemanticState` keys.
- Expanded the debug shell capability test to cover degraded image fallback,
  disabled-by-policy clipboard, unsafe required image output, unsupported
  optional mouse capability, and required-blocked counting.
- Updated README current actions, M2.10 milestone notes, TCS, RDT, and the
  decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep this as inspector aggregation over semantic state. Widgets/services
  still own capability requirements through `CapabilityResolution`; the debug
  panel only summarizes what the app has already published.
- Treat unsupported/unsafe/policy-blocked rows as attention items rather than
  assuming every item is fatal. Only required non-available terminal
  capability resolutions increment the required-blocked count.

Findings:
- Clipboard capability semantics do not always carry a capability requirement,
  so the required-blocked count intentionally applies only to normal terminal
  capability resolutions that publish both requirement and resolution.
- This closes the local RDT/TCS inspector gap called out in the previous entry.
  M2.10 remains incomplete until reviewed real-terminal matrix entries exist.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/debug/debug_panel.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  in `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart --name "task and capability"` in
  `packages/fleury` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Refined stale old-name/source-path scan returned no matches.
- Markdown local-link sanity check passed: 46 markdown files and 1103 local
  links.

Next:
- Continue with locally provable launch hardening only when a concrete
  benchmark, proof-app workflow, or tracker gap justifies it. M2.10 still
  needs reviewed real-terminal matrix evidence before launch compatibility can
  be claimed complete.

## 2026-06-01 - RRE Conservative Layout Dirtiness

Status: Complete

Context:
- The render-engine workstream still called out dirty layout/paint propagation
  as the remaining top-tier performance gap.
- The existing base render-object protocol always ran `performLayout` when a
  parent called `layout`, while repaint boundaries only cached paint.
- A general layout cache could become incorrect if existing layout-affecting
  setters only marked paint dirty, so this slice needed a conservative
  correctness-first shape.

Changes:
- Added render-object layout dirtiness to `RenderObject`: same-constraint clean
  layout calls return the cached size, constraint changes still force layout,
  and child layout dirtiness bubbles up to parents.
- Added public `markNeedsLayout()` and debug-only `layout:` dirty-source
  collection through `DebugInvalidations`.
- Kept `markNeedsPaint()` compatibility-safe by also marking layout dirty for
  now, preserving correctness while core and widget-package setters are
  audited.
- Updated `RenderObjectElement.update` and core render-object setters to use
  layout dirtiness where size, child constraints, offsets, or scroll/layout
  state can change.
- Added focused render-object tests covering same-constraint layout caching,
  constraint-change relayout, child-to-parent layout bubbling, conservative
  paint-to-layout invalidation, and layout dirty-source collection.
- Updated RRE/RDT workstreams, M1.10 milestone notes, the roadmap, README
  current actions, and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat this as a first render-engine foundation, not the final optimization
  pass. The framework now has layout dirty propagation and skip capability,
  but paint-only invalidation remains conservative until setter coverage and
  benchmarks justify a sharper split.

Findings:
- `RenderText` already had an internal wrap cache, but the base layout cache
  needed explicit layout invalidation from text/wrap/profile changes to avoid
  stale direct render-object tests.
- Many widget-package render islands already mark paint dirty from setters;
  the conservative paint-to-layout bridge keeps those correct while preserving
  room for a later audit.
- Repaint-boundary documentation had become stale: boundaries still cache
  paint, but layout is no longer necessarily full-tree work under unchanged
  constraints.

Validation:
- `dart format ...` over changed core render/debug/widget/test files passed.
- `dart analyze ...` over changed core render/debug/widget/test files passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_object_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_text_test.dart test/rendering/render_layout_test.dart test/rendering/render_flex_test.dart test/rendering/render_stack_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/repaint_boundary_test.dart test/widgets/align_test.dart test/widgets/text_input_test.dart test/widgets/text_area_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart --name "debug frame"` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- Keep this layout-dirtiness foundation under scenario benchmark pressure.
  The next render-engine work should either add benchmark evidence that common
  proof-app updates now skip meaningful layout work, or audit setters enough to
  split true paint-only invalidation from layout-affecting invalidation.

## 2026-06-01 - RRE Layout Dirtiness Benchmark Evidence

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous slice added conservative layout dirtiness and layout caching,
  but the tracker explicitly needed benchmark evidence that the mechanism
  avoids real work.
- `SB.1` was too small to prove subtree wins: the entire tiny counter tree
  relayouts on update.
- `SB.4` was already reserved by the widgets LogRegion scenario, so the new
  core layout scenario needed a distinct ID.

Changes:
- Added `RenderLayoutFrameStats` and `RenderLayoutDebugStats` to collect
  performed versus same-constraint skipped layout calls for a single frame.
- Wired layout stats into `runTui` debug `FrameEvent`s, DebugPanel
  Live/Rebuilds rows, and debug-capture frame JSON.
- Exported layout stats through `fleury_test` so tests and scenario benchmarks
  can opt into frame-level layout measurement without making it a production
  app API.
- Added `SB.12 Layout Dirtiness Cache` to the core scenario benchmark runner.
  The fixture mounts a static pane beside a changing counter pane, invokes a
  counter command, then renders an idle frame.
- Saved the first 20-iteration SB.12 baseline at
  `packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`.
- Updated benchmark README, scenario lab, RRE/RDT workstreams, milestones,
  peer scorecard, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep layout stats as opt-in frame diagnostics. Normal frames pay only a
  branch; debug frames and scenario benchmarks enable collection when they need
  evidence.
- Treat a skipped render object as a skipped subtree root. SB.12's update frame
  skips one static-pane subtree while the idle frame skips the root layout.

Findings:
- The original SB.12 baseline recorded update-frame static-subtree skips and
  idle-frame root skips. It was later superseded by the paint-only refreshed
  baseline in the following journal entry.
- Layout skip counts are call counts, not descendant estimates. A single
  skipped static pane can avoid many descendant layout calls, so docs should
  describe the skipped subtree rather than overstating the numeric count.
- The debug Rebuilds tab needed a slightly taller focused test viewport after
  adding the Layouts row; otherwise the recent-frame assertion scrolled out of
  the rendered debug panel.

Validation:
- `dart format ...` over changed layout/debug/runtime/test/benchmark files
  passed.
- `dart analyze ...` over changed layout/debug/runtime/test/benchmark files
  passed.
- `dart test test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart --name "repaint boundary cache metrics"`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart --name "rebuilds tab renders frame reason"`
  passed.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart --name "capture snapshot serializes"`
  passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_object_test.dart` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=1 --iterations=2 --json`
  passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`
  passed and saved the baseline.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- The next render-engine slice should audit true paint-only setters enough to
  stop invalidating layout for style/visibility-only changes, then rerun SB.12
  and proof-app scenarios to see whether update-frame performed layout drops
  further without losing correctness.

## 2026-06-01 - RRE Paint-Only Invalidation Split

Status: Complete

Context:
- The previous SB.12 baseline proved same-constraint layout caching, but
  `markNeedsPaint` still conservatively dirtied layout for every paint change.
- The next RRE step was to audit enough paint-only setters to prove visual
  updates can repaint without relayout while preserving the conservative path
  for unaudited setters.

Changes:
- Added protected `RenderObject.markNeedsPaintOnly()` for audited visual-only
  mutations while keeping `markNeedsPaint()` as the compatibility-safe
  paint-plus-layout invalidation path.
- Moved core paint-only setters onto the new path for text style, overflow,
  alignment, border/color styling, cursor visibility/style, paint-time
  visibility, focus/anchor/pointer bounds, navigator paint occlusion, cell
  effects, scrollbars, and filled boxes.
- Removed the element-layer unconditional `markNeedsLayout()` after
  `RenderObjectWidget.updateRenderObject`; render-object setters now own
  invalidation.
- Guarded multi-child render-object sync so unchanged render-child identity and
  order does not call `replaceAllChildren()` and dirty layout.
- Kept `ListView` and lazy-list render objects explicitly layout-dirty on
  widget update because their mutable controller selection/jump state is read
  during layout.
- Extended SB.12 with a paint-only counter-style toggle and refreshed the
  saved baseline at
  `packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`.

Decisions:
- `markNeedsPaintOnly()` is protected and opt-in. Existing unaudited setters
  remain safe through `markNeedsPaint()` until scenario pressure justifies
  moving them.
- Render-object setters, not `RenderObjectElement.update`, are the source of
  truth for invalidation. This is required for Flutter-style retained updates
  to avoid relayout when only visual state changes.
- Multi-child reconciliation should not imply layout dirtiness when the render
  child list is already identical. Child layout dirtiness still bubbles from
  the child that changed.

Findings:
- The first paint-only SB.12 attempt still relaid out because
  `MultiChildRenderObjectElement` called `replaceAllChildren()` on every
  rebuild, and common multi-child render objects mark layout from that method.
  Adding the identity/order guard was necessary for paint-only updates inside
  normal `Column`/`Row` rebuilds.
- List views are an intentional exception: controller selection, visible-range
  jumps, and lazy mounted-child churn are layout inputs even when the
  controller object identity is unchanged. Focused list/scrollbar tests caught
  this and the render objects now mark layout explicitly on update.
- The refreshed 20-iteration SB.12 baseline records command-to-frame p95
  662 us, idle-frame p95 304 us, paint-only-frame p95 562 us, update-frame
  layout p95 7 performed / 2 skipped, paint-only-frame layout p95 0 performed /
  1 skipped, and idle-frame layout p95 0 performed / 1 skipped.

Validation:
- `dart analyze ...` over changed framework, render, widget, test, and
  benchmark files passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_object_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_text_test.dart` passed, including direct,
  widget-level, and multi-child paint-only layout-cache coverage.
- `dart test test/widgets/reparenting_test.dart test/widgets/global_key_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/list_view_test.dart test/widgets/scrollbar_test.dart`
  passed after restoring explicit mutable-controller relayout.
- Focused text input, text area, navigator, repaint-boundary, and runtime
  repaint-boundary tests passed during the slice.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=1 --iterations=2 --json`
  passed after the multi-child sync fix.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`
  passed and refreshed the saved baseline.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- Continue the widget-package setter audit only where a concrete benchmark or
  proof-app workflow shows layout work from visual-only updates. Good
  candidates are chart/dashboard widgets under SB.6 and image/markdown style
  fallbacks, but keep each move evidence-driven.

## 2026-06-01 - RRE Dashboard Paint-Only Widget Audit

Status: Complete

Context:
- SB.12 proved the core paint-only invalidation path, but launch-facing
  dashboard widgets still needed a scenario-shaped audit.
- `SB.6 Dashboard Update Pressure` is the right first widget-package pressure
  test because it updates many progress, gauge, sparkline, and chart surfaces
  without changing the surrounding app structure.

Changes:
- Added paint-only invalidation to `ProgressBar` value/style updates. This also
  fixed a correctness issue exposed by the previous core split: the widget had
  been relying on element-level relayout after updates, and its render setters
  no longer invalidated anything after that compatibility path was removed.
- Audited `Gauge` setters so value/style changes repaint without relayout while
  label and percentage-chrome changes still mark layout. Gauge intrinsic width
  now reserves the max percentage suffix so value-only changes do not alter
  layout requirements.
- Audited `Sparkline` and `BarChart` setters so same-length data/bar updates
  are paint-only, while data-length, bar-count, width, gap, label, value, and
  legend/chrome changes remain layout-affecting.
- Extended `SB.6` to record first-frame and update-frame layout stats, plus
  per-update performed/skipped layout distributions.
- Refreshed the saved dashboard baseline at
  `packages/fleury_widgets/benchmark/results/phase2-dashboard-update-2026-06-01.json`.

Decisions:
- Keep widget-package paint-only migration scenario-led. Moving every visual
  setter at once would obscure which changes actually matter and risks
  misclassifying layout-affecting widget chrome.
- Treat stable item count/series length as the boundary for chart paint-only
  updates. Data shape changes remain layout-affecting because intrinsic and
  viewport assumptions can change.

Findings:
- The refreshed 20-iteration SB.6 baseline records update-total p95 267 us,
  update-frame p95 120 us, update-pump p95 97 us, semantic-query p95 439 us,
  update-frame layout p95 45 performed / 29 skipped, and zero unsafe frames
  over 400 dashboard ticks and 23 surfaces.
- Dashboard updates still perform layout in parts of the tree because several
  surfaces legitimately change semantic/widget wrappers, counters, and layout
  chrome. The useful win here is not zero layout for the whole dashboard; it is
  that audited stable visual surfaces now avoid relayout and the benchmark
  reports the remaining pressure explicitly.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury_widgets/test/bar_chart_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/progress_bar_test.dart test/gauge_test.dart test/sparkline_test.dart test/bar_chart_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/progress_bar.dart lib/src/gauge.dart lib/src/sparkline.dart lib/src/bar_chart.dart test/progress_bar_test.dart test/gauge_test.dart test/sparkline_test.dart test/bar_chart_test.dart benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.6 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-dashboard-update-2026-06-01.json`
  passed and refreshed the saved baseline.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Legacy name/path scan passed outside generated benchmark results.
- Local markdown link check passed: 37 files and 1106 local links resolve.

Next:
- Continue RRE work only where scenario evidence points to visible launch
  value; likely next candidates are resize/overlay regressions or a targeted
  widget-surface audit if SB.6/SB.10 starts showing layout pressure from a
  specific component.

## 2026-06-01 - Terminal Matrix Audit Label Matching

Status: Complete

Context:
- M2.10 is waiting on reviewed real-terminal matrix captures, but the audit
  gate had a practical collection problem: it only counted exact target labels.
- The matrix docs already encouraged labels like `tmux-kitty`; with exact-only
  matching, a valid tmux context capture would still leave the default `tmux`
  launch target marked missing.

Changes:
- Updated `terminal-matrix-audit` target matching so clean terminal targets
  accept exact labels and target-prefixed labels such as `iterm2-3-5` or
  `windows-terminal-powershell`.
- Added context-aware matching for `tmux` and `ssh` labels so captures such as
  `tmux-kitty` and `ssh-iterm2` satisfy those context targets.
- Kept context-prefixed labels from satisfying clean terminal targets:
  `tmux-kitty` covers `tmux`, not `kitty`.
- Added `matchKind` to matched audit entries and the human audit summary so
  reviewers can see whether coverage came from an exact label, target prefix,
  or context token.
- Updated terminal compatibility docs, the terminal-matrix README, TCS
  workstream, milestones, implementation README, and decision log with the
  label convention.

Decisions:
- Prefer target-first clean labels and context-first tmux/SSH labels. This
  keeps matrix collection flexible enough for version/profile suffixes without
  letting multiplexed or remote captures stand in for clean terminal coverage.
- Keep audit matching deterministic and string-based. Real-terminal review
  still depends on `review.status`, platform evidence, probe status, and human
  matrix review rather than inferred terminal identity alone.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed.
- Synthetic audit fixture with `iterm2-3-5` and `tmux-kitty` confirmed
  `iterm2` and `tmux` coverage while leaving `kitty` missing.
- Synthetic strict audit passed for `iterm2`/`tmux` targets and failed for the
  intentionally missing `kitty` target.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit` passed against the current
  matrix directory and still reports zero collected entries, 0/9 ready targets.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Legacy name/path scan passed outside generated benchmark results.
- Local markdown link check passed: 37 files and 1106 local links resolve.

Next:
- M2.10 still needs real reviewed matrix entries from actual terminals. Do not
  treat the synthetic audit fixtures or Codex non-interactive captures as
  compatibility evidence.

## 2026-06-01 - Terminal Matrix Diagnostic Summary

Status: Complete

Context:
- The matrix wrapper already exposed platform, terminal, capability, active
  probe, and compatibility summaries, but reviewers still had to inspect raw
  diagnosis JSON to see fallbacks, warnings, or unsupported features.
- For launch compatibility claims, an entry being interactive and
  `readyForReview` is not enough; expected degradation should be visible in
  the compact review path too.

Changes:
- Added `summary.diagnostics` to terminal matrix entries with fallback,
  warning, and unsupported-feature counts plus fallback/warning codes and
  unsupported feature names.
- Added the same diagnostic fields to terminal-matrix audit entries and a
  compact aggregate `Diagnostics:` line in human audit output.
- Updated terminal compatibility docs, the terminal-matrix README, TCS
  workstream, milestones, implementation README, and decision log to describe
  the diagnostic summary.

Decisions:
- Keep diagnostic summaries compact and allow-listed. The matrix wrapper should
  expose counts and stable codes, not duplicate every full diagnostic message
  or terminal payload.
- Do not turn fallback/warning counts into automatic audit failures. Some
  reviewed launch entries will legitimately have expected degradation; the
  point is to make that visible before human review.

Validation:

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit` passed against the current

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Legacy name/path scan passed outside generated benchmark results.
- Local markdown link check passed: 37 files and 1106 local links resolve.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.8 Process Task Accessibility Fallback

Status: Complete

Context:
- The local launch-blocker pass moved back from terminal-matrix tooling to
  proof-driven semantic/fallback gaps.
- `ProcessPanel` already published command display, exit code, process success,
  output safety, and cancelability through task semantics, but text-first
  accessibility fallback only described generic task/output counts.
- Process workflows are a real proof-app and developer-tool surface, so prompt
  fallback and debug artifacts should not need rendered-cell scraping to
  understand whether a process ran, failed, or can be canceled.

Changes:
- Extended task accessibility fallback to include allow-listed process facts:
  command display, exit code, success/failure, and `can cancel`.
- Added core accessibility coverage for process task state on generic task
  semantics.
- Added `ProcessPanel` widget coverage proving a completed process panel
  exposes the same process facts in text-first fallback.
- Updated M2.8/SAG/EWP/RDT docs and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep process fallback evidence on the generic task semantic path. Process
  screens are task workflows with command/result metadata, not a separate
  semantic role or accessibility model.
- Keep raw output out of task fallback. Output content remains in `LogRegion`
  and task-output safety metadata; fallback only projects command/result facts
  that the widget already exposes semantically.
- Close M2.8/SAG.5 for the MVP after this slice. Further role-specific
  fallback summaries should be opened as proof-driven hardening, not as a
  perpetual blocker, and native OS accessibility claims still require adapter
  evidence.

Validation:

- Marked ADW.4 complete for the MVP: the first workflow widget set is present

## 2026-06-01 - Semantic Inspection Snapshot V0

Status: Complete

Context:
- The semantic graph, accessibility fallback, debug capture, and proof-app
  tests are now substantial, but automation/agent inspection still depended on
  either live `SemanticTree` objects or debug-capture-specific JSON.
- M3.6 needs a bounded first protocol slice before any browser/devtools bridge
  or external adapter can consume Fleury semantics without scraping rendered
  terminal cells.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticInspectionSnapshot`, `SemanticInspectionNode`, and
  `SemanticTreeInspection` in `src/semantics/inspection.dart`.
- The inspection snapshot emits schema-versioned JSON with node count, role
  counts, action count, focused node id, a redacted/sanitized node tree,
  structured state, and query helpers.
- Debug capture now serializes semantic trees through the same inspection
  snapshot instead of maintaining a separate private semantic serializer.
- `DebugCaptureArtifact` now exposes semantic role-count and action-count
  helpers over that inspection summary.
- `FleuryTester` now exposes `semanticInspectionSnapshot()` and
  `semanticInspectionJson()` so tests can assert adapter-facing semantic JSON
  without creating debug-capture artifacts.
- Exported the inspection API through `fleury_core.dart`, `fleury.dart`, and
  `fleury_test.dart`.
- Added semantic inspection tests and expanded debug-capture tests to assert
  the new semantic summary fields.
- Updated the SAG/RDT workstreams, Phase 3 M3.6 notes, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep this as a v0 inspection protocol, not a frozen external automation
  standard. It is stable enough for local tests and capture artifacts, but a
  real adapter/devtools consumer should drive field compatibility policy.

  depending on role/label uniqueness.

  tests and future adapters to target an inspected node id directly.
- Expanded semantic inspection tests to inspect a command node, assert the
  inspection id lookup, and invoke `SemanticAction.activate` by that id.
- Added a core semantic tree test for `SemanticTree.nodeById`.
- Updated SAG/RDT workstreams and the decision log.

## 2026-06-01 - Remote Protocol Boundary Hardening

Status: Complete

Context:
- M3.5 remote app/session work was open, but the current code already has a
  meaningful remote foundation: `RemoteTerminalDriver`, Unix-socket shell
  transport, browser `serve` bridge, spawn-mode isolated sessions, and focused
  remote tests.
- The weak local hardening point was the wire-protocol boundary. Malformed
  control frames or oversized payload declarations could escape as raw parser
  exceptions, and a pre-INIT transport error could leave
  `RemoteTerminalDriver.enter()` waiting forever.

Changes:
- Added `defaultMaxRemoteFramePayloadLength` and `RemoteProtocolException` to
  the remote protocol layer.
- Added payload-size checks in `FrameDecoder` before waiting for the declared
  payload body.
- Converted malformed INIT/RESIZE size fields and invalid UTF-8 control
  payloads into typed `RemoteProtocolException`s.
- Updated `UnixSocketFrameTransport` to forward decoder errors through the
  incoming stream and close the transport instead of letting decoder failures
  escape the socket callback.
- Updated `RemoteTerminalDriver` so transport errors before INIT fail the
  handshake future instead of hanging startup.
- Added remote protocol and remote driver tests for oversized frames,
  malformed resize payloads, invalid UTF-8 control payloads, and pre-INIT
  transport errors.
- Marked M3.5 as started with current evidence and remaining completion
  criteria.

Decisions:
- Treat malformed remote protocol frames as typed protocol errors at the
  transport boundary. Remote shell/browser sessions are a framework capability,
  so bad wire data should fail predictably and visibly without wedging app
  startup.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/remote/remote_protocol.dart lib/src/remote/unix_socket_transport.dart lib/src/remote/remote_driver.dart test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/remote/remote_protocol.dart lib/src/remote/unix_socket_transport.dart lib/src/remote/remote_driver.dart test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart` and
  `dart test test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart` passed again after
  simplifying the raw-frame test helper.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Legacy name/path scan passed outside generated benchmark results.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 files and 1119 local links resolve.

Next:
- Later M3.5 work should define the remote security posture, harden
  browser-client behavior, decide public API boundaries, and validate real
  shell/browser workflows.

## 2026-06-01 - Serve Browser Boundary Hardening

Status: Complete

Context:
- After hardening the Dart remote frame decoder, the browser side of M3.5 still
  had two local security/robustness gaps: `fleury serve` accepted browser
  WebSocket upgrades regardless of `Origin`, and the embedded xterm.js client
  decoded frame lengths without a payload cap.
- A remote/browser session can expose app output and user input. It should not
  be attachable by arbitrary cross-origin browser pages, and the client should
  fail closed on malformed frame sizes.

Changes:
- Added same-origin WebSocket upgrade checks to `fleury serve` bridge mode and
  spawn mode. Non-browser/no-origin clients remain allowed for tests and
  explicit tooling, but browser requests with a non-matching `Origin` receive
  `403 Forbidden`.
- Updated the served xterm.js client to define `MAX_FRAME_PAYLOAD`, decode
  frame lengths with unsigned `DataView.getUint32`, and close the WebSocket
  with code `1009` when a frame declares an oversized payload.
- Expanded the HTML sanity test to lock the browser-side frame cap.
- Expanded the real `fleury serve` integration test to reject a cross-origin
  WebSocket upgrade and to prove the normal app/browser byte-flow path still
  works with an explicit same-origin header.
- Updated M3.5 notes and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat the browser remote surface as same-origin by default. The CLI remains
  bound to `127.0.0.1` by default, but browser origin checks protect local
  serve sessions from drive-by pages and keep host-widening explicit.

Validation:
- `dart format bin/fleury.dart lib/src/remote/serve_index_html.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart lib/src/remote/serve_index_html.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` after replacing SDK-missing
  `HttpHeaders.originHeader` with the literal `origin` header.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Legacy name/path scan passed outside generated benchmark results.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 files and 1119 local links resolve.

Next:
- Later M3.5 work should validate real browser behavior manually, define
  whether any opt-in cross-origin/network mode exists, and decide which remote
  APIs become public.

## 2026-06-01 - Spawn Browser Handshake Hardening

Status: Complete

Context:
- The next M3.5 gap was real browser validation for `fleury serve`.
- Running `fleury serve --spawn dart run example/counter_quickstart.dart` in
  the in-app browser initially loaded xterm.js and opened the WebSocket, but
  the page stayed at `connected - waiting for app...` with no counter output.
- The existing spawn fixture did not catch this because it sends output without
  needing the browser INIT frame.

Changes:
- Updated spawn mode to attach a browser WebSocket listener as soon as the
  browser connects, before starting and waiting for the subprocess.
- Buffered bounded pre-app browser binary frames and replayed them into the
  app socket once the spawned process connected.
- Reused the same byte-pump path with an optional browser-input stream so
  bridge mode keeps its existing behavior.
- Added a `serve --spawn` integration regression that runs the real
  `example/counter_quickstart.dart` `runTui` app and proves the browser INIT
  frame sent immediately on WebSocket open reaches the app before first paint.
- Updated M3.5 notes and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat early browser frames as part of the remote-session contract, not as a
  browser timing detail. A remote display may send INIT/resize/input before a
  subprocess has finished starting, and spawn mode must preserve that bounded
  input rather than relying on app startup timing.

Findings:
- Real browser validation was necessary here; the previous socket fixture was
  too synthetic to reveal the lost INIT frame.
- Browser automation validated render/handshake behavior: the served page
  loaded xterm.js, status reached `connected`, and the xterm rows contained
  `count: 0   (space to increment, Ctrl+C to quit)`.
- Browser automation did not produce a reliable xterm Space key event in this
  environment, so this slice treats browser input as covered by the existing
  WebSocket byte-flow integration test rather than claiming manual browser
  keyboard evidence.

Validation:
- `dart format bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart --name "preserves browser INIT"`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- Real in-app browser validation passed for
  `http://127.0.0.1:5779/` served by
  `dart run bin/fleury.dart serve --port=5779 --spawn dart run example/counter_quickstart.dart`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Precise stale-name scan found no old framework name, extraction branch path,
  or stale legacy Dune command-name wording.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 files and 1119 local links resolve.

Next:
- Later M3.5 work should define whether an opt-in network/cross-origin mode
  exists, decide which remote/session APIs are public, and validate the real
  shell workflow. Browser keyboard evidence should be revisited in a normal
  browser or with a page-level xterm input test if launch claims depend on it.

## 2026-06-01 - Bridge Browser Handshake Hardening

Status: Complete

Context:
- The spawn-mode browser fix showed that browser clients can send INIT before
  a Dart app has connected to its session socket.
- Bridge mode had the same ordering shape: the browser WebSocket could be
  pending while waiting for a manually launched app process, but no WebSocket
  listener was attached until pairing.

Changes:
- Introduced a shared `_BufferedBrowserInput` helper for `fleury serve`
  browser sessions.
- Updated bridge mode to create the buffered browser input as soon as the
  WebSocket is upgraded, clear stale pending browsers when they disconnect
  before app attach, and replay bounded early binary frames after pairing.
- Updated spawn mode to use the same helper instead of a private inline
  buffering path.
- Expanded the bridge integration test so browser-first binary input is sent
  before the app socket connects.
- Added a bridge-mode regression that connects the browser first, sends the
  real browser INIT frame, launches `example/counter_quickstart.dart` with
  `FLEURY_HANDLE`, and waits for the first `count: 0` render over the
  WebSocket.
- Updated M3.5 notes and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Treat early browser frames as a `fleury serve` contract for both bridge and
  spawn modes. The browser side owns the remote display handshake timing, so
  serve must preserve bounded early INIT/resize/input frames independently of
  whether the app process is spawned or attached manually.

Validation:
- `dart format bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart --name "browser-first"`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Precise stale-name scan found no old framework name, extraction branch path,
  or stale legacy Dune command-name wording.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 files and 1119 local links resolve.

Next:

## 2026-06-01 - Serve Origin Policy Opt-In

Status: Complete

Context:
- M3.5 still had an explicit security-posture gap: decide whether any
  cross-origin/network browser mode exists.
- The current default same-origin check protected local browser sessions, but
  there was no intentional operator-controlled way to support a known
  cross-origin browser client or embedding surface.

Changes:
- Added `--allow-origin=<origin>` to `fleury serve`. The flag can be supplied
  before `--spawn`; `--spawn` remains greedy and owns all later arguments.
- Preserved the default policy: browser WebSocket requests with no `Origin`
  are allowed for non-browser tooling, same-authority browser origins are
  allowed, and unrelated browser origins are rejected.
- Added explicit cross-origin opt-in for normalized `http`/`https` origins and
  `--allow-origin=*` for operator-chosen allow-any behavior.
- Reused the same origin policy in bridge and spawn modes.
- Added integration coverage for an explicitly allowed cross-origin WebSocket
  and invalid `--allow-origin` values that must fail before `.fleury` binding.
- Updated M3.5 notes and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep `fleury serve` same-origin by default and make wider browser access an
  explicit CLI decision. Binding to a wider host and allowing unrelated
  browser pages are separate risks, so origin policy should not silently widen
  when host binding changes.

Validation:
- `dart format bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart` passed
  in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.

  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_stale_handle_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Precise stale-name scan found no old framework name, extraction branch path,
  or stale legacy Dune command-name wording.
- Local markdown link check passed: 46 files and 1119 local links resolve.

## 2026-06-01 - Remote Public API Boundary

Status: Complete

Context:
- M3.5 still needed a public remote/session API decision.
- Current code already routes app usage through `runTui` auto-discovery and
  the `fleury shell` / `fleury serve` CLI, while tests and the CLI use
  `src/remote` internals directly.

Changes:
- Added a public API boundary regression for the remote session surface.
- The test asserts that `package:fleury/fleury.dart`,
  `package:fleury/fleury_core.dart`, and `package:fleury/fleury_test.dart` do
  not export `src/remote`, `RemoteTerminalDriver`, or `RemoteFrameTransport`.
- The same test keeps the intended extension surface visible by asserting
  `runTui` and `TerminalDriver` remain present in the public libraries.
- Updated M3.5 notes and the decision log.

Decisions:
- Keep remote protocol, transport, and driver internals out of the public
  launch API. The stable launch surface is `runTui` auto-discovery, the
  `fleury shell` / `fleury serve` CLI, and generic `TerminalDriver`
  extensibility. A future devtools/browser/adapter consumer can drive a
  purpose-built public API later.

- Later M3.5 work should validate the real shell workflow.

## 2026-06-01 - Shell Workflow Validation And Cleanup

Status: Complete

Context:
- M3.5 still had one explicit gap before closure: validate the real
  `fleury shell` workflow outside focused protocol and serve tests.
- The validation needed to exercise the operator path, not only socket
  primitives: start `fleury shell` in a real PTY, attach the counter
  quickstart app from a separate process, observe first paint, send input
  through the shell terminal, and shut the session down.

Findings:
- The first live shell run rendered `count: 0`, proving shell startup,
  handle discovery, app attach, remote handshake, and first paint.
- Sending Space through the real shell PTY did not increment the counter.
  Root cause: terminal printable bytes parse to `TextInputEvent`, but
  app-level `.space` bindings listen for `KeyEvent(char: ' ')`. Existing
  tests that injected `KeyEvent` hid the real-terminal mismatch.
- Shell Ctrl+C initially exited the proxy without making the attached app
  process exit.
- A first shutdown fix exposed a PTY cleanup edge where restoring
  `stdin.lineMode` could throw during signal teardown.
- A focused lifecycle test then showed that a real `runTui` app did not exit
  after the shell peer sent `BYE`. Root cause: `RemoteTerminalDriver` became
  inactive on peer disconnect, and `restore()` returned early instead of
  closing frame subscriptions and transport resources.

Changes:
- Updated `InputDispatcher` so focused text claimants still receive
  `TextInputEvent`s first, but unclaimed single-grapheme text falls through as
  a character `KeyEvent` for `KeyBindings` and leader sequences.
- Added input-dispatcher regressions for unclaimed Space activation and
  unclaimed printable text completing a leader sequence.
- Hardened `fleury shell` shutdown so Ctrl+C signals the active session,
  sends `BYE`, closes the app-facing transport, tolerates PTY restoration
  failures, and keeps handle cleanup idempotent.
- Updated `RemoteTerminalDriver.restore()` so cleanup always closes the frame
  subscription, transport, and event stream even when the peer already sent
  `BYE`; it only sends an outbound `BYE` when the driver was still active.
- Added `test/remote/shell_lifecycle_test.dart` covering direct real
  Unix-socket driver shutdown and a real `runTui` counter app exiting when the
  shell peer sends `BYE` and closes the transport.
- Marked M3.5 complete in the milestone tracker.

Decisions:
- Real terminal input should match Fleury's shortcut ergonomics. Printable
  bytes remain text input first, but when no text claimant handles a single
  grapheme they should route to character keybindings.
- Remote driver cleanup is a resource invariant, not an active-state branch.
  Peer disconnect can start app cleanup, but cleanup still owns transport
  shutdown.
- `fleury shell` signal handling should close the active app session before
  process exit. Terminal restoration is best-effort during signal teardown,
  but failures there should not strand the app.

Validation:
- Manual PTY workflow passed:
  - `dart /Users/dan/Coding/fleury/packages/fleury/bin/fleury.dart shell`
    from a temp directory.
  - `dart /Users/dan/Coding/fleury/packages/fleury/example/counter_quickstart.dart`
    from the same temp directory.
  - Shell rendered `count: 0`.
  - Space sent through the shell PTY updated the counter to `count: 1`.
  - Ctrl+C exited the shell with code 130, restored terminal mode sequences,
    and the attached app exited with code 0.
- `dart analyze bin/fleury.dart lib/src/runtime/input_dispatcher.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/remote/remote_driver.dart test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart test/remote/shell_lifecycle_test.dart bin/fleury.dart lib/src/runtime/input_dispatcher.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart test/remote/shell_lifecycle_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test --concurrency=1 test/remote/public_api_boundary_test.dart test/remote/remote_driver_test.dart test/remote/remote_protocol_test.dart test/remote/serve_integration_test.dart test/remote/serve_spawn_test.dart test/remote/serve_index_html_test.dart test/remote/serve_stale_handle_test.dart test/remote/shell_lifecycle_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Notes:
- Running all remote integration tests at default test concurrency created
  subprocess and socket scheduling pressure: the new shell lifecycle test
  timed out waiting for a `dart run` process to connect, and an existing
  stale-handle integration test timed out waiting for a second CLI process.
  Both passed in isolation. The lifecycle test now starts the quickstart
  script directly and the broader remote/input batch is validated serially
  with `--concurrency=1`, which is the right mode for these local service and
  subprocess tests.

Next:
- M3.5 can stay closed unless later real terminal/browser workflows expose a
  new remote-session issue.
- Next milestone selection should prefer the highest remaining launch leverage
  rather than further remote API expansion: likely semantic inspection
  stability, public adoption assets, or real-terminal matrix evidence,
  depending on what the next implementation pass wants to de-risk.

## 2026-06-01 - Semantic Inspection Protocol Stability

Status: Complete

Context:
- With M3.5 closed, the next code-bearing launch gap was M3.6: make semantic
  inspection stable enough for automation/devtools-style consumers instead of
  leaving it as serializer-only JSON.
- Existing `SemanticInspectionSnapshot` already emitted schema-versioned,
  redaction-aware JSON and powered tester APIs, debug capture, and the debug
  Tree tab. The remaining M3.6 gaps were compatibility policy, stable field
  guarantees, and a consumer path that parses the public JSON shape.

Changes:
- Added `SemanticInspectionSnapshot.currentSchemaVersion`,
  `minimumCompatibleSchemaVersion`, `stableJsonFields`, and
  `SemanticInspectionNode.stableJsonFields`.
- Added `SemanticInspectionSnapshot.fromJson` and
  `SemanticInspectionNode.fromJson`.
- Made schema v1 additive-forward-compatible for readers: unknown top-level
  and node fields are ignored, while malformed roots and unsupported schema
  versions fail with `FormatException`.
- Recomputed node counts, role counts, action counts, and focused-node
  fallback from the parsed root instead of trusting stale summary values.
- Re-applied redaction flags during parse, so a consumer-created or
  future-produced JSON node with `redactedValue`, `obscureText`, or
  `clipboardRedacted` cannot reserialize sensitive value/text/token fields by
  accident.
- Added `DebugCaptureArtifact.semanticInspectionSnapshot` as the first parsed
  protocol consumer.
- Expanded inspection tests to cover additive future fields, summary
  recomputation, redaction-on-parse, malformed-root rejection, stable field
  sets, and an adapter-shaped inspect-then-act flow that parses tester JSON,
  finds a command node by protocol fields, and invokes its advertised action
  by node id.
- Expanded debug-capture artifact tests to parse and query the embedded
  semantic inspection snapshot.
- Marked M3.6 complete for the launch v1 inspection contract.

Decisions:
- Treat semantic inspection schema v1 as stable at the envelope and node-field
  level. Future role-specific state can continue to mature, but the base
  fields (`schemaVersion`, summary counts, root, node identity, role, label,
  value, hint, state flags, actions, and children) are the launch contract.
- V1 schema evolution is additive. Readers ignore unknown fields; breaking
  browser/devtools or AI-adapter protocols belong in later M3.3/M3.8 work, not
  silent mutation of v1.
- Debug-capture artifacts are the first parsed protocol consumer. This proves
  the JSON boundary before adding transport, browser, ACP, provider, or
  external automation scope.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/semantics/inspection.dart lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart test/semantics/inspection_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/semantics/inspection.dart lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart test/semantics/inspection_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/semantics/inspection_test.dart test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/semantics test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- M3.6 can stay closed for launch unless a concrete external adapter needs a
  protocol field that cannot be represented additively.
- Next implementation pass should choose between M2.10 real-terminal matrix
  evidence, M2.12 adoption/public assets, or a concrete M3.7/M3.8 engine or
  extension boundary if code pressure is more valuable than documentation.

## 2026-06-01 - App Extension Registry Seam

Status: Complete for first M3.8 slice

Context:
- M3.8 was still open as a broad plugin/extension story. The launch-safe next
  step was not a plugin runtime, ACP adapter, package discovery model, or
  Dune-specific integration. The app shell already owned commands, screens,
  and status, while the theme system already proved typed extension lookup at
  theme scope.
- The narrow useful seam is app-level typed extension objects: package/domain
  code can register app-owned services, data sources, workflow models, or
  integration handles once, then retrieve them from widgets and commands
  without coupling core Fleury to provider lifecycle or protocol details.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryApp.extensions` as an immutable app-level registry of typed
  extension objects.
- Added `FleuryAppController.extensions`,
  `FleuryAppController.maybeExtension<T>()`, and
  `FleuryAppController.extension<T>()`.
- Added `FleuryApp.maybeExtension<T>(context)` and
  `FleuryApp.extension<T>(context)` for descendant widget lookup.
- Added `CommandContext` helpers through `FleuryCommandContext`:
  `maybeAppExtension<T>()` and `appExtension<T>()`.
- Wired extension updates through `FleuryApp.didUpdateWidget` so host apps can
  replace the registry without recreating the app shell.
- Added tests covering descendant lookup, nullable missing-extension lookup,
  controller lookup, immutable exposed extension lists, widget-update
  propagation, and command-context access.
- Updated the implementation tracker, app-kernel workstream, agent workflow
  workstream, decision log, and M3.8 milestone notes.

Decisions:
- App extensions are a typed registry seam, not a plugin runtime. They do not
  load packages, discover plugins, own lifecycles, serialize extension values,
  or introduce ACP/Dune-specific state.
- Extension objects are app-owned. Widgets and commands may use them to reach
  domain services, data sources, and workflow models, but safe user-facing
  facts should still flow through widgets, semantics, diagnostics, or explicit
  debug surfaces.
- Keep M3.8 partial. A larger plugin story still needs proof from real
  widget/data-source/workflow packages before adding lifecycle API.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart test/semantics/inspection_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Standard stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths,
  and old CLI labels found no matches.

Next:
- Continue M3.8 by proving extensions through a real package-shaped
  integration: likely a widget/data-source or workflow service extension that
  contributes commands/themes/status through the existing app kernel.
- M3.7 remains the next code-heavy alternative if performance hot-path
  boundary work is more valuable than package integration.
- M2.10 still needs reviewed real-terminal matrix captures before launch
  compatibility claims can strengthen.

## 2026-06-01 - Proof-App Extension Integration

Status: Complete for second M3.8 slice

Context:
- The first M3.8 slice added typed app-level extension lookup in core, but a
  registry seam is not credible until a package-shaped app uses it in real
  framework paths.
- The proof app is intentionally the current-cycle package pressure test
  before Dune/`dune_cli`, so it is the right place to prove extension
  ergonomics without inventing a generic plugin runtime.

Changes:
- Added `ProofConsoleExtension` to `fleury_example_console` as a package-owned
  app extension for proof workflow state and actions.
- Registered `ProofConsoleExtension` through `FleuryApp.extensions`.
- Read the extension from `FleuryApp.extension<T>(context)` in screen builders
  for Overview and Diagnostics.
- Read the extension from `CommandContext.appExtension<T>()` for command
  enablement and the diagnose command action.
- Read the extension from `FleuryAppController.extension<T>()` in the app
  status builder.
- Kept the extension object package-owned and protocol-neutral. Core Fleury
  still does not own package discovery, lifecycle, ACP schemas, or Dune state.

Decisions:
- Use the proof app as the first package-shaped extension proof. This gives
  M3.8 real command/status/widget evidence without committing to reusable
  plugin lifecycle APIs too early.
- Let app extensions carry callbacks and read-model facts when the app owns
  lifecycle. Safe user-facing facts still flow through status, widgets,
  semantics, diagnostics, and debug capture.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.

Next:
- M3.8 should next define extension package conventions only where existing
  evidence is clear: how extension-owned commands, themes, status, data
  sources, and workflow services should be bundled and registered.
- Avoid adding plugin lifecycle or discovery until a reusable package outside
  the proof app proves it needs lifecycle beyond ordinary Dart objects plus
  app/scope registration.

## 2026-06-01 - Widget Paint-Only Invalidation Audit

Status: Complete for first M3.7 slice

Context:
- M3.7 asks for an optional high-performance engine boundary, but the current
  evidence says the best next move is not a public engine API. Fleury already
  has retained layout dirtiness, `markNeedsPaintOnly`, render islands, frame
  layout stats, and dashboard/layout scenario benchmarks.
- Several widget-package render objects still used conservative
  `markNeedsPaint()`, which intentionally dirties layout. That is correct for
  unaudited setters, but stable-shape visual updates should reuse cached
  layout and only invalidate paint.

Changes:
- Moved stable-shape visual setters from `markNeedsPaint()` to
  `markNeedsPaintOnly()` for `LineChart`, `Canvas`, `RangeSlider`, and visual
  `Image` policy/style updates.
- Split shape-sensitive widgets more carefully:
  - `Heatmap` value updates are paint-only only when the matrix shape is
    unchanged; cell width and row/column labels remain layout-dirty.
  - `CalendarHeatmap` value/min/max/color/style updates are paint-only; date
    range, week-start, labels, and cell width remain layout-dirty.
  - `Digits` same-width text updates and style changes are paint-only; text
    changes that affect intrinsic width remain layout-dirty.
  - `Image` fit/glyph/protocol/color policy updates are paint-only; decoded
    image replacement remains layout-dirty because unbounded intrinsic size can
    change.
- Added `paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`, which renders each audited
  widget once, updates a stable-shape visual property, and asserts the next
  same-size frame performs zero layouts while recording skipped cached layouts.

Decisions:
- Keep `markNeedsPaint()` conservative. The high-performance path is not a
  blanket refactor; it is a setter-by-setter audit with focused layout-stat
  regression tests.
- Keep public high-performance extension APIs unresolved. This slice improves
  first-party render-object discipline and gives better evidence for M3.7, but
  it does not yet prove a stable public render-island/engine boundary.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/line_chart.dart lib/src/heatmap.dart lib/src/calendar_heatmap.dart lib/src/canvas.dart lib/src/digits.dart lib/src/range_slider.dart lib/src/image.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/line_chart.dart lib/src/heatmap.dart lib/src/calendar_heatmap.dart lib/src/canvas.dart lib/src/digits.dart lib/src/range_slider.dart lib/src/image.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/line_chart_test.dart test/heatmap_test.dart test/calendar_heatmap_test.dart test/canvas_test.dart test/digits_test.dart test/range_slider_test.dart test/image_test.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.6 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed with `SB.6 Dashboard Update Pressure: pass`,
  `update_total_p95_us=1312`, and update-frame layout counts still captured
  at 45 performed / 29 skipped for the dashboard fixture.

Next:
- Run the full quick gate after this docs update.
- Future M3.7 passes should either continue scenario-led setter audits where
  benchmarks reveal layout churn, or define what a public render-island/engine
  boundary would expose without splitting semantics/focus/testing from the
  Flutter-style widget model.

## 2026-06-01 - Terminal Matrix Audit Black-Box Coverage

Status: Complete for this M2.10 hardening slice

Context:
- M2.10 still depends on externally collected real-terminal matrix entries,
  which cannot be fully produced from a non-user terminal session.
- The repo-local matrix capture/audit tooling is now the path developers will
  use to gather those entries, so its target matching and strict-mode failure
  behavior need direct evidence instead of relying only on manual script runs.
- The audit already had a policy that target-prefixed clean labels such as
  `iterm2-3-5` should satisfy clean terminal targets, while context labels
  such as `tmux-kitty` should satisfy only the context target and not the
  underlying clean terminal.

Changes:
- Added `packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`, a
  black-box test that launches the actual
  `dart ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit` command against
  temporary matrix entries.
- Covered clean target-prefix matching, context labels, missing-target
  `collectionPlan` output, invalid JSON entries, and `--strict --json`
  non-zero exit behavior.
- Fixed `_terminalMatrixTargetMatchKind` so `tmux-*` and `ssh-*` labels are
  classified as `contextToken` evidence for the tmux/SSH targets before the
  generic target-prefix rule runs.
- Updated the implementation README, M2.10 milestone notes, terminal
  compatibility matrix tracker, and terminal/security workstream.

Decisions:
- Keep this as black-box launcher coverage rather than extracting private
  script helpers into a package API. The developer-facing command is the
  contract that matters for matrix collection.
- Treat matrix-audit tests as readiness-tooling evidence only. They do not
  replace reviewed real-terminal entries.

Validation:
- `dart format ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- M2.10 still needs reviewed real-terminal captures for macOS Terminal, iTerm2,
  Kitty, Ghostty, Alacritty, WezTerm, Windows Terminal, SSH, and tmux where
  feasible.
- If the next implementation pass stays in M2.10, prefer either real capture
  review workflow improvements or platform-specific probes backed by primary
  protocol documentation and latency/security analysis.

## 2026-06-01 - DataTable Render-Island Paint-Only Invalidation

Status: Complete for this M3.7 hardening slice

Context:
- M3.7 is still open because Fleury needs measurable high-performance behavior
  without splitting away from the Flutter-style widget model, semantic graph,
  focus, testing, and debug surfaces.
- The previous paint-only audit covered chart and visual widgets. The remaining
  high-leverage target was `DataTable`, the first major render island and one
  of Fleury's strongest data-heavy differentiators.
- `RenderObjectElement.update` now expects render-object setters to own their
  own invalidation. `RenderDataTable` setters were still mostly bare field
  assignments, which made layout/paint invalidation implicit rather than an
  audited part of the render-island contract.

Changes:
- Made `RenderDataTable` setters explicitly invalidate layout or paint.
- Kept row count, columns, column spacing, and header-separator changes
  layout-dirty because they affect viewport, size, column starts, visible row
  range, or header geometry.
- Kept cell-builder replacement, selection range/mode, separator style,
  selected style, and visible selected-row movement paint-only.
- Selected-row movement relayouts only when the new selected row is outside
  the current visible window and therefore may change the viewport.
- Added paint-only regression coverage for visible DataTable selection changes
  and visible cell-content refreshes in `paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`.

Decisions:
- Hot render islands should own explicit invalidation in their setters instead
  of relying on element reconciliation side effects. That keeps layout skips
  intentional and testable.
- Do not broaden this into a public performance extension API yet. This slice
  strengthens first-party render-object discipline and keeps the public API
  question open until more render/data hot paths prove the same shape.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/data_table.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/data_table.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart test/data_table_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart test test/data_table_test.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.3 --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --json`
  passed with `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows: pass` and
  `page_move_p95_us=11566`, under the candidate 16000 us page-move threshold
  for the short smoke run.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- Future M3.7 passes can either audit another hot render island/widget family
  with layout-stat tests or define the evidence needed before a public
  high-performance extension API is justified.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.8 Extension Command And Status Convention

Status: Complete for this M3.8 slice

Context:
- M3.8 already had typed app-level extension lookup, but package-shaped
  integrations still needed a concrete convention for contributing app shell
  affordances without turning Fleury into a plugin runtime.
- The proof app was already registering `ProofConsoleExtension`, making it the
  right package-shaped place to prove extension-owned command and status
  registration.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryAppExtension` as an optional app-level extension base for static
  app command and status contributions.
- `FleuryApp` now folds extension commands into its app command registry and
  extension status items into the app status controller.
- App-owned commands are registered before extension commands, so app commands
  remain authoritative when command IDs collide.
- Added focused core tests for extension-contributed commands/status and app
  command override behavior.
- Updated `ProofConsoleExtension` to extend `FleuryAppExtension`, contribute
  the terminal diagnostics command, and own the stream/debug status items.

Decisions:
- Keep extension contributions static and explicit. This is a package
  convention for app-owned integration objects, not package discovery,
  lifecycle management, async plugin activation, ACP, or Dune coupling.
- Expose extension facts through commands, status, widgets, semantics, and
  debug capture rather than serializing arbitrary extension objects.

Validation:
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart lib/fleury_core.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.

  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- Remaining M3.8 work should focus on conventions for package-owned themes and
  data sources, or on proving a reusable non-example extension package if the
  proof app reveals a lifecycle need. Do not add plugin lifecycle/discovery
  until real package pressure justifies it.

## 2026-06-01 - Composition Table Explicit Invalidation

Status: Complete for this M3.7 hardening slice

Context:
- M3.7 is still improving performance through measured first-party
  render-object discipline instead of introducing a public high-performance
  extension API too early.
- `RenderTable` was still relying on bare field assignment in
  `updateRenderObject`. After element reconciliation stopped forcing relayout
  for every render-object widget update, those setters needed explicit layout
  or paint invalidation like the other audited widgets.

Changes:
- Made `RenderTable` setters explicitly mark layout or paint.
- Kept column count, column widths, spacing, header presence/separator,
  children, and row-window-changing selection moves layout-dirty.
- Kept separator style, selected-row style, and visible selected-row movement
  paint-only.
- Added a layout-stat regression test proving visible `Table` selection
  movement reuses cached layout.

Decisions:
- Apply the same explicit-invalidation discipline to composition render
  objects, not only render islands. The public high-performance extension API
  question remains open.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/table.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/table.dart test/paint_only_invalidation_test.dart test/table_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_widgets`.
