## 2026-06-01 - M3.8 Extension Theme Defaults

Status: Complete for this M3.8 slice

Context:
- The previous M3.8 slice made extension-owned commands and status static
  app-shell contributions. The remaining package-convention gap was how
  integration packages can bundle widget styling defaults without becoming a
  plugin runtime.
- Fleury already has `ThemeData.extensions`, and `fleury_widgets` already uses
  `FleuryWidgetTheme`, so the smallest coherent convention is to let
  `FleuryAppExtension` append package theme-extension defaults under
  `FleuryApp`.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryAppExtension.themeExtensions`.
- `FleuryApp` now wraps its app subtree in a merged `Theme` when extension
  theme defaults are present.
- Ambient host `ThemeData.extensions` stay first, so host apps override package
  defaults by type through the normal theme path.
- Added app-shell tests proving extension theme contribution, update behavior,
  and host override precedence.
- Moved the proof app's `FleuryWidgetTheme` defaults onto
  `ProofConsoleExtension`, proving extension-owned theme defaults in the
  separate proof-app package.

Decisions:
- Keep theme contributions static, synchronous, and app-owned. Do not add
  package discovery, lifecycle hooks, or provider adapters for styling.
- Treat extension-provided theme entries as defaults rather than overrides.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart lib/fleury_core.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- Remaining M3.8 work should focus on package-owned data-source conventions or
  on a reusable non-example extension package if proof-app pressure shows a
  real lifecycle gap.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.8 Extension Data Sources

Status: Complete for this M3.8 slice

Context:
- M3.8 had static extension contributions for commands, status, and theme
  defaults. The remaining package-convention gap was how integration packages
  expose typed read models or data adapters without making Fleury a provider
  framework.
- The proof app's global search corpus was already package-shaped and
  app-owned, making it a good first data-source proof without adding
  networking, persistence, paging, or lifecycle policy.

Changes:
- Added `FleuryAppExtension.dataSources`.
- Added typed data-source lookup on `FleuryAppController`,
  `FleuryApp.dataSource<T>(context)`, and command-context
  `appDataSource<T>()`.
- Kept the exposed `FleuryAppController.dataSources` list immutable.
- Added app-shell tests for widget lookup, controller lookup, command-context
  lookup, data-source updates, missing-source behavior, and immutable exposed
  lists.
- Added `ProofSearchDataSource` to the proof app and contributed it from
  `ProofConsoleExtension`; global search now uses that package-owned read
  model to build the search corpus.

Decisions:
- Data sources are lookup seams only. Fleury does not fetch, refresh, page,
  cache, subscribe, dispose, serialize, or lifecycle-manage them.
- Keep data ownership app/package-local until a reusable non-example extension
  package proves a lifecycle API is needed.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart analyze lib/src/app/app.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart lib/fleury_core.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/fleury_app_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart format lib/fleury_example_console.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart analyze lib/fleury_example_console.dart test/proof_console_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart test test/proof_console_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury_example_console`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- M3.8 should not grow plugin lifecycle/discovery unless a reusable
  non-example extension package proves the need. Otherwise the next best work
  is likely M3.7 scenario-backed performance proof or M2.10 real-terminal
  evidence.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.7 Core RenderText Same-Shape Text Invalidation

Status: Complete for this M3.7 hardening slice

Context:
- M3.7 had already proved paint-only invalidation for stable-shape visual
  widgets, DataTable render-island updates, and composition `Table` selection.
- The next audit pass looked for core hot paths where layout stability could be
  proven without adding a public high-performance API. Lazy `ListView.builder`
  selection remains conservative because selected row builders may change
  arbitrary widget shape, but core same-shape text swaps have a tighter
  invariant.

Changes:
- Updated `RenderText.text` so same-width, single-line content swaps can use
  `markNeedsPaintOnly()` when the current layout shape is known to stay stable.
- Kept wrapping, newline, empty/non-empty, and intrinsic-width-changing text
  updates on the layout-dirty path.
- Updated the current line cache on paint-only text swaps so skipped layout
  still paints the new content.
- Added render-text regression coverage for no-wrap same-width swaps,
  soft-wrap same-width swaps that currently fit, and same-width wrapping text
  that must still relayout.

Decisions:
- Keep text paint-only invalidation narrow and shape-based. Do not infer that a
  `ListView.builder` selection update is paint-only unless the list API later
  grows an explicit row-layout-stability contract.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_objects.dart packages/fleury/test/rendering/render_text_test.dart` passed.
- `dart analyze lib/src/rendering/render_objects.dart test/rendering/render_text_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_text_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- M3.7 should continue only where the stable-shape boundary can be stated and
  tested clearly, or where scenario benchmarks show a concrete relayout hot
  path. Good next candidates are broader scenario proof for the current
  invalidation foundation or M2.10 real-terminal matrix evidence.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.7 SB.12 Same-Shape Text Benchmark Evidence

Status: Complete for this M3.7 evidence slice

Context:
- The previous M3.7 slice made same-width single-line `RenderText` updates
  paint-only and covered the invariant with focused render tests.
- That was necessary but not enough for the performance story: the active
  scenario evidence still only covered layout-affecting counter updates, a
  style paint-only frame, and an idle frame.

Changes:
- Extended `SB.12 Layout Dirtiness Cache` with a separate same-width text
  paint-only frame after the style paint-only frame.
- Started the SB.12 counter model at 9 so the command update still changes
  `9 -> 10` and remains layout-affecting while the later same-width text swap
  is the dedicated text paint-only proof.
- Added `textPaintOnlyFrameUs`, `textPaintOnlyLayoutPerformed`,
  `textPaintOnlyLayoutSkipped`, `lastTextPaintOnlyFrameLayout`, and
  `textVariant` to the scenario JSON metrics.
- Refreshed
  `packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`
  with 20 measured iterations on Dart 3.12.1.
- Updated the benchmark README, scenario lab, reactive-render workstream,
  milestone notes, and implementation tracker so the current SB.12 evidence
  describes style paint-only, text paint-only, and idle frames.

Decisions:
- Preserve SB.12's split between a layout-affecting update frame and
  paint-only follow-up frames. Letting the counter update become same-shape
  would have made the benchmark less useful because it would no longer prove
  both paths in one scenario.
- Keep the text optimization private and invariant-based for now. The measured
  contract is same-width, single-line content under known stable layout shape;
  wrapping/newline/width-changing text remains layout-dirty.

Evidence:
- Refreshed baseline records command-to-frame p95 11799 us, idle-frame p95
  692 us, paint-only-frame p95 1210 us, text-paint-only-frame p95 3251 us,
  update-frame layout p95 7 performed / 3 skipped, style paint-only layout p95
  0 performed / 1 skipped, text paint-only layout p95 0 performed / 1 skipped,
  and idle layout p95 0 performed / 1 skipped.
- Short validation run also passed with the same layout invariants:
  update layout 7 performed / 3 skipped, style paint-only 0 performed /
  1 skipped, text paint-only 0 performed / 1 skipped, and idle 0 performed /
  1 skipped.

Validation:
- `dart analyze benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=1 --iterations=2 --json`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --save=benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-2026-06-01.json`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- M3.7 now has scenario evidence for the current stable-shape render-object
  invalidation boundary. The next best work is either M2.10 real-terminal matrix
  evidence, M2.12 adoption/distribution assets, or a broader peer-shaped
  benchmark only if it will sharpen launch claims beyond the current internal
  evidence.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.10 Terminal Matrix Capture Notes

Status: Complete for this M2.10 tooling slice

Context:
- M2.10 already had passive diagnosis, opt-in active probes, compatibility
  findings, repo-local matrix capture, automatic review triage, strict audit
  checks, collection-plan output, and black-box audit launcher tests.
- The remaining launch evidence still requires reviewed real-terminal captures.
  Running another local non-interactive capture would not satisfy that evidence
  gap.
- Real captures often need human context at collection time: terminal version,
  profile name, tmux/SSH host shape, non-default settings, and explanations for
  automatic `needsAttention` findings.

Changes:
- Added repeated `--review-note=<text>` support to
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix`.
- Preserved those notes in the generated matrix entry `review.notes`.
- Kept notes additive only. The launcher still computes `review.status` and
  `review.issues` from the diagnosis and compatibility report.
- Added a black-box capture test that runs the actual launcher, writes a
  non-interactive control entry, and verifies the notes are preserved while
  automatic non-interactive issues remain visible.
- Updated the terminal matrix tracker, terminal-matrix directory README, M2.10
  milestone notes, TCS workstream, implementation README, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Do not add a `--review-status` override. It would make capture files easier to
  misuse by hiding non-interactive sessions, missing probes, passive-unverified
  features, or inconclusive compatibility findings behind an optimistic manual
  label.
- Treat capture notes as reviewer context, not compatibility evidence by
  themselves.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed.
- `dart analyze ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix --help` shows
  `--review-note=<text>`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --json` passed and reported
  `entryCount: 0`, `readyTargetCount: 0`, and all nine default targets still
  missing, confirming no fake local evidence was introduced.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and old
  extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- M2.10 remains open until reviewed real-terminal entries exist. The capture
  command is now better prepared for those passes because reviewers can attach
  context while preserving automatic triage.

## 2026-06-01 - M2.12 Internal Why Fleury Positioning

Status: Complete for this M2.12 adoption slice

Context:
- M2.10 is ready for real-terminal collection but still depends on reviewed
  captures from actual launch terminals.
- The highest-value local work was to make Fleury's adoption argument explicit
  enough to guide implementation without turning it into public launch copy.
- The user direction is still to prove Fleury through the example subpackage in
  this cycle, keep Dune/`dune_cli` as the later flagship, and keep ACP in a
  fast-follow package rather than core launch scope.

Changes:
- Added internal `docs/implementation/why-fleury.md` positioning that states
  the launch wedge, target users, developer-visible wins, proof-app role,
  later Dune/`dune_cli` role, launch-ready claims, and claims not yet ready.
- Refreshed the peer source snapshot for Nocterm, Bubble Tea v2, Textual,
  OpenTUI, Ratatui, and Ink on 2026-06-01.
- Updated the peer scorecards for Bubble Tea `v2.0.7` and OpenTUI `0.3.1`.
- Closed ADE.1 in the adoption/distribution workstream with evidence pointing
  to the internal Why Fleury draft.
- Linked the positioning page from the implementation README and marked M2.12
  as in progress with public package docs, comparison pages, docs site,
  scaffolding, and release collateral still open.
- Added a decision-log entry keeping "Why Fleury?" internal and
  evidence-backed until peer-equivalent runs and real-terminal matrix entries
  support public claims.

Decisions:
- Lead Fleury positioning with developer-visible outcomes: app kernel,
  semantic tests, text/input strength, data/workflow widgets, tasks/process
  safety, terminal diagnostics, and app-shaped benchmarks.
- Do not claim "best TUI framework overall" externally yet. That remains the
  ambition, not a launch claim, until peer and terminal evidence is stronger.
- Keep peer comparisons honest: Nocterm already claims Flutter-like Dart TUI,
  Bubble Tea is mature and ecosystem-backed, Textual is the mature full-app
  reference, OpenTUI has strong renderer/protocol ambitions, Ratatui is the
  Rust explicit-rendering standard, and Ink owns React familiarity.

Validation:
- Peer refresh command returned Nocterm `0.6.0`, Bubble Tea `v2.0.7`, Textual
  `8.2.7`, OpenTUI `0.3.1`, Ratatui `0.30.0`, and Ink `7.0.5`.
- Active implementation docs link `Why Fleury?` from the README, M2.12 notes,
  and the adoption workstream.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale peer-version scan found old Bubble Tea/OpenTUI values only in
  historical execution-journal entries, not in active positioning or scorecard
  docs.

Next:
- M2.12 remains open for public docs/comparison/showcase/scaffolding work.
- The next local implementation slice should target a provable framework
  capability or evidence gap, not broaden public release collateral before the
  core launch surface is fully supported.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.8 Reusable Extension Package Proof

Status: Complete for the M3.8 launch extension story

Context:
- The app-kernel extension seam already supported typed app extensions,
  extension-owned commands/status/theme/data sources, proof-app
  `ProofConsoleExtension`, and app-owned override precedence.
- M3.8 still had one credible gap: the seam was proven in core tests and the
  proof app, but not by a reusable non-example package that could plausibly
  ship beside Fleury.
- The goal was to pressure the package boundary without turning Fleury into a
  plugin runtime or adding lifecycle/discovery APIs.

Changes:
- Added `packages/fleury_git`, a small Git integration package with:
  - `GitRepositorySnapshot` for immutable repository read-model facts.
  - `GitRepositoryDataSource` and `StaticGitRepositoryDataSource` for typed
    app-owned lookup.
  - `FleuryGitExtension`, which contributes Git commands, app status, widget
    theme defaults, and the repository data source through
    `FleuryAppExtension`.
  - `GitStatusPanel`, a widget consumer that reads the typed data source and
    exposes Git status semantics.
- Added package tests for snapshot labels, extension-contributed commands,
  status, data-source lookup, theme defaults, host command precedence, host
  theme precedence, disabled command behavior, and package-owned semantic
  state.
- Updated the local launcher so `bootstrap` and `check --quick` include
  `packages/fleury_git`.
- Updated README/local distribution docs and M3.8 tracker notes.
- Marked M3.8 complete for the launch extension story.

Decisions:
- Keep Git process execution, repository discovery, refresh, mutation, caching,
  subscriptions, and disposal app-owned. `fleury_git` proves package-shaped
  contributions, not provider lifecycle ownership.
- Keep package-specific semantic state package-owned via generic
  `SemanticState` map access. Do not grow core's stable convenience getter
  list for a single integration package.
- Close M3.8 on static typed contributions. Plugin discovery, package loading,
  adapter lifecycle, and provider protocols remain future proof-driven work.

Validation:
- `dart pub get` passed in `packages/fleury_git`.
- `dart format packages/fleury_git/lib/fleury_git.dart packages/fleury_git/lib/src/git_extension.dart packages/fleury_git/test/git_extension_test.dart tool/fleury_dev.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze` passed in `packages/fleury_git`.
- `dart test` passed in `packages/fleury_git`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, `fleury_git`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed before this journal entry.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- Do not add plugin lifecycle/discovery unless a concrete reusable package
  proves that static typed contributions are insufficient.
- Good next local implementation targets are M3.7 public/private
  performance-boundary decisions, M3.9 peer-shaped comparative benchmark
  fixtures, or M2.12 adoption metrics. M2.9/M2.10 still need external Windows
  and real-terminal evidence before they can close.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Comparative Benchmark Manifest

Status: Complete for the first M3.9 contract slice

Context:
- Fleury has internal scenario benchmarks and proof-app evidence, but external
  comparison claims need peer-equivalent fixture definitions before any result
  numbers are credible.
- The current evidence gap is not only performance data. Cross-framework
  claims also need correctness gates, test/semantic query shape, app
  ergonomics notes, terminal mode, runtime versions, and explicit peer package
  sources.
- M2.9 Windows evidence and M2.10 real-terminal matrix evidence remain
  external. This slice does not pretend to close them.

Changes:
- Added `docs/implementation/comparative-benchmark-manifest.json` as a
  source-linked benchmark contract for Nocterm, Bubble Tea v2, Textual,
  OpenTUI, Ratatui, and Ink.
- Mapped `SB.1` through `SB.12` to local Fleury benchmark commands,
  peer-target sets, scenario contracts, required metrics, claim gates, and
  intentionally empty `peerRuns`.
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest` with JSON and human
  output, input/output path support, checked-in manifest validation, duplicate
  ID detection, and unknown-peer rejection.
- Added focused launcher tests that parse the checked-in manifest and reject a
  malformed manifest with an unknown peer.
- Linked the manifest from the implementation README, benchmark README,
  scenario benchmark lab, peer scorecards, and M3.9 milestone notes.

Decisions:
- Start M3.9 with a machine-readable evidence contract before implementing
  peer fixtures or collecting peer results.
- Keep `peerRuns` empty until a peer fixture is implemented and run from a
  source-linked version under documented machine/runtime/terminal conditions.
- Separate timing claims from correctness, semantic/test-query coverage, and
  ergonomics evidence. A fast peer run without equivalent behavior or safety
  checks should not support a launch claim.

Validation:
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest` passed and reported 6 peers
  and 12 scenarios.
- `dart analyze ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `git diff --check` passed before this journal entry.

Next:
- The next M3.9 slice should implement peer-run result ingestion or the first
  real peer fixture, likely starting with Nocterm for `SB.1`/`SB.2` because it
  is the closest Dart comparison.
- Keep public comparison language conservative until peer-equivalent fixtures,
  real terminal evidence, and result artifacts exist.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Peer Result Ingestion

Status: Complete for the first M3.9 result-governance slice

Context:
- The comparative benchmark manifest defined peer-equivalent scenario
  contracts, but it still lacked a governed path for adding peer evidence.
- Allowing manual edits to `peerRuns` would make it too easy to add numbers
  without proving the peer target, source version, required metrics, terminal
  mode, fixture command, or correctness gates.
- This slice should still not add fake peer evidence. The checked-in manifest
  remains empty until actual peer fixtures exist and run.

Changes:
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result`.
- The command reads the checked-in manifest by default, validates one
  `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` artifact, and optionally writes a manifest copy with
  that run appended to the matching scenario's `peerRuns`.
- Validation requires schema v1, known peer, known scenario, peer listed as a
  target for that scenario, source name/version/url, machine/runtime/terminal
  environment, fixture command and iteration counts, every manifest-required
  metric, and every manifest claim gate passing.
- Added black-box launcher tests for a Nocterm `SB.1` fixture-shaped peer run
  and rejection of a run missing a required metric.
- Updated the launcher README, benchmark README, scenario lab, peer scorecards,
  implementation tracker, milestones, and decision log.

Decisions:
- Peer benchmark evidence enters through validated run artifacts, not manual
  `peerRuns` edits.
- `benchmark-result` writes manifest copies through `--output`; it does not
  mutate the checked-in manifest as a side effect.
- Keep failing or incomplete peer attempts out of the manifest for now. They
  can be recorded in the journal or fixture notes until a later evidence model
  needs explicit failed-run history.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart analyze ../../tool/fleury_dev.dart test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --help` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed across `fleury`,
  `fleury_widgets`, `fleury_git`, and `fleury_example_console`.
- `git diff --check` passed before this journal entry.
- Exact stale-name scan for former framework names, source branch paths, and
  old extraction labels found no matches.

Next:
- The next M3.9 implementation slice should create the first real peer fixture
  and artifact producer, with Nocterm `SB.1` as the likely first target.
- Keep peer fixture code separate from core package APIs so comparison tooling
  cannot leak into the launch runtime surface.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.1 Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the first real M3.9 peer-fixture slice

Context:
- M3.9 had a manifest contract and result ingestion path, but no real peer
  fixture source producing a validated run artifact.
- Nocterm is the closest Dart peer, so `SB.1 Time To Counter App` is the right
  first fixture for validating the benchmark-result pipeline against real peer
  package APIs.

Changes:
- Added [peer-fixtures/README.md](../../peer-fixtures/README.md) to document
  comparison-only fixture rules.
- Added [Nocterm SB.1 counter fixture](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter)
  with an exact Nocterm `0.6.0` dependency, a `StatefulComponent`,
  a `NoctermTester` behavior test, and `bin/sb1_counter_benchmark.dart`.
- The fixture producer emits `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with source,
  environment, fixture command, required `SB.1` metrics, correctness gates,
  ergonomics fields, source/test artifacts, and conservative notes about
  tester-harness timing.
- Saved the local 20-iteration run at
  [nocterm-sb1-counter-2026-06-01.json](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter/results/nocterm-sb1-counter-2026-06-01.json).

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size
  `80x24`, and `nocterm-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: first-frame p95 1926 us, command-to-frame p95
  2053 us, test-query p95 2385 us, max RSS delta 1044480 bytes, app LOC 25,
  test LOC 14.
- All three `SB.1` claim gates passed: visible counter update, normal input
  path through `NoctermTester.sendKey(LogicalKey.space)`, and documented test
  shape.

Decisions:
- Keep peer fixture packages under `peer-fixtures/`, outside Fleury runtime
  packages and public API exports.
- Keep checked-in `comparative-benchmark-manifest.json` with empty `peerRuns`
  for now; use `benchmark-result --output=...` to create manifest copies with
  accepted peer runs.

Validation:
- `dart pub get` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter`.
- `dart format .` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter`.
- `dart analyze` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter`.
- `dart test` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter`.

  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb1_counter/results/nocterm-sb1-counter-2026-06-01.json --output=/tmp/fleury-nocterm-sb1-manifest.json --json`.

Next:

  until repeated runs and real-terminal evidence exist.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.2 Text Editing Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the second real M3.9 peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.2 Text Editing Composer Stress` is a better launch-relevance comparison
  than the counter app because Fleury's text stack includes grapheme-safe
  editing, selection, undo/redo, paste policy, completion, history, redaction,
  and semantics.
- Nocterm `0.6.0` has a real `TextField` with multiline, selection, clipboard
  paste, and obscured text. It does not expose Fleury-equivalent built-in
  primitives for undo/redo, submission history, or completion.

Changes:
- Added
  [Nocterm SB.2 text editing fixture](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing)
  with an exact Nocterm `0.6.0` dependency.
- The fixture app mounts composer, editor, and secret fields. Nocterm owns the
  `TextField` behavior for normal editing, multiline rendering, selection,
  paste, and obscured text.
- Added fixture-owned adapters for editor undo/redo, composer
  submission-history navigation, and completion acceptance so the peer fixture
  can exercise the full `SB.2` behavioral contract while making the extra
  app code visible.
- Added a behavior test that drives insertion, selection replacement,
  undo/redo, paste, completion, history restore, and redaction through
  `NoctermTester`.
- Added `bin/sb2_text_editing_benchmark.dart`, producing
  `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with all `SB.2` required metrics and claim
  gates.
- Saved the local 10k-character, 10-iteration run at
  [nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-01.json](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing/results/nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-01.json).

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size
  `80x24`, and `nocterm-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: cursor-move p95 7279 us, insertion/deletion p95
  9915 us, selection p95 20243 us, undo/redo p95 8590 us,
  history-navigation p95 9811 us, completion-accept p95 10309 us,
  paste-complete p95 23541 us, test-query p95 860 us, max RSS delta
  1691648 bytes, app LOC 278, test LOC 64.
- All three `SB.2` claim gates passed: mixed-width text remained valid,
  selection plus undo state was correct, and the redacted value stayed out of
  the terminal buffer.

Decisions:
- Peer fixtures may include app-owned adapters when the peer lacks a
  Fleury-equivalent primitive, but the artifact must record that ownership in
  ergonomics and notes.
- Keep this evidence out of checked-in manifest `peerRuns`; use
  `benchmark-result --output=...` for accepted manifest copies.

Validation:
- `dart pub get` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- `dart format .` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- `dart analyze` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- `dart test` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- A two-iteration smoke artifact validated through `benchmark-result`; the
  temporary smoke file was not retained.
- Saved artifact validation passed with:
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing/results/nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-01.json --output=/tmp/fleury-nocterm-sb2-manifest.json --json`.

Next:
- Add a non-Dart peer fixture next, preferably Textual `SB.3` or Bubble Tea
  text editing, so M3.9 is not only Fleury-vs-Nocterm.
- Keep Nocterm comparison language precise: this fixture shows Nocterm can
  support the behavior with app-owned adapters, while Fleury owns more of the
  behavior as framework primitives.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Textual SB.3 DataTable Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the first non-Dart M3.9 peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` is the most important data-heavy launch wedge:
  Fleury already has a saved `DataTable` baseline with bounded visible row
  rendering, selected-row copy, semantic table state, and test queries.
- Textual `8.2.7` is the strongest mature full-app framework peer for this
  slice because it has a first-party `DataTable`, app test harness, command
  binding model, and public widget state. PyPI currently lists `8.2.7` as the
  latest release on 2026-06-01.

Changes:
- Added
  [Textual SB.3 DataTable fixture](../../peer-fixtures/textual/sb3_datatable)
  with pinned `textual==8.2.7` requirements.
- The fixture app mounts a 100k-row, eight-column Textual `DataTable` with
  stable row keys, row cursor mode, fixed header, priority `End` binding for
  jump-to-final-row, and priority `Ctrl+C` binding for selected-row TSV
  copy/export.
- Added a direct behavior script,
  `test_sb3_datatable.py`, that drives down/page/end/copy through Textual
  `run_test`, then asserts visible window bounds, selected final row, and exact
  sanitized TSV export.
- Added `sb3_datatable_benchmark.py`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON
  with all `SB.3` required metrics, correctness gates, environment metadata,
  ergonomics ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-row, 3-iteration run at
  [textual-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json](../../peer-fixtures/textual/sb3_datatable/results/textual-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json).

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Textual `8.2.7`, Python `3.12.13`, macOS,
  terminal size `80x24`, and `textual-run-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 10128627 us, first-render p95
  76752 us, arrow-move p95 304428 us, page-move p95 113432 us,
  jump-to-end p95 141400 us, selected-row copy p95 90152 us,
  widget-state query p95 307 us, max RSS delta 31932416 bytes, app LOC 104,
  test LOC 30.
- All three `SB.3` claim gates passed: visible window stayed bounded, final
  row selection was correct after jump, and selected-row copy/export was
  sanitized and exact.

Decisions:
- Non-Dart peer fixtures should stay pinned and local-harness-labeled. They
  can use fixture-owned commands around peer widgets, but the artifact must
  label which behavior is framework-owned versus app-owned.
- Textual exposes useful public widget state for this slice, but not a
  Fleury-style semantic app graph. Scorecard language should call this a
  widget-state query, not semantic parity.
- Keep checked-in `comparative-benchmark-manifest.json` with empty `peerRuns`;
  use `benchmark-result --output=...` to create accepted manifest copies.

Validation:
- `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/fleury-textual-site .../python3 test_sb3_datatable.py`
  passed.
- `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/fleury-textual-site .../python3 -m py_compile
  sb3_datatable_app.py test_sb3_datatable.py sb3_datatable_benchmark.py`
  passed.
- A 1000-row smoke artifact validated through `benchmark-result`; the
  temporary smoke file was not retained.
- Saved artifact validation passed with:
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/textual/sb3_datatable/results/textual-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json --output=/tmp/fleury-textual-sb3-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.

Next:
- Continue M3.9 with either Ratatui/OpenTUI `SB.3` equivalents for raw
  table-rendering comparison, or a Textual/Bubble Tea `SB.4` log/viewport
  fixture where subprocess and streaming safety become more visible.
- Keep public comparison claims conservative until repeated runs and
  real-terminal evidence exist.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Textual SB.4 LogRegion Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the second Textual M3.9 peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.4 LogRegion Tailing And Scrollback` is launch-relevant because Fleury's
  log/output story combines append pressure, tail anchoring, scrollback,
  filtering, selected-entry copy, and unsafe-output handling.
- Textual `8.2.7` has a first-party `Log` widget and app test harness. PyPI
  currently lists `8.2.7` as the latest release on 2026-06-01, and the Textual
  Log docs expose `write_lines`, `scroll_end`, `scroll_to`, `line_count`, and
  `lines`.
- Textual `Log` does not provide a Fleury-equivalent semantic app graph,
  sanitizer/redaction policy, filter/search primitive, or selected-entry copy
  model. The fixture records those as app-owned.

Changes:
- Added
  [Textual SB.4 LogRegion fixture](../../peer-fixtures/textual/sb4_log_region)
  with pinned `textual==8.2.7` requirements.
- The fixture app mounts a 100k-line Textual `Log`, appends 1000 generated log
  rows, injects unsafe ANSI/OSC/control/secret payloads into generated raw
  lines, sanitizes before Log ingestion, jumps to scrollback, returns to tail,
  copies the selected entry, filters for appended rows, and reports widget/app
  state for the manifest query metric.
- Added `test_sb4_log_region.py`, a direct behavior script that drives append,
  scrollback, tail, copy, filter, and unsafe-leak assertions through Textual
  `run_test`.
- Added `sb4_log_benchmark.py`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with all
  `SB.4` required metrics, correctness gates, environment metadata, ergonomics
  ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-line plus 1000-append, 3-iteration run at
  [textual-sb4-log-region-2026-06-01.json](../../peer-fixtures/textual/sb4_log_region/results/textual-sb4-log-region-2026-06-01.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone notes,
  decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and evidence.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Textual `8.2.7`, Python `3.12.13`, macOS, terminal size
  `120x32`, and `textual-run-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 1291095 us, first-render p95
  171195 us, append-burst p95 125833 us, scrollback-jump p95 52529 us,
  scroll-to-tail p95 38089 us, selected-entry copy p95 73883 us,
  filter-query p95 59517 us, widget/app-state query p95 101 us,
  unsafe artifact leak count 0, max RSS delta 38879232 bytes, app LOC 177,
  test LOC 55.
- All three `SB.4` claim gates passed: tail anchoring was correct, copied text
  was sanitized, and unsafe output leak count was zero.

Decisions:
- Preserve ownership boundaries in peer fixtures. Textual owns append rendering
  and scroll state through `Log`; sanitizer/redaction, filtering,
  selected-entry state, and copy/export are fixture-owned app code.
- Continue keeping checked-in `comparative-benchmark-manifest.json` with empty
  `peerRuns`; use `benchmark-result --output=...` to create accepted manifest
  copies.

Correction:
- The first behavior run failed because the fixture helper property was named
  `_log`, shadowing Textual `App` internals during `App.__init__`. Renamed it to
  `_log_widget`; behavior and compile checks then passed.

Validation:
- `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/fleury-textual-site .../python3 test_sb4_log_region.py`
  passed.
- `PYTHONPATH=/tmp/fleury-textual-site .../python3 -m py_compile
  sb4_log_app.py test_sb4_log_region.py sb4_log_benchmark.py` passed.
- A 1000-row/128-append smoke artifact validated through `benchmark-result`;
  the temporary smoke file was not retained.
- Saved artifact validation passed with:
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/textual/sb4_log_region/results/textual-sb4-log-region-2026-06-01.json --output=/tmp/fleury-textual-sb4-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.

Next:
- Continue M3.9 with a non-Textual peer for `SB.4` or a raw-rendering peer for
  `SB.3`, likely Bubble Tea/OpenTUI/Ratatui depending on which fixture can most
  honestly mirror the contract.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: these are pinned local harness
  runs, not repeated real-terminal evidence.

## 2026-06-01 - M3.9 Ratatui SB.3 DataTable Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the first Ratatui M3.9 peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` still needs raw-rendering peer evidence beside the
  Textual full-app fixture. Ratatui is the strongest Rust immediate-rendering
  peer for this shape because it provides first-party `Table`, `TableState`, and
  `Buffer` primitives.
- The comparison boundary is different from Fleury and Textual: Ratatui renders
  widgets into a buffer, while the fixture app owns retained rows, selected-row
  state, visible-window slicing, navigation policy, copy/export, and the query
  surface used for the manifest's `semanticOrTestQueryUs` metric.

Changes:
- Added
  [Ratatui SB.3 DataTable fixture](../../peer-fixtures/ratatui/sb3_datatable)
  as a pinned Cargo package using Ratatui `0.30.0`.
- The fixture app builds 100k eight-column rows, renders a Ratatui `Table` into a
  `Buffer`, drives arrow/page/jump-to-end movement, copies the selected row as
  sanitized TSV, and queries rendered buffer text plus app-owned state.
- Added `tests/sb3_datatable_test.rs`, which proves navigation, visible-window
  bounds, selected final row, selected-row buffer visibility, and sanitized exact
  TSV copy.
- Added `src/main.rs`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with all `SB.3`
  required metrics, correctness gates, environment metadata, ergonomics
  ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-row, 20-iteration run at
  [ratatui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json](../../peer-fixtures/ratatui/sb3_datatable/results/ratatui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone notes,
  decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and evidence.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Ratatui `0.30.0`, Rust `1.93.1`, macOS, terminal size
  `120x32`, and `ratatui-buffer-render-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 31690 us, first-render p95 272 us,
  arrow-move p95 223 us, page-move p95 235 us, jump-to-end p95 257 us,
  selected-row copy p95 10 us, buffer/state query p95 24 us, RSS delta
  36175872 bytes, app LOC 232, benchmark LOC 458, test LOC 31.
- All three `SB.3` claim gates passed: visible window stayed bounded, final row
  selection was correct after jump, and selected-row copy/export was sanitized
  and exact.

Decisions:
- Keep immediate-renderer peer fixtures labeled as buffer-render harnesses. They
  can show raw render throughput, but they are not evidence of app-kernel,
  semantic-graph, retained-widget, or developer-tool workflow parity.
- Preserve ownership boundaries in scorecard language: Ratatui owns
  `Table`/`TableState`/`Buffer` rendering; the fixture app owns retained data,
  virtualization policy, navigation, copy/export, and query state.
- Continue keeping checked-in `comparative-benchmark-manifest.json` with empty
  `peerRuns`; use `benchmark-result --output=...` to create accepted manifest
  copies.

Corrections:
- The first behavior test used an 80-column buffer, which truncated the selected
  row ID and made the final-row visibility assertion fail. The test now uses the
  same 120-column width as the benchmark contract.
- The first saved artifact reported `lineOfCodeCount` for app plus benchmark
  runner. The fixture now reports app LOC from `src/lib.rs` and separately
  records benchmark LOC from `src/main.rs`.
- The first query metric only cloned cached state and could round to 0 us. The
  fixture now scans rendered buffer text for the selected row ID while also
  reading app-owned state.
- Process high-water RSS did not show allocation growth after warmups on this
  local run. The fixture now records current process RSS through `ps -o rss=`
  and takes the max of per-sample and run-level RSS delta.

Validation:
- `cargo fmt` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- Saved artifact generated with
  `cargo run --release -- --warmup=2 --iterations=20 --rows=100000 --json --output=results/ratatui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json`.
- Saved artifact validation passed with:
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/ratatui/sb3_datatable/results/ratatui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-01.json --output=/tmp/fleury-ratatui-sb3-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.

Next:
- Continue M3.9 with either an OpenTUI `SB.3` fixture to compare another
  renderer-forward peer, or a Bubble Tea/OpenTUI `SB.4` log/viewport fixture to
  broaden the streaming-output comparison beyond Textual.
- Keep public comparison language conservative until there are repeated runs,
  real-terminal variance, and at least one more non-Dart peer fixture for the
  same scenario family.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Bubble Tea SB.4 LogRegion Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the first Bubble Tea/Bubbles M3.9 log/viewport
peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.4 LogRegion Tailing And Scrollback` needed a non-Textual
  log/viewport peer to test whether Fleury's log story is actually
  differentiated beyond one Python framework comparison.
- Bubble Tea `2.0.7` supplies the model/update/view shape, and Bubbles
  `2.1.0` supplies the viewport component used for content and scrolling.
  Local `go list -m -versions charm.land/bubbletea/v2 charm.land/bubbles/v2`
  confirmed those module versions for this fixture pass.
- This is a component/model harness, not a real-terminal run. It measures a
  Bubble Tea model driven directly through messages and Bubbles viewport state,
  so comparison language must not imply app-kernel, semantic-graph, or
  real-terminal parity.

Changes:
- Added
  [Bubble Tea SB.4 LogRegion fixture](../../peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb4_log_region)
  with a pinned Go module using Bubble Tea `2.0.7` and Bubbles `2.1.0`.
- The fixture builds 100k starting log entries, appends 1000 generated rows,
  sanitizes unsafe ANSI/OSC/control/secret payloads before viewport ingestion,
  drives scrollback and tail movement, copies the selected entry, filters for
  appended rows, and reports app/model-state query timing.
- Added `app_test.go`, which proves append behavior, tail anchoring,
  scrollback selection, selected-entry copy, filtering, and unsafe-leak
  assertions against the local model.
- Added `main.go`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with all `SB.4`
  required metrics, correctness gates, environment metadata, ergonomics
  ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-line plus 1000-append, 5-iteration run at
  [bubbletea-sb4-log-region-2026-06-02.json](../../peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb4_log_region/results/bubbletea-sb4-log-region-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone
  notes, decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and
  ownership notes.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Bubble Tea `2.0.7`, Bubbles `2.1.0`, Go `1.25.0`,
  macOS, terminal size `120x32`, and `bubbletea-viewport-model-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 1753125 us, first-render p95 350 us,
  append-burst p95 902997 us, scrollback-jump p95 441959 us, scroll-to-tail
  p95 445006 us, selected-entry copy p95 9 us, filter-query p95 329016 us,
  app/model-state query p95 18 us, unsafe artifact leak count 0, RSS delta
  5074944 bytes, app LOC 304, benchmark LOC 508, test LOC 57.
- All three `SB.4` claim gates passed: tail anchoring was correct, copied text
  was sanitized, and unsafe output leak count was zero.

Decisions:
- Preserve ownership boundaries in Bubble Tea/Bubbles fixtures. Bubble Tea
  owns the model/update/view structure and Bubbles owns viewport content/scroll
  primitives; sanitizer/redaction, filtering, selected-entry state,
  copy/export, and query state are fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as app/model-state evidence, not semantic graph
  evidence. Bubble Tea/Bubbles does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app
  graph for this fixture.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: this is local harness evidence
  and should not support superiority claims until repeated runs and
  real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first compile pass used old assumptions from Bubble Tea v1/Bubbles v1:
  `View()` now returns `tea.View`, and the viewport uses `PageUp`/`PageDown`.
  The fixture now uses `tea.NewView(...)` and the Bubbles v2 viewport methods.
- The first unsafe-leak counter treated normal viewport line breaks as unsafe
  output. The fixture now separates visible-output leak counting from
  single-entry copy counting so normal rendered newlines do not fail the
  safety gate.

Validation:
- `gofmt -w app.go app_test.go main.go` passed.
- `go test ./...` passed.
- Saved artifact validation passed with:
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb4_log_region/results/bubbletea-sb4-log-region-2026-06-02.json --output=/tmp/fleury-bubbletea-sb4-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name scan for the legacy framework name, old research paths, and
  temporary smoke artifact names returned no matches.
- Peer-fixture cache scan found no `target`, `.python`, `__pycache__`,
  `.pytest_cache`, `node_modules`, `dist`, or `.turbo` directories.
- Process scan found no lingering Bubble Tea fixture or Go test/run processes.

Next:
- Continue M3.9 with either an OpenTUI `SB.3` or `SB.4` fixture for another
  renderer-forward comparison, or a Nocterm `SB.4` fixture to broaden Dart
  peer coverage.
- Start collecting repeated-run and real-terminal variance only after at least
  one more peer fixture exists for the same scenario family.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.4 LogRegion Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the Nocterm M3.9 log/viewport peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.4 LogRegion Tailing And Scrollback` had Textual and Bubble Tea/Bubbles
  peer evidence, but the closest Dart peer still lacked a log/scrollback run.
- Nocterm `0.6.0` supplies `ListView.builder`, lazy list rendering,
  `ScrollController`, `Text`, `NoctermTester`, and terminal-state test queries.
  It does not expose a Fleury-equivalent `LogRegion` with sanitization,
  retained-log filtering, selected-entry copy/export, or semantic app graph
  nodes.

  Comparison language must treat terminal/app-state queries as semantic
  substitutes, not as Fleury-style semantic graph parity.

  [Nocterm SB.4 LogRegion fixture](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb4_log_region)
  with a pinned Nocterm `0.6.0` package.

  sanitizes ANSI/OSC/control/secret payloads before Nocterm `Text` rendering,
  drives scrollback and tail movement through `ScrollController`, copies the
  selected entry, filters for appended rows, and reports terminal/app-state query
  timing.
- Added `log_region_benchmark_test.dart`, proving append behavior, tail
  anchoring, scrollback selection, selected-entry copy, filtering, and unsafe
  visible/copy artifact assertions.
- Added `sb4_log_region_benchmark.dart`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun`
  JSON with all `SB.4` required metrics, correctness gates, environment

  notes, decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and
  ownership notes.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size
  `120x32`, and `nocterm-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 1894263 us, first-render p95 8508 us,
  append-burst p95 88462 us, scrollback-jump p95 15173 us, scroll-to-tail p95
  12426 us, selected-entry copy p95 8560 us, filter-query p95 135920 us,
  terminal/app-state query p95 456 us, unsafe artifact leak count 0, RSS delta
  6713344 bytes, app LOC 244, benchmark LOC 461, test LOC 42.
- All three `SB.4` claim gates passed: tail anchoring was correct, copied text
  was sanitized, and unsafe output leak count was zero.

Decisions:
- Preserve ownership boundaries in Nocterm fixtures. Nocterm owns
  `ListView.builder`, `ScrollController`, `Text`, and terminal-state test
  queries; sanitizer/redaction, filtering, selected-entry state, copy/export,
  and query state are fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as terminal/app-state evidence, not semantic graph
  evidence. Nocterm does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app graph for this
  fixture.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: this is local harness evidence
  and should not support superiority claims until repeated runs and
  real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first filter test updated selected state but did not re-scroll the
  filtered viewport, so the final appended row could be selected while not
  visible in `tester.terminalState`. The fixture now calls
  `scrollDisplayedToEnd()` after filtering in both the behavior test and
  benchmark, and the filter metric includes that viewport correction.

Validation:
- `dart format lib test bin` passed.
- `dart analyze` passed.
- `dart test` passed.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 OpenTUI SB.3 DataTable Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the OpenTUI `SB.3` peer-fixture slice

Context:
- M3.9 still needed OpenTUI evidence for a renderer-forward TypeScript/native
  peer. The scorecard listed OpenTUI as a native-core throughput target, but
  `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows` behavior was unmeasured.
- OpenTUI `@opentui/core` remains `0.3.1` on npm. The fixture pins that
  version and uses local Bun `1.3.14`.
- `TextTableRenderable` maps cleanly to `SB.3`, but OpenTUI does not provide a
  Fleury-equivalent app kernel or semantic app graph for this fixture. The
  comparison must separate table rendering from app-owned retained state,
  navigation, copy/export, and query behavior.

Changes:
- Added
  [OpenTUI SB.3 DataTable fixture](../../peer-fixtures/opentui/sb3_datatable)
  with pinned `@opentui/core` `0.3.1` and local `bun` `1.3.14`.
- Added `lib/table_app.js`, which builds 100k generated rows, sanitizes
  unsafe title payloads, adapts cell text into OpenTUI styled chunks, keeps a
  bounded visible row window, drives arrow/page/jump navigation, copies the
  selected row as TSV, and exposes frame/app-state query snapshots.
- Added `test/table_app.test.js`, proving navigation, visible-window bounds,
  final-row rendering, exact sanitized copy/export, and zero visible/copy
  unsafe leaks.
- Added `bin/sb3_datatable_benchmark.js`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun`
  JSON with all `SB.3` required metrics, correctness gates, environment
  metadata, ergonomics ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-row, 5-iteration run at
  [opentui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json](../../peer-fixtures/opentui/sb3_datatable/results/opentui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone
  notes, decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and
  ownership notes.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses OpenTUI `0.3.1`, Bun `1.3.14`, macOS, terminal size
  `120x32`, and `opentui-test-renderer-memory` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 42056 us, first-render p95 1290 us,
  arrow-move p95 1725 us, page-move p95 1795 us, jump-to-end p95 1648 us,
  selected-row copy p95 17 us, frame/app-state query p95 29 us, unsafe artifact
  leak count 0, RSS delta 142475264 bytes, app LOC 242, benchmark LOC 357, and
  test LOC 54.
- All three `SB.3` claim gates passed: visible window stayed bounded,
  jump-to-end selected and rendered `RUN-099999`, and copied TSV matched the
  generated row with no escape/control/secret leakage.

Decisions:
- Preserve OpenTUI ownership boundaries. OpenTUI owns `TextTableRenderable`,
  styled text chunks, and the native-backed test renderer; sanitizer/redaction,
  retained row generation, visible slicing, navigation, selected-row
  copy/export, and frame/app-state query are fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as frame/app-state evidence, not semantic graph
  evidence. OpenTUI does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app graph in this
  fixture.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: this is local test-renderer
  evidence and should not support superiority claims until repeated runs and
  real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first smoke command used `--warmup=0`, which the initial benchmark
  parser rejected. The parser now accepts zero warmup iterations while still
  requiring positive measured iterations and row counts.
- The initial benchmark unsafe-frame check used the copy sanitizer helper for a
  rendered frame. The benchmark now uses the visible-frame unsafe-text helper.

Validation:
- `npm install` passed in `peer-fixtures/opentui/sb3_datatable`.
- `npm test` passed.
- Smoke artifact generated with
  `npm run benchmark -- --warmup=0 --iterations=1 --rows=1000 --json --output=<tmp-smoke-json>`.
- Smoke artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=<tmp-smoke-json> --output=<tmp-smoke-manifest-json> --json`.
- Saved artifact generated with
  `npm run benchmark -- --warmup=2 --iterations=5 --rows=100000 --json --output=results/opentui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json`.
- Saved artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/opentui/sb3_datatable/results/opentui-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json --output=/tmp/fleury-opentui-sb3-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.3 DataTable Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the Nocterm `SB.3` peer-fixture slice

Context:
- M3.9 still needed the closest Dart peer measured against
  `SB.3 DataTable 100k Rows`. Nocterm had counter, text-editing, and
  log/viewport evidence, but the scorecard still listed table/list depth as
  unmeasured.
- Nocterm `0.6.0` supplies `ListView.builder`, lazy list rendering,
  `ScrollController`, `Text`, `NoctermTester`, and terminal-state test queries.
  It does not expose a dedicated `DataTable` widget in the package source.
- This is a `nocterm-test-harness` run, not repeated real-terminal evidence.
  Comparison language must treat terminal/app-state queries as semantic
  substitutes, not as Fleury-style semantic graph parity.

Changes:
- Added
  [Nocterm SB.3 DataTable fixture](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb3_datatable)
  with a pinned Nocterm `0.6.0` package.

  duration, progress, warning, and update columns; sanitizes unsafe title
  payloads; renders a header plus a lazy Nocterm list; drives arrow, page, and
  jump-to-end navigation; copies the selected row as TSV; and exposes
  terminal/app-state query snapshots.
- Added `table_benchmark_test.dart`, proving bounded visible rows, navigation,
  final-row rendering, exact sanitized copy/export, and zero visible/copy
  unsafe leaks.
- Added `sb3_datatable_benchmark.dart`, producing `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun`
  JSON with all `SB.3` required metrics, correctness gates, environment
  metadata, ergonomics ownership flags, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 100k-row, 5-iteration run at
  [nocterm-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb3_datatable/results/nocterm-sb3-datatable-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone
  notes, decision log, and implementation README with the new fixture and
  ownership notes.

Evidence:
- The saved run uses Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size
  `120x32`, and `nocterm-test-harness` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: mount p95 451662 us, first-render p95 712 us,
  arrow-move p95 6480 us, page-move p95 5660 us, jump-to-end p95 4543 us,
  selected-row copy p95 1194 us, terminal/app-state query p95 1056 us, unsafe
  artifact leak count 0, RSS delta 5754880 bytes, app LOC 283, benchmark LOC
  434, and test LOC 48.
- All three `SB.3` claim gates passed: visible window stayed bounded,
  jump-to-end selected and rendered `RUN-099999`, and copied TSV matched the
  generated row with no escape/control/secret leakage.

Decisions:
- Preserve Nocterm table/list ownership boundaries. Nocterm owns
  `ListView.builder`, `ScrollController`, `Text`, and terminal-state test
  queries; sanitizer/redaction, table formatting, retained row generation,
  visible-window policy, navigation, selection state, selected-row copy/export,
  and terminal/app-state query are fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as terminal/app-state evidence, not semantic graph
  evidence. Nocterm does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app graph in this
  fixture.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: this is local test-harness
  evidence and should not support superiority claims until repeated runs and
  real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first test pass used a nonexistent matcher name,
  `lessThanOrEqual`; it now uses the Dart matcher
  `lessThanOrEqualTo`.
- The first unsafe-copy check treated ordinary TSV tabs and row newlines as
  unsafe control leakage. The fixture now separates visible-frame unsafe text
  checks from TSV copy safety, allowing expected tab/newline separators while
  still rejecting terminal controls and secret payloads.

  fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as app-state evidence, not semantic graph evidence.
  Bubble Tea/Bubbles does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app graph in this
  fixture.
- Keep public comparison language conservative: this is local model/component
  harness evidence and should not support superiority claims until repeated
  runs and real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first behavior test showed the selection marker appeared repeatedly in
  generated text, so replacing one instance did not prove the selected range
  changed as intended. The fixture now emits a unique `segment-alpha` marker
  and repeats `segment-beta` for the rest of the generated corpus.
- The first smoke run showed `textinput.SetValue` does not refresh matched
  suggestions after `SetSuggestions`; Bubbles updates suggestions through the
  normal input `Update` path. The fixture now prepares completion by resetting
  the composer and typing `git che` through Bubbles `Update` calls before
  pressing Tab.
- The first benchmark history sequence measured previous/previous/next while
  the correctness snapshot expected the second previous item. The saved run
  measures two previous navigations so the state evidence matches the intended
  history recall behavior.

Validation:
- `go mod tidy` passed in `peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb2_text_editing`.
- `gofmt -w app.go app_test.go main.go` completed.
- `go test ./...` passed.
- Smoke artifact generated with
  `go run . --warmup=0 --iterations=1 --text-chars=2000 --json --output=<tmp-smoke-json>`.
- Smoke artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=<tmp-smoke-json> --output=<tmp-smoke-manifest-json> --json`.
- Saved artifact generated with
  `go run . --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --text-chars=10000 --json --output=results/bubbletea-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json`.
- Saved artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb2_text_editing/results/bubbletea-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json --output=/tmp/fleury-bubbletea-sb2-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Stale-name scan for the legacy framework name, old research paths, and
  temporary smoke artifact names returned no matches.
- Bubble Tea SB.2 cross-link scan found the fixture, run artifact, README,
  scenario lab, peer scorecard, milestone, decision-log, and journal
  references.
- Peer-fixture cache scan found no `target`, `.python`, `__pycache__`,
  `.pytest_cache`, `node_modules`, `dist`, `.turbo`, or `.bun` directories.
- Process scan found no lingering Bubble Tea SB.2 fixture, Go test, Go

Next:

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Ink SB.2 Text Editing Peer Fixture

Status: Complete for the Ink text-editing peer-fixture slice

Context:
- `SB.2 Text Editing Composer Stress` had Fleury, Nocterm, Textual, and
  Bubble Tea/Bubbles evidence. Ink remained an important React/npm peer because
  it is a natural choice for JavaScript/TypeScript developers who want a
  reactive CLI UI.
- Current package metadata was refreshed with the bundled workspace Node
  runtime because Ink `7.0.5` requires Node `>=22`; the system Node in this
  environment is `v20.13.1`, while the bundled runtime is `v24.14.0`.
- The fixture should credit Ink for React terminal rendering and ecosystem
  input widgets while recording which app behaviors still have to be supplied
  outside the peer framework/components.

Changes:
- Added
  [Ink SB.2 text editing fixture](../../peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing)
  with pinned Ink `7.0.5`, React `19.2.7`, `react-ink-textarea` `0.1.3`,
  `ink-text-input` `6.0.0`, and `ink-testing-library` `4.0.0`.
- Added `lib/text_editing_app.js`, which builds a mixed-width
  `react-ink-textarea` editor, a command-composer `ink-text-input`, a masked
  secret input, fixture-owned selection replacement, fixture-owned undo/redo,
  fixture-owned submission history, fixture-owned completion acceptance, and
  app-state snapshots.
- Added `test/text_editing_app.test.js`, proving mixed-width retention,
  selection replacement, undo/redo, history recall, completion acceptance,
  paste insertion, and masked secret redaction in the rendered Ink frame.
- Added `bin/sb2_text_editing_benchmark.js`, producing
  `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` JSON with all `SB.2` required metrics, correctness
  gates, ownership flags, package versions, and source/test artifact links.
- Saved the local 10k-character, 5-iteration run at
  [ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json](../../peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing/results/ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, implementation README, scenario lab, peer
  scorecard, milestone notes, and decision log with the new fixture and
  ownership notes.

Evidence:
- Current registry checks: `npm view ink version engines` returned Ink
  `7.0.5` with `node >=22`; `npm view ink-text-input version` returned
  `6.0.0`; `npm view react-ink-textarea version` returned `0.1.3`; and
  `npm view ink-testing-library version` returned `4.0.0`.
- The saved run uses Node `v24.14.0`, Ink `7.0.5`, React `19.2.7`,
  `ink-text-input` `6.0.0`, `react-ink-textarea` `0.1.3`, macOS, terminal
  size `120x32`, and `ink-testing-library-memory` mode.
- Metrics from the saved run: cursor-move p95 1357 us,
  insertion/deletion p95 503 us, selection p95 346 us, undo/redo p95 128 us,
  history navigation p95 13 us, completion accept p95 3 us, paste completion
  p95 619 us, app-state/frame query p95 422487 us, RSS delta 17924096 bytes,
  app LOC 197, benchmark LOC 339, and test LOC 40.
- All three `SB.2` claim gates passed: mixed-width text stayed valid,
  selection/undo state stayed correct, and the raw secret value stayed out of
  rendered frames.

Decisions:
- Preserve Ink ownership boundaries. Ink owns the React terminal renderer;
  `react-ink-textarea` owns the multiline textarea display/editing component;
  `ink-text-input` owns the single-line and masked input display. Selection
  replacement, redo, submission history, completion acceptance, and app-state
  query snapshots are fixture-owned app code.
- Treat the query metric as rendered-frame plus app-state evidence, not
  semantic graph evidence. Ink does not expose a Fleury-style semantic app
  graph in this fixture.
- Keep comparison language conservative. This is local
  `ink-testing-library-memory` evidence and should not support superiority
  claims until repeated runs and real-terminal variance exist.

Corrections:
- The first smoke artifact generated successfully, but its correctness evidence
  text hard-coded "10k" for the mixed-width editor even when `--text-chars`
  used a smaller smoke size. The benchmark now reports the configured
  character count in that evidence string.

Validation:
- `PATH=/Users/dan/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH npm install` passed with 42 packages added and 0 reported vulnerabilities.
- `PATH=/Users/dan/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH npm test` passed.
- Smoke artifact generated with
  `npm run benchmark -- --warmup=0 --iterations=1 --text-chars=2000 --json --output=<tmp-smoke-json>`.
- Smoke artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=<tmp-smoke-json> --output=<tmp-smoke-manifest-json> --json`.
- Saved artifact generated with
  `npm run benchmark -- --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --text-chars=10000 --json --output=results/ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json`.
- Saved artifact validation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-result --input=peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing/results/ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json --output=/tmp/fleury-ink-sb2-manifest.json --json`.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-manifest --json` passed.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Repeated-Run Benchmark Variance Evidence

Status: Complete for the first repeated-run variance tooling and Ink evidence
slice

Context:
- M3.9 had a growing set of single saved peer artifacts. Those artifacts prove
  fixture shape, manifest compatibility, ownership boundaries, and correctness
  gates, but they are too weak for performance or superiority claims.
- The next evidence gap was not another one-off fixture. It was a way to make
  repeated local peer runs explicit, comparable, and auditable without
  hand-editing `peerRuns` into the checked-in comparative manifest.

Changes:
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-variance`, which accepts repeated
  `--input` files or directories of `fleuryPeerBenchmarkRun` artifacts,
  validates every run through the existing comparative benchmark manifest,
  requires one peer/scenario group, reports comparable source/environment/
  fixture identity, summarizes correctness gate counts, and computes
  per-metric spread over each run's primary metric value.
- Added strict-readiness behavior: `--strict` exits non-zero unless the run set
  meets `--min-runs`, keeps one peer/scenario/source version/terminal mode/
  terminal size/fixture directory/fixture command group, and all correctness
  gates pass.
- Added focused tests for `benchmark-variance` under
  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart).
- Saved three comparable Ink `SB.2` run artifacts under
  [peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing/results/variance](../../peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing/results/variance).
- Saved the first strict-passing repeated-run summary at
  [ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json](benchmark-variance/ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the benchmark README, peer fixture README, scenario lab, peer
  scorecard, milestone notes, and decision log with the variance workflow.

Evidence:
- The strict Ink `SB.2` variance summary covers three comparable local
  `ink-testing-library-memory` runs with Node `v24.14.0`, Ink `7.0.5`, React
  `19.2.7`, `ink-text-input` `6.0.0`, `react-ink-textarea` `0.1.3`, terminal
  size `120x32`, one warmup iteration, and five measured iterations per run.
- The summary reports `strictPass: true`, `sufficientRunCount: true`,
  `comparable: true`, no errors, no comparable issues, and all three `SB.2`
  correctness gates passing in all runs.
- Key variance metrics from the summary: cursor-move p95 median 2449 us with
  59.004% relative spread; insertion/deletion p95 median 961 us with 54.422%
  spread; selection p95 median 442 us with 54.072% spread; paste p95 median
  997 us with 90.171% spread; app-state/frame query p95 median 152763 us with
  25.67% spread.

Decisions:
- Keep variance evidence separate from the checked-in
  `comparative-benchmark-manifest.json`. The manifest remains a contract with
  empty `peerRuns`; variance summaries are saved as explicit evidence
  artifacts.
- Treat `strictPass` as local repeated-run readiness, not launch-level
  superiority. Real-terminal variance, cross-machine variance, and repeated
  runs across the other peers are still required before public performance
  claims.
- Use each metric's primary artifact value for variance: scalar metrics use the
  scalar value; metric maps prefer `p95`, then `median`, `max`, and `value`.

Corrections:
- The first attempt to run the Ink repeated-run shell loop used invalid zsh
  syntax, `PATH=... for run ...`. The corrected loop assigns the bundled Node
  runtime path inside the loop before each `npm run benchmark` command.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` completed.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed.
- `npm install` in `peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing` passed with 42 packages
  added and 0 reported vulnerabilities.
- Three saved Ink variance input artifacts were generated with
  `npm run benchmark -- --warmup=1 --iterations=5 --text-chars=10000 --output=results/variance/<run-json>`.
- Strict variance summary generation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-variance --input=peer-fixtures/ink/sb2_text_editing/results/variance --min-runs=3 --strict --output=docs/implementation/benchmark-variance/ink-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json --json`.

Next:
- Repeat the variance workflow for other high-value peer/scenario groups,
  especially Fleury-vs-Nocterm `SB.2`, DataTable `SB.3`, and LogRegion
  `SB.4`, then start real-terminal variance where the harness can run through
  an actual terminal mode.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.2 Repeated-Run Variance

Status: Complete for the closest-Dart-peer text-editing variance slice

Context:
- After adding `benchmark-variance` and proving it with Ink `SB.2`, the next
  strongest comparison target was Nocterm `SB.2` because Nocterm is Fleury's
  closest Dart/Flutter-like peer.
- The existing Nocterm fixture was already pinned to Nocterm `0.6.0` and had a
  validated single saved `SB.2` artifact. The open evidence gap was whether a
  local repeated run stayed stable enough to support any timing interpretation.

Changes:
- Generated three comparable Nocterm `SB.2` run artifacts under
  [peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing/results/variance](../../peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing/results/variance).
- Saved the strict-passing variance summary at
  [nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json](benchmark-variance/nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the peer fixture README, implementation README, scenario lab,
  milestone notes, and peer scorecard with the new variance artifact and the
  high-spread interpretation.

Evidence:
- The summary covers three comparable local `nocterm-test-harness` runs over
  Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size `80x24`, 2 warmups, and
  10 measured iterations per run.
- The summary reports `strictPass: true`, `sufficientRunCount: true`,
  `comparable: true`, no errors, no comparable issues, and all three `SB.2`
  correctness gates passing in all runs.
- Key variance metrics from the summary: cursor-move p95 median 24555 us with
  186.231% relative spread; insertion/deletion p95 median 90846 us with
  180.534% spread; selection p95 median 46276 us with 100.253% spread; paste
  p95 median 45691 us with 438.988% spread; test-query p95 median 4042 us with
  731.173% spread.

Decisions:
- Preserve the high-spread run set rather than rerunning away the outlier. The
  variance artifact exists to reveal instability, not to curate a flattering
  timing set.
- Continue treating the Nocterm fixture as local harness evidence. The test
  path emits an OSC-52-style clipboard sequence while exercising copy behavior,
  so this is not terminal-safety or real-terminal evidence.
- Keep Nocterm `SB.2` timing language conservative until repeated harness
  runs, real-terminal mode, and cross-machine variance tell a clearer story.

Validation:
- `dart pub get` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- `dart analyze` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.
- `dart test` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing`.

  `dart run bin/sb2_text_editing_benchmark.dart --warmup=2 --iterations=10 --text-chars=10000 --output=results/variance/<run-json>`.
- Strict variance summary generation passed with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart benchmark-variance --input=peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb2_text_editing/results/variance --min-runs=3 --strict --output=docs/implementation/benchmark-variance/nocterm-sb2-text-editing-2026-06-02.json --json`.

Next:

  the `SB.3` and `SB.4` groups where single-run peer evidence already exists.
  After that, start real-terminal variance where a fixture can run outside the
  in-memory/test harness.

  pinned dependency set successfully.

  runs over Ratatui `0.30.0`, Rust `1.93.1`, macOS, terminal size `120x32`,
  two warmups, and twenty measured iterations per run.
- The summary reports `strictPass: true`, `sufficientRunCount: true`,
  `comparable: true`, no errors, no comparable issues, and all three `SB.3`
  correctness gates passing in all runs.
- Key variance metrics from the summary: mount p95 median 78320 us with

  app-state/buffer query p95 median 198 us with 159.091% spread; RSS delta
  median 36208640 bytes with 0.407% spread.

Decisions:
- Preserve the high-spread run set. The first run's page movement was slower
  than the later runs, and that is exactly the kind of variance the summary is
  meant to expose.
- Treat this as local buffer-render harness evidence only. Ratatui owns table
  and buffer rendering; the fixture owns retained rows, visible slicing,
  navigation policy, selected-row copy/export, and state/buffer query.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.9 Nocterm SB.3 Repeated-Run Variance

Status: Complete for the closest-Dart-peer data-widget variance slice

Context:
- After the Ratatui `SB.3` variance pass, the next highest-value data-widget
  peer was Nocterm because it is Fleury's closest Dart/Flutter-like peer.

  `ListView.builder`, `ScrollController`, `Text`, and terminal-state test
  queries, while the fixture owns table formatting, retained rows, selection,
  visible-window policy, copy/export, and query state.

  Nocterm `0.6.0`, Dart `3.12.1`, macOS, terminal size `120x32`, two warmups,
  and five measured iterations per run.
- The summary reports `strictPass: true`, `sufficientRunCount: true`,
  `comparable: true`, no errors, no comparable issues, and all three `SB.3`
  correctness gates passing in all runs.
- Key variance metrics from the summary: mount p95 median 1170683 us with
  41.409% relative spread; first-render p95 median 2185 us with 72.952%
  spread; arrow-move p95 median 18291 us with 52.939% spread; page-move p95
  median 22230 us with 150.99% spread; jump-to-end p95 median 8622 us with

  terminal/app-state query p95 median 2751 us with 105.234% spread. RSS delta

Decisions:

  older single artifact. The variance artifact should reveal local timing

- Treat this as local Nocterm test-harness evidence, not real-terminal,
  terminal-safety, or Fleury-equivalent semantic graph evidence. The fixture
  test path emits an OSC-52-style clipboard sequence while exercising copy
  behavior.
- Keep DataTable public-claim language gated on more `SB.3` repeated runs,
  real-terminal variance, cross-machine variance, and ergonomics comparisons.

Validation:

- `dart analyze` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb3_datatable`.
- `dart test` passed in `peer-fixtures/nocterm/sb3_datatable`.

  and five measured iterations per run.

  correctness gates passing in all runs.

  filtering, selected-entry state, copy/export, and app-state query shape.

  side note, because log/viewport claims are only useful if untrusted terminal
  payloads stay contained.

  `charm.land/glamour/v2` and requires Go `>=1.25.8`, so the fixture records
  Go `1.25.8` rather than the local `1.25.0` used by earlier Bubble Tea
  fixtures.

Changes:
- Added `peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb5_streaming_markdown`.
- Implemented `Sb5StreamingMarkdownModel` around Bubble Tea model/update/view,
  Bubbles viewport state, and Glamour full-document terminal Markdown

  selected-block copy, Markdown metadata, and app/model-state query.
- Added behavior test and benchmark generator.

  [bubbletea-sb5-streaming-markdown-2026-06-02.json](../../peer-fixtures/bubbletea/sb5_streaming_markdown/results/bubbletea-sb5-streaming-markdown-2026-06-02.json).
