- Source/current assumptions: Bubble Tea `2.0.7`, Bubbles `2.1.0`, Glamour
  `2.0.0`, canonical Glamour docs at `https://pkg.go.dev/charm.land/glamour/v2`.
- `go mod tidy` passed and captured the `go 1.25.8` requirement.
- Behavior test passed.
- First saved artifact uses `--rows=10000` / 100 chunks, no variance, and
  `benchmark-result` accepted it.
- Metrics: chunk-update p95 145270 us, chunk-parse p95 852 us,
  chunk-frame p95 16742 us, final-render p95 13567 us,
  selected-block-copy p95 72 us, app/model-state query p95 63 us,
  unsafe frame count 0, sanitized chunks 41, unsafe links 8.

Decisions:
- Treat this as Charm ecosystem Markdown evidence, not evidence that Bubble
  Tea/Bubbles provides an incremental Markdown widget. The fixture re-renders
  the full document through Glamour after each append.
- Keep sanitizer, visible URL fallback, selected-block copy, Markdown
  metadata, and model-state query classified as fixture-owned app code.
- Do not count renderer-owned SGR styling or OSC 8 hyperlink wrappers as
  unsafe-frame leaks. Continue counting OSC 52 clipboard payloads, secrets,
  BEL/CR, and non-SGR escape residue as unsafe output leakage.
- Treat the 100-chunk artifact as first peer evidence only. Repeated,
  full-scale 1000-chunk, cross-machine, and real-terminal Bubble Tea evidence
  remains open.

Validation:
- `go test ./...` passed.
- `go run . --warmup=1 --iterations=3 --rows=10000 --json --output=results/bubbletea-sb5-streaming-markdown-2026-06-02.json` passed.

Next:

  OpenTUI/Ink `SB.5` equivalents.
- Keep M2.10 real-terminal matrix evidence open until a real terminal capture

## 2026-06-02 - M3.7 Same-Identity Child-List Replacement No-Op

Status: Complete for this render-engine hardening slice

Context:
- After M3.9 peer expansion was stopped for the MVP cycle and M2.10 audit
  readiness was tightened, the next locally provable code-heavy launch gap was
  M3.7 render-engine discipline.
- Multi-child render objects still marked layout dirty whenever
  `replaceAllChildren` was called, even when element reconciliation supplied
  the same render child identities in the same order. That made ordinary
  rebuilds depend on accidental layout invalidation instead of explicit
  shape-changing changes.

Changes:
- Added a protected core helper for same-identity, same-order render child
  list checks.
- Made `RenderFlex`, `RenderStack`, `RenderIndexedStack`, `RenderWrap`, and
  internal `RenderNavigatorStack` treat same ordered children as a layout
  no-op while preserving layout invalidation for reorders, additions, and
  removals.
- Applied the same rule to `fleury_widgets` composition `RenderTable` with a
  package-local helper, keeping the public core export surface unchanged.
- Extended `SB.12 Layout Dirtiness Cache` with a `childListNoOpFrame` sample
  and layout performed/skipped metrics.
- Saved refreshed SB.12 evidence at
  [phase2-layout-dirtiness-child-list-2026-06-02.json](../../packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-child-list-2026-06-02.json).

Evidence:
- `SB.12` refreshed run passed on Dart 3.12.1, 20 measured iterations.
- Metrics: command-to-frame p95 3559 us; child-list no-op frame p95 4363 us;
  child-list no-op layout p95 0 performed / 1 skipped; paint-only,
  text-paint-only, and idle layout p95 remain 0 performed / 1 skipped.

Decisions:
- Keep this as first-party render-object discipline, not a public
  high-performance extension API.
- Do not export the child-list helper as package API for now. The
  `fleury_widgets` table render object uses a local helper so the optimization
  does not force a public core surface.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart packages/fleury/test/rendering/render_object_test.dart packages/fleury_widgets/test/table_test.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_object.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_flex.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_stack.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_wrap.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_navigator.dart packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/table.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_object_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/table_test.dart` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --json --save=benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-child-list-2026-06-02.json` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed after cleanup.

Next:
- Continue M3.7 only where a scenario or focused render test can prove a
  stable-shape boundary. M2.10/M2.9 still require external terminal/Windows
  evidence before they can close.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.7 ScrollView Viewport Scratch Paint

Status: Complete for this render-engine hardening slice

Context:
- The user clarified that more peer benchmark comparison work should be
  deferred until post-MVP, after the API is stable and major core development is
  done.
- With M3.9 stopped at the SB.5 variance checkpoint for this MVP cycle, the
  next locally provable core work was M3.7 performance-boundary hardening.
- `ScrollView` was still painting arbitrary tall children into a full
  child-height scratch buffer before copying the visible window. That was
  correct, but it made long document/form/panel scrollables allocate and scan
  more cells than the visible viewport needed.

Changes:
- Changed `ScrollView` paint so it still lays out the arbitrary child at full
  natural height, but paints through a viewport-sized scratch buffer using a
  translated negative child offset.
- Preserved `screenOffset` and `clipRect` semantics so selection, hit-testing,
  and nested clipped paint still use the child's full screen-space origin and
  the visible viewport clip.
- Made `CellBuffer` write APIs clip out-of-bounds translated paint writes while
  keeping reads strict. This lets generic render-object paints work against a
  viewport-sized scratch buffer without throwing on scrolled-off rows.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 milestone notes, and reactive render
  workstream so M3.9 peer expansion is post-MVP and M3.7 local core hardening
  is the active lane.

Evidence:
- Added `CellBuffer` regression coverage for clipped out-of-bounds grapheme
  writes, negative-column `writeText`, row-clipped `writeText`, and
  out-of-bounds protocol anchors.
- Added a `ScrollView` regression proving the child now receives a
  viewport-sized scratch buffer, a negative paint offset, and the expected
  screen-space clip while still rendering the visible scrolled content.
- Re-ran existing ScrollView selection tests to verify visible selection,
  scroll bounds, and auto-scroll behavior still work with translated scratch
  paint.

Decisions:
- Keep this as first-party render-engine discipline, not a public
  high-performance extension API.
- Do not add more peer fixtures or full comparison runs in this MVP cycle. Use
  the current peer evidence to inform API/core implementation, then resume
  public comparison work post-MVP when the launch API is stable.
- Do not add a new scenario benchmark for this slice yet. The optimization is
  covered by a focused render test; broader ScrollView/document benchmarks
  should be added only if local app scenarios expose viewport paint pressure.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/cell_buffer.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/scroll_view.dart packages/fleury/test/rendering/cell_buffer_test.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/scroll_view_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/cell_buffer_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/scroll_view_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/selection/scrollview_selection_probe_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/selection/scroll_bounds_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/selection/auto_scroll_test.dart` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.

Next:
- Continue local MVP core/API stabilization and M3.7 performance-boundary work
  only where local scenarios or focused render tests expose concrete pressure.
- Keep M2.10/M2.9 open until external real-terminal/Windows evidence is
  collected; do not fill those gates with tool-PTY or fake local captures.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.7 RenderFlex Offscreen Paint Culling

Status: Complete for this render-engine hardening slice

Context:
- After `ScrollView` moved to viewport-sized scratch buffers, the next
  adjacent hot path was child paint traversal. A long `Column` inside a
  scrollable viewport could still ask every child to paint even when most child
  bounds were translated outside the scratch buffer.
- This is a local M3.7 render-engine improvement, not a peer-comparison slice.
  Peer benchmark expansion remains deferred until post-MVP/API stability.

Changes:
- Added `RenderFlex` paint culling for child subtrees whose laid-out paint
  bounds cannot intersect the current `CellBuffer`.
- Preserved the existing paint context for visible children, including
  translated local offsets, `screenOffset`, and inherited `clipRect`.
- Added a selectable-subtree guard: `RenderFlex` still paints offscreen
  selectable descendants so they can refresh full screen-space `cellBounds`
  while their `visibleBounds` remain null under the viewport clip.
- Recorded the selection rule in the decision log and updated the
  implementation README, M3.7 milestone notes, and reactive render workstream.

Evidence:
- Added focused `RenderFlex` tests proving vertical and horizontal offscreen
  child culling, visible child paint offsets, screen offsets, and painted
  output.
- The first selection-bounds rerun failed when culling skipped offscreen
  selectable text; this proved that selectable paint has required geometry
  side effects. The final implementation preserves those offscreen selectable
  paint calls.

Decisions:
- Keep flex paint culling first-party and private. Do not expose a public
  high-performance render API from this slice.
- Treat paint as not purely visual in Fleury: selection, hit testing, anchors,
  and future adapter geometry can be refreshed during paint. Culling must prove
  that a skipped subtree has no required offscreen paint side effects.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/rendering/render_flex.dart packages/fleury/test/rendering/render_flex_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/render_flex_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/scroll_view_test.dart` passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/selection/scroll_bounds_test.dart` passed after the
  selectable-subtree guard.
- `dart test test/widgets/selection/scrollview_selection_probe_test.dart test/widgets/selection/auto_scroll_test.dart` passed.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed.

Next:
- Continue M3.7 only where local focused tests or scenario benchmarks expose a
  concrete render/data hot path. Candidate follow-ups: visible-row paint
  culling in other multi-child layout renderers, or a local scenario benchmark
  only if the proof app exposes measurable viewport paint pressure.
- Keep peer benchmark expansion post-MVP and keep M2.10/M2.9 open for real
  external terminal/Windows evidence.

## 2026-06-02 - M3.7 SB.12 Viewport Paint Evidence

Status: Complete for this evidence slice

Context:
- The previous two M3.7 slices changed `ScrollView` to paint into
  viewport-sized scratch buffers and changed `RenderFlex` to skip pure
  offscreen child paint while preserving selectable offscreen geometry.
- Focused tests proved correctness, but M3.7 needs measured local evidence for
  launch-facing performance claims.

Changes:
- Extended `SB.12 Layout Dirtiness Cache` with a viewport paint sub-journey.
- The sub-journey mounts a 2,000-row non-selectable `Column` inside
  `ScrollView`, renders the initial viewport, jumps to offset 1000, renders the
  scrolled viewport, and records painted row counts plus first/scroll frame
  timing.
- Saved the refreshed baseline at
  [phase2-layout-dirtiness-viewport-paint-2026-06-02.json](../../packages/fleury/benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-viewport-paint-2026-06-02.json).
- Updated the benchmark README, scenario benchmark lab, implementation README,
  M3.7 milestone notes, and reactive render workstream evidence.

Evidence:
- `SB.12` passed over 20 measured iterations on Dart 3.12.1.
- Viewport metrics: 2,000 total child rows, 24-row viewport, p95 24 painted rows
  on the first viewport, p95 24 painted rows on the scrolled viewport,
  viewport-first-frame p95 4773 us, and viewport-scroll-frame p95 1245 us.
- Existing layout-dirtiness invariants still hold: paint-only, text-paint-only,
  child-list no-op, and idle layout p95 remain 0 performed / 1 skipped.

Decisions:
- Keep the viewport paint evidence inside `SB.12` rather than creating a new
  peer-facing scenario number. This is local render-engine proof for the MVP
  cycle, not a renewed cross-framework comparison target.
- Treat painted row count as a first-class correctness gate for viewport paint:
  the benchmark must prove it visits visible non-selectable rows, not merely
  produce a fast frame.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart` passed.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --iterations=3 --warmup=1 --json` passed as a smoke check.
- `dart run benchmark/scenario_benchmarks.dart --filter=SB.12 --json --save=benchmark/results/phase2-layout-dirtiness-viewport-paint-2026-06-02.json` passed and saved the baseline.

Next:
- Continue MVP core/API stabilization. M3.7 should stay scenario-led; avoid
  additional render tuning unless a local benchmark or focused test exposes
  concrete pressure.
- Keep peer benchmark comparison deferred until post-MVP/API stability.

- The user clarified that additional peer benchmark comparison work should be

- M3.7 had enough MVP evidence from focused render tests and `SB.12`:
  layout-dirtiness skips, paint-only setter audits, same-identity child-list
  no-ops, viewport-sized scroll paint, flex offscreen paint culling, and
  viewport painted-row counts.
- The remaining M3.7 question was whether those internals should become a
  public high-performance extension API for launch.

Changes:
- Added a performance API boundary test proving production public libraries do
  not expose layout-debug counters, child-list helper functions, paint-culling
  internals, or render-island extension machinery.
- Kept render diagnostics available through `package:fleury/fleury_test.dart`
  for tests and scenario benchmarks.
- Marked M3.7 complete for MVP as first-party render-object discipline.
- Clarified M3.9 as a completed governance/checkpoint slice for this MVP
  cycle, with further peer fixtures, full-scale parity, cross-machine runs,
  showcase comparison work, and public superiority claims deferred until the
  launch API stops moving.
- Updated the implementation README, milestones, decision log, and reactive
  render-engine workstream.

Evidence:
- New test:
  [performance_api_boundary_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/rendering/performance_api_boundary_test.dart).
- Existing companion tests:
  [render_object_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/rendering/render_object_test.dart) and
  [public_api_boundary_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/remote/public_api_boundary_test.dart).

Decisions:
- Keep M3.7 high-performance render machinery first-party/private for launch.
  Public render-island APIs can be reconsidered after package authors or
  proof-app pressure reveal a durable contract.
- Use current peer evidence to inform implementation, not to keep expanding
  comparison coverage before API stabilization.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/test/rendering/performance_api_boundary_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/rendering/performance_api_boundary_test.dart test/rendering/render_object_test.dart test/remote/public_api_boundary_test.dart`
  passed.

Next:

- Keep local scenario benchmarks and focused tests in scope when they protect a

- Keep broader peer benchmark comparison, full-scale peer parity, cross-machine
  runs, and showcase comparison work out of scope until post-MVP/API stability.

## 2026-06-02 - Terminal Public API Boundary Stabilization

  builders out of production public libraries.
- Updated the implementation README, M2.9 milestone notes, terminal

  App authors can use `WindowsTerminalDriver` and the generic terminal

## 2026-06-02 - BuildOwner Root Replacement Hardening

Status: Complete for this core lifecycle/API stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- `BuildOwner.updateRoot` still had an older limitation: compatible root
  updates preserved state, but an incompatible root type/key threw
  `UnsupportedError`.
- That behavior was stricter than Fleury's own subtree reconciliation rule and
  weaker than Flutter-style expectations. App roots, resize rebuilds, hot
  reload wrappers, and direct framework tests should tolerate a root shape
  change by disposing the old tree and mounting a fresh one.

Changes:
- Changed `BuildOwner.updateRoot` so compatible root updates still update the
  existing element in place and preserve state.
- Added explicit stale-root protection: callers must update the current managed
  root, so a stale handle from a previous incompatible replacement throws a
  `StateError` instead of mutating a defunct tree.
- Added incompatible root replacement: the old root is unmounted, `_root` is
  cleared, and the new widget is mounted as the fresh root through the normal
  `mountRoot` path.
- Updated reconciliation tests so a root key change proves the old state is
  disposed/unmounted, the replacement state is fresh, `owner.root` points at
  the new element, and stale root handles are rejected.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, milestones, and reactive
  render-engine workstream.

Decisions:
- Keep root replacement aligned with ordinary widget reconciliation: preserve
  state only for compatible `runtimeType` plus `Key`; remount and dispose for
  incompatible roots.
- Require callers that hold root elements to keep the return value from
  `updateRoot` when a root identity change is possible.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/framework.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/reconciliation_test.dart`
  passed.
- `dart test test/widgets/reconciliation_test.dart test/widgets/state_test.dart test/widgets/reparenting_test.dart test/testing/fleury_tester_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 42 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Exact stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no
  matches for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or
  `claude/research`.

Next:
- Continue final launch-scope audit for remaining core/API sharp edges.

## 2026-06-02 - Async Command Contract Hardening

Status: Complete for this app-kernel launch-contract slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The app-kernel workstream still listed async command behavior as an open v0
  rule even though `AppCommandCallback` already supports `FutureOr<void>` and
  `CommandRegistry.invoke`/`invokeCommand` await callbacks before recording
  results.
- The important launch boundary is to define async commands without turning the
  app kernel into a worker/task system.

Changes:
- Added command-registry tests proving direct `invoke` waits for asynchronous
  command completion before recording `completed`.
- Added command-registry tests proving asynchronous command failures are
  recorded as `CommandInvocationStatus.failed` with error details.
- Added shortcut-dispatch coverage proving keyboard-triggered async commands
  do not block input dispatch but still publish the registry's latest result
  once the callback settles.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.3 milestone notes, and
  app-kernel workstream to describe the async command v0 rule as explicit and
  tested.

Decisions:
- Keep app commands as awaited `FutureOr<void>` callbacks with completed,
  disabled, notFound, and failed result recording.
- Keep long-running progress, cancellation, subprocesses, and replay in the
  effects/workflow layer; app commands may start that work but should not own
  task semantics.

Validation:
- `dart format test/app/command_registry_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/command_registry_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury`
  with 10 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Exact stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no
  matches for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or
  `claude/research`.

## 2026-06-02 - TaskController Disposal Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this task/effects lifecycle slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- `ChangeNotifier` still intentionally avoids global disposed-after-use
  assertions because async work may settle after app or screen teardown.
- `TaskController` owns active async work directly, so it needs a concrete
  disposal contract instead of silently allowing late completion to mutate
  state or no-op through a disposed notifier.

Changes:
- Added a disposal guard to
  [task.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/effects/task.dart) so
  `TaskController.dispose` cancels an active run, completes the active future
  as `TaskResult.canceled`, records a canceled event when disposing a running
  task, invalidates the run id, and clears active completer state.
- Made post-dispose `start` and `reset` throw `StateError` while keeping
  `cancel` idempotent after disposal.
- Added focused coverage in
  [task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart) for
  active-run disposal, late progress/output suppression, post-dispose misuse,
  idempotent cancel, and idempotent dispose.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.6 milestone notes, and
  effects/workflow/process workstream.

Decisions:
- Scope lifecycle hardening to `TaskController` for now. A global
  `ChangeNotifier` disposed-after-use assertion may still be useful as a debug
  diagnostic later, but task/effect teardown needs cancellation semantics, not
  a broad crash-on-late-listener policy.

## 2026-06-02 - CommandRegistry Disposal Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this app-kernel lifecycle slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The previous async command slice made direct command invocation await
  `FutureOr` callbacks and made shortcut-triggered commands publish their
  result after callback settlement.
- A command can still be in flight when a screen or `CommandScope` unmounts.
  That future should resolve for the caller, but the disposed registry should
  not mutate `lastResult` or notify listeners after teardown.

Changes:
- Added a disposal guard to
  [commands.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/commands.dart) so
  `CommandRegistry` rejects post-dispose command mutations and invocations.
- Made late async command completions return their result to the original
  caller without storing or publishing `lastResult` after the registry is
  disposed.
- Kept `dispose` idempotent and detached the parent registry listener during
  teardown.
- Added focused coverage in
  [command_registry_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/app/command_registry_test.dart)
  for disposal during async invocation, post-dispose mutation errors,
  post-dispose invocation errors, and idempotent disposal.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.3 milestone notes, and
  app-kernel workstream.

Decisions:
- Preserve in-flight command future results for callers that already started a
  command, but treat the disposed registry as inert application state. This
  keeps shortcut/input responsiveness while avoiding stale command-result state
  after screen teardown.

Validation:
- `dart format lib/src/app/commands.dart test/app/command_registry_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury`.
- `dart test test/app/command_registry_test.dart test/app/fleury_app_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 34 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.

## 2026-06-02 - TextEditingController Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this text-controller stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- App-kernel and task/effect controllers now reject post-dispose mutations, but
  `TextEditingController` could still mutate internal editing state after
  disposal because base `ChangeNotifier.notifyListeners` silently no-ops.
- Strong text/input is a core launch claim, so the editing controller should
  have predictable lifecycle behavior before broader API freeze work.

Changes:
- Added a disposal guard to
  [text_input.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/text_input.dart) so
  `TextEditingController` rejects post-dispose mutations through setters,
  edits, cursor movement, undo/redo, paste, range replacement, and composition
  APIs.
- Kept disposal idempotent, preserved the last readable `value`/`text`, and
  cleared transient undo/redo/composition state during teardown.
- Tightened controller coverage in
  [text_editing_controller_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/text_editing_controller_test.dart)
  for idempotent disposal, post-dispose mutator errors, and the existing
  one-undo-step composition commit contract.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.2 milestone notes, and
  text-editing workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat text-controller mutation after disposal as a lifecycle error while
  keeping final values readable for diagnostics and teardown inspection.

Validation:

  browsing navigation, reset, and clear calls while keeping stored history
  entries readable.

  [text_completion.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/editing/text_completion.dart)

## 2026-06-02 - Core List And Scroll Controller Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this core widget-controller stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- Text, app-kernel, and task/effect controllers now reject post-dispose
  mutations, but core viewport/list controllers still inherited base
  `ChangeNotifier` disposal behavior.
- `ListController` and `ScrollController` are foundational app-facing state for
  lists, menus, completion menus, scrollbars, and data-heavy surfaces, so silent
  post-teardown selection/scroll mutation would undermine framework robustness.

Changes:
- Added disposal guards to
  [list_view.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/list_view.dart) so
  `ListController.selectedIndex`, `ListController.jumpToIndex`, and
  `ListController.pinToBottom` reject post-dispose mutation.
- Converted `ListController.pinToBottom` from a raw mutable field to a guarded
  getter/setter while preserving the constructor argument and read API.
- Kept final list selection, item count, visible range, and pin state readable
  after disposal, while clearing transient pending jump commands.
- Added disposal guards to
  [scroll_view.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/scroll_view.dart) so
  `ScrollController` rejects post-dispose offset, jump, and scroll mutations
  while preserving final offset and layout metrics for diagnostics.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [list_controller_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/list_controller_test.dart)
  and
  [scroll_view_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/scroll_view_test.dart).
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M3.7 milestone notes, and
  reactive render-engine workstream.

Decisions:
- Keep core list/scroll final read state available after teardown, but make
  stale navigation and viewport mutation a clear lifecycle error.

## 2026-06-02 - App Kernel Controller Disposal Hardening

Status: Complete for this app-kernel lifecycle slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- `CommandRegistry` now rejects post-dispose command operations and avoids
  late async result publication.
- The remaining app-kernel controllers still represented structural app state
  that could be mutated after disposal while `ChangeNotifier.notifyListeners`
  silently no-opped.

Changes:
- Added disposal guards to
  [app.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/app.dart) so
  `ScreenController.activate`, `ScreenController.updateScreens`,
  `FleuryAppController.title`, and `FleuryAppController.updateExtensions`
  reject post-dispose mutations.
- Added a disposal guard to
  [status.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/app/status.dart) so
  `StatusController.update` rejects post-dispose mutation.
- Kept disposal idempotent and kept `FleuryAppController.dispose` responsible
  for detaching child-controller listeners before the child controllers are
  disposed by `FleuryApp`.
- Added focused coverage in
  [fleury_app_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/app/fleury_app_test.dart)
  for screen/status/app controller post-dispose errors, idempotent disposal,
  and app-controller listener detachment from child controller updates.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.3 milestone notes, and
  app-kernel workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat post-dispose app-kernel controller mutation as a lifecycle error.
  These controllers are structural app state, so silently mutating them after
  teardown hides bugs during screen/app shutdown.

## 2026-06-02 - Form Controller Lifecycle And Async Validation Hardening

Status: Complete for this form-controller stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- Widget, document, workflow, navigation, app-kernel, text, list/scroll, and
  task/effect controllers now reject post-dispose mutation at their public
  controller boundaries.
- Forms needed a separate lifecycle pass because `FormController` can have
  in-flight async validators that settle after a form screen or wizard unmounts.

Changes:
- Added disposal guards to
  [form.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/form.dart) so
  `FormWizardController` rejects post-dispose step navigation and reset while
  keeping final step state readable.
- Added disposal guards to
  [form.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/lib/src/form.dart) so
  `FormController` rejects post-dispose value, error, reset, validation, and
  submit mutations while keeping final values, errors, submitted state, and
  snapshots readable.
- `FormController.dispose` now invalidates the async validation generation and
  clears transient validating field state without notifying after teardown.
- In-flight `validateAsync`, `validateFieldAsync`, and `submitAsync` futures
  can still resolve for callers, but late validator results no longer restore
  busy state or write obsolete field errors after disposal.
- Added focused lifecycle and stale-async-result coverage in
  [form_test.dart](../../packages/fleury_widgets/test/form_test.dart).
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M3.7 milestone notes, and
  data/widgets workstream.

Decisions:
- Apply the launch controller lifecycle contract to forms: final read state
  remains available for diagnostics, every app-facing mutation after disposal
  is a clear lifecycle error, and async validators are generation-invalidated
  so stale futures cannot mutate disposed form state.

  `runTui` debug shell, semantic Tree tab, terminal diagnosis rows, paint-flash
  state, and debug hotkey dispatch. It still needed a predictable lifecycle
  contract before treating replay/devtools foundations as stable.

  cursor, and provider mutations while keeping final mode/tab/paint/cursor
  state readable.

  diagnosis providers so disposed debug state cannot hold stale app-root or
  terminal-driver hooks.

  [debug_shell_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_shell_test.dart)
  for idempotent disposal, read-after-dispose state, provider cleanup, and
  post-dispose lifecycle errors.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.10 milestone notes, and
  replay/devtools/testing workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat debug/devtools controller state like other launch-adjacent controller

## 2026-06-02 - Debounced Task Controller Lifecycle Coverage

Status: Complete for this task/effects lifecycle evidence slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- `TaskController` already had launch lifecycle hardening for active async
  work, and `DebouncedTaskController` already had disposal guards in code.
- The gap was evidence: the debounced wrapper is a public core primitive for
  typeahead/search/index workloads, so disposal behavior needed focused tests
  covering pending debounce futures and externally owned task controllers.

Changes:
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [task_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/effects/task_test.dart) proving
  `DebouncedTaskController.dispose` is idempotent, cancels a pending debounced
  future as `TaskResult.canceled`, prevents the pending runner from starting,
  keeps final task state readable, and leaves `cancel` idempotent after
  teardown.
- Added post-dispose misuse coverage for `schedule`, `runNow`, and `reset`
  with the controller-specific `DebouncedTaskController has been disposed.`
  lifecycle error.
- Added external-ownership coverage proving a wrapper created with an app-owned
  `TaskController` detaches without disposing that task controller.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M2.4a/EWP notes, and
  effects/workflow/process workstream.

Decisions:
- Keep `DebouncedTaskController.cancel` idempotent after disposal to match
  `TaskController.cancel` cleanup semantics. Scheduling, immediate runs, and
  reset create or mutate work state and therefore remain explicit
  post-dispose lifecycle errors.
- Disposing a debounced wrapper should cancel wrapper-owned timers/futures and
  detach listeners, but an externally supplied `TaskController` remains
  app-owned and usable.

## 2026-06-02 - Runtime LogBuffer Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this runtime output-buffer stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The launch-surface audit after debounced task coverage found `LogBuffer`, a
  public native export used by `LogView`, `LogConsole`, and
  `TerminalOutputRegion`.
- Before this slice, `LogBuffer.add` could mutate captured output after
  disposal while `ChangeNotifier.notifyListeners` silently no-oped.

Changes:
- Added disposal guards to
  [output_capture.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/output_capture.dart)
  so `LogBuffer.add` rejects post-dispose appends with
  `LogBuffer has been disposed.` while keeping final captured lines readable.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [log_view_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/log_view_test.dart)
  for idempotent disposal, bounded final readable lines, and post-dispose append
  errors.
- Updated the implementation README, decision log, M1.6/M1.11/M2.4 notes,
  effects/workflow/process workstream, and terminal capability/security
  workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat runtime captured output as diagnostic read state after teardown, not as
  a mutable append target. Late appends after disposal are explicit lifecycle
  misuse because they would otherwise mutate output state without notifying
  listeners or consumers.

## 2026-06-02 - Animation Ticker Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this animation primitive stabilization slice

- `TickerMode` and widget-owned blink/image tickers update mute state during
  normal mounted lifecycle before disposing tickers, so guarding post-dispose
  setter calls does not block the intended owner path.

  [ticker.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/ticker.dart) and
  [frame_ticker.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/frame_ticker.dart)
  so `muted` setter calls after disposal throw explicit lifecycle
  `StateError`s.
- Kept final animation state readable after teardown: disposed tickers still
  expose active/disposed flags, mute state, and last timing/frame values for
  diagnostics.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in

  for readable final state and blocked post-dispose mute toggles.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and

  freezes the last diagnostic state but does not permit stale mutation through
  app/widget handles after a subtree has unmounted.

## 2026-06-02 - Animation Model Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this public animation model stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- After hardening `Ticker` and `FrameTicker`, the exported animation audit
  moved to `Animation<T>`, Fleury's public continuous-animation model.
- `Animation.to`, `loop`, and `run` already rejected post-dispose calls, but
  `snap` and `stop` could still mutate disposed animation state. Reading
  `value` during build could also re-register a dependent on a disposed
  animation, making a supposedly final-state read carry side effects.

Changes:
- Added disposal guards to
  [animation.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/animation.dart) so
  `snap` and `stop` reject post-dispose calls with explicit lifecycle
  `StateError`s, matching `to`, `loop`, and `run`.
- Made disposed `Animation.value` reads inert in widget builds: final value is
  still renderable, but disposed animations no longer add new element
  dependents or attach back to a scheduler.
- Added focused coverage in
  [animation_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/animation/animation_test.dart)
  for final readable value/target/movement state, active future cancellation,
  stopped scheduler state, and post-dispose mutation errors.
- Added focused coverage in
  [animation_reactivity_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/animation/animation_reactivity_test.dart)
  proving disposed animation values can render without reattaching.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and
  reactive-render workstream to include `Animation<T>` in the animation
  lifecycle rule.

Decisions:
- Treat `Animation<T>` as an app-facing state model, not a cleanup-only helper.
  Disposal freezes its diagnostic/read state and cancels active work, while
  stale `snap`, `stop`, `to`, `loop`, and `run` calls fail explicitly. Plain
  value reads remain safe and side-effect-free after teardown.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/animation.dart packages/fleury/test/animation/animation_test.dart packages/fleury/test/animation/animation_reactivity_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.

  passed in `packages/fleury` with 27 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Exact stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no
  matches for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or
  `claude/research`.

Next:

  pass is to enumerate public production exports that extend `ChangeNotifier`
  or own dispose/mutation APIs and classify any remaining gaps as either
  closed, intentionally cleanup-idempotent, or requiring a focused hardening
  slice.

## 2026-06-02 - Selection And Overlay Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this selection/overlay stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The exported/stateful audit found two core primitives that were still
  mutable after disposal:
  `SelectionContainerDelegate` and `OverlayEntry`.
- Both have teardown cleanup paths that should remain idempotent:
  `SelectionContainerDelegate.remove` may be called by selectable cleanup, and
  `OverlayEntry.remove` is the natural cleanup operation for detached entries.

Changes:
- Added disposal tracking to
  [selection_container_delegate.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/selection/selection_container_delegate.dart).
  `add`, `dispatchSelectionEvent`, `clear`, and cursor movement now reject
  post-dispose work with `SelectionContainerDelegate has been disposed.`;
  `remove` remains cleanup-idempotent.
- Added disposal tracking to
  [overlay.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/overlay.dart).
  Disposing an inserted `OverlayEntry` removes it from its overlay, while
  post-dispose `markNeedsBuild`, `opaque` changes, and insertion fail with
  `OverlayEntry has been disposed.`; `remove` remains cleanup-idempotent.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [selection_delegate_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/selection/selection_delegate_test.dart)
  and [overlay_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/overlay_test.dart).
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and
  reactive-render workstream.

Decisions:
- Preserve cleanup idempotence where framework parent/child teardown order can
  reasonably call cleanup more than once, but reject any new selection or
  overlay work after disposal because it mutates app-visible state through
  stale handles.

## 2026-06-02 - Focus Manager Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this focus manager stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- `FocusManager` is exported and owns keyboard routing, traversal, modal focus
  boundaries, and node registration. It had no disposal contract beyond
  `ChangeNotifier` listener cleanup, so post-dispose focus movement could
  silently return false or interact with stale registrations.
- Runtime and tester cleanup both unmount the root before disposing the
  manager, so manager disposal is a terminal lifecycle boundary and child
  deregistration should already have happened in the normal path.

Changes:
- Added disposal tracking to
  [focus.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/focus.dart).
  Manager disposal now detaches attached nodes, clears focused node and modal
  scope state, and keeps `FocusNode` cleanup idempotent after the manager is
  gone.
- Post-dispose `requestFocus`, `focusNext`, `focusPrevious`, `dispatchKey`,
  modal-scope registration, and node registration now fail with
  `FocusManager has been disposed.` instead of mutating or dispatching through
  stale manager state.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [focus_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/focus_test.dart) for
  node detachment, idempotent manager/node cleanup, and blocked post-dispose
  focus movement/key dispatch.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and
  reactive-render workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat `FocusManager.dispose` as a terminal app/tester lifecycle boundary:
  detach nodes and reject new focus work. `FocusNode.dispose` remains
  cleanup-idempotent after manager disposal so app-owned nodes do not need
  fragile teardown ordering.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/focus.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/focus_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/widgets/focus_test.dart test/widgets/focus_modal_traversal_test.dart test/widgets/focus_bootstrap_test.dart test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 56 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Exact stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no
  matches for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or
  `claude/research`.

Next:
- Run one more exported/stateful audit pass and classify any remaining public
  dispose-owning primitives. If no comparable local lifecycle gaps remain,
  move toward the explicit MVP completion audit while keeping M2.9/M2.10
  external terminal evidence and deferred-scope items separate.

## 2026-06-02 - Scheduler And Binding Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this scheduler/runtime-root stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The exported dispose-owner audit found that `TickerScheduler.dispose`
  stopped existing callbacks but did not prevent later `register`,
  `registerReassembleCallback`, or `addPostFrameCallback` calls from
  restarting scheduler work after runtime shutdown.
- `TuiBinding` delegates app-facing ticker and post-frame creation through the
  scheduler, so it needed the same terminal lifecycle boundary.

Changes:
- Added disposal tracking to
  [ticker_scheduler.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/ticker_scheduler.dart).
  Disposed schedulers now reject new tick, reassemble, and post-frame
  registration, plus post-frame registration hook assignment, with
  `TickerScheduler has been disposed.`
- Kept scheduler cleanup idempotent: unregister, unregister-reassemble,
  reassemble, flush, and repeated dispose remain safe after disposal.
- Added disposal tracking to
  [tui_binding.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/tui_binding.dart).
  Disposed bindings reject new tickers and post-frame callbacks with
  `TuiBinding has been disposed.`, while flush and repeated dispose remain
  cleanup-idempotent.
- Added focused coverage in
  [ticker_scheduler_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/animation/ticker_scheduler_test.dart)
  and [tui_binding_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/widgets/tui_binding_test.dart).
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and
  reactive-render workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat scheduler and binding disposal as a terminal runtime boundary. Shutdown
  drains/cleanup may remain idempotent, but disposed runtime roots must not be
  restartable through new ticker, reassemble, or post-frame registrations.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/animation/ticker_scheduler.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/widgets/tui_binding.dart packages/fleury/test/animation/ticker_scheduler_test.dart packages/fleury/test/widgets/tui_binding_test.dart`

  passed in `packages/fleury` with 68 tests.

## 2026-06-02 - Input Dispatcher Lifecycle Hardening

Status: Complete for this runtime input-root stabilization slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The exported dispose-owner audit found `InputDispatcher`, an app/runtime
  input root that canceled pending sequence timers on disposal but still
  accepted later dispatch calls and global-binding replacement.
- Since the dispatcher can route key/text/paste/mouse events into the focus
  tree, post-dispose dispatch is a stale input path and should be explicit
  lifecycle misuse.

Changes:
- Added disposal tracking to
  [input_dispatcher.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/runtime/input_dispatcher.dart).
  Disposed dispatchers now reject `dispatch` and global-binding replacement
  with `InputDispatcher has been disposed.` while keeping pending-sequence
  cleanup idempotent.
- Changed `globalBindings` from a public field to an equivalent getter/setter
  property so assignment can be lifecycle-guarded without changing the
  app-facing property name.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [input_dispatcher_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/runtime/input_dispatcher_test.dart)
  for pending sequence cleanup, idempotent disposal, blocked dispatch, and
  blocked global-binding replacement.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log, and
  reactive-render workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat `InputDispatcher.dispose` as a terminal input lifecycle boundary.
  Cleanup clears pending sequence timers and remains idempotent, but stale
  input events and global binding mutation after teardown should fail
  explicitly instead of reaching a disposed focus tree.

## 2026-06-02 - Debug/Test Runtime Lifecycle Hardening And Exit Propagation

Status: Complete for this final local exported dispose-owner slice

Context:
- The active lane remains MVP core/API stabilization.
- The final exported dispose-owner audit found two remaining public/test
  surfaces that owned session resources and could still accept new work after
  disposal: `DebugCaptureRecorder` and `FakeTerminalDriver`.
- The same audit classified the remaining candidates:
  `HotReloadController` exposes static attach plus idempotent async cleanup
  but no post-dispose app mutation method; `ProcessCommandRunner` delegates to
  the already-hardened `ProcessTaskController`; `ProcessTaskController`
  inherits `TaskController` lifecycle; `ExternalEditor` and `Clipboard` are
  stateless/free-function or no-dispose surfaces; `NavigatorState` is
  framework-owned `State` and already clears root registration plus completes
  pending route futures on teardown.

Changes:
- Added disposal tracking to
  [debug_capture.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart).
  `DebugCaptureRecorder` now keeps final captured state and snapshots readable
  after disposal while rejecting post-dispose `attach`, `record`,
  `recordOutputSummary`, task-event recording, and time-marker recording with
  `DebugCaptureRecorder has been disposed.`
- Added disposal tracking to
  [fake_driver.dart](../../packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/fake_driver.dart).
  `FakeTerminalDriver.dispose` is idempotent, closes the event stream once,
  leaves final size/output/mode/call-count state inspectable, and rejects
  post-dispose `enter`, handoff, `write`, `clearOutput`, `enqueue`, `resize`,
  and `isInteractive` mutation with `FakeTerminalDriver has been disposed.`
  `restore` remains cleanup-idempotent.
- Added focused lifecycle coverage in
  [debug_capture_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart)
  and
  [fake_driver_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart).
- Fixed the selection demo root Ctrl+C binding in
  [selection_demo.dart](../../packages/fleury/example/selection_demo.dart) to
  call `event.bubble()`. The validation run exposed that the demo intended to
  let framework exit handle Ctrl+C when the selection layer had no text, but
  its root binding was consuming the event.
- Updated the implementation README, M3.7 notes, decision log,
  reactive-render workstream, replay/devtools/testing workstream, and
  terminal-capability/security workstream.

Decisions:
- Treat debug/test runtime roots as part of the launch API quality bar when
  they own app-session resources. Final evidence should remain readable after
  teardown, but new debug capture or fake terminal activity after disposal is
  lifecycle misuse.
- Keep app-level Ctrl+C opt-outs explicit: handlers that intend `runTui` to
  preserve the framework exit path must call `event.bubble()`.

Validation:
- `dart format packages/fleury/lib/src/debug/debug_capture.dart packages/fleury/lib/src/terminal/fake_driver.dart packages/fleury/test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart packages/fleury/test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 20 tests.
- `dart test test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/effects/external_editor_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 39 tests.
- Initial combined dependent validation including
  `test/widgets/selection/selection_demo_e2e_test.dart` exposed two Ctrl+C exit
  timeouts in the selection demo. After fixing event bubbling,
  `dart test test/widgets/selection/selection_demo_e2e_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with 2 tests.
- `dart test test/debug/debug_capture_test.dart test/terminal/fake_driver_test.dart test/runtime/run_tui_test.dart test/terminal/diagnostics_test.dart test/effects/process_task_test.dart test/effects/external_editor_test.dart test/widgets/selection/selection_demo_e2e_test.dart`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 61 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root. The gate
  analyzed `fleury`, `fleury_widgets`, `fleury_git`, and
  `fleury_example_console`, then passed the counter quickstart, dashboard demo,
  git extension, and proof-console quick tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Exact stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no
  matches for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or
  `claude/research`.

Next:
- Begin the explicit MVP completion audit against the implementation tracker
  while keeping M2.10 real-terminal matrix evidence, Dune/`dune_cli`, ACP
  package work, full replay/devtools, and expanded peer benchmark comparisons
  out of the MVP completion claim.

## 2026-06-02 - MVP Completion Audit Snapshot

Status: Complete for the first explicit local MVP audit snapshot

Context:
- After the final local lifecycle/API hardening pass and quick gate, the next
  task was to audit the tracker without redefining success around only the work
  already done.
- The audit needed to separate locally proven framework implementation from
  external launch evidence and explicitly deferred work.

Changes:
- Added
  [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md) as the current audit
  snapshot. It records locally proven areas, latest evidence, remaining
  launch-evidence gaps, and explicitly deferred work.
- Linked the audit from
  [README.md](README.md) and updated the current milestone text from core/API
  stabilization to MVP completion audit after local core/API stabilization.
- Updated
  [agent-adapter-boundary.md](agent-adapter-boundary.md) because its launch
  readiness checklist was stale: semantic graph, `FleuryApp`, worker/task
  state, sanitized output, data/output widgets, debug capture hooks,
  capability/security visibility, and ACP-free core boundaries are now checked.
- Reclassified M2.6 Dune/`dune_cli` and M2.7 `fleury_acp` as deferred
  current-cycle work in
  [milestones.md](milestones.md), not failed MVP implementation.
- Marked the Phase 2 production-toolkit exit criterion as locally satisfied,
  kept real-terminal compatibility partial, and marked the M3.9 ecosystem
  superiority criterion as local evidence only with public claims deferred.
- Reclassified public distribution polish in
  [local-distribution-path.md](local-distribution-path.md) as deferred until
  launch APIs are credible.

Audit result:
- Do not mark the overall goal complete yet.
- Local implementation is close to a strong MVP stopping point.
- Remaining launch-proof gaps are M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix entries,
  M2.9 real Windows validation, and a product decision about whether M2.12
  public adoption assets are in the next cycle.
- Explicitly deferred work remains Dune/`dune_cli`, `fleury_acp`, full
  replay/devtools, expanded peer comparisons, and broad public distribution
  polish.

Validation:
- `rg -n "^- \[ \]" docs/implementation` now reports only terminal matrix
  per-entry review criteria, post-MVP Phase 3 items, `fleury_acp`
  fast-follow tasks, and adoption metrics.

Next:
- Run final docs/diff/stale-name hygiene checks after these audit-doc edits.
- If clean, leave the goal active with the next choices clearly framed:
  collect external terminal/Windows evidence, work M2.12 public collateral, or
  run a broader release-candidate validation pass.

## 2026-06-02 - Terminal Matrix Collection Plan Tooling

Status: Complete for this M2.10 collection-workflow slice

Context:
- The MVP completion audit identified M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix
  evidence as a remaining launch-proof gap.
- Running `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit` showed the current
  matrix has 0 entries and 0/9 ready launch targets.
- The current Codex shell is not valid launch evidence: `test -t 0` and
  `test -t 1` both fail, `tty` reports `not a tty`, and `TERM=dumb`.
  Capturing from here would create a non-interactive control entry, not a
  real-terminal matrix entry.

Changes:
- Added `--write-plan=<path>` to
  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart) for
  `terminal-matrix-audit`. It writes a Markdown collection/review checklist
  generated from the same target reports, next-action states, suggested
  capture commands, matched entries, and review checklist as the JSON audit.
- Added black-box launcher coverage in
  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart)
  proving `--write-plan` writes target status and suggested commands while
  preserving valid JSON stdout when `--json` is also passed.
- Generated
  [terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md](terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md)
  from the current empty matrix. It records 0/9 ready targets and exact
  capture commands for macOS Terminal, iTerm2, Kitty, Ghostty, Alacritty,
  WezTerm, Windows Terminal, SSH, and tmux.
- Updated
  [terminal-compatibility-matrix.md](terminal-compatibility-matrix.md),
  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md),
  [README.md](README.md), [milestones.md](milestones.md),
  [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md),
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  with the generated-plan workflow and current 0/9 state.

Decisions:
- Do not collect M2.10 launch matrix entries from the Codex non-TTY shell.
  Treat this environment as unsuitable for real-terminal compatibility claims.
- Keep terminal matrix collection plans generated from audit state so the
  external checklist cannot drift from the actual readiness gate.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with 10 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --write-plan=docs/implementation/terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md`
  passed at the repo root and wrote the current plan.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed with no issues.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --json` passed and
  reported 0 entries and 0/9 ready targets, matching the generated collection
  plan.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `rg -n "[ \t]+$" ...` over the touched tool, test, and implementation docs
  passed with no trailing whitespace matches after cleanup.
- `rg -n "\bember\b|research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob|claude/research" ...`
  over `README.md`, `docs`, `packages`, `tool`, and `peer-fixtures`,
  excluding this journal, found no stale old-name matches.

Next:

## 2026-06-02 - Local RC Gate Hardening

Status: Complete for the local RC validation slice

Context:
- After terminal-matrix tooling, the remaining MVP-completion items were
  mostly external evidence: M2.10 real-terminal matrix review and M2.9 real
  Windows validation.
- The current Codex shell is still not suitable for launch terminal captures,
  so the highest-value local action was a full release-candidate validation
  pass to find any remaining local test or artifact drift.

Changes:
- Updated the `fleury_widgets` command-palette open golden so it matches the
  current visible `Search…` placeholder in the palette input.
- Hardened the stale-handle integration test so the second `fleury serve`
  process gets a realistic full-gate timeout and is killed before failing if
  it does not exit. This avoids mistaking full-suite startup pressure for a
  production stale-socket regression and prevents leaked child processes after
  timeout failures.
- Updated [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md) and
  [README.md](README.md) to record the clean full local RC gate and remove the
  broader local RC validation pass from the remaining-work list.

Decisions:
- Treat the local RC gate as satisfied for the current MVP checkpoint.
- Keep the overall goal active because real Windows validation and reviewed
  real-terminal matrix evidence still require external sessions.

Findings:
- The first full RC gate failed in `fleury_widgets` because the
  command-palette golden expected the pre-placeholder rendering. The widget was
  already passing `widget.placeholder` into `TextInput`, so the failure was a
  stale test artifact.
- The second full RC gate failed only in the stale-handle integration test.
  A focused run passed, and the failure mode was the old 5-second process-exit
  timeout returning `-1` under full-suite load. The production live-handle
  path still looked correct.

Validation:
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check --quick` passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/goldens_test.dart` passed in `packages/fleury_widgets` with
  20 tests.
- `dart format test/remote/serve_stale_handle_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with no formatting changes.
- `dart test test/remote/serve_stale_handle_test.dart --chain-stack-traces`
  passed in `packages/fleury` with 2 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check` passed at the repo root. The gate analyzed
  and tested `fleury`, `fleury_widgets`, `fleury_git`, and
  `fleury_example_console`, including 1467 core tests, 798 widget tests, 4 git
  extension tests, and 26 proof-console scenario tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Touched-file trailing-whitespace scan found no matches.
- Stale-name scan outside execution-journal validation prose found no matches
  for `ember`, `research-dune-cli-client-Ctoob`, or `claude/research`.

Next:
- The remaining goal work is external evidence and scope disposition:
  M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix captures, M2.9 real Windows validation,
  and a decision on whether M2.12 public adoption collateral is part of the
  next cycle or remains deferred until API freeze.

## 2026-06-02 - Terminal Matrix Review Packet

Status: Complete for this M2.10 review-workflow slice

Context:
- The local MVP audit still leaves M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix entries
  open.
- The collection plan tells external testers what to capture, but once entries
  arrive we also need a durable reviewer packet that groups targets, entries,
  issues, notes, terminal/platform facts, probe summaries, and unmatched files
  without hand-reading every JSON entry first.
- This Codex shell remains unsuitable for real-terminal launch evidence, so the
  local work should improve the external collection/review loop instead of
  fabricating captures.

Changes:
- Added `terminal-matrix-audit --write-review=<path>` to
  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart).
- The generated review packet is built from the same audit model as JSON,
  strict mode, and the collection plan. It includes target status, next action,
  capture/recapture commands, matched entry checkboxes, issue/note details,
  terminal/platform/probe/compatibility summaries, unmatched entries, invalid
  entries, and a final review checklist.
- Added black-box launcher coverage in
  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart)
  proving `--write-review` writes ready, non-ready, missing-target, issue,
  note, and unmatched-entry information from real audit state.
- Generated
  [terminal-matrix-review-packet.md](terminal-matrix-review-packet.md) from the
  current empty matrix. It records the current 0/9 ready-target state and exact
  capture/review checklist.
- Updated
  [README.md](README.md),
  [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md),
  [milestones.md](milestones.md),
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md),
  [terminal-compatibility-matrix.md](terminal-compatibility-matrix.md),
  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  to link and explain the review packet workflow.

Decisions:
- Generate terminal matrix review packets from audit state rather than writing
  them by hand. The source JSON remains authoritative, but review artifacts
  should stay mechanically aligned with target matching, readiness status, and
  strict-mode evidence.

Findings:
- The current matrix directory still has 0 entries and 0/9 ready targets.
- The new review packet is most useful after captures exist, but even with an
  empty matrix it gives the next external pass a single target-by-target
  checklist and makes the open state explicit.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with 11 tests.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --write-plan=docs/implementation/terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md --write-review=docs/implementation/terminal-matrix-review-packet.md`
  passed at the repo root and wrote both generated Markdown artifacts.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed with no issues.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-audit --json` passed and
  reported 0 entries, 0/9 ready targets, and `strictPass: false`, matching the
  generated plan and review packet.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --dry-run terminal-matrix-audit --write-review=docs/implementation/terminal-matrix-review-packet.md --strict`
  passed and reported the expected scan/write/enforce actions.

Next:
- Run final docs/diff/stale-name hygiene checks after this journal update.
- Remaining goal work is unchanged: collect reviewed real-terminal matrix
  captures, validate real Windows sessions, and decide whether M2.12 adoption
  collateral is part of the next cycle or remains deferred until API freeze.

## 2026-06-02 - M2.12 MVP Scope Disposition

Status: Complete for the M2.12 MVP scope decision

Context:
- The MVP completion audit still listed M2.12 public adoption/release
  collateral as a partial, undecided item.
- Existing planning already cut broad adoption assets first, and the adoption
  workstream already said public comparison pages, docs, and distribution work
  should wait until post-MVP API stability.
- User direction has also consistently prioritized framework capability,
  proof-app pressure, API robustness, terminal correctness, and local evidence
  over release hygiene and public collateral during this implementation cycle.

Changes:
- Marked M2.12 as complete for the MVP-scoped adoption positioning and
  public-scope disposition in [milestones.md](milestones.md).
- Updated [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md) so M2.12 is no
  longer an active launch blocker. Public docs, public comparison copy, docs
  site, scaffolding, adoption metrics, and release collateral are explicitly
  deferred until API freeze.
- Updated
  [adoption-distribution-ecosystem.md](workstreams/adoption-distribution-ecosystem.md)
  to make ADE.4 adoption metrics post-MVP and to keep public adoption work from
  replacing product/evidence work before API freeze.
- Updated [README.md](README.md) and [why-fleury.md](why-fleury.md) so the
  implementation index and positioning draft use the new M2.12 boundary.
- Added a durable decision in [decision-log.md](decision-log.md): defer public
  adoption collateral until API freeze.

Decisions:
- M2.12 is satisfied for the current MVP cycle by internal positioning, local
  distribution planning, peer-scorecard scaffolding, and explicit deferral of
  public collateral until API freeze.
- Public launch collateral should restart after API freeze or a concrete
  external launch date, not before terminal/Windows evidence is collected.

Findings:
- After this disposition, the active completion blockers are external evidence:
  M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix captures and M2.9 reviewed real Windows
  captures.
- Public adoption work remains important for launch, but it is not part of the
  current implementation goal's MVP completion gate.

Validation:
- `rg -n "M2\\.12 public adoption assets|Decide whether M2\\.12|public adoption assets are part|adoption assets if the next cycle|adoption/release collateral remains partial" docs/implementation -g '!docs/implementation/execution-journal.md'`
  found no stale blocker wording.
- `rg -n "M2\\.12|API freeze|public collateral|public adoption" ...` over the
  edited implementation docs shows only the intended disposition/deferred
  wording.

Next:
- Run final docs/diff/stale-name hygiene checks after this journal update.
- Remaining goal work is external terminal and Windows evidence: collect,
  review, and gate real-terminal matrix entries and real Windows validation
  entries.

  evidence.

  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart).
- The command combines the launch-terminal matrix audit and Windows validation
  preset audit into one machine-readable and human-readable external-evidence
  readiness result.

  Strict mode exits non-zero until both external evidence lanes pass.

  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart)
  proving strict failure when evidence is missing and strict success when
  fixture entries cover both launch and Windows target sets.

  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  to use the combined readiness gate.

Decisions:
- Use `mvp-readiness --strict` as the combined external-evidence completion
  gate. It does not replace the local RC gate; `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart check`
  remains the command for fresh local implementation validation.

Findings:

  are still no reviewed matrix entries.
- This command turns the remaining external blockers into a single executable
  status check instead of scattered tracker prose.

## 2026-06-02 - MVP Final Gate Command

Status: Complete for this final-gate tooling slice

Context:
- `mvp-readiness` made external evidence executable, but the actual completion
  sequence still required remembering to run both the local RC gate and the
  external readiness gate.
- The final handoff should have one command that pairs fresh local validation
  with external evidence enforcement.

- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-final-gate` to
  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart).

  combined external evidence readiness audit.

  `--skip-local`. `--skip-local` is intended only for CI/test/review contexts
  where a fresh local gate already ran.

  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart)
  for dry-run planning, missing-evidence failure with `--skip-local`, and
  fixture-evidence success with `--skip-local`.

  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  with the final-gate command.

Decisions:
- Use `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-final-gate` as the single final
  completion command. It does not create external evidence; it enforces that
  reviewed evidence is present after running the local RC gate.

Findings:
- The current final gate correctly cannot pass because the real terminal and
  real Windows matrix entries are still missing.
- `mvp-final-gate --skip-local` is useful for focused tests and for CI stages
  that have already run the local gate, but the real completion command should
  run without `--skip-local`.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with 17 tests.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed with no issues.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --dry-run mvp-final-gate --quick --write-report=docs/implementation/mvp-readiness-report.md`
  passed and showed the local quick gate, matrix scan, report write, and
  external evidence enforcement.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-final-gate --skip-local --write-report=docs/implementation/mvp-readiness-report.md`
  exited non-zero as expected, wrote the readiness report, and reported 0/9
  launch terminal targets plus 0/4 Windows validation targets ready.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-readiness --write-report=docs/implementation/mvp-readiness-report.md`
  regenerated the current readiness report with the final MVP gate listed.

Next:
- Run final docs/diff/stale-name hygiene checks after this journal update.
- The next real completion step is external: collect reviewed terminal and
  Windows matrix entries, then run `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-final-gate`
  without `--skip-local`.

## 2026-06-02 - Reviewed Matrix Entry Acceptance

Status: Complete for this matrix-review workflow slice

Context:
- Strict matrix coverage previously counted only `readyForReview` entries.
- Real terminals can produce explainable `needsAttention` findings, such as
  passive/probe disagreement or active-only confirmations, that a reviewer may
  want to accept after preserving context.
- Without an explicit acceptance command, reviewers would either need to
  recapture indefinitely or hand-edit JSON in a way the gate did not document.

Changes:
- Added `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart terminal-matrix-accept` to
  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart).
- The command finds a matrix entry by exact label and updates its `review`
  object to `acceptedForLaunch`, preserving original issues and notes while
  adding `previousStatus`, `acceptedAt`, optional `acceptedBy`, and
  `acceptanceNotes`.
- Strict target coverage now treats `readyForReview` and `acceptedForLaunch`
  as ready statuses.
- Non-interactive entries are refused by default and require
  `--allow-non-interactive`, keeping control/degradation evidence out of launch
  coverage unless a reviewer explicitly accepts it.
- Added black-box launcher coverage in
  [terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart](../../packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart)
  for accepting a `needsAttention` entry into strict coverage and refusing a
  `nonInteractive` entry without override.
- Updated [README.md](README.md),
  [milestones.md](milestones.md),
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md),
  [terminal-compatibility-matrix.md](terminal-compatibility-matrix.md),
  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  with the accepted-entry workflow.
- Regenerated evidence artifacts with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-evidence-refresh` so generated checklists
  mention `acceptedForLaunch`.

Decisions:
- Count explicitly accepted matrix entries as ready launch evidence, but only
  when acceptance is recorded in the entry JSON with reviewer notes and the
  original issues remain visible.

Findings:
- This closes a practical review-loop gap: reviewers can now accept explainable
  findings without losing evidence context or weakening strict readiness.
- Current real evidence remains empty; this changes how future reviewed
  captures can be consumed, not the current readiness state.

Validation:
- `dart format tool/fleury_dev.dart packages/fleury/test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart`
  passed at the repo root.
- `dart test test/tool/terminal_matrix_tool_test.dart` passed in
  `packages/fleury` with 21 tests.
- `dart analyze tool/fleury_dev.dart` passed with no issues.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-evidence-refresh` passed and rewrote all
  generated evidence artifacts.
- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart --dry-run terminal-matrix-accept --label=example --note=Reviewed`
  passed and reported the expected scan/accept action.

Next:
- Run final docs/diff/stale-name hygiene checks after this journal update.
- After external captures land, reviewers can use `terminal-matrix-accept` for
  explainable `needsAttention` entries, then run `mvp-evidence-refresh` and
  `mvp-final-gate`.

## 2026-06-02 - Windows Validation Deferred Out Of MVP

Status: Complete for this scope-boundary update

Context:
- The user decided to defer real Windows validation out of the MVP.
- The existing tooling treated Windows in two ways: `windows-terminal` was part
  of the default launch target set, and a separate `windows` target preset
  enforced Windows Terminal, conhost, PowerShell, and IDE terminal captures.
- Keeping both as MVP blockers would contradict the new scope and continue to
  require a Windows host before closing the MVP goal.

- Removed `windows-terminal` from the default MVP launch target set in
  [tool/fleury_dev.dart](../../tool/fleury_dev.dart), reducing MVP launch
  evidence from 9 targets to 8 targets.
- Kept `terminal-matrix-audit --target-preset=windows` and the generated
  Windows plan/review packet as the post-MVP validation lane.
- Updated `mvp-readiness` so Windows validation is reported with
  `requiredForMvp: false` and `mvpStatus: deferred`, but no longer contributes
  to `strictPass` or `remainingBlockers`.
- Updated `mvp-evidence-refresh` output wording to distinguish MVP launch
  evidence from post-MVP Windows evidence.
- Updated [README.md](README.md), [milestones.md](milestones.md),
  [decision-log.md](decision-log.md),
  [mvp-completion-audit.md](mvp-completion-audit.md),
  [terminal-compatibility-matrix.md](terminal-compatibility-matrix.md),
  [terminal-matrix/README.md](terminal-matrix/README.md), and
  [terminal-capability-security.md](workstreams/terminal-capability-security.md)
  so MVP readiness is the non-Windows launch matrix and Windows validation is
  deferred.
- Regenerated [terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md](terminal-matrix-collection-plan.md),
  [terminal-matrix-review-packet.md](terminal-matrix-review-packet.md),
  [windows-validation-plan.md](windows-validation-plan.md),
  [windows-validation-review-packet.md](windows-validation-review-packet.md),
  and [mvp-readiness-report.md](mvp-readiness-report.md) with
  `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-evidence-refresh`.

Decisions:
- Windows driver/scaffolding remains in MVP because it is already built and
  tested locally.
- Real Windows host validation is post-MVP evidence. The validation lane stays
  available, but `mvp-final-gate` should not fail because Windows captures are
  absent.

Findings:

  blocker: M2.10 reviewed real-terminal matrix coverage is incomplete at 0/8.
- Windows validation remains visible at 0/4 ready with `requiredForMvp: false`
  and `mvpStatus: deferred`.

- `dart tool/fleury_dev.dart mvp-readiness --json` passed and reported

- `git diff --check` passed.
